# Thread: One humanoid escapee, 1001001

### Theka — 2015-08-27T18:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-175881>

I've been reading and listening and sinking further into data overload here. Here is my epic part one. Starting with a personal history, I was born in 1963 in San Diego--normal natural childbirth, breastfed but a fussier baby than my 4 younger sibs. No early vaccines or antibiotics. We moved to Pittsburgh (Lat 40) when I was 4 and I grew up there. I had Rubella as a toddler, later chicken pox and then walking mono as a teen and recurrent mystery abdominal pains as a preteen, but other than a mouthful of amalgams was pretty healthy. I spent a lot of time walking in the woods and reading. I discovered Tae KwonDo in High School and lived to work out.
I married a German guy and moved to Germany in 1987, had a healthy, home-birth baby in 1990. We generally ate a high-carb diet and followed that effing food pyramid, except while I was pregnant, I was reading Adele Davis and eating lots of liver--even for breakfast. We settled in MeckPom (Lat 54) and I complained about swollen sore spots on my fingers that we attributed to doing dishes outside in cold weather. My finger nails also became painfully thin. I fantasized about vacations in the sun and I had a few bladder urgency complaints that gyno thought were normal for a mom. We moved back to Pittsburgh in 1997 and I had a 2nd healthy baby, another quick, natural home-birth. About 9 months later, I had a bout of optic neuritus which resolved without any treatment or mention of demyelinization. We moved into a big old barn of a house in the city and I developed a real estate investment business. In 2002, I 'sprained' my ankle although looking back, I my leg would simply turn to jelly and I'd fall. I would have to sit on the ground and wait until my leg felt like a leg again to stand. I thought it was a residual problem from the sprain but I'm not so sure now. I also had 2 minor car accidents--neither my fault, but both were head-on collisions, one with airbags deployed. I had chiropractic care afterwards, neck and back were fine but my left hand was partly numb. Eventually a spinal MRI showed white spots and I got a diagnosis of ms. I started on LDN (lowdose naltrexone) and various supplements. From that point, it has been a slow steady decline.

My maternal grandmother had ms with that followed a very similar progression. Interestingly, her doctor eventually told her that if she wanted any QOL, she needed to move away from Pittsburgh. I also know she seldom ate fish. My mother learned to cook fish in California, but we didn't eat it often, even as fishsticks, unless we had bass that we caught. Although we bought into the margarine health scam, we ate a lot of organic and gardened.

When we realized that the big old stairmaster of a house we lived in would not work and I had read studies showing that children relocating to lower latitudes prior to puberty took on the lower risk ratio for ms of the native population. So we moved to Panama(Lat 9) and learned Spanish. My husband was an IT bootcamp trainer, so he would fly to teach the classes. I had my amalgams and root canals removed in 2006. I still progressed, although oddly enough, the heat and humidity didn't bother me despite not sweating. I also didn't sunburn and my fingernails grew long and strong. I could spend 2-3 hours in the pool at noon with no consequences and without becoming super dark. When the economy tanked we eventually, after a global jobsearch, had to return to Germany (Lat 40) in March 2011. We moved into an apartment on the 17th floor in the outskirts of Frakfurt. We have a small garden plot next door and a park a block away. We look outside at green trees. In April, I noticed my nails were splitting and breaking and started supplementing vitamin D3. I was using a rollator but moving rather well. I was treated for ccsvi in Brooklyn, opening up several significant venous stenoses but not really improving any symptoms. By November, I started thinking about Terry Wahls and eating more kale. I tried her protocol (only version 1 at that time) and ate more liver, hearts and raw meat---and gained weight, clothes did not fit. I tried tmj/jaw alignment treatment and initially felt stronger, could walk further and stand longer. It also affected my bladder function some. In 2013, I felt good enough to plan a 2 week family holiday in Greece--and I learned some basic Greek). We drove to northern Greece, stopping only to see the Tesla museum in Belgrade. I mostly managed with my rollator and a walker in normal accommodations----no one had to carry me. I even got part way into the ruins at Philippi in the blistering hot sun! Last year, I rapidly lost ground. My muscles became weak and I could barely shuffle with the rollator. I was prescribed 65mg nature-thyroid and cortisol and iron. Cogfog also was significant. An ultrasound showed ijv blockages and a totally blocked renal vein (nutcracker phenomenon). We went back to Brooklyn and had my rijv ballooned, the left was totally blocked and couldn't be opened even with a rendezvous procedure. 2 stents were placed in my renal vein. We think the mystery ab pains in childhood could be attributed to the nutcracker.

So, now I can think again and have interest in doing things. My mobility still sucks as does my bladder, although it has been worse, it still isn't anywhere normal. I was running out of ideas in April and decided to try the candida protocol (Ann Boroch) because it is helping some people. The heat seems to bother me now. I still do not sweat but if I am in the sun too long, I just can't move. Cold weather has been difficult for years. I had raynauds phenomenon for years too. I just seem to be cold intolerant and recently heat intolerant too. (yay me) The candida protocol just didn't resonate with me although I figured it couldn't hurt--esp losing sugar and gluten. By June, I had read a lot more and David Perlmutter's Brainmaker seemed to make sense. I started eating fresh sauerkraut-onion salad with a kefir dressing to get cheap pre, probiotics. This felt good. He promoted the Future of Healing Conference and I was diligently listening and taking notes, when I heard Jack. Then I listened to him again with my 18 yr old son, then again with my husband. Eureka! I can safely say I really don't understand very much but I'm trying. We're Greek Orthodox and don't eat meat the first 2 weeks of August, so I figured that was a very easy time to try and eat lots of DHA. Currently, I can't really cook anymore. My left hand is useless and mobility is very limited. I usually plan more food on the weekends but very simple stuff week days. We've been making salmon or tuna spreads and seafood salad. My pants are a bit looser at the waist now. I wasn't ever overweight but the distribution wasn't cool. I've never had a weight problem or a shape problem and I really don't like it. I am not an elastic waist-mom jeans kind of person. I started getting up at sunrise. I wish I had written down the date, but about the 1st or 2nd week of August. I can only sit in front of the open window looking east, but it feels good. I am working on finding a property on the ground for us to move. It is a hard sell since we all love the view and everything is bloody expensive. But we rent here and so I'm looking for an investment property where we could live on the ground and have some rental income. It would be so much easier if I were mobile. We are going to look at a complex Monday with a 150 yr old house and a barn. I have been wearing amber glasses once the sun goes down and getting to bed by 11pm. I've ordered a cold facial mask to try and start some CT. CT is a real challenge for me. I have terrible uncontrolled reflex-like movements if I am splashed by hot or cold. I tried a 'cold' foot bath during a recent heat wave (39C) and just to get my feet into the cold tapwater was awful. I couldn't do it myself. The weak dead legs suddenly get a mind of their own! My husband finally just forced one foot at a time in and held them in until the shock was over and I felt nothing. I seldom get a shower as it isn't accessible(one of the good reasons to move!), but any shocking water splash causes a massive spastic response. If I am not holding on securely, I'll fall. I only sleep with a sheet most of the year although I like to have my neck and shoulders covered. In winter, I use a summer-weight quilt---often only over my shoulders and neck with a sheet for the rest of me.. We keep the windows open. It seems recently that my legs, below the knee are bothered by being covered by anything. If I shave my legs, the slightest stubble is almost painful, but if I don't the hair also bothers me. It is a damned if I do, damned if I don't. I don't sense temperature well there, but as the cold foot bath showed, I obviously still sense something. I just listened to a podcast with Jack on CT and there was a bit about T Wahls, ms and being ready for CT. Is there a point where CT is easier? Any way to ease into it without being able to get into a bath? Lately the brakes on the rollator aren't holding well enough for me to get my head in the sink to wash my hair, let alone dunk my face. My balance sucks. I feel good sitting in a chair. When I move, it all goes to hell.

Another little puzzle piece is my hair loss issue---handfuls every day along with super thin static-y almost singed looking arm hairs and thin uneven leg hair growth. Recently, I have about 10cm of normal hair above my left wrist, where the hair lays down normally. I've figured the hair was an indicator of screwed up hormones and once they get sorted, the hair will also improve. My hair is also almost all grey-white. I started to use henna in 2013 to make it thicker. I've been pretty good at coping mechanisms, but not so good at finding solutions. Right now, I'm seeing the sun in the morning and avoiding blue light in the evening and eating DHA every day. It is often 12-16C in the early morning and I sit in that cool air---when DH says it is too cold! I figure at least, I can try and acclimate to cooler temps w/o lots of 'appropriate' clothes. We've also been turning off our wifi at night, although there is plenty around us and a cell mast above.

Well, enough of the epic part one.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-08-27T19:41:19+0300

### Theka — 2015-08-27T21:52:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-175936>

Isolation tanks sound like something that might be available at a special clinic? Complicated. Not to whine, but that sounds incredibly beyond remotely possible. It also sounds horrifically miserable given my cold intolerance---thanks, but childbirth sounds easier. But personal feelings aside, I have no idea where to find that kind of tank. What else would it be used for?

If I can figure out how to get my face in cold water, maybe an ice pack on my neck, would that at least be enough of a start to be noticeable? If I could do that myself?? Maybe we could try and follow it with having my son put me in a tub in shorts and slowly add cold water. He is strong enough to lift me that way. He works out with a group of eastern Europeans! We have tried so much and spent so much money and I've gotten worse regardless. I don't have much family support with this yet. DH has switched to starting work at 7 and getting up early with me and once school starts, my son will have to get up early. I think part of the problem is that the environment is affecting my husband too. He is 51, ex-military, but way too tired and irritable and somewhat overweight with sleep apnea (in my unqualified opinion). He started a new job 9 months ago and is still scrambling. He is frankly skeptical. My sons are both grown although one still is at school and living at home. (the product of changing countries/languages). This is why I'm looking for any kind of quantifiable progress. I do the shopping online, so I am trying to change overall dietary choices more epi-paleo. But everything would change radically if they could see progress in me. It doesn't help that I move best in the morning when no one is home. By 6-7pm, my legs are shit. It gets better in an hour or two. I don't understand it, but it is really bad timing. Are there any baby steps I can do myself until I can get them onboard? I studied persuasion and propaganda, so I will eventually convince them! It just sucks to be so dependent.

### Theka — 2015-08-29T18:37:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176037>

Celebrating my birthday today with Peruvian ceviche to be followed with mini-heart and liver anticuchos. Diet is an easy target. I am still struggling to wrap my head around the idea of 'a disease of light'. How in the hell am I supposed to fix that? I've been starting the day at dawn, wearing uv glasses and I just got a cold facial mask. It doesn't seem to stay cold very long. I put my hands and wrists in cold water every time I pass the sink. It seems so little and I'm a mess. I needed to find this 10 years ago. Do I still even have a chance?

We are having a last blast of summer this weekend with temps in the 90s. It does make CT much more enticing! It is just much harder to sleep when it doesn't cool down much at night.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-05T18:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176549>

One example is some recent research from the Oklahoma Medical Research Foundation (OMRF). It reveals a new complexity to nerve cells in the brain that could affect future therapies aimed at _**altering mood and memory**_ in humans. It did not discuss the quantum mechanisms that are behind these thermodynamic changes that cause protein folding changes epigenetically by altering redox potential. **It did, however, link these flows to the action of calcium in signaling.**

OMRF scientist Kenneth Miller, Ph.D., studied the function of a common protein in these disease called CaM Kinase II in tiny roundworms called C. elegans. His research appeared in a recent issue of the journal Genetics.

“CaM Kinase II is very abundant in the brain, so it has been heavily studied,” Miller said. “But this is the first time anybody has seen results like this.”

Using a method called “forward genetics,” Miller’s lab randomly screened thousands of mutant worms for defects in neuropeptide storage and unexpectedly identified mutant worms lacking CaM Kinase II. Further analysis revealed that CaM Kinase II plays a significant role in controlling when and where neuropeptides are released from neurons. These peptides seem to be related to the **_“redox state”_** of the cell under the control of **_circadian signaling._** Circadian signaling is controlled by light and dark cycles. During the day time the DC electric current is very active in EEG signaling. We can also assess magnetic fields from mitochondria using magneto-encepholograms. At night time, optical signaling (optogenetics) seems to control CaM kinase II. So what happens when this enzyme is missing?

Neuropeptides are small protein-like molecules that nerve cells in the brain use to communicate with each other. Excellent signaling is a hallmark of health. Poor signaling is associated with poor health. Disruptions in those communication pathways can affect learning, memory, social behaviors and mood. They are created and stored in containers called _dense-core vesicles in cells._ Dense cores tend to be pigmented proteins that absorb and emit light. Under normal conditions they are only released from those containers in response to appropriate signals in the brain. These things are controlled by signaling from the cell membrane to the interior of the cell.

In Miller’s lab “they tagged the neuropeptides with a **_fluorescent protein_** so we could see where they went”. **All fluorescent proteins happen to ABSORB UV light frequencies.** “In the worms that were missing the gene that makes CaM Kinase II, the neuropeptides were _virtually missing altogether_ in the parts of the neurons where we expected them.”

That’s because without the protein, the dense core vesicles couldn’t hold onto the neuropeptides “[electrostatically](http://whatis.techtarget.com/definition/electrostatic-field)“. _Electrostatic binding is a quantum atomic effect involving both electric and magnetic field actions._ Instead, Miller found, they were all released before they got transported to their storage location, like an apple would release from a tree branch before it was ready. In humans, such an event would be extremely unpredictable, possibly even causing a psychotic break episode clinically. **This suggests why we might be why we see depression, mood disorders, and ADHD exploding today.**

“This is a very significant demonstration of how neurons and likely other neuroendocrine cells package neuropeptides, move them around the cell, and release them where they will be most effective,” said Michael Sesma, Ph.D., of the National Institute of Health’s National Institute of General Medical Sciences, which partially funded the research. “The high-resolution visualization inside entire living neurons achieved by Dr. Miller and his colleagues is a technical tour de force, and also demonstrates the enormous value of the genetic model system C. elegans for studying the internal workings of living cells.” This work was done with scanning electron microscopes that are able to show us the smallest subatomic scales of cells.

By understanding more about how neurons work, at this scale, we might be able to finely hone our targets when working with patients.

“Before this research was done, we didn’t even know that neurons had this special mechanism to control neuropeptide function,” he said. This is why it’s important to understand how neurons work, down to the subcellular and molecular levels.” I think he needs to shrink his perspective, further, to the subatomic level

### Theka — 2015-09-05T23:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176568>

Wow. So much to think about. Thank you. I will read it again in the morning, I'm about to pumpkin out here.

Just popped on to write a few comments. This evening, my son had his kungfu school over for a bbq. Weather was windy and cool (50s F). I went down for a couple hours--took a jacket but wore capri pants and a tank top. I didn't need the jacket! I actually sent a couple squall jackets down for others to wear. I wasn't uncomfortable and i was barefoot! I've only been able to take baby steps with the CT but cool! I was also shocked at the crap people choose to eat and drink. I guess I don't get out much! I had a veal steak and cevapcici--chilled spring water when I got back.

I've been sitting in the open window every morning looking at the sun in my sports bra for about 30 min. Yesterday, I got outside for an hour mid-morning and sat with my feet in the grass. School starts Monday, so outside will be harder to do except on weekends. I really want to move so I can get outside myself. Last night, I didn't really sleep well. I was restless. I only sleep with a coarse sheet and we had the windows open. Maybe the breeze bothered me---I wear a sleep mask, so it wasn't light. Even with disturbed sleep, I had enough energy all day.

### nonchalant — 2015-09-06T22:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176631>

> [Theka said:](/goto/post?id=176568)
>
> Last night, I didn't really sleep well. I was restless. I only sleep with a coarse sheet and we had the windows open. Maybe the breeze bothered me---I wear a sleep mask, so it wasn't light. Even with disturbed sleep, I had enough energy all day.

Theka, I have found that light on my skin does disturb my sleep, and it doesn't have to be as strong as moonlight. I purchased a red sheet and I love the way it makes me sleepy. In the mornings, before dawn, enough light creeps around the light-blocking shades to brighten the east-facing room a bit, and all I have to do is to remove the sheet from my arms or torso to wake up fully in a few minutes. If the shades are lifted, it takes less than a minute. But if I want to sleep longer, I just cover up and nod back off, even if the sun is already risen.

### Theka — 2015-09-07T17:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176664>

Interesting! I wish I had light-blocking drapes. The windows are recessed and awkward to hang curtains. We've tried to figure a good way to totally cover the glass to block solar gain during hot spells. I got the sleepmask for the interim, but it bothers me to have my arms/shoulders uncovered----wonder if that is because of the light! I have been getting up for sunrise everyday----I figure I really need my batteries charged, so I can't miss a morning. Now, it is also much cooler, so I also get a bit of cold with it. Sunrise time is around 7 am atm, and school just started when everyone needs to be gone before 7. Last month was so much nicer. I like sunrise at 6:30. I'm waking up easily though.

I am wondering about the effect of emfs and radiation from a laptop on bladder. I have a macbook air, so it uses wifi even if I'm using it on battery. I get that it isn't good but how bad is it? I try not to have it directly on my lap. I really can't wait to be able to measure it. My tri-meter has shipped but the say shipping will take a month.

### Theka — 2015-09-07T18:54:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-176679>

I seem to start off the day moving well---after the first 5 minutes. I am walking faster with the rollator, with better balance------until 6-7pm. On fact, when I get up to move and legs do not want to move and I drag myself to the loo, I know it must be around 7pm. It has been this way for quite a while and has not improved yet. Later, after a couple hours, it gets better. Lunch or Supper are irrelevant. Why? As good as I am doing earlier, by the time DH gets home, mobility is shit. It is frustrating.

### Theka — 2015-09-08T17:29:14+0300

My core muscles are holding me better---I thought I would need to slowly work and build them up. I haven't done any extra therapy/exercise. I seem pretty energetic in the morning and walked around the apartment more today. This is truly exciting. Nothing else has helped like this.

Welcome to the forum and your journal is interesting...you are being pointed in a great direction with all the comments posted....All I can add is this:

Keep going. Do the things you can with vigour. Each step builds on the last step. Little things end up being big things. Things that you are discarding could unlock pandora's box....

### Theka — 2015-09-11T21:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177008>

I haven't taken any CoQ10 recently, I'll order some. Sorry, I don't have any cell phone/iphone. The only drugs I take are LDN and that at 2.5mg and LD aspirin 3x/week. Of all the labs listed, what would be the order of priority?

I am really enjoying am sun and I'm persisting in sitting in front of the open window minimally clothed. I know air isn't the best for CT, but I figure it can't hurt although I'd prefer less wind. I've been taking my laptop and working there. I ordered a bunch of canned sardines to make into a spread for quick easy meals on gluten free crackers. I am starting to dislike dusk. I get tired but I can't sleep long because my legs/knees/hips hurt and wake me up. Sometimes I get up and go to the bathroom just to move my legs so I can try and sleep the rest of the night. This is usually after about 4 hours. On those rare nights when I sleep deeply through the night, I feel great. I suspect a lot of this is environment----magnetism, but I can't afford to do much there yet.

I have noticed that I am drinking more but seem to pee less. Maybe I'm becoming less dehydrated. This is so cool. I feel like I'm doing so little but stuff is changing! For so many years every change has been bad.

### JanSz — 2015-09-11T22:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177010>

Jack Kruse said:
**Muscle fatigue goes away with UV AM**

**...
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-clearing-lactic-acid.10911/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-clearing-lactic-acid.10911/page-2)**

To be able to benefit from UV light one must have good RBC.
RBC - red blood count.
If RBC are low one never absorb enough UV via their RBC's because porphyrins are not there in the deep skin to get it and deliver it to muscle.

### Theka — 2015-09-12T22:50:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177043>

I've noticed that I seem to be processing water differently. Since incontinence has been a big part of my ms experience from the beginning, I've watched my liquids for years. I typically would sip on a half a glass of water and pee like a horse anyway! Lately, I've been thirsty and enjoyed drinking several glasses of water without running to the bathroom. Before it seemed like I peed out more than I drank, now it seems reversed. It is so nice to simply enjoy a glass of cold water! A cup of coffee in the morning doesn't seem to bother me anymore either. The incontinence isn't history, but it is improved. The biggest change is that I get thirsty and can truly enjoy drinking water without feeling waterlogged!

Today, to make DH happy, we made gluten free waffles for breakfast. (I already had eaten some tuna earlier after sunrise) I had 1 waffle and some quark (soft fresh cheese) with blueberries. Either the dairy or the grains just didn't work for me. I got a mild headache and felt cold for most of the day----until we grilled marinaded chicken and pork steaks for dinner---with a handful of red raspberries from the garden.

### Theka — 2015-09-15T21:01:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177191>

Weather here is changeable, sun, clouds, rain----temps 10-21C (50-68F), maybe a bit cooler at night. Some days, I feel fine, and other days, I just feel totally chilled. I am trying to dress lightly, but some days are really hard. It often seems to get worse in the evenings. Maybe as I get a bit tired, the internal fires burn lower.

I am trying to move us to the ground. Work won't allow us to totally move yet, so I'm looking for ground floor away from the cell towers. I figure maybe this way we can at least take a week somewhere in the sun in January/Feb and check out various locations. I wish we could go south right away---I really kind of dread the winter, it is so dark----guess that ought to be good for my melatonin! But in the same way, I dread the 35+C days here. My feet swell when it is hot, especially my left.

### Theka — 2015-09-22T14:36:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177559>

Well, we had a cloudier week, last week. I got up anyway but I missed feeling that red early sunlight hit my eyeballs. I was dragging a bit over the weekend although we did go out to Ikea and drive around the small enclave of 200 year old buildings where we are looking at a homestead-- a property with house and old barn, garages arranged around a courtyard. It is the most country I have found in the city and we are hoping to convince the bank to lend us money for it. Yesterday was gloriously sunny---finally! I sat in front of the open window all morning. I only managed to do the ice pack once last week. I do my wrists under cold water several times a day. This morning there was just very filtered sunlight at dawn. My bladder has been bothering me with sudden urgency, no warning, just bang, NOW! I have had a bit more dairy recently, so maybe that is behind it. Otherwise, I'm mostly eating tuna and herring for breakfast.

This morning, I decided to try and wash my hair in the sink. I haven't been able to wash it since early June because I was so unsteady, even perched sitting on the rollator. I was happy to find I could balance and shampoo! I then rinsed and cooled the water down until it was cold. Cold water on the back of my head prompted an instant sharp headache there. I stopped and it faded. Is this normal? Should I have kept on anyway or is just try again another time and build up? It was strange that it didn't hurt the top of my head, just the back. I skipped the blow dryer to avoid the nnemfs.

My CoQ10 arrived yesterday, so I started that too.

### Inger — 2015-09-23T16:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177647>

I almost never blow dry my hair anymore either.....  just let it air dry

Can you eat raw heart instead of taking CoQ10? I bet it will do you better
It is delicious as carpaccio

Lets hope you get that house... it would be so much better........
I would gradually build up the cold exposure to your head.. are you doing face dunks? They are super easy
or icing the carotids...

### Theka — 2015-09-23T18:17:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177660>

We eat heart and liver, not exactly raw, but 'scared' on the grill! We marinade it Peruvian style (anticuchos). I'm sure it loses a bit, hopefully not too much. I'll have to try serving it as carpaccio! I wonder how raw pork does? In Germany, you can get fresh raw pork like beef tartare but served on bread with onions. I usually have it piled on gluten free crackers.

I have tried to do face dunks but have difficulty---the angle is wrong for me sitting on the rollator and I really have an issue with my nose under water! I know I need to just grow a pair and get on with it, but..... I got over using a neti pot, so I don't know why this seems different.

Today was very grey---thick cloud cover. ugh I spent the day working out a plan for renovating the property so we have talk to the bank. I wish I could do some of the work. I used to rehab properties. DH is skeptical, especially as winter is approaching. We have to be able to get some of it rented quickly because property prices are so obscene here. We have done this so many times already, we have truly enjoyed living in a nice pretty apartment. I have convinced my husband that we need to move to a place we own, but this is a big project. We are meeting with the tax guys next week to find out the tax implications. I know it is better to be down on the ground and obviously not have cell towers over me, bu up here, I see such fabulous sunrises! We are above the trees, and I love the super early red light that slowly turns yellow then bright white. On the ground, I think I'll miss the color.

### Theka — 2015-09-24T21:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-177794>

Hearts, in general, seem to be the highest rated food---of course raw minimizes loss, but I haven't found the actual numbers. Do we need daily CoQ10 intake or is a 2-3x/week enough if we're not deficient?

Today was a grey dawn--no flashes of color at all but an hour later, the sun shone through the clouds! I got a good hour early. Then for the first time in years, I was able to hand wash a shitload of dishes! The sink and counter were full---my son isn't keeping up at all---teenagers. I am slow because of my numb/weak left hand, but I managed a couple hours and am very happy! I figured I had to try early because I move well early. Once again, around 6pm, my legs are weak and I have difficulty. I had fish for breakfast and cured sausage/french cheese for supper and I'm feeling pretty good with that. Drinking gerolsteiner water---cold if we can manage it.

I am wondering about several things I've been doing. I take LD aspirin 3x/week to prevent restenosis after CCSVI. This used to be daily but recently, I was bruising easily and had mystery, for no obvious reason nosebleeds, so it was reduced.

I also have taken LDN at various doses for years---with no dramatic obvious benefits. I have read many explanations for what LDN is supposed to do, like provide a temporary endorphin blockade. That makes some sense for evening dosing, but now many say am dosing is also effective. It doesn't add up to me. What is the quantum answer?

Since April there has been buzz about high-dose biotin for progressive ms. The studies used 300mg/day. Patients are of course trying it. Some report increased stiffness and spasticity and most feel they need to just 'push' through it. Obviously progressive patients are desperately looking for answers. But I am increasingly hesitant because nothing seems to do as much as light.

Another weird thing I wonder about is my feet foot gets cold every evening, sometimes really cold, sometimes just cool. Last night it was icy when I went to bed. I was laying on my side with that foot against the mattress. When I turned over an hour and a half later, the mattress was cold where my foot had been! It didn't warm up at all. It was still cold at 2:30 when I went to the loo. By 6:30, both feet are always warm, even with just a sheet.

I also notice in the evenings, my right hip flexor and aductors get painfully tight. If we stretch them out, it is ok??? just weird. I really wish I could do a consult and upgrade, but maybe next time I can play. sigh

### Theka — 2015-10-01T10:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-178339>

It has been 2 months on DHA and am UV! I am feeling better! I am wading through Jack's blogs and starting to get it. It is now noticeable enough that family is starting to come along. CT is slow. I can do my wrists easy but I now have enough core strength and stability to get the top of my head in the sink! It still gives me an instant headache but it doesn't last when I pull my head out. I've been able to do the dishes for about a week now! I am also working on getting us moved. I'm chasing financing atm. Already talked with contractors and architects to get a rough estimate of costs and possibilities. The tax structure seems to work in our favor. I just need to find a bank that will play with us.

### Theka — 2015-10-10T23:22:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-179018>

Interesting couple days---My brother-in-law is moving back to Michigan. This marks the end of family living nearby. It has been so nice to be able to get together, whether watching Formula 1, playing board games or grilling in the garden. We will miss them. I got my hair cut short this morning-----the henna is gone. I am now foxy silver. No time for bs haircolor. I really don't care and don't want to waste time on that crap anymore. Hope I can suck up more uv this way. We met with the realtor--- and shocked him with our offer, but my numbers are correct! I found they had counted the square meters wrong! So my offer is within 10-30k and that is just bargaining space! We will meet again next week! I don't know whether they will agree to the price, but it is a fair price. I will keep looking. It is suppose to be sunny again tomorrow. I really don't like grey mornings.

I definitely do best in the am. By evening, my legs are weak regardless of whether or not I've sat in a chair or done stuff. Yesterday was crappy. I had to pee way too often. My legs were shit. Made french onion soup for dinner, served with a German red wine (trollinger!). After a crap day, I slept really well, albeit shorter because I got to bed late. Today has been better, although still peeing too often. What changes day to day? I eat DHA every morning for breakfast and often again later. I drink 1 cup of black coffee. I get to bed between 10 and 11. Except for Sat night when Dr Who is on, I wear blue blockers after sunset. I've been doing face dunks. I ended up just putting a clothespin on my nose--it doesn't really hurt when it is cold. I just couldn't get past the idea of water in my nose. (I don't understand why a neti pot is different, but it is.)

### JanSz — 2015-10-11T00:41:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-179020>

Month ago, 9/10/2015 I posted post #42 (above).
Any news on that?

### Jude — 2015-10-11T03:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-179032>

What do you think about using a black light to supplement the uva/b, especially for those grey days?

### Theka — 2015-10-16T14:00:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-179645>

I've been reading and thinking about black lights, red lights, and reptile lights. I just haven't been sure of which frequencies and type of bulbs to get. There are dozens of reptile lights. Are CFL black lights ok? I just don't want to buy the wrong stuff. When should I be using these bulbs to supplement? Is it one type for day supplementation and another to minimize evening light damage?

I haven't gotten anywhere with labs. It is on the list. It has been a very busy month so far. We have family moving back to the US that we will miss a lot. I have learned the RE market in the past month and a half. I found a few possibles and we made an offer on one last night. If it is agreeable, I have to get financing worked out within 10 days. Closing works differently here than in the US which will give us some time to get building permits--it is a historic property. There is so much to learn and it must be done quickly because the entire idea hinges on getting rental income quickly. This would give us the opportunity to live on the ground and have some control over lights and nnemf. I think we can also use it to fund our later relocation south.

I have found a source for fish that delivers! They offer 1.3kg mixed boxes of assorted smoked fish for cheap. I am anxious to see what we get, but I figure anything is good----they also make a salmon salami! Our package is arriving today! I also ordered some different blue blocking glasses. I am craving seafood and really miss the sun on cloudy mornings. I am feeling more energetic but my muscles are still stiff. Walking is painfully slow.

I wake up warm and gradually cool, especially from my knees down where my legs just get really cold to the touch. It takes hours in bed to get both my legs warm. By 3am, one is usually still cold, usually the left. It doesn't seem to matter if I wear socks, shoes or go barefoot.

### JanSz — 2015-10-16T17:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-179660>

historic property----------> mold

..
My opinion about blue blocking glasses. =confusion
Blue light during day (sunrise to sunset) is desirable.
Blue light after sundown is not desirable, do not block anything, just use red light to see way to bed, specially with so many health problems.

Using full spectrum light one can extend short fall and winter days to 12 up to 14 hours.
Using full spectrum light one can brighten dark days.
Personally I settled at Solar Glo 160W.

Pages 15 & 18
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-15](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-15)

Numerous of this type bulbs can be strategically placed around residence.
Practically it is ok to be within two of this type bulbs within distance of 3' indefinitely.

Most of the energy in those bulbs is converted to IR, heat, that will reduce heating bill.

................................

........

### Theka — 2015-11-18T20:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-182276>

Well, finally I've got a few minutes. We have celebrated our Thanksgiving early here with all of the accompanying cooking and baking. My son and I baked 3 different gluten free pies, pumpkin, dutch apple and pecan. The turkey was a ballotine with homemade sage sausage, feta, macadamia farce. It was 2 days of work but I was ok and played a board game over wine in the evening! This was totally different from last year.

I am not moving quite as well as I was a month ago. I suspect the shorter days are to blame. I've ordered blacklight bulbs. I got a short haircut so dunking in the sink is much easier. I feel like I'm not slipping anymore but I don't feel like I'm making progress either.

Our 3rd fish ordered arrived! We've been happy with the mystery boxes of smoked fish/eels, so that is what we've been eating. It has to be better than canned tuna/salmon.

I wonder what high-dose biotin does for progressive ms patients. How does it work? It seems to help a majority of patients but improvement don't seem to be durable. That could be a light problem, of course. The pilot study was done in France with 100-300mg/day. Improvements were generally after a couple months. What is the biotin doing?

I also wonder about the mechanism behind LDN. For some, it is a wonder, others it just seems to slow progression. Some is seems to do nothing. What is it doing? I have heard it causes a temporary endorphin blockade which the body then overcomes and the extra endorphins are behind the benefits. Now they say it doesn't matter if you take it am or pm because it works differently.

I think both of these have to truly be working differently than the experts think.

### fitness@home — 2015-11-18T21:21:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-182277>

Nice update Theka. I can't answer your questions about biotin or LDN in regards to MS, but someone will chime in.

I agree, short hair does make dunking in the sink easier!

### Theka — 2015-11-19T09:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-182315>

I've heard that MD1003 which is the biotin 'drug' being developed is thought to have a twofold effect, activating acetyl-CoA carboxylases that are the rate-limiting enzymes in the production of fatty acids required for myelin synthesis, and also activating the Krebs cycle in axons that have been demyelinated, increasing their energy supply.

### Theka — 2015-12-02T13:22:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-183040>

I still am not really sure of how to supplement light here. The past month has been increasingly difficult. I managed to cook a shrimp bisque myself yesterday evening. This doesn't sound like much, but it is. It was a simple trial of an idea I have for a lobster bisque I want to do Christmas eve. The past month, I have been slowing down, physically and mentally. I hate feeling dull. If I could stay in bed longer, I'd want to sleep all day but I get very uncomfortable after 4-5 hours and after 7-8, I have to get out. Lately, getting out in the am has been hard. My legs just aren't there when I wake up. This morning, I had to sit for 15min, waiting for my legs to feel like legs to stand up and get to the loo. If I try sooner, my legs won't support me and I either hold myself with my arms or collapse to the floor. I am usually warm when I wake up and after I sit for an hour, I can move pretty well. It seems I need to cool down and be vertical for a bit.

It reminds me of a very early symptom. I thought I had sprained my ankle because I would just suddenly fall--no warning and I couldn't just get up. I remember sitting in front of a liquor store on the sidewalk refusing help because 'I had to wait until my leg felt like a leg again'. My leg just was like wet spaghetti, like trying to make a rag doll stand. Sometimes my legs 'feel' strong and other times they feel different and I know they won't hold. It used to be that once I got them straight, I could just lock my knee and stand enough to get to the toilet. Along with this, incontinence is a problem. Other than run to to sun, which is our ultimate goal, I don't know how to manage.

It is hard because I have other ms patients asking me for answers and I'm struggling. I've upgraded to listen to the webinars but I don't know enough to know what to ask. My leg collapsed yesterday making me land hard on the toilet seat--unfortunately where I had developed a pressure sore in April. It seems healed on the surface but it is still tender and I guess vulnerable. Even a memoryfoam pillow is painful to sit on. But I still managed to make a wonderful shrimp bisque, creamy with just a touch of fire!

### Lahelada — 2015-12-02T14:32:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-183045>

> I've been reading and thinking about black lights, red lights, and reptile lights. I just haven't been sure of which frequencies and type of bulbs to get. There are dozens of reptile lights. Are CFL black lights ok? I just don't want to buy the wrong stuff. When should I be using these bulbs to supplement? Is it one type for day supplementation and another to minimize evening light damage

did you see the ubi 24 picture of the black light the doc uses ? It is a cfl and to my mind they are the better type. The tubes are usually stronger andtherefore a bit more of a headache to position the docs light is a Duracell. I will find you the exact spec .Rule of thumb the less illumination you get from a black light bulb the better. I will buy a Chinese one by mp electric that states 340-380 nm as I cannot he the Duracell ones.my suspicion is that it may well help you to get out of bed in the am . don't forego getting out in the open air though. As far as timing is concerned I will be playing around with times. When on the tablet it will be on,rainy days too. Start small and work out what feels best.
Congrats on the bisque. A great re ipe idea and achievement.
Rooting for you.
I had another thought. Do you use the Epsom saltwater mix for your skin when you are out in the sun?. I soul even consider an Epsom SLT foot bath immediately straight after getting up still sitting on the bed if someone can prepare that for you. It does not need to be hot so you can have the mix prepared the night before if am time is precious.

### Theka — 2015-12-02T15:33:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-183049>

Thank you so much for responding. It is getting tougher here.
When are you using the blacklights? Morning?
I need to find a way to add more bulbs here. We have huge east/south-east windows and a chandelier/wall sconces. So we don't have normal light fixtures for standard bulbs. I've been using lots of candles because I figure that light is safe but they don't help during the grey days. In the kitchen, I have halogen task lights.
I have been at minimum getting in front of an open window every morning even rainy stormy mornings. Getting outside is difficult---one big reason I am trying to relocate.
Why the epsom salts? Is magnesium chloride ok instead? I have it in salt flakes and liquid form in a spray bottle is easy to manage.

### fitness@home — 2015-12-02T16:09:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-183055>

Theka, I am using black lights in the morning the first hour I am awake (get up at 4 AM). I feel it is a positive thing.

Grey days used to be the bane of my existence when I lived in Michigan - November thru March. February was the absolute worst month for me mood and energy-wise. Since moving to Texas the SAD symptoms have subsided during the fall/winter months.

Still don't like grey days (we just had a run of them) but sunshine is on for today. Can't wait for 11 AM and my lunch break to get outside.

### Lahelada — 2015-12-02T16:40:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-183059>

"What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally in humans?

UV light lowers adrenalin and norepinephrine the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system.

What else does it do at the same time inside a cell to improve the EZ?

UV photons re zip collagen that cortisol initially released at 4AM to begin the process of waking us up.

Why does that occur?
Because blue light stimulates water flows through AQA-4 gates between glial cells and neurons to wake us up. That process needs to be curbed by light and nothing else.

When it is not curbed, seizures can occur. Depression can also present as a symptom.
This is why vagal stimulation works to curb seizures and treat depression too!

Light between 465nm and 500 nm began to unwind to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep.
AM sunrise has equal parts of blue, green and red light.
The blue light balanced by red light is what wakes us and turns on hormone release in the pituitary gland.
Uv light is what turns the effect off........and make depression better."

I just copied that from Lilreddgirls post on "explain jacks sun to me" It may help you find your solution.

### Theka — 2015-12-02T19:32:35+0200

### Theka — 2016-01-09T20:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-185870>

I've been thinking about my history and where and how I got sick. I think there were a few early flickers of trouble in my 20s while I was living in Bavaria and later Mecklenburg. I started wearing contacts--long-wearing ones for a week at a time there. I stopped this mid-nineties and moved to Pittsburgh. It was in Bavaria that we got our first computer---a Nec 386sx. I didn't spend a lot of time on it, but I did use a typing tutor program often. When we moved to Mecklenburg, we initially lived in the barn and worked on the house/yard. We had limited electric and no phone until we bought a cell phone that was as big as a shoe box! I didn't really use the computer. We did have a tv and watched StarTrek every afternoon. When we moved to Pittsburgh for work in 1996, I was expecting. I started to use the computer more since my husband started working in IT. I had optic neuritis. I started investing in houses, rehabbing and renting them. I did more on the computer but also read, made jewelry and refinished furniture. I preferred to walk around town. I did gardening/landscaping work. I was working all of the time and busy with 2 young boys. When I was finally diagnosed after a mri in 2002, I was sure I would do well----I didn't eat a crap diet--I was healthy.

My research convinced me that for the kids' sake, as well as mine, we needed to move to the sun. We opted for Panama and moved in June 2005. At that point, I was using a cane for stability when walking, although May 2005, I flew to Panama and forgot my cane as I loaded the car to go to the airport! I had to buy one at Sol de India because I knew I couldn't walk fast enough to cross the street without a prop to slow traffic. I didn't walk much anymore, it was harder and tiring. I gradually starting spending a lot of time online. By October 2007, I was spending pretty much all day online with an hour or two break midday in the pool. Walking was tiring and I avoided any distances. I did walk around the pool, cleaning algae with no complaints. I never got sunburned or too hot. In 2009, I was still driving, but walking with a rollator. I would just toss it in the back seat. In May 2010, I started an online receptionist job, spending all day at the computer answering the phone. I would park my rollator next to my desk but could still manage to go to the bathroom and shower without it. I could manage to travel alone. In March 2011, we moved to Germany.

When I arrived, I could walk shorter distances with the rollator and often used it to rest while cooking. I could sit on a bench. Our flat was on the 17th floor with a view of the park and rivers but I couldn't get outside myself. I switched to using a laptop and spent my days online. Looking back now, my disability has increased more rapidly since moving. I can no longer travel alone. I need an electric lift bed and chair to stand up. I wish I had known about Jack when I lived in Panama. I had a location where I could have soaked up uv. Here, I'm in a nnemf hellhole at 50N. I think I understand that even though I lived in Panama, I really wasn't taking advantage of it like I needed to. I have been thinking about moving here. I don't really want to live here. We looked for jobs all over the globe, but this was the only offer after years looking. Our youngest still has 2 years of school here. We really can't move until he graduates. Every time, we have changed school systems/countries, he is set back. I feel like a rat in a maze with no exits. I need sun and seafood. Can I really fix this in my condition? If I were just a bit more fit, I could try and go south alone but I can't manage alone. We can't afford help. I can try and find a reasonable place to live on the ground, but the whole area is seriously overpriced. It is really hard to wrap my head around the idea of 500,000 for a lousy duplex or condo. There are some nice places/reasonable prices in Greece but no work there, besides we really only speak a few words of Greek. If I could visit someone in the south, how long would it take to see improvement? Can I really improve enough? Can I minimize the damage here until my son finishes and then move somewhere? I've ordered a quantlet. Can it do enough to offset the negatives here? I feel so trapped and so stupid. I had a pool in the tropics for 5 years and didn't know what I had. I did know that sun made me feel good unless I got too hot. I haven't sweat properly for years. I guess I got enough uv to slow it down in Panama. We didn't eat nearly as much fish/seafood as I expected though. Everybody eats chicken, rice and beans there. Now I seemed to be trapped in a negative spiral.

I have a couple other questions. With regard to the PVN, could the atlas affect it? People had had various improvements with atlas alignment treatment. Could PVN explain it? Second, about cooling just a few degrees C making a big difference, what about people with chronically lower body temperatures?

### Theka — 2016-01-12T16:59:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-186081>

I appreciate the suggestions, although I am unable to implement them all. Like seeing Dr Hertoghe in Belgium. I think I need to clarify my situation. I have deteriorated significantly since moving from Panama. I cannot leave the apartment myself. I can walk with a rollator about 25 feet, at best. I shop online. Since I started following Jack in August, I have been able to do a bit in the kitchen, washing dishes and yesterday, I pan seared some tuna steak and a couple scallops! This is a big improvement as I hadn't be able to do anything there the previous year. In September, I actually was feeling bored and walked a few laps up and down the hall here. Unfortunately, now I am struggling and lucky to manage 25 ft. I figure it is due to the light, or lack thereof, since the equinox. My husband is very cautiously supportive. He is having sleep issues and I am sure he is significantly affected by this environment too, compounded by the stress of a handicapped wife. We both struggle with negotiating the health and insurance system here. When we lived here before, we were young, healthy and privately insured and everything was simple. We will have been married 29 years in February.

My diet approach has been to shift to more seafood/fish. I have also been a posterchild for morning uv! August was easy because sunrise coincided with when we needed to be up anyway. I have been struggling to figure out where to move to, knowing my husband's job is largely based here atm, and my son still has a couple years before he graduates. This is tougher because I can't go anywhere alone. I think it is working because my husband has started picking up a seafood snack for a treat for us---smoked salmon or grilled fish or shrimp! He also drives to work with the window down to get sun.

Our diet is pretty good imho. We don't eat processed stuff. It is stuff like bigos, stewed kale, liver, shrimp or seafood bisque and chowders or various other seafood dishes, loads of onions and garlic and other veggies and herbs. I got lobster bodies before christmas and made a lobster stock for bisque. I figure it has to be pretty full of goodness, the bodies simmered 24hrs! The bodies aren't expensive and they are full of flavor. I keep a reserve in the freezer! I drink local mineral water and a coffee with heavy cream in the morning. Jack says the light is more important but I figure a real food diet can only be a plus and besides I enjoy cooking. It turns out Frankfurt is a major inland 'port' for imported seafood and I'm getting good at preparing it. I think I overdosed on the smoked fish in the Fall so I'm giving it a break.

I am dunking my head in cold water just about every day but that is not being so quickly adopted! Oddly, when we were dating, I was always cold and he wore a t-shirt and leather jacket all winter!

### Theka — 2016-01-14T17:43:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-186278>

Jack just posted this on fb, "To be intuitive is to possess the characteristic of anticipation. You become able to see trends before most. Your decisions are better with less information. Paucity of information becomes a benefit because it limits choice eliminating decisions while elevating satisfaction. Foresight of phenomenon and power over them depend on knowledge of their sequences. How things are connected, related and correlated. It has little to do with any notion we may have formed respecting their origin or inmost nature. Life is about correlated novelties and seeing that trend without our eyes. Light builds trend foresight by creating dopamine."

Am I right to be encouraged by this? I have been really good at anticipating trends. I think I am still good. Maybe my dopamine isn't totally trashed. I am still quite lost with all of this physics and especially electricity. Those have never been my fields. But I think by starting with light and DHA, I can slowly claw my way to the surface. You can't see trends in areas where you are ignorant. I have always loved to read. I will find a way.

### Theka — 2016-01-20T18:11:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-186900>

Well, finally I can post again! Our internet has been off since last Thursday. Loads has happened. My knee gave out and I fell last week and seem to have bruised a rib. My legs have been feeling weak and I don't trust them. My left foot/ankle has also swollen a lot. Far worse than it has ever been before. I don't know what has changed. I usually only have trouble with swollen feet in the heat.

My son doesn't think he is going to make the cut here for university, due to spelling errors destroying his grades. He still wants to study science though. So after talking it through, we may try and send him to do a senior year in the States. I think this may change everything for us here.

### Theka — 2016-01-20T21:41:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-186923>

Jack says that "Altering your environment to minimize the light effects and total avoidance of nnEMF until the plaques and symptoms reverse." How quickly do symptoms reverse? I was doing better until the Equinox and things have been slipping since. I only started trying this in August, so I was already on the down slope of light here. I get that going on a vacation won't suffice but is it a matter of months, years or decades? I have to figure out how to do this.

### Theka — 2016-01-22T17:59:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187092>

I think things are starting to shift. It looks increasingly likely that my son will go to the states next year. My husband asked last night if I wanted to move to Stuttgart?! I answered no, there isn't enough more sun to be worth the effort and showed him the sun maps from [http://www.businessinsider.com/map-...the-us-that-get-the-most-sunshine-2014-6?IR=T](http://www.businessinsider.com/map-here-are-the-parts-of-the-us-that-get-the-most-sunshine-2014-6?IR=T) He said he didn't want to move somewhere we didn't really speak the language, eliminating southern Europe (besides the abysmal economics there). Then he started singing 'all my ex's live in Texas.....sweet home Alabama'.....etc. So the initial seeds of another transatlantic move have been planted. I really hope I will be able to heal because political environment there appalls me. We will have to see what kind of job opportunities we can find. The furthest south that we have family is NC and I think the Gulf coast seems better, job permitting. We'll see what happens. We still haven't paid for the last international move yet and we just took delivery of a new car. It would be really awesome to get a moving allowance for once. DIY was easier when I could do stuff. Even in Panama, I packed boxes. Buying new appliances gets really old. I've bought so many irons, vacuums, coffee makers......ugh

Now that still leaves me trying to not completely fall apart here. I wish I had a quantlet now. My legs are giving me problems. The joints are painful to move and my left foot/ankle is still swollen. I have iced it, elevated it, and used my theracycle to try and get the fluid moving. We've massaged it, all to no avail.

### Theka — 2016-01-24T01:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187218>

Today my foot was so swollen I couldn't get any shoes/boots on. I wore slippers to go shopping. Mostly it is the ankle that is so thick. I'm going to try ice, the theracycle and then compression socks overnight and hope I can get some kind of shoe on for church. Maybe kineseo tape would help too. My joints hurt---legs, hips. I am so stiff and where in September, I felt strong and was walking around the apartment, now I don't move unless I have to. I feel weak and drag myself around. I still do the dishes once/day but if bed was more comfortable, I'd want to stay there.

The next step is to find a job so we can move. I don't know where to begin. Years ago, we hunted on monster.com but now I guess we need to network, find a headhunter or something, get the word out that we want to relocate to the US. Cybersecurity is a good field, maybe some southern University??? He is currently working at the German stock market for Deloitte. That project seems o go on forever but at least it is close to home.

We had a 20€ coupon for seafood for this month, so we bought a several kilos of shrimp and langostinos, bought swordfish steaks to have for dinner too....still ended up costing way too much. I want to make coconut shrimp curry next weekend for our annual Groundhog Day movie nite. It is hard. We have struggled to really get the boys settled here and it has worked out. I think they feel at home here and now we have to totally change everything again. We just took delivery of a car that we ordered back in July. I also just got an induction cooktop and oven with a pull out drawer type door which I love! It seems that we finally have things together and it is time to start over again. Our 29th anniversary is next month. I think we have to plan on this move being our last. I really don't know the southern states much. I was at a conference in Houston once, and once in New Orleans. I have driven through parts of Florida, Jacksonville, St Augustine. We spent several vacation on Hilton Head Island in SC, but I really don't know what it is like to live there. Where should we look? Sure it depends upon the job, but where should we begin? It is hard and even harder long distance, especially at our age. But DH needs new boots, maybe Texas is the place to go!

### Theka — 2016-01-25T19:28:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187343>

I've posted on fb about the lack of excellence in the tropics. Singapore is different but that could be due to their years of authoritarian leadership. Since the renaissance, education and innovation have largely come from countries further from the equator. Maybe it is just cultural but it isn't a challenging environment. Shouldn't those peoples have more dopamine and make better choices?

While we lived in Panama, my son built a tesla coil from an old tv and a beer bottle capacitor for the National Science Fair. He did demonstrations like in a science center. Another kid did a city planning project with traffic analysis and models. There were a few excellent projects, but none won an award. The awards went to the dancing girls-----in a fucking science fair! That was when we flew him to the US where during a 10 day vacation, he got his GED and took the SAT. Maybe the country is concentrating on developing the tourism market, but research is done by the Smithsonian Tropical Institute! Why aren't the tropical areas beacons of excellence? They seem like great places to retire but not to raise a family. Within a couple years of our moving, at least 3 of the girls in the neighborhood had babies.....one was at least 18 although not in a long term relationship. As technology becomes more available, this downward trend will get worse.

### Theka — 2016-01-26T18:39:14+0200

Well, this was interesting. On fb, I have been told to read some history! No one has talked about light and DHA and human excellence. It seems most people believe the more tropical peoples have just been relentlessly screwed and are hopeless. This may not matter to a young person looking for adventure or even a young family, but when their kids start to think about careers and spouses, I think these places will suddenly be less attractive. When instead of studying to be a doctor or whatever, the kids look at fixing cars, selling lottery tickets or if they are really ambitious working in a bank! People move to the tropics with their northern educations but what happens next?

### Theka — 2016-01-26T19:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187392>

I've been struggling pretty much since October. I was improving before then. It is small changes at first, I just don't feel as good. Before, I was excited to see what changed each month. I first tried to not lose, then not lose too much. Now it is survive until Spring. I am desperate for sun. I don't know how to change things. My kids don't want to move now. Life gets complicated with girlfriends. It is easy to move with little kids. It is very hard to think about moving away from your kids. It is different when they move away from you as they grow up.

My feet are hurting now all of the time. My standing is wobbly and my legs are weak. I have fallen again and bruised my ribs. I am miserable. At least the sun came out for a bit this morning. It always helps. I also dunk my head in cold water---I still hate it---well it actually is ok once my head is in, but I look at the water and ugh. But I do it. I've got a reptile light now but I can't tell what it is doing for me. My family sees the state I'm in but they can't tell if this is just 'normal' progression and I'd be getting worse regardless. I am always hopeful and I believe that with better light, I'd improve, but how do I prove it? I am asking to totally uproot the family and tear it apart. We are a close family. If I could travel alone, I'd go myself for a while and test it, but I can't manage alone anymore. I can't even feed the cats myself.

### Theka — 2016-01-26T20:19:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187413>

The trouble is I can't manage alone. We can't afford to take lots of vacations even if he didn't have to work but he'd have to go with me and we can't do that. Looking back, I have always been desperate for the sun in January. Even 25 years ago, I booked a package trip to Morocco--not the great cities, just Agadir for the sun. I wish I had known then what I know now. If we had been able to find a job anywhere else, we wouldn't have moved back to Germany. I need sun but how can I leave my kids? We have no one in the US Gulf south, but I we speak the language. Moving to Greece, Italy or Spain is expensive and I only speak a few words of the language. There are no jobs there. My sons want to stay in Germany. I know they are big now but I don't want to leave them. They aren't quite independent, my youngest is still in high school. None of us like the political situation in the US either. My husband would move again but he isn't really sure it will help. How can I guarantee I'll get better? I progressed in Panama although I wasn't following Jack then. I know if I hadn't progressed there, he would be planning a move. We have tried so many things and spent so much money and I am a mess. I also think the next job would have to be until retirement. It is so hard.

### Theka — 2016-01-26T23:10:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187425>

I think the most obvious gauge of my state is that I can't read the science atm. I can't concentrate on it. It reminds me of the Dorothy Sayer's story, Hangmans Holiday, where there victim slowly descends into a blubbering helpless mass when her evil doctor/husband withholds thyroid. He does it every year so she can regain her wits and then know she is slipping back.

### Theka — 2016-01-27T21:23:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187494>

Well, my son definitely wants to stay here for school. That means 2 more years until he graduates and starts university. I think we need to move to the sun...some place with good flight connections for family to visit. My husband says he is willing to move if it will help me feel better. We just need to find a good job and house. I am thinking Florida might be a good fit. The tough part is hanging on until we can move.

### Theka — 2016-01-27T21:32:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-187496>

We used to live in Mecklenburg but the short days drove me crazy! It was a small village--less than 100 people! They turned the street lights off after midnight! We didn't get much DHA being inland. I think I need to be in a lower latitude. The sun really affects me a lot. Even years ago, I was reading about SAD and dreaming of vacations in the sun. Maybe the rural location helped slow the development of symptoms. I only had a few there. It got nasty later.

### Theka — 2016-02-03T20:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188097>

I took a few days off to promote the quantlet and my video, not really anxious to spend more time in front of a screen atm. I am trying to hold on. I hope the Groundhog speaks for us here as well. I need Spring. There was a bit of sun---about 45min today and I sat in it! I wish I could just crawl in a hole and wait for Spring.

### Theka — 2016-02-07T21:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188480>

Yesterday, we were blessed with a gorgeous sunny day! We had the windows wide open and sun streaming in all morning! It makes such a huge difference. It was also a busy day. My son's visitor from China's last day with us. I decided to cook an American meal, so I spent the day making chili, ribs, and loaded twice baked potatoes. I make my own dry rub and chili stock so it takes time. Then we watched a movie that she recommended, Lost in Thailand. This morning there was only a muted red orange glow at sunrise. We are back to grey again. I want to move now but we can't leave until our son finishes school. I hope the quantlet will help me hold on and not lose until we can move. I hope it isn't too late. I don't know how much is reversible. How much can I count on brain plasticity and muscle memory? I want to be able to work in the garden and get my hands dirty again.

### Theka — 2016-02-09T14:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188603>

I have started very cautiously using nicotine. I cut a patch and used 1/4 to start with no ill effects. Today I used 1/2, no problem. I figure I'll very slowly increase it. My husband was very opposed----of course he had a bad experience with some gum in high school! It was a monstrously stormy grey day today----the Rose Monday parades were cancelled. I've got to get out of this place. I am looking at places in Florida, of course the job will decide a lot. I did see a great home on a lake with a pool, sigh. I know I can deal with the heat if there is a pool. Worst case, I'll sit in a electric wheelchair and just go back and forth from sun to a/c. This Spring/summer will be a test. I want to prove that light will make a big difference. In another month, we ought to have some sun worth talking about here, almost equinox.

### Theka — 2016-02-09T15:05:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188604>

Well, the previous post was from last night, but I couldn't get it to post. Today, I decided to do another 1/2 patch and by noon, I felt good, alert, a bit salty, but even better, I fancy that I'm standing with better balance and moving a bit better. There was a bit of very filtered light this morning before the cloud cover got really thick. I think that I'll just keep on this month and see what happens.

### fitness@home — 2016-02-09T15:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188605>

Sun makes a big difference for me. While I am not in an ideal location (NW Houston) the amount of sunshine here is wonderful.

I moved here in 2007 and haven't had SAD symptoms since. February and March were always major health/mood slump times for me while living in the Great Lakes region...even as a child.

Most of my family is still up there. They really don't understand why I will not consider moving back.

Theka, I hope you get to move this year. Sending positive thoughts your way.

### Theka — 2016-02-10T23:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-188723>

I really envy you the sun in Houston....maybe not the traffic and big city, but definitely the sun! My family is urging us to move to North Carolina rather than further south. NC would be better than Germany but I don't know if it is enough. My brother and sister are there.

I did another 1/2 patch (17.5mg patch) today and I definitely feel steadier on my legs. I feel more like doing things, even walking a few steps further to dig in my closet or do dishes. Standing up from a chair is easier but I still need to push myself up with my arm. Should I try and increase the nicotine more to try and get more benefit?

I'm still trying to get what uv I can here. Today was very gray but I did have tuna salad and then smoked salmon later. I use a reptile light and it is ok, but doesn't feel even close to sitting in real sun. I guess it os better than nothing. I have it clipped on a shelf over my bed, so I can lay down and relax----today I had a cat on either side of me soaking up light!

### Theka — 2016-02-15T20:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-189113>

As the grey days drag on, I am trying to figure out a way to move sooner. I have several job alerts set up for positions in Florida. This summer will be a real test. I want to get as much light and sun as I can so there is no doubt about the toll of the environment here. When I think back, in September I still thought getting on the ground here would be enough....we didn't want to leave Europe. I finally got the stove combination that I wanted in November. I can't take electrical appliances. I am so incredibly tired of buying vacuums, irons and hair dryers. At least I still have my vitamix. My son is heavily influenced by his gf who is finishing her studies in chem here. She has to finish here. Additionally, lots of Europeans really don't want to go to the states---the presidential reality show going on with the election isn't helping. Life is complicated.

### Theka — 2016-02-23T13:36:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-189631>

It is cold and grey here, freezing rain to snow and back and fog from the rivers. My left foot is swelling again. I can't see any pattern or connection to anything I've done. I thought perhaps last time there was a adjustment to the high doses of biotin I am taking, but that hasn't changed in the past month. I am taking 100mg, 3x/day.

With respect to relocating and healing, we are looking at the US, southeast. Is there enough of a qualitative difference between Florida and South Carolina to choose Florida? We will be relocating to get the environmental advantages for me. If it has to be Florida, we'll find a way to settle there, but with family in NC and a love of gardening---many many plants have a chill requirement.... Florida is almost tropical---it gets a very little chill, enough to turn the oranges orange. But not enough to grow the many berries and fruits that I love to grow, raspberries, blackberries, currants, gooseberries. A bit further north, there are 4 seasons and more variety is possible. Obviously none of this matters if I need to be in FL to heal. It would be nice to be within a reasonable drive to family. It is difficult to plan this from so far away, overwhelming.

### Theka — 2016-02-24T21:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-189720>

I notice that in addition to the cognitive dullness, I just generally feel weaker, and things aren't working well. My feet hurt and the left is swollen again. I just want to crawl in a hole and wake up when it is Spring. I can't understand the blogs much now either.

### Theka — 2016-02-26T19:30:54+0200

Thanks guys. I am afraid of getting worse and not being able to move. Already, a short 'holiday' away is problematic as few places are accessible enough especially at the moment. Our 29th anniversary is this weekend and I know we will use the occasion to discuss this. I don't like the options we have. I was feeling good in September and I am shocked at how shitty I feel now. I really prefer to not be home alone now and I have to be able to manage better. Light and temperature have changed the most since September, so I assume it is the light.

My feet are swollen, left much more than the right and my legs feel weak. This happened a month ago and resolved itself. I am taking high dose biotin (300mg), but I have been since last April. I think I ought to get an ultrasound of my veins. I wish I was still privately insured, things would be much easier. I don't understand how the biotin works, but I know there are several studies showing it was helpful for progressive ms patients. I suspect those doing the studies really don't understand how the stuff is working either.
