### Theka — 2016-03-08T19:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-190424>

As there have been more sun/sunscreen posts and reports, I've been thinking about Panama. I am white northern european descent. I never got a super dark tan in the tropics. In fact, I used fake tan to darken up for my sister's wedding because people expected me to be darker! I could spend several hours in the pool at noon with no sunburn. After about 3 hours, my skin might feel a bit tight and I'd go in, but I never got uncomfortable. I never wore any sunscreen---nobody does there unless they are spending an entire day at the beach.

In contrast, when I moved to Germany, March 2011, I went to an Easter festival in April and sat in the sun all afternoon. People cautioned me to be careful. I had no problems that year. Since then, I have been surprised to get sunburned after just an hour outside! I am afraid all of my sun calluses are gone. I also don't seem to be able to handle the heat now. I just turn to mush. My son has had to haul my sorry ass back inside and get me to bed so I can cool down. After about 20 minutes, I usually can manage again. I don't sweat, so I assume that is part of it. I also usually haven't drunk enough when out. This year, I will pay more attention to water. It is just difficult when incontinence is so embarrassingly public. I need to understand this better.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-08T20:58:41+0200

Any time light changes to mass certain specific things happen quantum mechanically. Anytime this happens biologically you can bet the Warburg metabolism is also present for a deep physical reason. Any cell that loses sensory confinement must exhibit this specific form of metabolism because this form of metabolism is how light released best by a eukaryotic cell to be transformed into matter. Vander Heiden’s lab is now pursuing a more comprehensive understanding of how the Warburg effect may help cells reproduce. “It refocuses the question,” he says. “It isn’t necessarily about how the Warburg effect helps cells put glucose into cell mass, but more about why does glucose-to-lactate conversion help cells use amino acids to build more cells.” Guess what he will find.........mitochondria have chosen to use red light or heat for one physical reason........it confines light in a cell. ELF-UV light to be specific. When you lose it your mitochondrial swells and you release light from cells and get cancer.
Pressure builds heat = why free diving works ideally for MS patients outside the tropics.......and why getting naked in the tropics works best for them
Heat expands most things in the universe but one thing. And you need it..........badly - Water.
and mitochondria have chosen to release heat and water for one specific and physical reason...............because heat shrinks water and shrinking water around your ECT breaks nature symmetry by shortening the distance of the respiratory proteins on ECT..........this is why fish swim below ice, and why icebergs float on liquid water, and how and why mitochondria make water and heat to confine light using the electric and magnetic charge in them. Kruse 101 Science

When you factor in that the photoelectric effect acts instantaneously, with no time delay, then you begin to see how 100,000 biochemical reactions can occur per second using light frequencies from the visible spectrum easily.

People tell us all the time to block the sun with sunscreens and glass. Nonsense. We have built in natural sunscreens for UV light. Vitamin C is one way in how we modulate light assimilation and transport at surfaces.....This is why equatorial fruits have vitamin c in them. A rising SHBG is a beacon of a light problem for the person with the condition of poor light assimilation.............It affects ceruloplasmin and the carotenoids cycles in the skin and without dopamine in the eye, you make no hormones and the ones you do make are released incorrectly.
DNA breakdown on the surface layers of the skin is the another ingenious way to do it using non linear optics.........and then their is the carotenoid system on surfaces as another fail safe for UV light assimilation. This is why vegetarians do OK around the equator and no where else...........veggies have them. You dont need veggies outside the tropics. It worsens UV assimilation. ### nonchalant — 2016-03-09T20:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-190543>

> [Theka said:](/goto/post?id=190424)
>
> As there have been more sun/sunscreen posts and reports, I've been thinking about Panama. I am white northern european descent. I never got a super dark tan in the tropics. In fact, I used fake tan to darken up for my sister's wedding because people expected me to be darker! I could spend several hours in the pool at noon with no sunburn. After about 3 hours, my skin might feel a bit tight and I'd go in, but I never got uncomfortable. I never wore any sunscreen---nobody does there unless they are spending an entire day at the beach.
>
> In contrast, when I moved to Germany, March 2011, I went to an Easter festival in April and sat in the sun all afternoon. People cautioned me to be careful. I had no problems that year. Since then, I have been surprised to get sunburned after just an hour outside! I am afraid all of my sun calluses are gone. I also don't seem to be able to handle the heat now. I just turn to mush. My son has had to haul my sorry ass back inside and get me to bed so I can cool down. After about 20 minutes, I usually can manage again. I don't sweat, so I assume that is part of it. I also usually haven't drunk enough when out. This year, I will pay more attention to water. It is just difficult when incontinence is so embarrassingly public. I need to understand this better.

I was having difficulty getting a dark tan in summer, though I'm darker than most everyone I meet in winter. Then I discovered that carrying a spray bottle of water to spritz on when my skin gets warm is the trick. My cooler skin develops a nice tan fairly quickly. Also just a washcloth in my purse that I can moisten with my ever-present bottle of water does the trick in a pinch. And once my tan comes, great sleep follows.

Sleeping on a Magnetico pad cured incontinence instantly, for me. No more worries about sneezing or laughing!

### Theka — 2016-04-18T23:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-192994>

Loads has been happening here. I can feel subtle improvement as the days get longer but I am still not back where I was in September. We are actively looking for work at 30N or lower. We had guests for dinner Sat/Sun and I realize that I really have no stamina for that. I can handle 1 day but not 2 back to back. I had planned fairly easy seafood meals (chioppino and a Newburg-inspred casserole), served a nice Spanish red wine /German white and protected my eyes but even with a nicotine patch on Sunday, my legs were limp. I was still toast this morning. Fortunately it was sunny! I also seem to be emotionally on a knife edge. I'm still doing my conservative head-dunking CT. With the earlier dawn, it is harder to get enough sleep. My DH has been getting home from work so late, it really screws our schedule. He also has always enjoyed late nights and there isn't much evening to enjoy with his hours lately. I am concerned that this will be worse in the US. Here in Europe, there is still more respect for vacations and weekends/holidays. Sundays, most businesses and shops are closed. But light is trump.

### Theka — 2016-05-04T14:50:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194143>

In a discussion on magnetism/grounding, this was said about blood: "On earthing, seems zeta potential for blood flow is greatly improved and less clumping of RBCs after an earthing session." This reminds me of the blood flow issues that so many ms patients have, including CCSVI. Would magnetism ameliorate the flow issues? Obviously in cases where there are structural factors such as inverted or malformed valves, it might not be enough, but it ought to be helpful regardless. I think.

In thinking about Panama, grounding was so much more. I lived barefoot and the house was poured reinforced concrete. I only wore shoes to go into town. Here, nnemf and lack of grounding are my daily existence. It is as big a difference as the light, just less visible. I don't know what grounding would do for me now. Would just getting my bare feet on the ground daily be enough to notice or is it pissing in the wind with cell towers on the roof?

We know moving is the real answer, but until there is a job offer, we have to wait. Preliminary applications are out, we can crank it up after the close of the fiscal year in June.

### Lahelada — 2016-05-04T15:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194144>

Theka, I will ask the question back at you. What do you have to lose to lose trying? You know that i am not being an ass in asking ...

### Theka — 2016-05-04T19:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194156>

I try whenever I can get outside. This is tougher since my electric scooter died over the winter. I have to have someone push me out. We are going through the process of getting an electric wheelchair approved, but with insurance, it takes way too long.

### Theka — 2016-05-04T19:28:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194158>

I'd really like to get to meet Jack and Ruben in London this month. It is so much harder and expensive to travel now. If we fly, taking the Tube in London is not a good idea---not enough of the stations are accessible. Last time we tried, the station was supposed to have a lift, but it was out of order! Some kind stranger helped my husband carry me up the stairs. That leaves a couple expensive cab rides. If we try and drive it, it is at least 10 hours plus ferry. We have found ideas about accessibility vary widely and often leave much to be desired in London. The BioHacking summit people have nothing about accessibility on the website. I wish we could simply meet Jack and Ruben in the airport. I would like to see the film Supercharged but the summit is really expensive at £500 ($725) for 2 of us plus travel, either air or car/ferry. Mostly, I'd like my husband to have the opportunity to meet Jack. He is willing to relocate and find a new job in the US because he trusts my research, but I know he really isn't convinced yet. Another transatlantic move is really a horrifying idea. I can't help at all now and it is so expensive. He has resumes updated and out with a few applications already submitted.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-04T19:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194161>

I stay away from an electric chair........FYI

### Theka — 2016-05-04T23:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194177>

I have stayed away from an electric chair up up until now. I use a folding travel chair to go anywhere outside of the apartment but I can't move it myself---I only have one strong arm so I go in circles. Increasingly this winter, I have needed someone to push me the few steps to the bathroom. I figured with an electric chair, I ought to be able to manage alone all day since after we move, I won't have anyone to help me while my husband is at work, especially to come in the house if/when I get too hot. I have become heat intolerant since moving to Germany. I hope this reverses quickly but I can't go outside without help to get back inside. Even being able to get moved to a shaded area out of direct sun is a big deal. Our plan is to get an electric chair paid for here to enable me to live without a carer once we move-----here I would be able to get to the garden. Should we do differently? What other options do I have? If I want to move below 30N, we will have no friends/family to help us. We will be leaving our guys here and my husband will have to work at least until 65. I don't want to end up in a care home.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-05T00:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194180>

Tough choices but living outside of 30N has given you what you got now.........think about that reality. I know what I would do because I did it to my family.

### Theka — 2016-05-05T08:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194212>

Jack, that decision has been made, we are just trying to work out implementation. We are looking for work in the southern US--south FL/or gulf coast, possibly Texas. I just don't see any other option to getting an electric chair. I will have to manage alone most of the day. We aren't considering consulting jobs like he does now because they would require significant travel.

This is just much more complicated. We had expected to retire here. Now, we want to be across the pond by winter.

"I cannae change the laws of physics." Scotty

### Theka — 2016-05-06T23:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194301>

I'm really having difficulty tolerating sun/heat. Even though it really isn't hot yet. We have had a few days of 23C, if I sit in the sun too long, I turn to mush. Initially, I can sit and be fine, then I suddenly get warm. I don't sweat, but I feel pleasantly warm. That is too much. My muscles give up and my legs won't support me. 'Too long' seems to be about an hour. I think if I could be in water, I could last longer, but that isn't an option here. I don't know how to handle this. I love the feel of sun on my skin. I wish I could handle more.

### Theka — 2016-05-11T21:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194575>

I've been reading about the placebo effect. I wonder how effective I have been with nocebo. I am generally an optimistic person, but I am also very analytical/logical, that is not suggestible. I am never chosen by a stage hypnotist. I have a lot of experience in positive thinking and motivation, even some nlp. It was soon after my diagnosis when I had Marshal Sylver as a speaker at our investors convention. I remember being at a loss as to how to handle something that wasn't 'in my head'. How could positive thinking help nerves that were shorting out because of damaged myelin? I am not very suggestible. I am too much MR. Spock and want to see proof. Now, I think I accepted the separation of physical from mental. I believed that my mental state could not change my physical reality. I believed I had to figure the physical problem out. I did not believe the pharma industry had any answers or for that matter, the neuros, so I refused drugs. Have I been screwing myself?

My experience has been remarkably similar to what my maternal grandmother had. She died when I was 7 and I only have fuzzy memories of her sitting in a wheelchair. But it seems that every small comment I ever heard about her I remember and internalized. Did I program myself to repeat her ms?

It bothers me that every treatment or therapy I have tried has initially been positive but then fizzles out. It seems that nothing 'works' for me. Why? As far as placebo effects, I would have expected ccsvi to qualify or the various brightly colored capsules of LDN or maybe the dental amalgam/root canal removal....expensive and unpleasant are supposed to be better placebos regardless of actual benefits. Have I very rationally defeated the positive placebo effects while allowing the nocebo?

Lots to think about. We still have nice weather and I've been reading outside at middday. There is pretty stiff cool breeze, so I don't over heat, but there is a cold front coming this weekend. Well, back to the salmon melts I am trying to get made. It takes me forever to try and do anything in the kitchen. Once we move, I am going to need some gadgets to help for a while. When I think that I used to do everything by hand, from beating egg whites to whipped cream.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-12T01:08:19+0300

When nothing works it makes me think you have no charge in your cells..........

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-12T01:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194590>

or are in a situation where you cannot hold it every because you live in an area that constantly discharges it. That is the real issue for most people in the Beltway of the USA

### Theka — 2016-05-12T14:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194612>

My bet is on the 2nd option at present, probably a mix of both. But when I look back over the past 10+ years, we started out living in Pittsburgh. Every winter, by January, I was desperate for sun. We took a short vacation to Panama to check it out in February 2005, bought a house and moved there in June. Even not knowing what I know now about light, I still spent a lot of time in the sun. I could easily spent hours in the pool at mid day with no sunburn. I didn't eat tons of seafood, but we still often had ceviche. I still deteriorated, albeit not nearly as rapidly as since we've been in Germany. It bothers me that I didn't at least hold steady. We lived in the suburbs towards Cerro Azul, not in the city. At that time all the locals had cell phones, but few had computers or wifi. I, of course focused on diet and eating healthy. We had chickens and a garden.

Here, in Frankfurt, it is obvious the environment sucks. I live on the 17th floor with cell towers on the roof! In the 5 years, I've been here, I have gotten much worse. We are moving but Florida seems a lot like Panama. Of course, slowing progression down again is good but I want to figure out why I didn't do better before so I can do better this time.

### Lahelada — 2016-05-12T14:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194614>

Theka,in Panama did you tan after being in the sun?
For me ,what does you in is the 17th floor,the antennas and lack of sun.Think about yourself first.

### Theka — 2016-05-12T15:00:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194617>

I was tan, but not super dark. I never used sunscreen and never burned. I would spend hours out midday, reading, cleaning the pool. I didn't consciously greet the dawn, but we always got up 6-7 and often had coffee outside, either on the porch or balcony until it got hot. Our water was city water,stored in a couple tanks, then triple filtered, including uv before coming into the house. I realized that the cheaper cool white cfl bulbs bothered me, so I always had higher temperature bulbs. The house was poured reinforced concrete and I went barefoot.

### Theka — 2016-05-13T18:30:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194701>

Well, the sun has gone. It is raining and temps are dropping. The say it will be highs of 50F now.

Jack says you can't heal in the environment in which you got sick. For 30 years, we have moved every 6-7 years, mostly job related. It would be an easy diagnosis if we had stayed in the same general area, but Pittsburgh, USA, various Germany, Pittsburgh, Panama, Germany. I spent the early part of my life in SoCal (San Diego& Anaheim). Now, we are planning on the gulf coast or south Florida. The last 3 moves, we expected to be permanent. We joke that this has staved off midlife crisis. Life never has become same-old, same-old. We believed that life is either a bold, daring adventure, or it is nothing at all. We chose adventure over security.

### Theka — 2016-05-17T11:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194943>

Still trying to work out a way to meet Jack in London this weekend. The train looks like the best option because it would take us right into the city. The real problem is that DH has a full day of meetings in Berlin Friday. I had hoped we'd be able to leave by midday Friday. Another option that might work is having my son go with me Friday. The questionable accessibility in Europe does make the entire thought rather daunting---as well as the stomach/intestinal bug that caught up with me Sunday night. It seems to be slowly fading but it seems like everything is conspiring against me.

### Lahelada — 2016-05-17T12:24:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-194945>

Take son and sun,let loose in London....

### Theka — 2016-05-18T11:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-195004>

well, I thought I had it sorted. I am beside myself now, shattered. I entered to win a ticket and last night I got an email offering me a ticket plus a super reduced companion ticket. We have looked at the trains and planes. There are no cheap deals this close to the date. If we took the train, we would need an accessible hotel in central London for Saturday night. There are no late trains out. I can't wait in the station until 9am. I wrote them asking about special pricing for handicapped in March and got no reply. I had wanted to do a road trip with my husband, son and his gf to have enough drivers. (my son is 19 and he and his gf are really into fitness) There are cheap basic hotels in Dunkirk and the ferry is not expensive if you book it early. I just can't find any way to do this now. Why I couldn't get any response to make arrangements even 2 weeks ago, I do not understand. I tried again the beginning of the month. Now, nobody can take vacation days from work. Jack is planning to meet people and hang out Friday night. I can get to London with my son, but we can't get back. If we were to go Friday and stay for the Summit, we'd need 2 nights in a London hotel. We can't even afford the short notice travel, let alone hotel and food. It pisses me off because I could have arranged it had anyone answered my emails before. I can usually find deals with a couple weeks notice.

So we are moving across the ocean as soon as we have a job offer. (the resume is updated in 3 languages @ archimatrix.com/jphilipp). We are trusting Jack's wisdom. I really wanted the opportunity to meet Jack and more importantly, for Jan to meet him. He has listened to some interviews with me and he has seen the effect the sun has on me but he doesn't really believe this will be enough. We are afraid to hope. I would like him to have more confidence that we aren't just spending more money that we don't have to move leaving our kids and the social benefits and pension here. This is a major move and we're not in our 20s anymore where we can just pack a couple bags and go. 15 years of being sick has destroyed our finances. I was a real estate investor. Our plan was for me to take his earned income and convert it into passive income as quickly as possible. I was really good at it, but we had to cannibalize everything trying to stop the progression. It has seemed like trying to stop the tide. The sand just keeps sliding through our fingers.

### Theka — 2016-06-02T15:51:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-195867>

I was just listening to a talk, Confessions of a Mitochondriac, and he said fasting 12 hours increases mitochondrial biogenesis. He suggested fasting all night and then having a late breakfast w/o carbs. How do I balance that with the leptin reset idea of eating within 30min of getting up? Which is more important? I don't eat late, but with long days, it is difficult to eat early and I get up early. Lots to think about. Weather sucks here atm---haven't had a sunny morning in a while.

### Theka — 2016-06-08T20:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-196168>

I'm wondering about swollen feet. My feet ballooned in January and though they have varied in how fat they are, they have never gone back to normal. My left is worse, but my left leg has foot drop so I do not walk properly, swinging that leg from the hip the little bit of shuffling that I can manage. I was taking 1 grain of Armour until mid Sept when I ran out. It isn't available here, so I thought I'd see how I did without it. I used to have swelling from the heat in Panama, but it hasn't gotten hot here yet. There have been times, I couldn't get shoes on at all. I have no ankles at all. I sleep with my feet raised. I use a theracycle to pump them. I've had chinese massage and we've tried lymph drainage massage. I've been taking iodoral for about 3 weeks. I'm in menopause. I try and get outside to ground some every day. I live on the 17th floor with cell towers on the roof. Would the nnemf be the culprit? Is there anything else I can do to help until I can get out of here?

### Theka — 2016-06-09T15:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-196241>

It seems the best I can do now, is meditate and visualize and read more. I've figured out what needs to be moved with us and what left/sold here. I have a plan for the boys here which is evolving every day. My husband's fiscal year is just ending and he has qualified for the bonus we need to fund this move! I think I am finally making some progress getting our house in Panama sold. The teak has fallen into the black hole of Panama procrastination. Everything moves so slowly there. That was supposed to be sold for over a year now. Carnival is long over as an excuse. I plan to buy a property in Florida. I know how real estate works in the US. I've been watching the foreclosures to get a good feel for the market but the job will determine the location.

The biggest challenge is trying to drag the rest of the family along. I can see the lack of dopamine but it is hard to fix. My husband blames it on his job and that certainly is a challenge. But if he were optimal, he would be able to handle it. He is always tired and doesn't sleep well---of course he flat out refuses to go to bed early or wear blue blockers. He does have f.lux on his computer. I have switched our lights to incandescents and added some black lights (which he calls 'evil' lights!) I've shifted our meals more epi-paleo with as much seafood as I can get. He has agreed that we need to move and move to a sunnier place but wanted to wait until after the annual review to talk with headhunters. International job searches suck. An application from abroad does not fit a company's boxes. We use a US skype phone as a contact number but they assume a local candidate is easier. We hope a local headhunter will be able to overcome this. It is frustrating because high level IT security people are in demand. Despite every linkedin, glassdoor, whatever profile saying that he is looking in Florida, the only jobs we get notified for are in Düsseldorf, München or Berlin! Because we couldn't possibly mean what we are saying. He has his updated resume posted online in 3 languages, English, German and Spanish at [www.archimatrix.com/jphilipp](http://www.archimatrix.com/jphilipp) so he is working on it but slowly. He is also planning to take the cissp test just to finally have the sticker. I think we need to have headhunters actively engaged by the end of the month to get moved this year. We need to give 2 months notice here and admittedly 2 months is just doable for clearing stuff out and getting a container packed and shipped overseas. Moving the cats is easy from EU to US.

I just came in from sitting outside in the sun. I had the idea of moving to a smaller 2 bedroom flat here as soon as something opens up. This will be crowded but we ought to be able to then just leave it partially furnished to the guys. And every move streamlines life more and a smaller place would save money even if we had to pay to store some furniture for a couple months. If I can sell this idea and find a suitable flat, this could overcome initial familial inertia! I have to take advantage of the summer days. I had hoped the Quantlet would give me an edge, but now September is a long time.

Keep moving forward and your eyes on the prize (in this case, Florida).

### Theka — 2016-06-14T23:54:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-196576>

I am trying. When I am sitting down, I feel good. I can think again. I get so dull in the dark winter. I recognize it when I only have enough brains to read Georgette Heyer. As the days get longer, by the end of March, that is over. I am reading Jack and listening to podcasts and it is cool now. When I try and move, I feel like shit. I have to get out of here to a sunny place before I need care. The idea of being alone all day is intimidating but I think with an electric w/c and grabbars, I can manage. I am concerned about what to do for a bed in the us. German beds have a split mattress and I have an electic assisted bed here. I can raise it to get up. We will have to return it. Without it, I can't get up myself. I also have an electric tv/recliner type chair. My legs are so weak, I need the lift to get on my feet. Without it, I need help. My son or husband to lift me up. If I lose just a little bit more, I will need care. I came to Germany in 2011 with my natural dirty blond haircolor. A few highlights around the temples were all I needed to cover grey. I am now totally grey. My husband only has a couple grey streaks in his beard.

He isn't sleeping well and very unhappy and stressed at work. I suggested watching old Miami Vice recently to keep Florida on his mind---he always wants to watch a movie or show 'to relax'! I don't think he really understands the urgency. We have to move. I don't even think I can handle an exploratory trip to find a place to live. I'll have to manage long distance---not the ideal way to find a RE deal. I really wish I already had my Quantlet. I need every bit of an edge I can get. I've been reading about the placebo/nocebo effect, trying to not trigger my woo alarms. I absolutely can't do woo. I was raised doing logic for fun in elementary school. But I know the mind is powerful and neuroplasticity is true. And to quote from my favorite old calculus book, 'What one fool can do, another can.'

### Theka — 2016-06-24T14:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197174>

We finally got some summer yesterday! We've been trying to get highs of 66F/19C and yesterday, we got to 95F/35C!!! This came complete with warnings to stay out of the sun especially from 11Am- 3PM. Well, I was out from 11AM until after 1PM. I switched from sun to shade and back and felt good. However, when I got back inside, I couldn't move. My muscles were jelly. My son had to lift me out of the wheelchair but even with the rollator, my legs wouldn't support me at all. He ended up tossing me on the bed and setting up a fan. After an hour, I could get up and walk with the rollator.

This concerns me a lot since we plan on moving to hot and humid. I didn't have this problem 5 years ago living in Panama. I am trying to get out in the sun regardless atm. I told my son his job this summer was to haul my sorry ass inside when I turned into a quivering blob outside. I am not looking for help on cooling strategies. I want to understand why heat intolerance happens. What causes it? What fixes it?

### Theka — 2016-06-28T12:01:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197402>

Well, Summer lasted 2 days. It has been cool and cloudy since. There is no problem with heat intolerance when it isn't hot or even warm. meh

I am really bothered that I am not doing better now. We just passed the solstice and I don't feel as good as I did last September. I am getting up by 6am, getting outside as much as I can, sleep with an eyemask with just a sheet and a cat. I am eating epi-paeo with lots of seafood. I don't take lots of supplements, currently I take 65mg of armour thyroid, 12.5mg iodoral, and 80mg aspirin/day with 200-300mg biotin, 200mg magnesium, 200mg potassium and 1000mg vit C. I CT (face dunks). We turn our wifi off at night but we live in an nnemf hellhole in a apartment. If that is the problem, why did I feel better last September?

We are worried about me not having help once we move without family nearby. Germany is generous with vacation days and disability assistance programs and of course the many ms drugs which I refuse. I am afraid that if I don't get out soon, I won't be able to manage; my husband is afraid if we leave the social benefits, we'll be screwed, especially if we move to a more isolated location. It really is worrying.

I had counted on the longer days helping more. I am being evaluated for care/assistance. We are doing it for the cash---they will pay family if we don't want outside carers---from the date we first applied. But the fact is that I need help right now. I was moving slowly with the rollator in September. Now, I am barely moving and often need to sit on it and have someone push me, especially if I have to get to the toilet. It seems my legs don't want to work at all when I really need to get somewhere. My plan is to get an electric wheelchair so I can move more independently. I need a chair that has a seat lift function. My legs are so weak, I cannot get up from a normal chair myself and if I fall, I can't get up. I have one good strong arm with which I have to manage everything. I plan all of our meals to be simple so there is less prep, but I can't chop an onion and onions and garlic are usually step one! Usually my son preps everything and assists me in cooking, opening packages, getting things out of the fridge or freezer, even filling a pitcher with water so I can reach it from the stove. He also does the laundry. I shop online but they need to receive the bags and put everything away. We have a robot vacuum. I can clean the bathroom sink, but not the toilet or shower/tub. I hope with a powered chair, I can manage to do more but I am a bit afraid to be alone very long and I'm worried that it is too late to move because I'm not improving. It I don't improve during the long light cycles, what can I do? The US is not very helpful with medical assistance and everything related to health seems horribly expensive.

### Lahelada — 2016-06-28T13:09:00+0300

Theka , you need to get out now. If you cannot get out for good then get out whenever you can. Whatever that takes it has to be done. You are not far from pineforests. Not ideal, but better than where you are. Even if it can only be on a Saturday/Sunday. A Waldparkplatz with a seafood picnic and a Duerkheimer Feuerberg .It will be a break for yourfamily too , a change of wallpaper . Some other things may fall into place too. You cannot stand still and consider options. Grab what you can now. A change is a s good as a rest. It does not make sense to contemplate what you can do while in the same environment. Move house if you cannot move country yet.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-28T20:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197451>

Somebody just asked this of me. "So if you have MS and your taking Vitamin D supplements are you actually hurting yourself, or just a shitty band-aid?"
Answer: The key is my Sept 2015 webinar. You must stop the vibration of the skin's atoms so light can interact with it properly. If you work at night it becomes impossible. Think of this this way: Your skin and eyes are a piano......and it has many strings and they are all constantly vibrating. To tune the piano (to make Vitamin D) you must have relative quiet and lack of motion on those strings. When you live in a 24/7 microwaved world your strings never stop moving so even in full spectrum sunlight you must alter the environment to go to a quieter place to tune your piano so you can make Vitamin D3. To do so you need an equatorial existence until your cells and mitochondria begin to vibrate at 100Hz once again.

### Theka — 2016-06-30T12:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197573>

This is really interesting. I moved from Panama in March 2010. By Easter that year, my nails were splitting and cracking.....my proxy for low vit D. I was shocked that it happened so quickly. I supplemented with 10,000IU/day for a while and we had a glorious sunny Spring/Summer. By July when I was tested, my D was ok. At the time, I thought latitude was to blame, but now I think pop density, living in a high-rise apartment.

My question is if Florida is close enough to equatorial? Is it too crowded? We left Panama because of work....ie. there was none!

### Theka — 2016-07-02T00:35:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197719>

I lived in Meckenburg.Vorpommern in the early 90s. It was a village on the east side of the old inner German border. Population was under 100, including us. There were 2 landlines for everyone. We eventually bought a cellphone. We lived in the barn without electric while we worked on the house. I cooked on an open fire. Latitude was 53.5. By Autumn, I know I was vit D deficient and I developed cold blisters on my hands...maybe from washing dishes outside?? I also had the first bladder issues. By January, I was desperate to go anywhere sunny, but we couldn't afford it. One year after moving to Pittsburgh (suburb with woods behind, latitude 38), I developed optic neuritis. I didn't know about light so much then, but we spent evenings around a bonfire---they turned the street lights off at 10-11pm. We had no wifi. It was the restricted border area so very few people and no industry. With so few telephones, people lived according to the daylight hours. If it was still light out, it was ok to just drop in and visit! I would have expected to do better.

I know I have always felt that I really needed sun by January. Because of this, I think I, personally, need to be in a sunny place. I just don't know how remote and isolated I need. The nnemf is changing exponentially. We were in the city in Pittsburgh when I was finally diagnosed but we still had hardwired internet and our house was a big old victorian o a hill by itself (not typical urban).

### Theka — 2016-07-02T09:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197732>

Thanks for looking but that is one doctor that I don't need! Optic neuritis is a very common early symptom of ms which usually resolves with some minor type residual damage. Many patients are given steroids to hasten resolution. My vision was improving by the time I had an appointment with a specialist (who didn't tell me about the ms connection). When I saw an eye doctor in 2009, he thoroughly examined my eyes and there was absolutely no trace of the optic neuritis! It was as though it never happened!

Knowing about the healing aspects of light, I think 4 years of tropical sun fixed my eyes. Although I wear glasses, I spent hours in the pool without them.

### Theka — 2016-07-02T19:14:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-197752>

I can't wait for the webinar to go live!!

Today, I am struggling more again. I have gradually been getting more headaches....usually on the weekends. I thought it was skipping food on Sundays until after church, but today, I got up had bulletproof coffee and made omelets. Then I had to go to bed for hours with a blinding headache. It was a rainy grey morning, but we still had the windows open. I don't understand what is causing this.

I am really getting worried because I am not doing better and it is July!! My husband is skeptical that moving will actually help me enough and afraid that if we leave the social benefits that we have by moving to the US, we will be screwed. I have thought about trying to rent a place in southern Spain at first, but I really am not confident that I can manage alone all week----besides my Spanish really sucks. I wish I could stay near/with someone without 'free wifi'! I only have family in Pittsburgh and NC.

### Theka — 2016-07-07T18:45:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-198015>

We finally had another sunny day....not hot, mid 70sF (24-5C). The sun feels so good! I sat outside in the garden with my feet in the grass for an hour at noon! Although I was comfortable, I had to come in. I wasn't red but my muscles turned to jello. My son pushed me home and had to lift me out of the chair and help me lie down for an hour before I could function again.

I need the sun, but it seems so hard to get. Is there a way to build up tolerance? Would more DHA help? Would it be worth it to stay out longer anyway and just accept being temporarily incapacitated? I watched Jack on the webinar and was so envious of his ability to be in that heat and sun for so long and feel good! I know I can get my sun calluses back but I don't know about the heat.

### Theka — 2016-07-18T15:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-198539>

I have been struggling so much the past couple days with my bladder, much worse than normal. I don't know how to manage it, how to fix it or at least calm it down. This is so limiting. I want to go outside and more in nature but I am tied to the toilet.

This week is supposed to be summery with highs in the 80s today and then up to mid 90s. I'm not handling heat well at all either. I don't have to get uncomfortably hot. It seems the light waves penetrate the skin and affect me without me feeling hot. It just saps my strength totally. Even my good strong arm is too weak to hold my weight. My legs are useless for about an hour afterwards. How can I ever manage in a warm place like Florida?

### Theka — 2016-07-19T20:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-198613>

Today, I dunked my head and was outside hugging an icepack for 45min in 90F. I had to lay down for a bit afterwards to cool down but I did much better. I am not completely limp. I would like to get out again late, before sunset.

I've been taking high dose biotin which several studies have shown to be beneficial for progressive ms. I have read after initial symptomatic effects, it does promote real improvement in myelin regeneration. I am not convinced that it is beneficial for me. I am suspicious that it is somehow contributing to my foot/ankle edema. I think I am going to stop for a while and see. It is hard to be sure of causation but maybe I can get an indication.

### Theka — 2016-07-20T23:20:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-198699>

Today, I also managed to be outside---we hit 100F/38C today. I put my ice pack behind my back and only sat 15 min in full sun. Then I moved under a tree with dappled sun and a very light breeze. It was probably mid 90s then---it wasn't hot air to breathe. I dunked my head again in the afternoon. It is hard without a/c to have a chance to cool off. Tomorrow is going to be more humid too. I have to figure a way to do more and better CT. I read Appendix B about CT again today. It is easier to think about with these temperatures!

I wonder about clonus. I have read that there isn't anything that can be done for it. What bothers me is that I have it, sometimes. When I wake up with it, I gnerally don't feel as good---legs more crap, etc. If it can't be fixed, why does it come and go? It doesn't make sense. Something must make it worse. I guess I don't believe stuff anymore unless Jack says it.

### Theka — 2016-07-22T18:58:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-198791>

My fb 'reminder' today was a picture from 6 years ago as I was at the airport leaving Panama to fly to Germany for CCSVI. I was blond with touches or grey at my temples, could walk with a stick and an arm for shorter distances----longer ones, I used a rollator, airports a wheelchair. I could do my nails and use my left hand for cooking although I typed with one hand. I had difficulty with buttons and switched all of my pants to metal jeans buttons and I could stand to close them. I could lift my leg to step up a step or curb. I sat on my rollator to rest. I could stand up myself from the manual wheelchair in the airport--I just needed to push myself up with my hands.

Today, I can't stand up without a electric assist. I can't use my left hand even to hold a nail file. I am totally grey. I can drag myself with a rollator for 10m tops. My pants are elastic now so I can try and pull them up with one hand--quickly with one hand on the rollator for balance. I can't get out of a manual wheelchair--I need lifted. I need help throwing my legs on the bed and sitting up. Without a hospital type bed, I couldn't get up myself. I can't lift my leg to step into clothes much less a curb or steps! Sometimes, I can't get over the edge of the flat oriental carpet. I have to brace myself to stay upright.

This is a huge change in my opinion. It is only 6 years but so much change. I know we used wifi in Panama and cordless skype phone. The last year, I was working as a virtual receptionist with more cordless skype phones, sitting at a desk inside 11 hours/day, so I wasn't getting lots of sun every day. In fact, I started spending hours online there when I was a 'punk in Moms basement' for the Ron Paul campaign! I remember the 5th of November and the money bomb and the Tea Party in December. I thought I could get away with it. I was walking with a stick for longer distances then. Bladder was an issue though.
I have become a political atheist. I wish I had known. I am dependent on others now. I studied rhetoric and persuasion....I never thought I'd need it for family. Six years of increasing disability changes perceptions of competence---of course turning into a mental slug every winter doesn't help.

### JanSz — 2016-08-02T16:29:47+0300

[http://www.magdahavas.com/multiple-sclerosis-and-electrohypersensitivity/](http://www.magdahavas.com/multiple-sclerosis-and-electrohypersensitivity/)
**Multiple Sclerosis and Electrohypersensitivity**
\--
Here are some tips on how to practice good **electromagnetic hygiene**.

1\. replace cordless phone with corded phone

2\. replace WiFi with ethernet cable for computer

3\. use cellphone as little as possible & only in speaker mode

4\. do NOT use compact fluorescent light bulbs

5\. do NOT use electric blankets or water beds

6\. keep alarm clock radios at least 2 meters from bed

7\. measure radio frequency in your home & install radio frequency-reflecting window film or fabric to shield from external sources

8\. measure dirty electricity in your home & install filters if values are above 50 GS units.

9\. ask utility to install “wired” smart meter (not wireless)

10\. do not live in a home within 100 m of transmission lines or within 400 m of cell phone antennas

Click here for peer-reviewed publication: [Havas. M. 2006. Electromagnetic Hypersensitivity: Biological Effects of Dirty Electricity with Emphasis on Diabetes and Multiple Sclerosis. Electromagnetic Biology and Medicine, 25:259-268.](http://www.magdahavas.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2011/01/06_Havas_EBM.pdf)

### Theka — 2016-08-04T18:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-199311>

We have no air conditioning---this is Germany, it doesn't get hot!! ;-)
I haven't tried nicotine for several months. It seemed really good at first, then not so good. I got light-headed and weak so I figured I'd better figure things out better.

I have been thinking about nnEMF and I know it is a problem. I pulled out my meters to check if anything had changed in the past 6-8 months and aside from the induction range, it doesn't appear so. Maybe some neighbor has changed their wifi, we still detect 14 wifi signals. We were without power for 24 hours in mid-June and I managed better than I expected. I use an electric lift chair to get up (it was is the up position, so I could stand up). I also use an electric bed to get up myself. I raise it to stand up. I could not get up myself as it was in a lowered position. It was a short time, but I felt ok. It is so subjective to judge whether I'm moving a little easier but I was surprised at how well I managed. I was just pissed about my frozen food!

The links were great! I sent them to my husband and he went around and checked with the trimeter himself! I also have a ghostbuster's emf meter that beeps! This is a huge step! He found the electric outlets on his nightstand were not good and decided to keep his phone on airplane mode at night.

I hope that 2 weeks away in October/November will be enough for him to feel better. He survived meningitis as a child and still is bothered occasionally by headaches or gerd-like digestive issues when he goes to bed. If he feels different himself, it will be so much easier. He is so stressed with work deadlines, he can't really try and think about quantum stuff. I planned our 'vacation' to be a few days in NOLA followed by a cruise with stops in Belize, Roatan and Cozumel. I won't be able to tender to Belize but I can get to the beach in Roatan and Cozumel. I have 2 weeks for him to get it. I sent him the NOLA Happy hour oyster link Jack posted as encouragement and motivation! I just don't know how to manage. I feel great sitting down with a toilet I know nearby. The travel is daunting. I have always been up for adventure but this is a lot. I need August to be sunny and it is grey and wet so far. I have to make it until October.

### Theka — 2016-08-05T23:46:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-199357>

I am increasingly suspicious of the effect of the used stove which we got mid November last year. I just wanted a drawer type oven but it came with an induction cooktop. I had been used to a halogen ceran top. I noticed that I could feel electric on the edge trim, then when I got a pan with a metal basket to deep fry, the metal handle of the basket was uncomfortable to hold. This evening after frying up some calamari, my hand was numb. (my 'good' hand!) Looking back through the year, I was doing better after starting to follow Jack. I started doing the dishes, canning tomato sauce and by mid Oct, walked with the rollator in church. I noted my legs were crap by Dec and since mid January, my feet have been swollen balloons. I haven't had any of the improvements I expected as the days got longer. This journal has been a tale of my deterioration since Christmas. I think I may have been missing the elephant in the room. 2015 was started to get exciting and 2016 sucks. I have been trying to figure out why. I know I have to move but something happened.

This makes energy and the light we allow become the controlling arm behind evolutionary trajectory. Because Mother Nature uses energy she creates some unusual circumstances for scientists. This explains why they remain befuddled to explain modern chronic disease epidemics. Evolution triumphs and fails at the same time because as she leans too much upon natural selection, the plodding accumulation of error begins to fills up our nuclear genome, simultaneously, while energy flows slow from our mitochondrial respiratory proteins. ### Theka — 2016-08-30T19:17:12+0300

Yesterday was my birthday. We made Frutti di mare salad and gluten-free tiramisu. It was quite a contrast from a year ago. Last year, I had been following Jack's leptin protocol for a month and felt great---had lost my tummy and never felt cold. I was walking with the rollator for exercise. I expected this year to be better but I am tired and nothing seems to be working well. My ms friends would tell me I need to follow the candida diet. That is always their answer after ccsvi and cannabis, so I don't talk about it. I already did ccsvi 3x, and pay-to-play sucks. My son thinks it has a significant psychological component because often, I get good results for a month or two and then it gets worse again.

That doesn't make sense to me. My feet started to swell badly in January. There are too many loose end I do not understand.

### Theka — 2016-09-12T21:31:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-201358>

I have been thinking about GERD and acid reflux. My husband has had episodes periodically for years. He took a supplement 10 years ago for it. Once we moved to Panama and he travelled often for business, I would welcome him home with fresh pineapple and papaya figuring the enzymes were good. He had no problems there at all. Now, after 5 years in Germany, it has gotten really bad. He frequently wakes coughing and vomiting. He blames various foods and sleeping flat in bed. He says he sleeps better on the sofa, but if he actually lies flat on the sofa, it is the same---after about 30 minutes, he suddenly starts coughing and runs to the toilet to puke. Yesterday, a girlfriend stopped in before a date to take another dose of Luvos medicnal clay which she says she now needs several times a day to settle her stomach. She is a fit 28 year old. She blamed a bad Turkish kabob about a month ago. I could count on one hand the times I've had heartburn type complaints......and never anything an aquavit didn't fix. I don't understand this. Some people say it is too much acid and take antacids, others say too little and take HCL I've heard it is blamed on dairy or eating too late in the evening.

### Theka — 2016-10-06T20:05:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-202410>

I haven't posted in a while. I have been absolutely flattened by cold/flu that hit me like a ton of bricks 3 weeks ago and evolved into a sinus infection. I finally gave in and went to get a prescription for amoxicillan yesterday and am slowly returning to the land of the living. The days are getting short so quickly. I am really looking forward to getting south at the end of the month. The construction site next door to us has progressed and now has a huge crane with a big advertising sign blasting us with blue light all night. We can't see any stars now, they sky is grey. I really hope that 2 weeks away will be a dramatic difference for my husband. He working long hours and just can't really accept that the cell towers and light, etc can be so debilitating. I realized that I needed to get him away to even have a chance to seriously talk about this stuff. He hasn't slept well for ages although he has adopted f.lux, and candles are cool. He complains that we live in a cave since I started following JK! It is not easy. He has an engineering background and far more hard science than I do. I know I can't explain this well enough. I can read and understand enough to go for solid actionable things and adopt them but I don't know it well enough to be persuasive and I am not a good example anymore. August 2015 was dramatically good and that gave me credibility. 2016 has just sucked---his job is taking everything and I have become more disabled. I have researched several options for relocating but all are complicated. We are still trying to get our money out of of Panama where we invested to obtain residency and bought a house. When they changed the rules, we learned never to trust a government. At least our kids grew up speaking Spanish and playing soccer barefoot outside, so they have options.

### Theka — 2016-11-18T21:42:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204068>

I have been back just over a week from a fantasticstic break in New Orleans and a cruise through the western Caribbean. We met up with Jack Friday morning and tried to absorb as much knowledge as possible. I had me 23andme results and it seems I am just
fucked. I need to move south right away. Interestingly, I moved best that day. Since we had rented a wheelchair, I needed help the entire trip. I wore a catheter for the first time this trip. I think it was the only way I could manage. Now, back home, my muscles are weak and I'm wearing absorbent pants. I am seeing a urologist Monday and expect him to push for a suprapubic catheter which I don't want. I didn't take lots of supplements/meds during the trip----no accetaminofen, ibupofen, LDN or magnesium, just ubiquinol LD aspirin and a muscle mix of d-ribose and L-arganine with an herb. Now that I'm back, I am in pain whereever I am, low back, legs, feet and especially the right hip flexor. I can't manage alone. I wish I had know this 5 years ago. It would have been so much easier to manage.

Unfortunately due to accessibilty constraints, we couldn't get to Playa del Carmen so we just walked along the shops and had some great ceviche!

### Theka — 2016-11-22T21:13:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204215>

My sister asked me for some advice about her middle child in preschool, who has a mouth full of cavities. This was a huge shock to her and I think it would be to most parents. She has tried to do the best for her kids regarding food. She gardens and buys from local farmers, including meats. She followed GAPS for years. For the past year, they've been adding much more seafood/fish and watching the light. They live in a more country-suburban area outside Raleigh, NC where the neighbors have livestock. It was a complete shock to discover the extensive decay. She has been referred to a pediatric dentist who wants to treat it by quadrants under nitrous if he can cooperate, otherwise it would be full anesthesia and everything at once. What should be done? What is to blame? With 20/20 hindsight, what should have been done differently? She has a toddler too.

This is upsetting to me. As a parent, you know you can't protect your child from everything, but you want them to reach a certain age and have it be their decision.

### Theka — 2016-12-05T12:42:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204799>

This morning as I was watching the sun rise. I was thinking about the longer length of time that sunrises and sunsets last in the northern latitudes. The red glow starts early predawn. When we lived in Panama, sunsets were disappointing because, they were over so fast. There was never time to take a picture. Blink and it was over. Does this mean that the higher latitudes are bathed with a extended period of healing red light?

### Inger — 2016-12-05T14:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204805>

IDK Theka but I suppose our winter sun has some amazing things to it! That is why I try to tan in bikini or nude whenever the sun is out and I have time off work... It really makes me feel good  It is not a strong sun but it feels very healing
Now as I use aluminium foil to make it stronger it feels even more recharging

I just use cartons (the big ones for moving, you know) and leave them flat as they are when you buy them.. and I cover them with foil, and put them behind me and on the sides.. and I have 3 extra suns shining on me  awesome

### Theka — 2016-12-05T17:34:26+0200

I feel good in unfiltered sun any time of the year! I just wondered about the frequencies in higher latitudes vs at the equator. Jack posted that comparison of different light sources--LED, incandescent, halogen and sun. I just wonder how the sun varies with latitude through the day. It definitely changes though the year.

I feel good in unfiltered sun any time of the year! I just wondered about the frequencies in higher latitudes vs at the equator. Jack posted that comparison of different light sources--LED, incandescent, halogen and sun. I just wonder how the sun varies with latitude through the day. It definitely changes though the year.

### Theka — 2016-12-05T18:20:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204823>

I've been thinking about Dr Cicero Coimbra's high dose vitamin D protocol. He postulates that ms patients have a genetic resistance to D3 requiring much higher amounts than normal people. It seems to me that various SNPs could cause that. He puts patients on a low calcium diet to avoid problems and also requires 2,5liter intake of water/day and daily exercise to prevent osteoporosis. He adjusts the vit D3 dose by testing PTH, which he says should be at the low end of the range. He says stress is a huge factor.

He does not consider light or environment yet, although most of his patients are from Brazil. I wonder about the healing effects of light. He has great results in stopping disease progression and even limited reversal. I wonder how his ultra high dose vit D3 results would be if one accounted for light. Would patients in good environments have more reversal and healing? Would they need to maintain the high dose indefinitely? Would they need the low calcium diet or would their SNPs make it less important? How would outcomes change for a patient if they change environments?

### Theka — 2016-12-06T21:45:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204875>

I've noticed that I'm becoming more and more uncomfortable in cooler temperatures. It is totally different from a year ago when I was loaned my coat to some freezing vegetarians at an Autumn picnic---I was fine in a tank top (with a hat). I can't get to sleep without my shoulder and neck covered, particularly the left side. Now I don't even like to wear a t-shirt in the house. I still sleep with a summer duvet but all day I am cold. We haven't turned on any heat but it is still 68F/20C . I figure this is just another demonstration of how much our environment sucks and how crappy my heteroplasmy is. Last year I preferred to have bare arms. It felt stuffy to wear long sleeves. This can't be good.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-06T23:15:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-204880>

means heteroplasmy rate is rising

### Theka — 2016-12-09T17:09:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-205052>

I've been trying to find out more about my haplotype U5. It is most common in northeastern Europe. The subclade U5a2b2 is found in Ukraine an northern Italy. I am not sure what to do with this. The furthest I have been able to trace my maternal line is to the Rhein valley area in Germany. I don't know what to do with this. U5 is considered the most ancient of European haplotypes and evolved mostly in Europe with U5a being younger than U5b. Most stone age hunter-gathers from central/eastern Europe seem to be U5 or U4.

"Genetic data obtained in this study allows the suggestion that during the LGM period, central European territories probably represented the area of intermingling between human flow from refugial zones in the Balkans, the Mediterranean coastline and the Pyrenees, as U5a and U5b gene flows occurred from there."

### Theka — 2016-12-12T12:03:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-205179>

Last night I woke up thinking about a guy who has recently been hospitalized several times for stomach pain which seems to be related to heart pain. The doctors don't understand why this is happening. He is a heteroplasmy mess, in his 50s. He never expected to make it this long. He is obese, despite bariatric surgery and diabetic. He had a kidney transplant 10 years ago and repeated angioplasties the last few years. He is a farmer and fucking brilliant, the only person I can say reminds me of an incarnation of Jack. I figure that his outdoor life hunting, fishing and farming have kept him alive. I woke up trying to figure out how I could help when I can't even explain Jack 5 minutes after I read him. I woke up right when the Q&A started. I suck at that technology but I found a link and got in. He is a train-wreck . There are no quick easy answers, but Jack did say the Quantlet would be good. I can't wait for it to ship. I expect to start hearing fantastic success stories that I can share. I wonder whether if he tested it for an hour or so, if he would be able to notice the benefits. I wish I was doing better myself. It is very hard to get anyone to listen when you're so fucked up yourself. Jack says I can get better in a better environment. Getting to a better environment is complicated. I plan to go to Panama in January. I haven't booked flights yet because I am hanging the trip over my son's head for leverage against the online world. We want to move but we need a job first. As a US citizen, my husband can get a green card, but it is complicated and he needs a job first. That would get us to the US which ought to be lots better being below 30N and as uncrowded as we can manage. Panama is a nice place to retire but lousy to work. I have even heard from a good Panamanian friend, now living in south Florida because it is so much cheaper and less stress----her husband has alzheimers and she has ms.

### Theka — 2016-12-18T14:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-205433>

Well, things are happening. I have been approved for 3 weeks of rehab/occupational therapy. I scheduled it for 2January. I hope I will be able to manage better myself. Airfares drop towards the end of January. I want to fly to Panama for a couple of months of real sun. I am anxious to see what intensive sun and grounding will do for me. I am still fighting with the insurance to get an electric wheelchair. I need to be able to manage autonomously. We think the quickest remedy is for me to snowbird in Panama or if we can't get that arranged, somewhere in Florida. But I have to be able to care for myself.

### Theka — 2016-12-21T19:25:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-205601>

I've been listening to Alexander Wunsch on light and skin. He said ATP makes constrained muscles soft and smooth. He was talking about skin but wouldn't that also be a factor in stiff and spastic muscles? I need to learn more about red light and how to get more naturally as well as supplement it therapeutically.

### Theka — 2017-01-06T17:25:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206225>

I am doing 3 weeks of in-patient rehab therapy. It is good to be away from the city although the lighting here is horrific. fluorescent tubes everywhere, even the massage rooms. I am lucky enough to have an east facing balcony--1st floor, so I can open the door and get morning sun in my room. I sleep with a mask and don blue blockers until dawn. They say I have 'sensitive' eyes! There is wifi everywhere so I am streaming 432hz music. I've unplugged the TV and only use the small bedside lamp. I've asked my husband to bring my salt lamp. I'm getting a reasonable amount of muscle work, a bit of massage and even some red light---at my request! Diet is harder to manage, but I have opted for fish whenever it is available---and asked for 2nds! The challenge is the standard German meal plan of continental breakfast, hot lunch and cold cuts for supper. They will only serve me an egg 2 days/week. So I am delivered a plate of cold cuts for breakfast and supper. I've told them no bread. So basically I eat a bit of protein for breakfast then a hot lunch and a very small snack in the evening. I expect to lose a little weight, but I also gained a bit over the holidays. I ate those wonderful German Christmas things that I love because I don't expect to be here next year. Now, the holidays are over and I've got to seriously work on me.

It is colder but sunnier here, so I have been able to watch the sun rise all week. It makes such a huge difference. I just want to be able to manage better before I travel to Panama at the end of the month.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-08T16:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206317>

You need to reach out to Sarah Hogdson on the Quantum FB group and ask her about what happened to her in Mexico with regard to her nnEMF issues. You might like it

### Theka — 2017-01-09T22:18:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206410>

That ship has sailed. We are changing our zip code. We just don't know exactly how yet. 2 months in Panama is a temporary fix, not a real solution. It seems the global xenophobia has reached Panama and politicians want to tighten immigration/visa rules because of the foreigners 'taking Panamanian jobs and spending the money elsewhere'!! The even mention Canadians, Europeans and American as well as the far more likely Venezuealans and Columbians. But currently, I can still spend 2 months there on a tourist visa. I really am looking forward to sun and hoping the wifi isn't ubiquitous, cell phones are bad enough and everybody has a phone there.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T22:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206411>

Sarah's change occured in 4 days. Amazing.

### Theka — 2017-01-12T21:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206714>

I am a little concerned about the heat. It was 88F and sunny yesterday in Panama. It will be sunny and hot, no clouds until rainy season starts in Apr/May. I doubt the people I will be staying with can afford the outrageous electric bill for a/c. I used to be fine with a fan but I have gotten so much more heat intolerant since living in Germany.

I was just chatting with a girl from Portugal with ms. She has tried 'everything' including 3 years on a high dose vitamin D protocol without results. Then she mentioned that the sun was not good for her....that she has severe heat intolerance. I would have thought she had a decent zipcode but obviously not good enough for her.

But her story made me think. How can I manage to get the sun I need without the heat intolerance kicking my ass. I can be in the pool sometimes, but I will still have to cope with heat the rest of the time.

### Theka — 2017-01-12T22:10:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206717>

I've still got another week here in therapy. I am doing better--walking short distances with the rollator and doing a bit better with the bladder. I have been rereading Anja Leitz book because I wanted to carry a German language book around to share the love! Now I start Roland van Wijk that I couldn't manage last year. Last January, I was reading romance crap because I couldn't think. I have my salt lamp in my room now and wear the blublockers a lot! Obviously this environment is not ideal, but it has to be a hell of a lot better than where we live.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-12T23:08:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206725>

You need to have a love affair with the sun.

### Theka — 2017-01-15T22:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206875>

One thing that hasn't improved here is sleep. I have no trouble falling asleep quickly but after 3-4 hours, I wake up and have trouble. I can't get comfortable enough to sleep. I'm sleeping in darkness with a sleep mask. Often I am checking the clock hourly until it is finally time to get up. Going to bed early, just makes the night longer, I'll wake at 12:30-1:30 and have six hours to wait and try and sleep. Very rarely, I sleep through and feel amazing. Usually I just dread going to bed.

### caroline — 2017-01-16T03:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206886>

why not unplug the clock?????? Checking the time all the time is not doing you any favours - plus the EMF .....unless it is an old-fashioned windup one?

what about your watch? does it have batteries???? = EMF

### Theka — 2017-01-16T12:46:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206900>

thanks Caroline, my clock is a battery back-up solar travel clock, no bells and whistles. I generally check the time to see how many hours I have to wait until morning.....when it feels like 4-5 and it turns out to only be 1:45, it sucks.

### Sue-UK — 2017-01-16T15:45:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206905>

Theka, I've had the same problem in the past, and the most helpful thing for me was moving the clock so I couldn't see it. My theory  is that when I look at the clock it stimulates the "inner critic" (in the prefrontal cortex somewhere?) which then starts a dialogue .....Is that the time?  you should be asleep ..... you're not getting enough REM .....you're not entering the regeneration cycle ..... If my inner critic doesn't know the time that can't start ...

### Theka — 2017-01-16T21:40:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-206947>

I think part of it is probably my spasticity, circulation and odd nerves in the legs. I get really stiff being in one position for too long. Moving is a struggle and I usually struggle. I doubt the clock is the cause, besides with the sleep mask in a very dark room... I want to get up because I end up very uncomfortable in bed after a few hours. It feels great when I first lay down. I don't know if there is an answer. I suspect I'd need drugs to stay in a hospital bed.

### Theka — 2017-01-23T16:44:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-207249>

Well, I am back at home now for a week before I'll be traveling to Panama for the sun! In my 3 weeks in rehab/therapy, I managed my light and ate as keto as I could with fish whenever it was available. I lost over 7 lbs and my clothes are now loose on me.

Last night,we had an interesting experience. We were invited to dinner at the new apartment of an Indian couple who have been living in Frankfurt for a couple years. We expected a vegetarian menu, but were shocked by the abundance of blue light. They said they had to search for the cool-white bulbs! It is like at home! They said you could tell the Indians' homes by the light. For us, they switched to a single hanging lamp for dinner----warm white. They have started to tentatively experiment a bit with non-veg food. I suggested they put any type of fish or seafood that they find appealing at the top of their list rather than chicken! I told them about DHA and explained that although they might do well with the veg at home, because of the environment here, they needed more. They are built for a sunnier place! This made sense and even more important since they have a new baby.

### Theka — 2017-01-23T19:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-207257>

Jack posted about SNPs and magnesium depletion....and blue light. I have been wondering about magnesium depletion as after all of my tests, only magnesium was low, despite regular supplementation. I am careful about my artificial light exposure. Dr Coimbra has just advised his protocol patients to take more magnesium (1500-3000mg mgcl) as well as the high dose vit D3. . I don't think he considers chronobiology at all. But I've been thinking about the idea of using his protocol to establish the physiological vit D3 dose and magnesium as well, to put out the fire, then provide the higher quantum yield environment to take it further. But I don't really understand the significance of the SNPs on magnesium depletion. Blue light causes dehydration in the cells but how does that connect to magnesium?

### Theka — 2017-02-06T22:14:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208075>

I've been in Panama for 1 week now. I am wondering about things. Wifi and cell towers are popping up everywhere here and the government has eliminated incandescent bulbs and every house glows with the energy-saving 6500K cfl bulbs. They are sold everywhere. I used to search for warmer white 2700K bulbs, but they are not easy to find and cost more. During the election campaign before we left, one candidate gave them away. But my laptop only detects 2 wifi signals atm. I wonder about the sun. I am outside for sunrise everyday. When it gets hot, I can only spend some time in the sun without getting fried. But sitting inside with windows and doors open ought to be good too. We are only using fans here.
