magnesium loves water(binds to it), and when you lose water, you also lose magnesium - I have found in addition to mag, you lose potassium also - coconut water anyone?

### Theka — 2017-02-07T22:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208143>

Today, I started with sunrise and then moved to the shade until late afternoon when I hit the pool for a while. My legs and hips were so stiff and spastic I mostly sat in the water. This evening I've been standing a bit easier. And I think my bladder is functioning better.

I actually do supplement both Mg--K plus I eat loads of seafood. I should run across fresh coconut water here soon too. I'm just having fresh green lemonade! Local diet is very carb heavy, yucca, rice, plantains, bananas and other fruits. Interesting new thing I found, they drink coffee sweetened and with a slice of queso blanco in it----even the kids! I've been taking astaxanthin for the sun and it helps enormously. I still think 4 hours at midday is 1 hour too many! I am only red in tiny spots where previous swimsuits covered differently.

### Theka — 2017-02-08T01:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208158>

One weird thing has happened. My eyes are itchy. I used to have allergies years ago and took chlor-trimeton (anti-histamine)in varying dosages since high school. But although I expected to need it in Panama, I never did when we lived here and I haven't taken any similar drugs for years. Now, I have itchy eyes! They have 2 mostly outdoor cats here-----that are most likely relatives of our 2 rescue cats which have never bothered me. Maybe they have fleas and the fleas are bothering me??? The trees and yard are the same.

### Penny — 2017-02-08T01:30:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208161>

I was thinking about you today - when I was thinking about my own dehydration hack - I was thinking of like 4 bases one must cover to use the sun:
You need collagen - so bone broths plus lemon/lime juice would be great for collagen - even jello might work actually - but seafood broths would be the most hydrating and adding black pepper and turmeric would be like adding sunshine as turmeric flouresces - green tea - iced - with turmeric might be great for you also
Then you need melanin - use protein + nicotine for that - if you have trouble with nicotine - try a face dunk - which would be one of the things of course is to shrink your respiratory proteins so the electrons can tunnel
Tons of water - if you are screwed up - I would pop for Fiji water - one of my friends got rid of 90% of her fibroids in 10 days by drinking one liter of Fiji water - as her job was staring into a laptop all day doing neurofeedback, that is a really awful environment - you might even add baking soda to the water - it increases your exclusion zone according to Dr. K - like a pinch of baking soda every time you drink water -
Limit anything that dehydrates you like salt/sugar/caffeine - and don't go crazy with acidic foods - in analyzing my own diet, I am a poster child for idiocy on both these fronts... this is why my vitamin d was only 41 when I spent hours in the sun - drinking coffee, then going into the sun is a total fail...

The more you can retain what you drink the better off you'll be obviously and obviously you can use this as a barometer on how hydrated you are - this is what I have found for me - I can put baking soda in coffee - which tastes disgusting if you put too much in - but it is less of a diuretic that way -

I will post the post that JK did concerning this tomorrow or whenever I find it again - 2 million words - it's kind of tough Love astaxanthin BTW

### Theka — 2017-02-15T03:10:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208616>

well, I've been thinking about Panama a lot here and the environment here. I don't like the huge cell towers and I really don't like the noise. I do feel pretty good here, particularly in the pool. The country as a whole rushes to embrace the newest and latest and totally disregards anything older. 'Bigger, better, faster, and better life through chemistry'. Monsanto isn't a bad word here, neither is 5G.

### Theka — 2017-02-15T03:19:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208617>

Penny, atm, most of those suggestions are a first world dream! I am currently living with local Panamanians. It has been quite educational! I have good sun and a pool to cool my core a bit. I saw bottled 'spring' water after church......but it tasted like chlorine to me.....

### Theka — 2017-02-15T03:59:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208620>

Another cool thing we've noticed! My son has been increasingly complaining about his skin itching at home. He says if he runs out to the store and gets warm or sweats, it gets worse. The morning of our flight, he had red scratch marks and said he hadn't been able to sleep. We hurried through the airport with him clawing himself as he got 'too warm'. I thought about trying to buy some antihistamines for the flight but no time. He was really concerned about the heat here. He has had absolutely no symptoms at all here. We assume the problem is the nnemf soup we live in in Germany. Now we're waiting to see how the girlfriend's stomach/gut complaints react to the environment here.

>
> Hi Penny - why fiji water exactly?

Hi Caroline I think because it's structured - right out of the ground - but really, it's because my friend drank one liter per day for 10 days and got rid of 90% of her fibroids - she does neurofeedback as a job - always in the dark - big glasses - was in perfect health until she thought it would be a great idea to go roller blading one fine day - fell and broke both elbows - after that, she got fibro - poster child for blue light toxicity - she stares at a lap top (the brain waves) graph all day - it's all blue... I thought she was nuts when she was telling me about water - how it had structure etc - she had a giant poster on her wall of all the different water crystals from that Japanese guy who took pictures of water - I guess he froze them - when a certain emotion was "sent" to them - his name is Masaru Emoto : [http://www.whatthebleep.com/water-crystals/](http://www.whatthebleep.com/water-crystals/)

It's funny, I gave my kid a Gaba Calm during his session, and 5 minutes later his brain waves were the same as they were after 1/2 hour session - it's the last time we went  I have no idea what he's talking about when he says the sun can make zinc - I wish I did - will keep plugging away I guess Oh, GABA is made from P5P which requires zinc as a cofactor - and that whole phosphate thing - except for lavender and maybe there are other plants like honeysuckle that increase your phosphates, I'm not sure how one raises phosphorous - it seems that most supplements that are popular have the word "phosphatidyl' in them, so I assume it is low in low mitochondrial redox - so increase that and one should be able to make whatever one needs...

I'd like to watch that movie sometime: What the Bleep do we Know?

Anyway, after that she migrated to Kanga water - and of course we now know all you have to do is stick a live mint leaf in the water and that alone would structure it... or just stick it out into the sun... funny story, in chapter 5 of Gerald Pollack's book the 4th phase of water, his Russian counterpart had this insanely overgrown garden - even though he was new to the neighborhood - he had buckets of water out in the sun to structure the water - using that water made his plants grow... I love using the knowledge I gain here about humans in my garden - in fact, today, after 6 inches of rain in one day yesterday, my celery was going crazy - I thought of Dr. K when he said all you need is water and sunlight - wish I knew what the minerals were for in a plant - I mean you can add nitrogen, but it seems like good garden bugs can take care of that cycle... there is so much more to learn

Sorry for the blatherfest but that's what happens when I drink wine My kitchen ceiling has developed a massive bulge in it, which is not good...

I was curious if someone could explain to me why California is currently drowning in water after 6 years of drought - did they increase the aluminum in the atmosphere or decrease it? I read that stuff on geoengineering watch. com and it goes right over my head... have a fun weekend Caroline My youngest son turns 18 tomorrow - unbelievable Hugs across the pond

One more really cool thing I learned yesterday - you were going to quit coffee because you think it affects sleep - it probably does, but I learned why salt is killing my sleep - in the educating doctors forum the is a cool thread about renin and salt - I noticed salt kills sleep even more than sugar and is a tie for glutamates... yeah, you're dehydrated but it's more than that... it's this thread...
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-the-exclusion-zone-of-csf.17896/#post-196220](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sleep-regeneration-plasma-and-the-exclusion-zone-of-csf.17896/#post-196220)

### Penny — 2017-02-20T23:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-208970>

Well, Caroline, I used to feel bad about not being able to remember all this stuff, but then I figured out that is probably typical... I live with a guy who hears something once and remembers it forever... this is probably what Dr. K has going on - lots of choline receptors - that ain't me...

I get salt from the chips I eat to get the oysters down - then there are those lovely macadamia nuts - but the worse one so far is pastrami - kiss it good-bye if I eat that - and herring is pretty salty -

When I analyze how acidic/salty/dehydrating my diet is, well, it's kinda embarrassing really... I cook so much, if I'm by myself, it is just so easy to eat a can of oysters and mackeral on some blue corn tortilla chips with some salsa - I chase this with dark chocolate and I'm in heaven - in fact, I seem to crave the salt - the 16 oz cup of coffee I drink every morning is just a diaretic let's-lose-more-potassium-and-magnesium stupidity-fest... I do put a lot of crap in my coffee and don't have to eat for like 6 hours after breakfast, there is so much fat in there, but then it'll be 3:00 and I'll eat my seafood-chip thing and I'll think I can make it through the night and most times I can, but like last night, I caved and ate garlic bread of all the stupid things to eat - but my son had made it from scratch and it just called to me - weirdly, I slept pretty well - I think if you have a high fat dinner - not with bacon - like shrimp with garlic over some cabbage - the garlic will kill a lot of inflammatory pathogens, and if you have a cup of broth, it will limit the inflammation to the gut - and if you can do it 4 hours before bed, I think it would help you sleep - for the laptop, just attach a USB keyboard and mouse to it - they make dongles with multiple USB receptors -

You can also try the pinch of baking soda in your water hack... it increases the EZ and you should lose less water that way - I tried it a few times in my coffee - put too much in and it tastes hideous - BTW, if you put turmeric and black pepper in your coffee, you don't even taste it - I also add cardomom, ginger, cocoa powder and cinamon in there -
going outside with the laptop might be a good move - frankly, I don't know how you can moderate all these posts without destroying your eyes... I can't be on here very long -

### Theka — 2017-02-24T05:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-209186>

Another brief update: I've been here 3 weeks now and 2 hours in the midday sun in the pool doesn't faz me now. It just feels really good. I feel good here, but I don't know how to live here. Cell towers and cfl cool white lights are everywhere. I was up in the highlands Monday---no cell signal, no paved roads, no electricity-- at a farm. There was a river flowing through the land...just beautiful. The old farmer and his son (a foundling that was dropped at his door), are both active and fit, remarkably unwrinkled, but they were victims of agricultural theft recently. Their entire field of papayas was taken, plus the gas tank for cooking. Security is a difficult issue and even more difficult for gringos. Any gringo is obviously rich and a target. I want to live away from people and their tech, but I don't relish being a victim of a home invasion or kidnapping. A great quantum zipcode has to provide safe living too. I wonder about Florida, aside from the political issues, 5G is advancing. Are there pockets of rural simplicity with higher quantum yields where one can live in peace wihout becoming part of grand experiment? I also wonder about Greece. Is it far enough south? Can we live safely as EU residents there? Is the electric 50hz? Can I manage the language? My Spanish sucks, but my Greek is worse

### Theka — 2017-02-28T03:55:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-209412>

I think the real problem here is that the dollar economy is killing the people. Prices are obscene. Normal wages are not. It is a good place to buy and build if you have drug money to use. The climate is wonderful but there are other places where the sun shines! I feel so good in the pool in the sun here.

### Theka — 2017-03-23T17:24:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-210945>

one curious change, very unexpected is that my vision has gotten worse---My husband was only here for 2.5 weeks and he noticed it first. Then yesterday as I was up at dawn an watching the various birds, the leaves got a lot more fuzzy and stayed blurry. It has not improved. I have great sun here, but something is not cool.

### Theka — 2017-04-04T14:42:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-211717>

I have been home 3 days now. It is Springtime in Germany and the garden is full of tulips and daffodils. The lilacs are ready to open. My strawberry plants have buds already! It will be hard to leave another garden. I have planted so many trees and never stayed long enough to see them grow and bear fruit.

I think I finally started to really understand things the last couple of weeks in Panama. I keep pages of comments about ms and I reread them and it seemed pretty obvious. I have always understood the sun/vit D connection to ms, but I didn't get the nnemf connection to vit D. I know why I felt best in the pool in the sun. I now have the darkest tan that I have ever had! I literally don't recognize my arms. I am darker than my son's Madagascan girlfriend! I also noticed that my left (bad) arm felt better in the pool. My hand was still numb, but the tingling up the arm would stop. It started with a vengeance after a short time on the flight home.

We are planning our German move now. I don't think there is much difference in a degree or two latitude within Germany, so we are looking for low population but still reasonable to commute to work. We plan to spend time in the winter in the sun and eventually move south. This is the first winter since I moved to Germany that I have not deteriorated----and I was away for most of it!

### Theka — 2017-04-14T12:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-212313>

Well, ten days later and numb, tingly limbs have become 'normal'. I also have become aware of a buzzed feeling in my head along with a very subtle humming, ears-full-of-water kind of noise when it ought to be quiet. I've also had a few headaches. I think these are all nnemf issues. We are going to look at a house a duplex next week. I think my best option is to move away from people, even if it is still at high latitude. This place is on the edge of a small town (pop 2000) and near a river and a protected old-growth forest. The house was built around 1600 so we have to see whether it will work for me. I will still need to get sun somewhere south in the winter. I am streaming 432hz music and have a fountain running to try and mitigate the nasties until we can get out. It just seems to be so little.

### Theka — 2017-04-25T18:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-212918>

We went to Thüringen over the weekend to see a possible house. I love the former East. There is space! The house is a half-timbered duplex built around 1600. It has a level entry and central heat/plumbing from the 90s. The big unexpected problem is the many steps between room on the ground floor. We thought we would only need to find a place for a lift to the upstairs bedrooms. But the house is on the outskirts of a small town (pop 2100) near the Werra river and a protected old-growth forest. I felt so good there, like my cells just relaxed. I always feel a little 'buzzy' here. We don't have a clue if it is feasible to level the floor or what that would cost. I was prepared to sleep in the living room for a while, but that isn't possible---2 raised thresholds and 2 steps separate the livingroom and the bathroom! It will never be a place in the sun but it seems good for Germany. I wonder whether part of the peaceful feeling came from the old construction methods, the massive thick walls. The windows were replaced in the last 30 years, so no old glass.
I don't know if I can actually manage all week alone. Right now, I can't. I had hoped that rehab/therapy and 2 months in Panama would be enough to start. We are hesitant to buy a place where I would need to be alone but we can't stay here.

### Theka — 2017-04-25T23:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-212933>

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/201...tiple-sclerosis-research-scientists-discover/](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2017/04/23/breakthrough-multiple-sclerosis-research-scientists-discover/)
Today, I have been trying to find out more about Rab32, a protein that a UK study believes could be a problem in ms patients. I think it was discussed at the Microbiology conference in Edinburgh earlier this month but I can only read the title of the talk,
'Regulation of the BLOC3- and Rab32 dependent anti-microbial (BRAM) pathway'. So I tried to find out more about the Rab32 protein and only find bits and pieces. I found a study about LRRK2 transport and Rab32 which is about Parkinsons, but I can read it and I still don't know the significance. What is an anchoring protein? What does it do?

I've also just read about the significance of magnesium on endothelium health and I wonder if a combination of magnesium and vitamin D levels would explain the vastly different results from CCSVI treatment, possibly also the resenosis cases. Magnesium and cholecalciferol seem to pop up everywhere.

### Theka — 2017-05-11T21:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-213872>

We went back to Thüringen over the weekend. This time, we stayed in a private vacation rental cottage----no cell signal!!! It was crazy, I was sitting at the table and I noticed my left hand was buzzy, tingly and asked if cell phones were on airplane mode and that is when we realized there was no signal! My husbands phone was on the table desperately seeking contact. We shut it off and had great evening playing cards in front of the fire. We all slept well too. I felt good there. It was like being in the pool in Panama. Out of the pool, there was nnemf everywhere. I think developing countries are getting burned with nnemf. They embrace new tech unquestioningly. Back here at home, it is hard to think. My head felt like a throbbing buzzing when we got home today. I put the Quantlet on to help. We just have to get out of here.

When we were in Mexico, I got a zultanite ring. It changes color with the light. I wear it as a light meter now! It is almost rose amethyst in candlelight, fluorescents turn it bright kiwi green. Outside, it is a nice brownish purple. It has been a lot of fun.

### Theka — 2017-05-22T20:21:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-214431>

Well shit. I thought I had a reasonable plan but Last night Q&A finished that idea. I am so fucked, a lower pop density/nnemf at high latitude just isn't enough. I need all that and strong UV too. We were set to make an offer and take it to the bank this week. I have no idea where to start now. It would be so much easier if I could manage alone. I looked at some assisted living places in Florida but they seem pretty expensive. I don't think we could manage that plus rent and I really don't want to live an ocean away from my husband. Heard this week that Cyprus wasn't so great and Europe just isn't low latitudes. Pittsburgh is 38 latitude and that is where I got sick.

I am trying the high dose vitamin D protocol, hoping I can regain enough to manage without help. It would be easier if I had a doctor that wasn't afraid to order the tests. If I can move a bit better then I can try and find some friends/family to visit in the south. I wish my sister lived further south than North Carolina (35 lat). My son will be starting his apprentice program here so I can't draft him as a companion. My husband really doesn't want to start over with a new job. It is harder after 50, especially if it involves changing countries because that really destroys a pension plan. We have to move out of this 17th fl apartment, regardless. I though we could move low pop density and plan a trip to Mexico this winter. sigh, I've got nothing. I think I just don't see myself as ready for a nursing home----shit I finally could read Van Wjk and Jack and get it. My brain feels alive. I was so ready to take on that 1608 house for a few years.

### Theka — 2017-06-21T15:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216710>

well, I'm trying to make an optimal place in the middle of Germany. I doubt many will realize why it is so good. I think it will slowly become obvious to family--the family that all want to stay in Germany. It won't be enough for me, but that train is long gone. I just didn't connect the dots soon enough. The place that I've found is very old and built on the edge of the village near the river. If I could manage alone, there are some shops nearby, but it is quiet. It is ironic in a very twisted way. I know what I need to do, when I am no longer capable. Life has been a glorious sunrise, so many hopes and dreams and full of promises. I am surrounded by intelligent people. My husband took time off in college to work programming(assembler) the End-of-train monitor to replace the caboose. My son built a Tesla coil with beer bottle capacitors in high school. It is still talked about in Panama! I was invited to join mensa when I was about 15. But I have struggled since 2003 to figure this out alone. My science background is weak. After I wasn't allowed to join mensa, I moved towards the liberal arts. We're all terribly allergic to woo, and unfortunately quantum biology and photonics seem a lot like woo. I know I ought to be able to put the pieces together, but they are sand in my fingers as the tide comes in. I'm so tired of endless futile explanations and the complaints about living in a cave since I found my 'guru'. Any of them could listen to some podcasts or read some blogs. They have time for online games, ages of empires, dota, wow, or that failsafe, chess. They complain about gerd, headaches, muscle pains, back pain but that is attributed to having to lift me. I go to bed by 11, but they have to stay up online until midnight, 1, 2 or whenever. The last time I really felt good was August 2015. I had started Ann Boroch's candida diet as a last ditch effort in May. I didn't think I had a candida problem, but I wanted to rule it out as a cause. I heard Jack for the first time in June.....had to listen twice because it was amazing. I thought about it and decided to jump in in August. We fast (seafood only) the first half of August, so I figured it was a perfect time. I got up for sunrise and ate DHA. I started face dunks too. By the end of the month, I was walking around the apartment with my rollator because I was bored. My abdomen was flat! I felt great. But in January 2016, my feet and ankles suddenly and seriously swelled. It was extreme and my husband asked if I wanted to go to the hospital.........I didn't. I don't like those places. My feet have not been normal since, although using the quantlet on them has helped a bit. I think something must have changed here but I did spend the first 3 months of this year elsewhere and did not get results, maybe too little too late, pissing in the wind. I felt good in the sun, but nothing concrete. So I am sitting here in a wheelchair wearing a diaper. I just don't have enough time in the bottle. I have used all that I had built up when I had my real estate. I can't afford any more pay-to-play medicine. #toooldtorocknroll

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-21T16:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216712>

Beliefs about things in life and science can strengthen or weaken your motivation. The key is what you focus upon.......woo or real science. The science of change is all about dopamine and dopamine is all about AM sunlight containing balanced IR-A with blue and UV-A light. It is never too late to change but the longer you wait to change the harder it gets because the dopamine bank account empties. That was the story I tried to get through to you and your husband in NOLA. You may encounter many defeats, but you must not be defeated by your own beliefs or by those of your family.

Today's facts are tomorrow's fallacies. Modern science has built its own logic and codified it in PEER reviewed literature as gospel. Real science is simply common sense at its best, that is, rigidly accurate in OBSERVATION and proven in experiment, and merciless to fallacy in modern scientific logic. Fallacies do not cease to be fallacies because they become fashionable in institutions of higher learning and printed in a textbook. When examining evidence relevant to a given paradigm built by education, people are inclined to see what they expect to see, and conclude what they expect to conclude. Information that is consistent with our pre-existing precepts is often accepted at face value, whereas evidence that contradicts them is critically scrutinized and discounted. Our precepts of truth may thus be less responsive than they should to the implications of new information. Concepts always should trump precepts but today this optic is lost.

_I'm reminded of this quote here:_
Nothing in the world can take the place of persistence. Talent will not; nothing is more common than unsuccessful men with talent. Genius will not; unrewarded genius is almost a proverb. Education will not; the world is full of educated derelicts. Persistence and determination alone are omnipotent. The slogan Press On! has solved and always will solve the problems of the human race.

**Calvin Coolidge**

### Theka — 2017-06-21T17:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216729>

My haplotype is U5B2A2. I'm at 50 latitude. My maternal great-grandmother was born in Germany near here and I have to assume her line goes back quite a ways there as there is a folk song about a hunter that is about her ancestor. (Der Jäger aus Kurpfalz) but I've run into a secret/drama inspired dead-end with her line and I can't spend more time on it atm..

I do have restricted mobility but my approach is to get moved to a level entry house where I can get outside, even if I just sit in my wheelchair holding a tree or a metal railing to ground. I will look into the lamps as well. I am looking at a himalayan infrared heat unit too. But I know the sun is not negotiable.

I will look into getting the tests but I couldn't get the very short list I wanted last month because the new GP refused, she didn't want to know and be responsible! I wanted D3, bun/creatinine, PTH, TSH and serum calcium because I am taking high dose vitamin D3 (coimbra protocol). I will be looking for another doc once we move. I have generally found the doctors in the former East to be more progressive. There is still too much of the 'white gods' mentality in the West. I think it is because the ties to big pharma have always been tighter here.

### Theka — 2017-06-21T21:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216749>

I don't see how this latitude can be right for me when I am such a train wreck. My maternal grandmother was also in a wheelchair with ms and also had strokes and heart attacks and lived at 38 latitude, admittedly there was heavy air pollution at the time but they also had a farm. Incidentally, she also had bad eyesight and was married to an optician. The doctor told them, if she wanted any QOL, they needed to move to the sun. They chose SoCal, where I was born. She died when I was in 2nd grade. I remember staying with my Dad and doing 'logic puzzles' from his old university textbooks while my Mom was at the funeral.

I've put energy into getting moved from this 17th floor apartment. But I really don't believe it will be enough. Right now, I don't know how I can manage alone 4-5 days a week when I can't get into bed myself. But I really think this location is horrific. There is a major construction site next door, maybe it is part of it. Since I returned in April, my skin has gotten worse. I have some eczema/psoriasis starting, possibly also on my feet/toes. I was managing the bed part in January when In was in stationary rehab. That was a fluorescent blue lit hell, but I played 432hz and had a salt lamp to help mitigate. I was up at dawn, with the door open and did my face dunks. I thought following that with 2 months in the tropics would be incredible and the sun felt good but no quantitative progress. Since, I've been back, I'm losing ground. I am desperately trying to hold back the tide.

### drezy — 2017-06-21T22:09:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216750>

For what it's worth @[Theka](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17862/) your post really resonated with me and I see that it comes from the depths of your heart. I wish you the best for your move. I hope to be reading your posts far into the future.

Many people may consider it morbid, but I think the ability to contemplate an end or abandon are assets to us higher order primates. It sometimes can put things in focus and help with creative thinking. My fingers are crossed for you and Jan may have the persistence for you. I swear the guy gains +1 human's persistence each year and he's up to 78x normal human persistence.

Sadly this poem got a bit overused and turned cliche, but still I'm reminded of it:

Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening Related Poem Content Details
BY ROBERT FROST
Whose woods these are I think I know.
His house is in the village though;
He will not see me stopping here
To watch his woods fill up with snow.

My little horse must think it queer
To stop without a farmhouse near
Between the woods and frozen lake
The darkest evening of the year.

He gives his harness bells a shake
To ask if there is some mistake.
The only other sound’s the sweep
Of easy wind and downy flake.

The woods are lovely, dark and deep,
But I have promises to keep,
And miles to go before I sleep,
And miles to go before I sleep.

^^^ Jan is the horse

Hopefully it's just a quick stop

### Theka — 2017-06-21T22:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216757>

Jack, I really don't think I'm being defeated by beliefs. I think it is just the wheels of time. Relentless. You say, "If you have _never_ changed your mind about some fundamental tenet of your belief, if you have never _questioned_ the basics, and if you have _no wish_ to do so, then you are likely ignorant. Check the lights you allow. You might learn something." I love that. I feel as though everything I learned in school was bullshit. I have changed most of my basic beliefs based on new information. I read. I love to think from different angles. What if.....X? Not changing my mind, but just trying it out! Sometimes it is garbage, but sometimes the pieces just fall into place! I was sure that I could manage to get healthy again---I wasn't worried. I wouldn't let the neuros who wanted a medication virgin bully me. I lived healthy. I ordered my first cane from the BBC catalog, a gentlemen's walking stick--just for balance. From there, it slowly got worse and now, I can't stand unaided. But slow progression is one thing, this has accelerated and I am out of work-arounds. I take MB, use the Quantlet, get up for sunrise, go to bed early, use blue blockers, eat DHA, some CT, don't use a cell phone and things are getting worse. I have read so much and tried so much. I remember the quote from the Bible about the woman who "had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse"

### Theka — 2017-06-22T10:43:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-216800>

Thank you all so much for your encouragement. This morning, it is sunny here--80F/27C in the apartment. It is supposed to be 95F/35C today. I had my MB then went outside from 7-8am. Now, everybody is gone and I used the infrared lamp for a bit, then strapped the Quantlet on and my 432hz music on. It is an ok start. I think I'm doing a bit better with the heat intolerance, but it is hard to quantify----but the temps aren't bothering me. It is a bit sad, the days are getting shorter. It is extreme right now with sunrise at 5:15 and sunset around 9:45. I like August when sunrise is around 6.

I suspect that this lamp (Osram Vitalux)(Price: $84.90)may be thought of as replacement for
Red Sperti
Sperti-Fiji-Table-Tanning-Lamp $ 319.00
rather than
Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W
Exo Terra Solar-Glo High Intensity Self-Ballasted Uv/Heat Mercury Vapor Lamp
Price: $30.44
\------------------------------------------
Not sure how good replacement that would be.
Red Sperti I use 16-32min/day, every day, 365 days /year, unless I am outside on the sun.
ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W works whole day, provides replacement sun light when I am indoor.
From distance used there is not much of UV.
Rather than estimates by D-minder app, I use blood testing to find my vit D level.
Goal --->Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
Up to twice of above is ok too.
\------------------------------------------
ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W
was designed to provide (from very close distance, inches) full sunlight requirement for a (sensitive) lizard that normally lives in the dessert, similar to what is found in Arizona.
In other thread, I assumed my condition, sitting at the desk, lamps 3-4 feet away.
To replicate condition seen by lizard at least 4-6 of those lamps should be used.

\-------------------------------------------
**Product description**
300 WATT R40 OSRAM SUN LAMP - E27 BASE - 230 VOLTS - HIGH UV LIGHT BULB - PLEASE SEE ADDITIONAL INFORMATION BELOW, THIS BULB REQUIRES 230 VOLT POWER SOURCE FOR OPTIMUM OPERATION - The Osram ULTRA-VITALUX lamp produces a mix of radiation very similar to that of natural sunlight and can have the same positive biological effects on the body as the sun. This blend of radiation is generated by a quartz discharge tube and a tungsten filament. The bulb is made of special glass which allows only that part of the output that is contained in natural sunlight to pass through. Some reported biological effects include: Tanning, regulating the vegetative nervous system, prevention of infectional diseases on account of bactericidal effects, reactivation of active substances, the development of vitamin D, regulation of calcium levels, improved blood circulation of the skin which becomes more elastic and smoother with a healthy suntan as cosmetic side-effect, and the treatment of acne.
**Radiation UVA 315-400nm after 1 hour: 13,6W
Radiation UVB 280-315nm after 1 hour: 3W.**
Because of its sun-like radiation properties, Vitalux lamps are used in industrial material testing, particularly for testing materials and equipment in different environments, and for photo-chemical industrial processes like accelerating varnish hardening. PLEASE NOTE: Before using the lamp to treat medical conditions, consult your doctor about the most effective therapy. Considerable heat is produced. Operate this lamp only in suitable equipment. The Vitalux bulb operates at optimum levels with applicable fixture designed for 220-230 volt and 300 watt lamps, including a porcelain socket. Not for use in portable lamps, table lamps, or household recessed fixtures. Bulborama is not responsible for improper installation or incompatible fixture application usage. Protect the lamp against moisture and splashes. Lamp emits high-intensity UV radiation that can cause **sunburn and conjunctivitis**.

### Theka — 2017-06-29T13:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-217441>

Can I use UK bulbs in Germany? I know US ones do not work. I have never tried UK ones.

But although I know I have to deal with lights, it makes more sense to me to do that after we move. They just came and replaced the last meter on the radiaror in my son's room. I thought they were just reading them, but no, they are all now radio-transmitted. So while I was away, they installed 7 new sources of nnemf. I am living in a microwave oven. No wonder I can't get any better. Everything I do to help ends up pissing in the wind of the storm of new nnemf here. Our top priority is getting moved where we can control more. I won't get more sun but I'll get a whole lot less nnemf and blue light.

Another thing I've been thinking about is creativity. What enables or drives one person create music, write or paint? There are different types of musical ability. My son wanted to learn the organ as a 5 yr old and when he started piano lessons, he flew threw them. He would master his new pieces by evening and his teacher said he only had 2 tempos, fast and faster! But when she tested him, he was not a composer. He has technical ability. I have always hated writing. In Sat honors classes (4th/5th grade), they offered creative writing and science----I chose microbiology and electronics!! I love to read but hate to write. What makes a Tolkien or CS Lewis? or Bach, Peter Gabriel or Louis Armstrong?

### Theka — 2017-07-12T13:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-218573>

I had an interesting experience over the weekend. Saturday morning, we were cleaning the apartment and my husband decided he'd turn the music on---my 432hz gets repetitive--so he was streaming a playlist and playing on the speaker, but he decided to try bluetooth rather than the cord. He was surprised at the sound quality plus it is better with the speaker high on a shelf. Well, he was mucking about in the kitchen and bitching about no one cleaning up properly. I was trying to stretch my legs out. The were extremely tight, spastic and painful. I remembered my grocery delivery had in their twisted logic decided when I ordered 3 lamb steaks, 2 slightly larger would be equivalent. I was ranting about this and grabbed my purse to go out to buy an additional steak. When I got down to the lobby, I couldn't share my rant with the concierge and when I was outside, I couldn't evenf feel mildly pissed about it! When I went back in the apartment, I told him to turn the fucking bluetooth off! and open the windows! As he walked over to his laptop, in front of the speaker, he could feel the bluetooth!

So, since then, he has read the leaky gut prescription, started a little MB, and decided to change his schedule to get up earlier. He went outside in the morning with me too. It is much easier not being alone in this. I have been trying to drag the family up and out of the mess but a month ago, just gave up. I just wear my eye mask and blueblockers and get up for the sun.

It is hard here. We have been making major decisions while away, in the sun. But follow through gets bogged down here. We decided to move while we were in Panama. We decided to make sure were take time to go to the sun when days are short here.

My legs are still giving me grief, especially the left. The spasticity is bad, mostly flexor. I have pain deep in the center of the back of my left thigh. I have a standing wheelchair, so I am standing a lot to relieve that pain some. I am unable to ground much strapped into this thing and I suspect that is a factor.

We are just waiting for a closing date for the place in Mihla. We will be downsizing in population too. Mihla only has a population of 2000. I am anxious to be where I can ground and control our lighting and nnemf. I hope this puts us in a good enough of a place to manage a few more years and keep Jan healthy. He is really having problems with gerd and sleep here. I think our allotment garden has saved us, but it isn't enough.

### Theka — 2017-08-09T22:29:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-221285>

It has been a few weeks---busy weeks. We're trying to get our house deal finalized. I have been thinking about one of the external Gewölbekellar (vaulted cellars). One is in the backyard and has a level entry in the hillside, like a hobbit house. It has been used for storage and is dry with a packed dirt floor and a stone arched ceiling. It would make a fabulous wine cellar, but I am thinking about cleaning it up and sleeping there. They ran electric there for a fridge years ago---prob after unification, but I have to look at it. What about using an infrared mat? Would that be good? I had been thinking about a magnetico but I think with the dirt floor, I can sleep grounded anyway. What do you guys think? It sounds almost cosy with infrared there. The door is above grade but 3 sides are earth-sheltered, but I haven't checked the temperature. What do you guys think?

I think sleeping on the ground basically sheltered in the earth would be amazing for you - there was some study done where a bunch of people lived underground for awhile - many of them wanted to go back after they returned to their microwaved lives... just turn off the breaker to the electricity in there - maybe you could get a battery operated IR light - like they use for cameras-

### Theka — 2017-11-16T13:32:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-228163>

So, once again September brought me a nagging cold that eventually turned into a raging frontal sinusitis in November so I stopped my supplements, basically D3, B2 and magnesium. My stiffness and spasticity are much less. I do not know what to think about this.

I am looking into DDW. I am thinking of buying some DDW-25 and dosing it along the veterinary weight guidelines and otherwise drinking alpine spring water which I think ought to be better. I am taking MB too. I can't really see a way to get into the tropics for an extended period of time this year and Germany sun isn't doing it any time of the year. Although, one thing keeps popping up, many people say they can eat everything here without problems unlike in the USA. They figure there is something seriously different with the food--maybe GMO, maybe antibiotics or pesticides???? But I do think I can get back to Panama for a few weeks with Jan and talk things over again. If we can make decisions there sitting in the pool, we can follow through here, even if it is a hard slog. I wish we could manage Mexico but travel is so difficult in my condition.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-16T17:14:01+0200

When the sun drops at your latitude with your condition there is only one answer that matters.

Anyone who thinks food can solve a quantum problem really needs to read a lot more. "It has long been reported that Scotland has among the highest rates of MS in the world, with environmental factors such as a lack of sunshine and genetics thought to be behind the greater prevalence."
"The highest rates were also seen in other countries in northern hemisphere, including Canada at 291 cases per 100,000 people, Denmark at 227 and Sweden at 189."
Is it just me that can see the obvious link? Quantum inquiry is fatal to "food certainty" in MS cases. [http://www.scotsman.com/news/ms-10-000-scots-living-with-multiple-sclerosis-1-3121299](http://www.scotsman.com/news/ms-10-000-scots-living-with-multiple-sclerosis-1-3121299)

### Theka — 2017-12-04T15:05:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-229473>

I've been thinking about dt, pregnancy and ms. The activity of ms decreases during pregnancy. They have speculated that it is due to hormones, especial estriol. They have been studies using estriol to try and mimic the protection that pregnancy offers. Jack mentioned that dt is offloaded to the fetus during the last trimester. Is this the real cause? How can I offload more dt without getting pregnant?!!! ;-)

I want to reread Epi-Paleo with a deuterium perspective. I don't know how keto I need to be now. Last year, I just avoided carbs as best I could. Last year I also got to the sun twice in Nov and Feb/Mar.

### Theka — 2018-04-21T12:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-239629>

Jack posted a comment about histamine, quercertain and onions that made me wonder more about histamine. This Spring, my allergies are back! I have itchy eyes and nose, sneezing, typical hayfever. What makes this odd is I haven't had any for over 10 years. I never had allergies in Panama and this is the first year here in Germany (we moved in 2011). I'm feeling a bit better, more energy. We've just had a gorgeous week of summer in April with loads of sun and 80F temps. I know the sun is a huge factor but how does histamine fit in. In 2005, I experimented with some topical histamine drops (to approximate Elaine DeLack's Procarin patches). I didn't notice much with it but could a natural histamine response be helping now?

### Theka — 2018-06-16T20:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-243533>

A very quick update...in the past month, my husband has gotten a new job (starting in Oct), so we will finally be moving out of Frankfurt!!! and out of the highrise apartment! He will be working for the Thieme publishing group located in Stuttgart. I have been house-hunting and I think I've found us a good choice. It is withing a reasonable commute, but in a village of 2700. The majority of those live in the 'new' development from the 60-70s. It is an old wine region and I have found a small place, built in 1809. I really think the old construction methods were better from a building biology perspective. We will have neighbors....2, but the stream to the old mill runs along the other side of the street and there is no through traffic. There is a wooded, undevelopable hill behind the house. The house does have electric----but rudimentary....a few outlets and lights. It doesn't have central heat------the bathroom has an antique wood or coal fired boiler for hot water to bathe and a few other wood/coal stoves for heat. I sat in the driveway/front yard for several hours and felt good! I am sure that it is a much better environment, I just wish Germany had more sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-06-17T02:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-243562>

"Researchers have unveiled a new cell death mechanism called pyroptosis — also known as “fiery death” — as the main factor driving neurodegeneration and loss of myelin in people with multiple sclerosis (MS)."

Guess what the stimulus is to this pathway? Activation of the NLP3 inflammasome. What stops it? DHA in the cell membrane. What stimulus causes it? Stimuli that can be received by the inflammasomes include viral double-stranded RNA, **UREA**, _**free radicals**_, and other signals associated with cellular danger from a lowered mitochondrial redox state. There is recent evidence even byproducts of other immune response pathways can cause it too. The key to avoiding it? IMPROVE YOUR REDOX AND IT NEVER HAPPENS!

[http://www.pnas.org/content/early/2018/06/11/1722041115](http://www.pnas.org/content/early/2018/06/11/1722041115)

### Jude — 2018-06-17T04:50:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-243567>

Theka, did you manage to make it to Vermont? I kept hoping for you! Do you use a Sperti lamp to keep your D3 level up during all that grey sky time? Do hope your tooth problem resolved ok.

### Theka — 2018-06-17T16:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-243592>

> [Jude said:](/goto/post?id=243567)
>
> Theka, did you manage to make it to Vermont? I kept hoping for you! Do you use a Sperti lamp to keep your D3 level up during all that grey sky time? Do hope your tooth problem resolved ok.

Yes! It was everything I had hoped and more! We landed in Montreal and picked up our quantum hitchhiker from Denmark and got to Vermont very late Thursday evening. Shelburne Farms is just beautiful. Heard some interesting talks, met great people and spent time with my sister! I really was happy to finally meet Sandy and any chance to hear Jack is worth the travel. The next challenge is to get us moved and still be able to make it to PDC. I am working on that.

I finally have my dental surgery next week. I think it is a brilliant illustration of the mess of the healthcare system here. I am really unhappy with the clusterfuck that has been masquerading as dental care and indeed healthcare here. I think we are doing ok despite the 'care'.

I haven't used a sperti. I do use red lights a lot. I have tried the Coimbra protocol---a high vit D protocol, but it is seen as a lifelong remedy and I think it is only a bandaid. I am off everything now and feeling good----I'm sure the long light cycles and early Spring we had are a major factor. I am very curious to see what cleaning up the teeth and moving away change.

### Theka — 2018-06-26T23:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-244378>

We are waiting for the bank, not at all patiently. We have so many plans and ideas for this place. It is so unique, being built on a hillside. It is a though one arrives at the basement, the front door opens to steps to the 2nd floor. The back door, however, opens level to the wooded hillside! There is about a meter of level 'patio'. There is an unheated storage room beside the door----oddly enough it has a concrete floor, with a metal hatch that opens to the dirt below!! I think we can make that space into a infrared sauna/salt room. The bathroom is really small (4 sq meters), so I have to make it a wet room and take the tub out to make it work as an accessible bath. I am really excited to try and make us an optimal retreat from the world. I wish I could walk. I would camp in the 'smurf' house barn while we worked on it. The smurf house is a half-timbered former stall with an earthen floor in the front yard that has been painted blue.

I finally have my dental surgery on Thursday. I hope it makes a noticeable difference. I was surprised to find many of my ms symtoms improved last week when I gave in and took antibiotics for a bladder infection. Many are slowly resurfacing. I really wonder what is infection and what is environment. I believe the only way to really find out is to get to Mexico.

### Theka — 2018-07-02T00:28:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-244720>

Well, we have a meniscus issue now. Jan had a mri of his knee and was diagnosed with a small radial meniscus tear. The orthopedist has recommended surgery to smooth it followed by pt. He has been using ice packs on it and generally babying it and doing pretty well. Surgery sounds drastic, especially as they want general anesthesia. Is surgery really needed? What could he do to avoid it?

### Theka — 2018-07-20T18:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-246277>

20 days later and we have bank approval! Now to see how quickly we can get a closing date! This is crazy fast for Germany! I am looking forward to having a place we can mod to our needs. It is solidly built, newer roof but the interior is pretty much 1910-40. Pop. is 2600---but the majority live in 2 newer developments and the geography doesn't allow more to build near us. It is built above an earth-sheltered barn. I am very excited to try some of my building hack ideas!

### Theka — 2018-07-26T21:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-246766>

Today the realtor called us--they have accepted our offer! She asked when we wanted sign closing papers and was shocked when we said, 'tomorrow'! So we have an appointment in a week---very fast for Germany! We want to give notice here at the end of the month and move quickly. So we shall see how quickly we can jump through the various hoops to take possession and do the essentials to move in. I've had to shift gears away from the blogs to get this done. Jan accepted his new job offer the end of May, right before Vermont. By mid June, we had narrowed our house options and looked at the best option, then got financing arranged and negotiated the sale price. We are trying to take advantage of the local geography and 200 year old building techniques to make our refuge in Germany.

### Theka — 2018-08-02T22:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247322>

we bought the 'Hearts-Ease' today.......drove down yesterday and stayed in a simple hotel nearby----new, wood construction, no wifi in the rooms but with a/c. We both slept really well and both noticed less joint pain.

Back in Frankfurt now and it is so hot. I dunk my head often and stick ice packs in my clothes, even to sleep. Tomorrow is supposed to be 37C, that is 98.6F. I will be outside early in the garden, but it isn't cooling off much nights. I think a/c is a good thing, just to have an opportunity to really cool down. It would be easier if I could get my body in cold water. We have planned a wet-room shower at Hearts-Ease.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-03T06:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247346>

98.6F is a good thing.

CHIRAL HEAT EFFECT ALERT: Repeated exposure to heat therapy for 6 days increased biomarkers of mitochondrial biogenesis, increased mitochondrial function by 28%, and boosted heat shock protein 70 by 45% in healthy volunteers. Black swans know the sun is the best source of "heat therapy" [https://www.physiology.org/doi/10.1152/japplphysiol.00383.2018](https://www.physiology.org/doi/10.1152/japplphysiol.00383.2018)

And it was 101 F here today and I spent 5 hours in the sun. Just sayin'

### Theka — 2018-08-03T14:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247367>

how do I deal with not sweating----in Panama, I sat in the pool for 5 hours in strong sun with no problems. When we spent the night with a/c, I was fine being out in the sun intermittently the next day until I had been home in the hot apt for 1.5 hours, then I could tell I was overheating. I'm sitting with an ice pack on my stomach now and I sleep with one. I could work in the garden for an hour this morning from 8-9, then the sun was too strong. Now despite cold alpine water and head/face dunks, I can't cool down. When I get overheated, my muscles turn to mush---not sure whether it is muscle or the neural signals. I know I think and feel like a slug. Sweating has been a problem for a while. I love being in the sun and it usually feels really good.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-03T15:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247368>

not sweating = bad mitochondria in apocrine sweat glands on skin = mitochondria that can't make water = melanopsin skin problem = 50th latitude is not a game changer

all laid out easy to get the chain of data. All the pieces fit.

### Theka — 2018-08-03T16:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247387>

50N isn't our goal, this is a step in a better place. We can't leave Germany now, but we don't need to live in a high-rise in Frankfurt! This is north of the Black Forest (48lat), 30km nw of Stuttgart in a village of 2600---2 newer suburban developments comprise most of the population. We will be in the old part, on a dead end street across from a stream with a steep hillside (Tobel 469m) behind us. Jan will have to commute but he can relax at home in a better environment. It is small at the moment, about 700 sqft but I have plans to add a lot of sq footage by converting and finishing existing space which should give us a good exit strategy when we are able to retire to the sun.

that study referred to repeated 2hr exposure to the heat-----what were their conditions the other 22h? When I can cool down, I can easily be in the sun for an hour and with cooling opportunities, I could do longer with supervision. I am just unconvinced that 24/7 in 30+ is comparable.

### Theka — 2018-08-06T14:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-247672>

I am so anxious to move. I know that it is still Germany and only 180km south, but up here, everything wrong with me is 'due to ms and to be expected'. It is just a weird coincidence that Jan increasingly has similar pain and complaints---that is obviously due to aging and arthritis! I want to know what getting out of the 17th floor in Frankfurt will do for us. I want to continue this experiment in a cenote. I was invited to join mensa in high school, so I figure I ought to be able to function even with a brain like swiss cheese so I am choosing not to multi-task now to get the important stuff done during the long light cycles. #onestepatatime

### Theka — 2018-10-21T19:25:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-253862>

We have been in our house for 2 weeks now. Jan's painful joints and gerd-ish issues are gone. He totally believes all the nnemf stuff now! I feel like all of my cells have just relaxed! The constant buzzing, tingling humming sensations are gone. My legs feel stronger and I can stand better, spasticity is greatly reduced. This is with no therapy drugs, supplements or exercises, just being here. Of course the actual move was an utter nightmare and the renovations are not done, not even begun---we are living in antique conditions, but it is all good. We just 'feel' better.

### Theka — 2019-02-10T11:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-262765>

Four and a half months here, still living very simply while waiting for to get our improvements done. We are replacing the corrugated roofing in the rear with a green roof. The big challenge is getting the floor even and level. It was actually built sloped for no good reason. Once the floor is done, we can do our bathroom. The existing bathroom is barely functional; the toilet requiring buckets of water and a plunger and having a wood fired hot water heater for the tub. But we hear the stream across the street and there are loads of birds in the trees in the back. The Tobel is 439m and directly behind us. We named it Hearts-Ease and it is.

### Theka — 2019-02-10T13:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-262768>

I have lost a good friend. She was in her 60s and had struggled with primary progressive ms for years, but I really don't think that killed her. She lived in Eastern Washington, first in the mountains, then on a farm closer to town as she needed more help, then in the Fall, they finally moved into town. She actually had taught in a one-room schoolhouse for years and worked in forestry. She grew her own food and gathered mushrooms and herbs. In December, she broke her femur whilst transferring. It was a nasty break and required surgery and a large metal plate and loads of screws to repair. She was in sub-acute rehab and progressing well when they took her to the ER. This is how her family described it.

"Yesterday morning (1/27/2019) She was taken from her rehab facility in Wenatchee to the ER due to painful muscle spasms. While at the ER she began experiencing seizures. After seizures meds were administered and she was still experiencing seizures the decision was made to transport her to a facility with a more specialized neurological department. It was decided she would be transported to Sacred Heart in Spokane. Flying was not an option due to extremely foggy conditions blanketing most of Eastern Washington. Ground transport it was. She is currently in a medically induced coma because EEG results show she is continuing to experience seizure activity internally even with sedation and seizure medications. She will be kept in this medically induced coma until they can get the seizures stopped entirely. An MRI which was done earlier this evening will hopefully shed some light on the cause of the seizures.
Jan 29, 2019 As we start Day #3 Connie is still in a medically induced coma. Last night’s MRI showed all negative results. It had been hoped that the MRI would be beneficial in isolating the cause of the seizures. The doctors upped Connie’s seizure meds again last night attempting to give her brain a night’s rest. That was marginally successful. The seizure activity wasn’t quite as strong or as frequent thanks to the increased meds, however, it still didn’t stop them entirely.
Now we wait. Along with the continued sedation and seizure meds they continue to treat her UTI. The list of possible reasons she initially started seizing is long and all possibilities are being considered.
Jan 30, 2019 Doctors have decided to attempt weaning her off the sedation meds today. It will be a very very slow wean so they can monitor how she handles it and watch for seizure activity. They started at 8 a.m. this morning and will lower her sedation meds a small amount every 3 hours. If this can continue with no negative effects they believe she will be somewhat conscious in about 2 days.
Everything else remains the same. She is on 3 seizure meds and will stay on those as they attempt bringing her slowly out of the medically induced coma. They also continue to treat her UTI.
Feb 1, 2019 As we welcome the first day of February we are starting Day #6 in Sacred Heart ICU. Today we are in Cardiac ICU instead of Neurological ICU. She had a little problem with her heart last night. We were called to come back to the hospital around 9 p.m. because She was experiencing irregular heart rhythm. She was immediately zapped once with the paddles which got her heart into rhythm again. As soon as her rhythm was stable she was taken to the cath lab to check her arteries for possible blood clots. Results showed her arteries are clear and no clots. Good news!! Her EKG results were also good. More good news!
Her cardiac specialists believe the irregular rhythm may have been caused by one of her seizure meds so that med has now been removed. She remains on two seizure meds which will hopefully keep further seizures at bay. She has not regained consciousness yet but that is not unexpected. We have been told it will take 24-48 hours and possibly more for her to regain some degree of consciousness.
Feb 2, 2019 Connie had a good day yesterday and a good night last night with no new issues. Unfortunately that record didn't hold as we start into Day #7. While the nurse and doctor were both in her room this morning she went into Atrial fibrillation which caused her BP to immediately plummet. After being shocked 3 times her heart rhythm is now marginally stable. Still not back into a "normal" rhythm but better than where she was a couple hours ago. Meds are being administered to bring her heart rate down, try to control the A fib and regulate her blood pressure. In addition to all the heart issues she now is, again, running a temperature so tests are being performed in an attempt to locate any possible infection which may be causing the temperature. The UTI she was formally battling is resolved and tests for that were negative yesterday so at least we know that is not the cause of this new temperature.
Feb 3, 2019 It has now been a full week since Connie arrived here in Spokane. The good news today is that she had a quiet night with no new issues. She remains unconscious. She remains on two seizure meds, a med to stabilize her heart rhythm and diuretics for the fluid retention. Her low grade temp of yesterday resolved itself so no need for antibiotics.
Feb 6, 2019 Yesterday she took a step back when her seizures resumed. She had been down to two seizure meds for several days after the seizures were initially brought under control. Unfortunately, she was only seizure free for a little over 48 hours. When the seizures resumed two more seizure meds were added back in bringing her back up to a total of 4 seizure meds being administered around the clock. Needless to say this is not a good sign.
Feb 7, 2019 As day #12 comes to a close I unfortunately do not have any good news to report. The deterioration continues as her seizures are only able to be controlled with strong IV meds.
With deep sadness, we must announce that she died at 4:38 (Feb. 8th, 2019). For two weeks we've hoped and prayed that excellent medical care together with her strong will, would successfully bring her out of the coma.

This is the second such death recently. An uncle fell and broke his hip around New Years, he went into the hospital and came through the surgery fine. Then they found that he needed a pacemaker and he didn't come through that surgery well and within a few days, he died. This was in Germany and he was in his 80s but it seems to me that despite modern medicine being so good for trauma, the consequences are getting higher especially for the elderly.

My neighbor was widowed in October. She had cancer surgery in August and her husband was in a nursing home while she was in the hospital. When she came home, he had developed pneumonia and was hospitalized. She brought him home and within a day, he died peacefully. He was unusual in that he was born and died in the same house. That doesn't happen much anymore. He was very unhappy in the nursing home and later the hospital.

I wonder how these lives would have ended without medical care.

### Theka — 2019-03-06T14:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-264584>

I've been thinking about things a lot here. We feel good here. My current mitohack is a trifecta of ms gym, light hygiene and guided meditations. The basis is Jack Kruse; sunrise, light hygiene, and nnemf mitigation. Dr Joe Dispenza for the meditation. He talks about epigenetics and measurable changes in the brain and heart as well as stuff like cortisol levels and telemere length following meditations. If those kinds of quantifiable changes happen, it sounds like some of the 'environment' is being changed in the mind, so I ignore the woo. The ms gym and Trevor Wicken talks neuroplasticity and neural pathways and 'movement meds'. I just need longer days to fit it all in!

This winter for the first time, despite being stuck here since the move, I am doing better. It is March and I feel better. I am stronger. My legs are working a little now and starting to feel like legs and feet. I am sure it would be infinitely more if I were able to have my skin in the strong Panama sun again, but I need to be able to manage alone to have more options. I suspect my D levels have dropped. My fingernails are my canary and I've cracked a nail. That never happened in Panama. We had planned to go to Mexico this past winter but the move and new job happened and getting out of the high-rise in Frankfurt was the priority.

Theka, I am so happy for you! That you finally have a healthy silent place to live.... now you can start to heal  Soon summer is here and you can start grounded nude tanning from morning to evening and you will get so much better
in fact, I am sure you will soon walk again!

### Theka — 2019-03-28T19:38:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-266674>

I've been thinking about things and I just want to say, that I really don't give a fuck about the mechanism. I have read so much for so long and it is just lost opportunity cost. Back in the early days, 2002 or 3, I found an abstract of a pharma investor report. I wish I had saved a screen shot. It told me all I needed to know about the DMDs for ms and I never drank that kool-aide. I also found an old paper about ms incidence in Israel after WW2. It prompted us to move to Panama in the hopes of protecting our boys. No one ever said ms could be stopped,let alone reversed, so we moved for the kids. Everybody opposed our decision to move and it cost a lot of money as well. It is bittersweet to know that we actually were in the right place, sort of. We needed to be more rural there too. Panama city has too many lights and now they are all 6500K cfl. With all of the street lights, it was never really dark at night, but I didn't know that it mattered. Still, we usually saw the sunrise, because it was at 6am and the kids had to get to school, but not intentionally. We did build a solar callus, but again, not with intention and not enough and I spent my last year there working inside on a computer as a virtual receptionist all day. When we had to move for work, we didn't want to leave the sun. We applied to jobs from Qatar, India, Texas....but finally had to take Germany, because Germany would take us. Immigration is a bitch.

When I first heard Jack on an online conference in June 2015, I was flabbergasted. I listened again with my hubby and started reading more. By July I wanted to try, and I started 1 August. It was a great month but I lost it as Fall progressed. By the time I got to meet Jack, I could no longer manage independently and then it no longer matters if you get it or not. We applied to the States but without a Greencard or a Sponsor, that was a no-go. I did get to Panama again for 2 months in 2017 with my son as he was in between schools. I felt great in the sun in the pool, but he had to lift me in. Then we tried to move to Mihla for lower costs and less people and that fell through. This was when I made my Quantum End playlist on youtube, mostly 432hz. I don't think anybody noticed it but I was only sticking around to take care of my cats. I avoid doctors and had still heard about how great a suprapubic catheter works and other wonderful interventions and had decided to opt out. It was impossible in that environment.

Finally, now we can have a refuge and a job here, but it has all taken too much time and it is still in Germany. It isn't enough, but maybe it will do for a few years. I do have a vision for it but I have a tired cheerleader, ground down from years of expensive, futile crushed hopes---the true opportunity cost. Now, I get it, but no one gives a damn. I would have given anything to have an advocate in my corner. Maybe I have saved a few others. Maybe.

### Theka — 2019-07-11T22:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-274092>

I am intrigued by a post Jack made in the RF thread:

Lai, H, Carino, MA, Singh, NP, Naltrexone blocks RFR-induced DNA double strand breaks in rat brain cells. Wireless Networks 3:471-476, 1997.
Previous research in our laboratory has shown that various effects of radiofrequency electromagnetic radiation (RFR) exposure on the nervous system are mediated by endogenous opioids in the brain. We have also found that acute exposure to RFR induced DNA strand breaks in brain cells of the rat. The present experiment was carried out to investigate whether endogenous opioids are also involved in RFR-induced DNA strand breaks. Rats were treated with the opioid antagonist naltrexone (1 mg/kg, IP) immediately before and after exposure to 2450-MHz pulsed a power density of 2 mW/cm2 (average whole body specific absorption rate of 1.2 W/kg) for 2 hours. DNA double strand breaks were assayed in brain cells at 4 hours after exposure using a microgel electrophoresis assay. Results showed that the RFR exposure significantly increased DNA double strand breaks in brain cells of the rat, and the effect was partially blocked by treatment with naltrexone. Thus, these data indicate that endogenous opioids play a mediating role in RFR-induced DNA strand breaks in brain cells of the rat.

This sounds catastrophic for recovering drug addicts that take naltrexone. But I wonder about the effects on those taking LDN. The theory behind it when I researched it was that LDN temporarily suppressed endogenous enddorphin production prompting upregulation and increased endogenous production, which would \[rovide an increased mediating effect and possible protection from RF. I have read so many different diseases which LDN is said to benefit. Could this be the true reason? If the various illnesses are worsened by RF...

On a side note, I realize how much I am challenged reading new stuff from Jack. I learned about naltrexone many years ago. I generally feel like I have half my brain tied behind my back. ugh

### Theka — 2019-08-07T15:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-275581>

I've been thinking more about Jack fb post:

Anyone with a 25(OH)D around 20ng/ml (EU units) will require around
[https://grassrootshealth.net/…/chart-serum-level-intake-5-b…](https://grassrootshealth.net/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/chart-serum-level-intake-5-by-3-ngmla-both-charts-single.pdf?fbclid=IwAR1WyV2AIX7TjcfdnwZ39w7VTesw11giEBPEOGCGqt9EfSmVgAb6hmwgPCI)
8000 IU cholecalciferol daily. 52 iu vitamin D3 daily for each POUND of bodyweight.
2000iu daily is fine for someone weighing around 40 pounds so fine for someone about 4 years old.

[https://grassrootshealth.net/…/2018/09/PTH-serum-D-curve.pdf](https://grassrootshealth.net/wp-content/uploads/2018/09/PTH-serum-D-curve.pdf?fbclid=IwAR2GP2m3XX2v3mQkSFM-UcFqFB2NvExxEBo5j-SaWPBXJE9Dfo0Fc78ogHc)
Humans at the bare minimum need to get 25(OH)D up to 50ng/ml 125nmol/l to optimize PTH.

Most people can avoid calcium stones by reducing/eliminating high calcium food sources or making sure they eat natural foods with Vitamin K2 (raw milk cheeses and charcuterie). We can easily increase our citrate intake UP to a limit TO PREVENT CALCIUM AND URIC ACID STONES.

Citrate is an intermediate in the TCA cycle which is the central metabolic pathway for animals, plants, and bacteria. Citrate synthase catalyzes the condensation of oxaloacetate with acetyl CoA to form citrate. Citrate then acts as the substrate for aconitase and is converted into aconitic acid. Aconitase is where metabolic water is recycled and this makes it vulnerable to deuterium kinetics. This is why processed food is dangerous for humans because of its hydrogens have no protection of the photosynthetic pathways to make sure of the fidelity of the isotope of hydrogen around specific carbons. The cycle ends with the regeneration of oxaloacetate in the TCA cycle.

[https://kidneystones.uchicago.edu/citrate-to-prevent-stones/](https://kidneystones.uchicago.edu/citrate-to-prevent-stones/?fbclid=IwAR00qdM4Y1CbS7w--PPQTBuqyFqIM7_DpB-T0rEzN-I5ISHMIVwCUb7HeCI)
My choice is to use lemon-lime in my water and use shellfish with high citrate concentrations like Royal Red Gulf Shrimp. You can also use a half teaspoon Magnesium citrate powder is simple to dissolve in 2 liters water and if consumed through the day with food. It usually does not cause diarrhea in my experience. Most people consume less than the magnesium RDA daily so have too much calcium in relation to magnesium. Most people who are Mg deficient have a high BUN/creat ratio's from melanopsin dysfunction dehydration. This implies they also have significant thiamine issues. If we all consumed more thiamine/magnesium we would see fewer people complaining about problems with vitamin D levels because it would support water formation in the matrix and skin to perform the photoisomerization step of LDL to Vitamin D intermediates. Magnesium speeds up the raising of 25(OH)D and reduces the rate of increase over30ng/ml so protects you from the results of excess calcium.

It seems to me that ms researchers are like blind men trying to identify an elephant. They have pieces of the puzzle but don't get the whole picture.
Dr Coimbra uses heroic doses of D3 based on the hypothesis that patients have a genetic inability to properly use normal amounts. Dr Klenner uses injected thiamine because patients absorb it poorly in oral form. Dr Paolo Zamboni theorized blood hypoperfusion with excess iron and a possible genetic predisposition.

I the early 2000s, I read a study, the Risk of Multiple Sclerosis relation to the Age at Immigration to Israel. Although they seem to have been looking for a virus or infection as a cause, their observations were good. Based on that study, we moved with our young children, to Panama hoping to stay permanently. I think I connected the dots better than the researchers.

### Theka — 2019-11-15T18:13:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-humanoid-escapee-1001001.16788/post-282318>

I got my vit D test results! I had 91 in November! The doctor was a bit concerned and said I should stop supplements!!! I haven't taken any supplements for at least a year and a half, maybe longer! I didn't even think we had that great of a summer here. I am very happy! I think my mesh skirts and cheap linen t-shirts worked! I figured if I could see pinpoints of sun on my skin through the clothes that it should work like a kiniki as long as I skipped a bra.

I am less excited about my blood glucose being 104 and my HDL being only 47. I should have written this earlier in the day. It is a bit too dark now to read anymore of the lab results by candle light. We have to do another makeshift winter here. The contractors took so long to get estimates and proposals to us to manage it this year. We installed a woodstove for heat and a basic new ADA height toilet that flushes without a bucket. We already removed the old electric heaters in the spring.
