The contractors took so long to get estimates and proposals to us to manage it this year. We installed a woodstove for heat and a basic new ADA height toilet that flushes without a bucket. We already removed the old electric heaters in the spring.

wow Theka, so happy for you!!   Looks like moving to the countryside really have helped you
How are you doing otherwise, how are you feeling?

### sjoshua — 2015-08-27T18:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-friends-life-updates.16789/post-175884>

Hi all,

I haven't been around for a while since life has been a roller coaster the last few months. The only constant is change so I am embracing it to the fullest. I wanted to touch base and say hi, and share my trajectory for those interested

My dogs and I are moving back to Upstate, NY in a week, and after catching up with family and friends for a bit, we will be spending this winter living in a Tipi on 40 acres of forest in the southern tier to reconnect with nature (tipi.com if interested in checking them out). After that is up in the air since who knows what will happen between now and then, but a likely scenario is nomadic living wherever I end up.

I have a model that I've been framing based on Jack's as well as all of the other information in infinity that I've been privied to that will represent a very simple method of describing how 'existence' works in its entirety. My goal is that if you can understand the basics of the light frequencies of a rainbow and simple math, you'll be able to grasp how your body works, and how the rest of the pieces around you all interact with it.

I am also executing my model's design on myself and have begun adapting the fleshpod further; Currently only eating once every other day with awesome results and plan to continue to phase this down every 3 weeks (i.e. go to once every 3 days, 4 days etc; targeting 1 every 7 days by Christmas) as my fleshpod/mitochondria continue to adapt to be capable of high spin/balanced states that allow full utilization of zero point energy.

I'll check back here as possible before my departure, and then continue to touch base as I can. I will share the model here when it is completed and vetted through a few people to assure full grasping... it is currently 30 pages of notes and pictures and I probably have about half of it out. End goal will be an easy read with pictures or possibly power point style presentation.

Meditate my friends, all the answers are inside!

Much love,
Josh

### sjoshua — 2015-11-08T00:36:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-friends-life-updates.16789/post-181356>

Hey friends!

It has been a couple months so I wanted to take a couple minutes to check in.

**First**: I have my tipi up!!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I have NOT moved in yet though. The land it is on is used by the owners primarily for hunting and right now is peak season. I will move in the first or second week in December. Until then I am camping there occasionally and prepping wood supply etc.

**Second**: My food phase out is going splendidly. I am currently only eating Friday night through Saturday or Sunday evening (the difference between length is going solely based on how my body responds, how I feel, and other surrounding circumstances). I do not eat Monday through Friday afternoon - I feel no weakness, hunger, etc. considering and lead an active life - walking almost everywhere, ride my bike on occasion, and keep busy with all sorts of random journeys helping people around me.

Until I move into the woods I plan to stay on this eating schedule based on intuitions (and desire) and not phase out food any further.... Namely because I seem to have difficulty fitting sufficient(/desired) foodstuff in the fleshpod in any smaller of a window. But also, because I still enjoy (and desire) food to some extent, and when eating this rarely there are NO restrictions to what I can consume with near zero repercussions to life experience aside from bouncing back from the slow all the digestion/congestion causes my fleshpod. Another reason is I still enjoy (desire) the act of eating, especially in the company of others....\*\*

Since most will be curious, I typically start my eating each weekend with 1-2 lbs of seafood, such as tuna steaks, salmon fillets, mussels, oysters, seafood medley bisques or other randoms as align. Then I eat whatever. I typically desire to eat some sort of steak, pork chops, burgers or similar, but do not always eat them on the first night. Lately I've set out to craft 'my' perfect dessert. So far it has evolved into an almond butter chocolate bark banana cream coconut cream chocolate pudding pie with coconut whipped cream on top... I've been eating about half of each progressive evolution of pie per weekend. Also, candy recently with Halloween, and gummies hit my taste buds right. My body flushes everything like magic, using what it wants and discarding the rest within a couple days.

Also noteworthy, I DO leave complete flexibility in the system, and do not consider embracing the random desire or occasion of having a meal out of schedule, but it is not excessive eating nor typical at all.

Further, my weight. When I first shifted to eating once every other day, my weight dropped 10-15 lbs, from ~155 to 140-145ish. Ever since then it has remained stable through all of the other transitions: 3 days, 4 days, then to weekends only. I typically check the morning before I break my fast... ocassional weeks since then it has been lower (the minimum 136) but it has always averaged the 140-145 range overall. I am 5'8" and have very low body fat while maintaining a very defined, dense muscular structure that only seems to be improving further and further.

\*\*I used desire a lot; this is since at this point I feel food is one of the desires (i.e. such as spoken about in the Buddhist tradition, as one of many examples of systems touching on such concepts in some way shape or form) that must be fully abandoned to truly even be capable of letting go of the concept of desire itself. At some intuitive level, I feel as if I already have no NEED for food. I choose to embrace the desire to eat while I still embrace the city life, since life must be lived to the fullest in each moment too!!!

**Third**: My water phase out has been happening COMPLETELY naturally in line with my food phase out. I have put no thought or effort to it, only perceived that ever since I have passed the eating every 3 days threshold, my water desires/needs have decreased naturally as my body continues to adapt to a more efficient state. After a couple days of fasting, there are some days that pass I won't drink anything and not ever realize/want/need it.

**Fourth**: My fleshpod is healing itself at a very deep level and at an exponentially escalating pace lately (i.e. untangling the tensions that I have held onto by experiencing moments of 'reality' in an other-than-balanced state from an energetic point of view - briefly stated in the manner in which I contemplate things). I can feeeeel a ridiculous energy charge that builds in/around me with such ease now. Meditation is magical. Breathing exercises can generate the energy charge to cause my entire body from head to toe to fingertips to shiverrr as if freezing cold even though they are perfectly warm. Intercourse is on the infinite plane that most would dream only a fairytale... (maybe TMI!!...) full on bodily experience of tingling orgasm without the need for ejaculation is now shifting to be the normal for me unless otherwise desired; bringing and holding a female in her orgasmic bliss for an endless amount of time/space is easily achieved through the charge potential I can generate/hold. Etching intentions into reality while floating at the vibrational frequencies possible while sharing the intimacy of an amplifying orgasm floating so near infinity is truly impacting the rate at which life trajectories have been rapidly 'gravitating' towards positivity and true passions/intentions.

**Fifth**: I have found a 'translator' for my rainbow model. She has a similar perspective on life and can easily relate to what I have scribbled. We plan to bring it to the world as soon as we can do it properly... This shit may end up a TED talk some day.

**Sixth**: Although I am not living in the tipi yet, I am still embracing the natural environments as much as possible. I typically only wear shorts all day long, and am outside as much as possible in upstate, NY. At night I may put on a shirt if desired, though not always. I also wear shoes as rarely as possible - even if I am going somewhere requiring shoes, I will carry them and put them on upon arrival.

**Seven**: MANY people have told me they can feel how intense my energy/charge is these days. I have gotten reiki a few times and TRULY surprised the practitioner... more to come as I continue to clear myself out.

Cheers! Please leave any questions and I'll try to address when I stop back

much love,
Josh

### lilreddgirl — 2015-11-25T07:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-friends-life-updates.16789/post-182631>

Been thinking about this update post a lot...

I feel concerned because it reminds me of me, feeling supercharged and powerful also using Taoist practices and breathing, especially while conducting a long month long fast where I was not eating much but using grounding a breath. This really supercharged me...

and then I pushed it too far into a 3 month minimal food fast and I got ill... my EMF sensitivity.

Thinking back I probably 'burned out' my mitochondria... but I was so used to myself as energetically strong and so used to fasting at that point that I did not stop at my original planned 30 days. I'm concerned here because I know how wonderful it can feel when your energy is strong to not eat for 1, 2 or 3 days and feel your body feed off grounding and breath.

But pushing it to not eating up to 7 days repeatedly I think might not be beneficial... the way it can seems when you're strong and gaining strength from fasting. Please be very careful and don't push yourself here.

There was a moment in my past when I chose to keep pushing fasting out of habit and sticking to mental goals... I regret not listening to that intuition that knew that increasing food intake would now be appropriate, although I was having so much success and energy in my fasting state. I did not catch on that I was hurting myself because the same technique had helped me previously, and the shift was SUBTLE...

and then my heaven turned into hell. I don't want that to happen to you. As strong as you know you are, be careful with not eating food for a long time - 7 days is a long time. Be very watchful and don't always push yourself to keep going longer and longer, going back to less is just as good a choice. Be watchful for that choice.

# Thread: nnEMF Causes Excess Calcium causing excess Nitric Oxide which (can) cause a potent oxidant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frank Peters — 2015-08-29T03:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-175997>

Here's the context of my question. It is coming from Dr Martin Pall's work:
(See YouTube -

)

In 14 minutes, Dr Pall lays it out as follows but do listen to his YouTube. At 4 minutes 30 seconds into it, he gets to this point.

-   nnEMF (hyper)activates L-Type Voltage Gated Calcium Channels in the cell
-   causing an increase in intracellular calcium
-   increased calcium activates two enzymes, nNOS and eNOS, which are nitric oxide synthases

These nitric oxide synthases are calcium dependent and can't activate unless there is increased levels of calcium in the cell and when calcium levels increase so too do levels of nitric oxide.

Nitric oxide can work along two different pathways; one pathway is normal but the other pathway is pathophysiological.

-   on the pathophysiological (bad) pathway
    -   the elevated levels of nitric oxide combine with superoxide to form peroxynitrite
    -   peroxnitrite (a potent oxidant) causes oxidative stress

HERE IS MY QUESTION.
Wouldn't this phenomenon show up in blood work? Increased calcium? Increased nitric oxide? Peroxnitrite?

### PaulG — 2015-09-03T13:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176307>

Good question.

See also [http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html) - there are a lot of papers linked there.

[https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/](https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/)

> Weak electromagnetic fields cause calcium ions to detach from cell membranes, a well-documented effect that has significant consequences. That “highly conductive salty fluid” that we call blood makes the human body an efficient antenna for picking up electromagnetic radiation, says Goldsworthy. “If an alternating electrical field is applied across the membrane, the negatively charged membrane and the positively charged \[calcium and potassium\] ions move in opposite directions so that the ions tend to bounce on and off the membrane.”
>
> Calcium ions with a double charge are more easily removed from the membrane than the single-charge potassium ions. Because potassium is more abundant in living cells than calcium, potassium ions are more likely to replace calcium, however, calcium ions give cell membranes a stability that potassium ions cannot. Weakened cell membranes tear more easily.
>
> The body has mechanisms for repairing torn membranes, but, before healing occurs, weakened membranes allow cell contents to escape, causing inflammation and free calcium to enter the cell. Calcium triggers metabolic changes in cells, including enzyme activity and gene expression. Its effects depend upon the cells involved.
>
> “Leakage in the hair cells of the cochlea give false sensations of sound (tinnitus),” explains Goldsworthy, “and those in the vestibular system (which sense rotation, linear acceleration and gravity) give false sensations of motions, which result in dizziness and symptoms of motion sickness, including nausea.” Too much calcium in brain neurons causes the cells to release neurotransmitters more easily.
>
> Goldsworthy hypothesizes, “It is possible that at least some forms of electrosensitivity could be due to the victims having their natural blood calcium levels bordering on hypocalcaemia.”
>
> Hypocalcaemia is a medical condition, usually caused by a hormone imbalance, in which the concentration of ionized calcium in the blood and in extracellular fluid is abnormally low. People with hypocalcaemia have fewer ions to complete with the potassium ions rushing to cell membranes disrupted by electromagnetic currents.
>
> Hypocalcaemia and electrosensitvity share many of the same symptoms: skins disorders, paresthesias (pins and needles, numbness, sensations of burning, etc.), fatigue, muscle cramps, cardiac arrhythmia, and gastrointestinal problems. Goldsworthy says, “If this \[hypothesis\] is correct, conventional treatment for hypocalcaemia may relieve some if not all of these symptoms.”

[http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy\_bio\_weak\_em\_07.pdf](http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy_bio_weak_em_07.pdf)

### Frank Peters — 2015-09-04T22:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176495>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=176307)
>
> Good question.
>
> See also [http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html](http://www.oscillatorium.com/id19.html) - there are a lot of papers linked there.
>
> [https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/](https://www.healthyhabits.com/emf-calcium-electrosensitivity/)
>
>
> [http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy\_bio\_weak\_em\_07.pdf](http://www.hese-project.org/hese-uk/en/papers/goldsworthy_bio_weak_em_07.pdf)

Hi PaulG. I than you for taking the time to refer me to these. But when I looked at them, I found them troubling. The first link, osillatoruim.com, is bogus because it does not link to a lot of papers. Rather clicking on any link on the web page downloads a screen shot of that web page in pdf format. So, nothing there links to a study.

The second link, healthhabits.com, is to a business that wants to sell stuff like vitamins. At this point, I am not interested in buying stuff because I am looking to learn more, first.

And the last link to Andrew Goldsworth's "The Biological Effects of Weak Electromagnetic Fields" is a link to his review and conclusions on some of the literature. I only saw little mention on calcium levels in the blood and these were levels going in the opposite direction, lower levels of calcium not higher. He makes no mentions in his review of the literature of VGCC's, nitric oxide synthases, nitric oxide or peroxnitrite. I am therefore ruling out his work in trying to answer my question.

I thank you again as I am hoping to find a definitive answer to my question above.

### PaulG — 2015-09-05T01:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-causes-excess-calcium-causing-excess-nitric-oxide-which-can-cause-a-potent-oxidant.16790/post-176505>

This is the pdf from the oscillatorium site, links do work
[http://oscillatorium.com/sitebuildercontent/sitebuilderfiles/calciumefflux102014.pdf](http://oscillatorium.com/sitebuildercontent/sitebuilderfiles/calciumefflux102014.pdf)

Also try Pubmed, quick search found this article
**[Electromagnetic fields instantaneously modulate nitric oxide signaling in challenged biological systems](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22940137)**

> This study shows that a non-thermal pulse-modulated RF signal (PRF), configured to modulate calmodulin (CaM) activation via acceleration of Ca(2+) binding kinetics, produced an immediate nearly 3-fold increase in nitric oxide (NO) from dopaminergic MN9D cultures (P < 0.001). NO was measured electrochemically in real-time using a NO selective membrane electrode, which showed the PRF effect occurred within the first seconds after lipopolysaccharide (LPS) challenge. Further support that the site of action of PRF involves CaM is provided in human fibroblast cultures challenged with low serum and exposed for 15 min to the identical PRF signal. In this case a CaM antagonist W-7 could be added to the culture 3 h prior to PRF exposure. Those results showed the PRF signal produced nearly a two-fold increase in NO, which could be blocked by W-7 (P < 0.001). To the authors' knowledge this is the first report of a real-time effect of non-thermal electromagnetic fields (EMF) on NO release from challenged cells. The results provide mechanistic support for the many reported bioeffects of EMF in which NO plays a role. Thus, in a typical clinical application for acute post operative pain, or chronic pain from, e.g., osteoarthritis, EMF therapy could be employed to modulate the dynamics of NO via Ca/CaM-dependent constitutive nitric oxide synthase (cNOS) in the target tissue. This, in turn, would modulate the dynamics of the signaling pathways the body uses in response to the various phases of healing after physical or chemical insult or injury.

### Aerose91 — 2015-08-29T07:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon.16791/post-176011>

As someone who is very sick I'm exponentially more sensitive to things than normal people. For a few days prior and during a full moon my disease goes haywire. Particularly my adrenaline levels go through the roof and therefore my sleep gets even worse than normal. I'm talking 30-60 minutes a night which leads me into even further disease.

Sometimes it will go as far as psychosis (which I am close to daily regardless) but whatever the symptoms may be, it's always a revved up situation.

Is there anything that can be done to counter this aside from the regular protocols? Nothing I can do seems to have any effect.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-08-29T08:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/full-moon.16791/post-176012>

Very similar situation here. This entire week has been extremely trying for me, and I'm sure today will be tough. I've been feeling as though I'm watching the things around me from a perspective other than my own; almost movie-like. In other words, my normal dissociation is greatly heightened. This induces brain fog because everything is so "weird", and then I become anxious about that, which exacerbates everything.

I think anything that would enhance one's own personal magnetism/stored charge in cells would help. Ground as much as possible, perhaps all day. Stay cold all day. Be ketotic. If possible, us various biohacker technologies such as CES, TENS, MET (microcurrent electro-therapy), etc. especially if combining it with other things. Example -> CES @ an alpha wave frequency while grounding and using the cool fat burner+cool gut buster and/or the CryoHelmet while also running a MET program at specified muscles/tissues.

# Thread: Unfortunate recent reaction to oysters -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JodyT2 — 2015-08-29T09:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176013>

I've been buying oysters for a while now. Very reliable source, I eat them over the days from Sunday to Wednesday. Zero problems.

Then 2 weeks ago something changed. We were having a heat wave and our refridge was working overtime to try to keep things cold. I knew the fridge had zones that were colder than others and it was particularly challenging that day because I had come home with 5 doz and I put them in separate bowls in various places in the fridge (I have 2 roommates so I can't hog every space). I have a refridge thermometer that I bought for CT but I keep it in the fridge, so I was able to verify temps and could see the struggle the fridge was having, hitting almost 55˚ even though it's set at 40˚. Lower sections of the fridge were colder so I was trying to put the oysters lower. The house has essentially no insulation so we tend to not use A/C much. But, we eventually put it on. This ended up over-cooling the fridge in places and I was pretty certain that some of the oysters froze... but it was such a gentle freeze that it wasn't like there was any ice on anything. However, the wet towels I put over the oysters to keep them from drying out... those had frozen in the way that wet fabric freezes into a shape. I didn't know if this would have insulated the oysters? Or if this was proof they froze. My roommate and I thought it over and decided to just eat a bunch that evening because we didn't want them to go to waste. Mentally, I found that I was having a bit of a hard time eating them though. I didn't want to be eating them, and they just weren't going down great. Neither of us got sick and my roommate was thrilled to have them. He seemed fine. But I couldn't say why exactly, but I just had this mental thing. Something was turning me off.

I still had some unfrozen oysters left over that seemed to be ok, and I ate those later in the week. If I'm remembering correctly, those did not sit well in my stomach but rather, made my stomach hurt. I did not get sick, it just made my stomach hurt and it took the better part of the day for me to feel better. I had made a protein smoothie thing a while after eating the oysters. It's a product my doctor has me taking. I thought maybe the combo of those just didn't work or something but I've been doing the protein thing for a while too. None of this stuff is new, and I'm pretty sure I've eaten them in close proximity before.

So, fast forward to this week. I bought oysters again at the Farmers Market on Sunday, brought them home and I ate some on Monday. Stomach ache again. I made sure to not have the protein smoothie thing. This time I had bought some different, larger oysters though. I thought maybe it was the 'new' oysters that weren't working for me. I had some others types remaining yet that were smaller which I have had many times before, so I decided to eat those later in the week, on Thursday (I didn't eat any oysters in between Mon & Thur). I was very careful to not eat anything around that time so as to not upset my stomach with other things. Still ended up with a stomach ache. I've since eaten shrimp and salmon with no reaction. Have not yet tried mussels, but seafood seems fine.

As a weird aside, I noticed recently... starting around the same timing as the oyster problem... that my nose is boogering up more. This is totally different than what I feel I have been experiencing for the last year (which was a low booger rate), so it's a noticeable change. It's not loose snot, it's the crusting up you get in the nose either when you wake up, or throughout the day. I have a nose ring so I'm perhaps a bit more aware of this than others.

I am not sure what all this means, but it is a noticeable difference that I can't attribute to anything. Maybe they aren't related but... I'm thinking they are. I'm not having trouble eating anything else... seems to just be the oysters. That is super unfortunate because I've been noticing improvements with my skin in general so I KNOW I was having a positive reaction to them.

I am supposed to be having blood drawn again - I have the paperwork and just need to go in for the draw so I will have something to reference, but I'm not sure it will point to anything. What would change with oysters if they are slightly frozen? They were eaten within just hours of potentially freezing and kept very cold the whole time... just don't know what might have changed??? Or, any ideas what tests could be done?

Any help appreciated -

Jody

### fitness@home — 2015-08-29T15:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176017>

Jody, can't really comment on the oysters but wanted to say that sometimes my body seems to need a break from certain foods. After binging heavily on Gulf shrimp for the last couple of months, I noted my body doesn't seem to like it as much, nor do i desire it.

When this happens I just take a break from that particular item (has happened with salmon and sardines before). In a few weeks I'm craving it again.

How about I eat your share of oysters for dinner today and you go for the shrimp I am no longer craving?

### JodyT2 — 2015-09-05T22:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176567>

Hi Fitness@home-
Thanks for your comments. I've seen a few people mention needing to take a break from things. I'm hoping that is what is happening at the moment. I see how it seems to be tied to that event of them freezing but I don't really know what to think about it. I'll just do that - - take a break - - - and gladly eat your shrimp!
Thanks

### cinnamon — 2015-09-06T03:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unfortunate-recent-reaction-to-oysters.16792/post-176587>

Hi Jody!

I'm wondering if your oysters were already shucked. I don't think they last too long in the fridge (maybe a couple of days?) once they have been shucked. And if the fridge wasn't working too well, they might have been a bit off.

On the other hand, if you were shucking them yourself and they were still firmly closed before you used your shucking knife, they were probably okay.

Even if they were okay, I have found that if you have a bad experience, it's best to back off for a few days. It's kind of like your gut reacts and says "Oh no, remember what happened last time!!"

# Thread: Coconut Oil & Weight Loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-weight-loss.16794/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frank Kaplan — 2015-08-31T04:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-weight-loss.16794/post-176088>

I've been eating quite a lot of coconut oil - and plenty of Coconut Manna. I've been losing weight - especially with a lot of seafood and very low carbs.

I just read this on Hyperlipid and how eating it can cause an increase in blood insulin levels (albeit for chow fed lab rats) was curious about anyone's take on it:

Anyone reading [Chris](http://conditioningresearch.blogspot.com/2008/04/its-not-just-carbs.html) or [Emma's](http://blog.plantpoisonsandrottenstuff.info/2008/04/30/aspirin-like-compounds-increase-insulin-secretion-in-otherwise-healthy-obese-people/) blogs will realise that aspirin, and possibly other related salycilates from plants, cause the pancreas the secrete extra insulin. Avoid. Gluten and wheat germ agglutinin (both from wheat, barley and rye) are (or contain) insulin mimetics, avoid. Casein stimulates insulin secretion, avoid. Pharmaceutical NSAID probably do the same as salycilates, avoid if possible.

**Coconut oil** is interesting. It has a reputation for assisting weight loss, but if gavaged in to the stomach of a chow fed lab rat it will decrease blood glucose and increase blood insulin levels. You don't want to increase your insulin levels if you want to loose weight. There are other plus and minus sides to coconut oil, but I'd keep life simple and avoid it.

# Thread: My Quest To Be Better Than The Rest
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 73
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alex Fergus — 2015-08-31T08:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-176097>

Hello everyone.
I have been reading Dr Kruses' work for a while now (I heard him on Ben Greenfields show a while back). I have only recently joined the forums and started reading the vast wealth of knowledge here. I want to know more about the body and how to achieve optimal health.
I am a personal trainer, competitive athlete, and a coach.

I am 28 year, Male, living in sydney. 100kg at about 8% bodyfat. I am strong, I have achieved a lot in sport and I'm fit. However I'm by no means perfect. In fact I'm far from perfect. My testosterone levels are very low, and a recent DUTCH (dried urine test) showed i have high cortisol metabolites. So this is why I'm here.

I want to improve, and I aim to journal it all here.

**My background:**
5 years full time personal trainer
Starting to do more online coaching now (shameless plug - you can see everything at [afcoaching.com.au](http://afcoaching.com.au)
Born in New Zealand, raised on a farm, moved to sydney to work in an Investment Bank, hated it.
Represented NZ in rowing, broke Australian powerlifting records, and have won national bodybuilding titles. All natural, I care about my body to ever go down that other path.

**My Situation:**
June 2014 I had a bunch of lab tests done. At this stage I had been following a Carb Backloading (john Kiefer) type diet, but was slowly becoming more aware of paleo and primal eating. I was eating grassfed beef but I wasn't eating organ meats or bone broth for example.
The test came back showing very low cortisol, low T, high E and all sorts of issues with my thryoid, iron, crp (was 2.0) and a few other markers.
You can see my BLOOD tests here:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/4yqs6jr9qv47fhp/ALEX FERGUS JUNE 2014 BLOOD.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/4yqs6jr9qv47fhp/ALEX%20FERGUS%20JUNE%202014%20BLOOD.pdf?dl=0)
And my Saliva tests here:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/36a9kdixwghcakj/ALEX FERGUS JUNE 2014 SALVIA.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/36a9kdixwghcakj/ALEX%20FERGUS%20JUNE%202014%20SALVIA.pdf?dl=0)

This was the turning point, and I started throwing myself at health and learning more.

I have since done a genetic test:
I have methylation issues.
Compound heterozygous for MTHFR and homozygous for COMT.
Followed up by a methlyation test:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/yby39a7yn1c626q/ALEX FERGUS Methylation.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/yby39a7yn1c626q/ALEX%20FERGUS%20Methylation.pdf?dl=0)

I have done urine organics acid test:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/z094j7dhq6bbf5e/ALEX FERGUS-1 Organic Acids.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/z094j7dhq6bbf5e/ALEX%20FERGUS-1%20Organic%20Acids.pdf?dl=0)
This showed poor gut health, maybe candida?

The last few weeks I have done another blood test looking at my thryoid:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/kod43z4b8073jdp/ALEX FERGUS Pathology August 15.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/kod43z4b8073jdp/ALEX%20FERGUS%20Pathology%20August%2015.pdf?dl=0)

And just received my DUTCH hormone results:
[https://www.dropbox.com/s/f7odyungynvzci2/alex DUTCH July 15.pdf?dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/s/f7odyungynvzci2/alex%20DUTCH%20July%2015.pdf?dl=0)
These done 14 months after my JUNE 2014 tests.

Nutshell - My thryoid has improved, but its still not great. CRP is still 2.0
My cortisol has gone from low to really high.
Test has stayed low.
Estrogen has gone from high to low.

**My Training.**
As a PT and athlete, I train hard. I have backed off BIG TIME since my June 2014 results (stage 3 adrenal fatigue?).
Nowdays, I use HRV every day to gauge whether I should train. but typically I do:
2-3x weights/strength sessions
1-2x HIIT sessions

I also walk alot and use a stand up desk.

**My Diet**
I think its pretty good:
Smoothie in the AM (egg yolks - free range, coconut cream - BPA free, 90% dark choc, 1/2 avocado, some cold pressed NZ whey powder - full info [HERE](http://afcoaching.com.au/blogs/blog/36986305-the-ultimate-breakfast-energy-smoothie-for-all-day-vigour)).
Lunmch - Leftovers - roast beef and vege, steak and vege etc
Snack - macadamia nuts & butter
Dinner - curries, slow cooked stews, wild salmon (once a week), roast
Dessert/Evening - Carbs - sweet potato, white rice, ripe banana. With a lot of raw milk

Rinse and repeat
I also eat organ meats eat plenty of raw saurkraut and drink only raw milk. I make and drink a lot of bone broth. I eat alot of vegies. I am careful with protein. My marcos would be 60-70% fat, 20/20 carbs/pro

**Supps**
Marine Phytoplankton (is this any good?) daily
5-MTHFR folate 5x a week (400mg)
Coq10 (thorne qbest 50)
Vit C
Curcumin
Tian Chi chinese herbs
Green Tea
Magnesium
D/K2 drops
Zinc
B5

**Lifestyle.**
Since June 2014, I have improved my sleep (it used to be rubbish). My room is pitch black \_ i can't see my hand. Lights off at 1030pm no matter what. Up around 630am. Sleep good 80-90% of the time (wake once or twice). I use blue blockers and candles at night. Flux if I have to use my computer.
I am outside walking to work 2-3x a day (I go home in between clients). So I move alot and sydney is very sunny.
I go to the park 3-4x a week
I don't drink booze or coffee.
I have a pretty low stress life - self employed, but I'd rather say no to a client and lose $80 than get up at 5am etc. Health is my priority. Performance is second.

I live in a dense apartment building - a lot of EMF
I don't do much for EMF protection - I don't use wifi but there are alot of networks that come up on my computer.

I wear a cool fat burner ice vest for 2-3 hours every day. I have just started doing this (2-3 weeks now) and I'm doing cold showers a few times a week.
Occasionaly I nap.

**The Problem.**
I don't know what it is.
Why is my Test so low. My Naturapath (Michael from planetnaturopath.com) said I don't have any genetic issues for low T.

He is surprised how I am 100kg with abs and plenty of muscle, doing some impressive things in the gym, yet I have low T.
My libido is non existent. It picks up for a day or two, then disappears for a month.
My recovery is pretty solid, but I'm keeping my volume low due to the high cortisol.

I do have skin issues - tinea outbreaks, and pimples on my arms (but no where else).
I also have a white coating on my tongue, and occasionally my poops look weird (soft and lots of little bits and pieces in there).

I was tested for a parasite last year - I was fine.

Oh, and I take digestive enzymes with meals. I don't have bloating or gas. But I think something is wrong with my gut.

My Naturopath & I are starting to think it could be Candida. Would this explain the low T, high cortisol and high CRP (2.0).
How is the best way to fix this? Low FODMAP diet?
What do you guys think? Is there something else I'm missing?

Appreciate any comments. I'll be adding to this as my journey continues.

Again, I'm pretty new with all of Dr Kruse's work, so I'm sure the more I read and learn, the more my lifestyle/diet/training/supplementation will change (and hopefully my hormone levels!)

Cheers
Alex

Hi Sue, thanks for your response

**Are you covering all the low hanging fruits of the 3 legged stool? light, water, magnetism**
To be honest, I am still learning the ways of Dr Kruse. I have been focusing on diet, exercise and sleep. But I'm trying to read and apply as much as possible. So I'm probably not doing that well on that front at the moment.

**Are you going outside as soon as you wake up to get the morning light on your eyes to help set your circadian rhythm?**
I walk to work 30mins after waking in the morning. Its a 10min walk outdoors. I try and look towards the sun during this walk. I also try and get out on our balcony (I live 5 floors up) and look towards the sun every morning. I will make this more of a priority (especially on weekends).

**Do you/ can you sunbathe nude when the sun is at its most healing? (On a man's tackle it should do wonders for his hormones, and if candida is an issue uv light is anti fungal - it can help change the terrain so the baddies can't thrive......)**
Unfortunately
not. I don't think I have every done this in my adult life. I think I would be arrested if I did this on bondi beach haha. Maybe I can find a nude beach. Otherwise I try to exposre as much skin as possible when sunbathing.

**how much and what type of water do you drink?**
A couple of litres a day. I'm actually making an effort to drink less, as I'm always going to the loo. I have a RO system and I only drink that. I even take this water to work/training. I removed the alkaline filter. Is this water ok? I've been drinking this for 2 years.

**Are you doing any barefoot walking or something else to regularly ground?**
I do alot of this on the weekend, not enough during the week. I may need to buy some of those grounding sandals. We (my girlfriend and I) used to sleep on a grounding mat. But we disconnected it recently after fearing that is was acting as an amplifier given that we are surrounded by wifi signals in our apartment. How effective are grounding straps/mats. Should I use one when working at my desk?

**Can you up your seafood intake?**
Yes and I will.

How effective is [Sunfoods Marine Phytoplankton?](http://bit.ly/afc_marine) I take this daily. Is this ok? Or should I use a fish oil capsule?
What about tinned wild salmon? I avoid it due to the BPA lining. But I've just found a BPA free wild caught salmon product. Are these ok? I'm really nervous about omega 3 oxidation.
Can I cook salmons? Or should I eat it raw (due to omega 4 oxidation)

Thanks for your time and helping me out  I'm very grateful, please let me know if I can help in anyway in return.

### Peertje80 — 2015-09-01T00:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-176131>

Salmon alone won't cut it. Try shrimp(you can get them frozen), mussels, oysters, mackerel, roo, sardines, herring ... I cook, grill or bake salmon unless I know it is very fresh and wild caught (which is like never were I live). I don't know about the plankton. Is that like, really just plankton? I think the fish needs to eat it first so you can make optimal use of it. Wouldn't go for fish oil either. A lot of brands are not of good quality. Also it needs to come in the fish package to give you more benefit. Only take caspule if you have nothing else.

Also I would do leptin reset when summer is over. And during the reset I would stop working out all together. Just some light walking maybe. It can help you maybe with the cortisol issue. But others may know or understand better then me why to do this.

_**Plankton** are microscopic organisms that float freely with oceanic currents and in other bodies of water.**Plankton** is made up of tiny plants (called phytoplankton) and tiny animals (called zooplankton). The word **plankton** comes from the Greek word "planktos" which means "drifting.

### shah78 — 2015-09-02T03:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-176217>

You are doing extremely well for beginner. My only issue with you is you are still over training. I was in exactly the same position . Pick one of your workouts each week and divide it into three or four days. Yes, cut down by about 80%. I know, I know you already cut down your training. This program will give you effortless body composition results, but you have to fix your problem first. I'm almost sixty yo and have never had such a body in all my thirty years as a personal trainer. This is Star Trek stuff. But you have to board the Enterprise first! No?

This program will give you effortless body composition results, but you have to fix your problem first. I'm almost sixty yo and have never had such a body in all my thirty years as a personal trainer. This is Star Trek stuff. But you have to board the Enterprise first! No?

Hi.

Thanks.
Yeah I have cut it right back - only 2 weights and 1 or 2 30min HIIT sessions a week. With plenty of walking etc.

I also track my HRV every day. I use an app called Elite HRV (you can read more about this [HERE](http://afcoaching.com.au/blogs/blog/38926081-operation-abs-week-4-heart-rate-variability-hrv)), and if it spits out a orange or red I don't train. No matter what. I'll do some stretching and or go for a walk instead.
However upon seeing my DUTCH results, I figured I will have to cut it more. So I will apply your advice and drop it back to a max of 3 sessions per week, only if my HRV is solid and keep the sessions short and sharp.

I don't train for my body, I am competitive so I train for sport. My body is great, I'm actually in the best shape of my life (and thats saying something - I've had 6 pack abs since I was a teenager). I'm ~100kgs, 10% bodyfat etc. But I want my health to be perfect, so I'll make the changes. It's not hard cutting back, it was last year when I first received my labs back, so that mountain has been conquered. I honestly just thought 4 sessions a week would be ok

### Alex Fergus — 2015-09-29T09:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-178112>

Hi all.

So an update.

I have been doing the Leptin Reset protocol for about 2-3 weeks now.

Every morning I have lots of bone broth, some meat and vege while sitting in the sun for 30-40mins. I've cut my training RIGHT back, sleep is solid (only candles from 7pm, no technology at all - waking naturally at about 5-6am after 8-9 hours deep sleep). And 3 meals no snacks is going great.

I'm also eating a lot more seafood. Sardines most days. Shell fish 2-3x a week. Wild salmon 1-2x a week. Prawns 1-2x a week. Drinking bone broth daily.

And I'm going to the park 1-2x a day. I'm taking my laptop to the park and doing work there when it's not wet. I've actually got a nice tan coming on! Someone asked me today If I had been on holiday!!

So things are going well. In fact my diet/appetite has changed big time.

I'm a solid guy, I used to eat a lot, but now I feel like I don't need much at all.

Today I had bone broth, a steak and some spinach at 6am. I felt like I should eat something at 1pm, so I had some more BB and a handful of macadamia nuts. Usually I'd have a sardine salad, but I just wasn't hungry. At the moment its now 5pm and I'm still not hungry. I'll make dinner soon, but it's so weird. Usually I eat like mad (100kg at 10% bodyfat usually depends a lot of food!).

The only downside I have is this - I still have fresh blotches of tinea on my skin. And I still have a white coating on my tongue. I should mention I'm eating a low fodmap and low carb diet. I think I have some issues in my gut with candida. I've noticed great changes in many areas of my life since starting the Leptin RX, but this is one aspect that hasn't changed much so far

### Alex Fergus — 2016-09-09T09:27:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-201170>

Hi all.

So it's been a year and I thought I would give you all an update.
My whole life has changed - I eat a lot of seafood, I hardly train any more, I'm in bed close to sun down and wake up before sun rise, I avoid EMF/wifi etc, I ground every day and ensure I get a lot of UV-B sun - even in winter...
But, I'm still far from great.

In fact, I have a lot of issues...

Recent stool tests showed massive gut dysbiosis (95% Strep count - should be below 5%, 2% e.coli - should be 70% + and next to no beneficial bacteria). I had two 3 day tests done with different companies and both showed similar results.

I was parasite and yeast free however.
There were traces of undigested particles in my stool.
Also - b-Glyucuronidase was high (7509 U/g - range is 300-4000)
M2 Pyruvate Kinase was 6.3 (range below 4 U/ml)

Vit D is great
Ferritin levels are high (393 ng/ml - range is 22-250)
HS CRP is still 2.0 (has been this level for 2-3 years despite all my changes)
Testosterone level is rock bottom along with estrogen (DUTCH test)
Testosterone - 24 ng/mg (ref range 40-100)
Estrogen - 7.8
DHEA 3673 (ref range 1000 - 5500)
Free cortisol was high - metabolised cortisol low.
Prolactin is low,
Free Testosterone (blood) is low)
Luteinizing Hormone is 2.5 mlU/mL (low - range is 1.2-8.8)
Histamine (blood) .0.7 umol/L (range .2 - 2.0)
Free T4 - low
Free t3 - low
Rev T3 - low

No thryoid antibodies
Zonulin good/low
O6:03 ratio = 2.5 to 1
Trans Fat index - great

Neurotransmitters - all low.
Serotonin - 72.6 ug/gCR (ref range 200-400)
GABA - 157 (range 600-1100)
Dopamine - 79 (ref 250 - 400)
Noradrenaline - 18 (30-50)
Adrenaline 2.3 (10-15)
Glutamate 2.7 (5-10)

I had a MRI brain scan - that came back clear.

And I just got my results from a 24 hour post DMSA chelation heavy metal urine sample.
Arsenic was high - 25.6 (should be below 15 ug/gCR)
Mercury was also showing a 'low toxic burden' - 1.13 (should be below 1, though a high toxic load is 2.8)
Copper was low in blood test and urine test.
Zinc was low in blood test, high in urine test

I am an under methylator .

I don't do any intense training any more, I am outside as much as possible. I am also moving to rural NZ in 2 months time (currently in dense sydney apartments). So I'm hoping this helps.
I have a EMF meter - my work/living area is in the 'red/severe' zone for nnEMF. my bedroom is green though.

I am currently doing IF for 14-16 hours, and then a paleo autoimmune keto diet. Trying keto for the gut dysbiosis. I only eat grass fed/organic etc. I drin spring water. I eat some seafood every day, organ meats 2x a week, seaweed a few times a week, broth daily etc. I sleep great (I use the oura ring on airplane mode and get 85-95% sleep - i sleep around 8.5 hours a night). I walk around 10-12k steps a day (barefoot as much as possible). I get a ton of sun - it's spring time here in Sydney and all winter people were hitting me up about my tan!. I CT a few times a week. I track HRV and it's pretty good.

The problem - low T, the gut issues, low libido, feeling 'flat' al the time (nothing excites me), poor memory and I feel like I'm becoming 'dumb'. It's scary.

I'm a bit lost as to what to do next.

My functional doc has been on a gut protocol for the high streptococcus levels. Then he wants to look at my methylation. He hasn't seen my metal results yet. I'm worried that I have mercury poisoning as I had 4 big silver fillings for 12 years, then had them removed without knowing about the dangers... I had a rubber dam, but then the dentist had to do more work after the dam was out, I remember tasting metal for days... That was 3 years ago. I didn't do any chelation therapy.

Whenever I take chlorella I am violently ill.

If I was Princess Leia on Star Wars, it would be at this time where I would say 'Help me Obi-Wan Kenobi... you're my only hope".

Any thoughts on what I should/shouldn't do?

I'm counting down the weeks (8) until the NZ move - it's not great from a latitude point of view, but the remoteness/back to nature element will be good (I'll be living on the coast). And I'll buy a IR sauna with some UV lamps for winter time.

Cheers

5/18/2016
EXTENSIVE NEUROTRANSMITTER PROFILE

Consider first to get clean test

and

and
Thyroid hormone test

Spectracell Micronutrient analysis and change to resulting supplemental protocol should be repeated not sooner than once every 6 months.

\==================================================
5/18/2016
COMPLETE DIGESTIVE STOOL ANALYSIS - Level 4+

Stool Semiformed

HIGH----->Short Chain Fatty Acids, Putrefactive - Putrefactive SCFAs are
produced when anaerobic bacteria ferment undigested protein,
indicating protein maldigestion.

Meat Fibres - The presence of meat fibres may indicate
maldigestion from gastric hypoacidity or diminished pancreatic
output.

Vegetable Fibres - The presence of vegetable fibres may indicate
maldigestion from gastric hypoacidity or diminished pancreatic
output.

Putrefactive SCFAs are ELEVATED:
Suspect hypochlorhydria, exocrine pancreatic insufficiency, or protein malabsorption.
Other causes include bacterial overgrowth of the small bowel, gastrointestinal disease, and/or rapid transit time.

Normal -->PANCREATIC ELASTASE: Normal exocrine pancreatic function.
Pancreatic Elastase reflects trypsin, chymotrypsin, amylase and lipase activity.
This test is not affected by supplements of pancreatic enzymes.

HIGH\---->b-Glucuronidase - Increased levels of b-Glucuronidase may
reverse the effects of Pase II detoxification processes.

**M2 Pyruvate Kinase is HIGH**
Comment - The majority of human tumours strongly over-express
the tumour M2 isoform of the glycolytic enzyme Pyruvate Kinase
(M2-PK), which is released from tumour cells and is quantitatively
detectable in body fluids. M2-PK is the key regulator of tumour
metabolism and its measurement in faeces identifies gastrointestinal
\>4 U/ml tumours, even in the absence of gastrointestinal bleeding.

M2-PYRUVATE KINASE: POSITIVE
M2-PK values greater than 4 U/mL may indicate gastrointestinal adenoma, colorectal cancer or other
gastrointestinal carcinomas.
M2-PK has a lower sensitivity and specificity in diagnosing pancreatic cancer compared to Ca 19-9.
However, in patients with adenocarcinoma there is a simultaneous increase of M2-PK and Ca 19-9. In
addition, M2-PK is more commonly elevated in metastatic disease and may be an additional criterium
to decide on radical surgery of pancreatic cancer.
Tumor M2-PK has a higher sensitivity than markers CEA and CA72-4, and is a valuable tumor marker
for the detection of gastrointestinal cancer.
**Raised levels can also occur in acute and chronic inflammatory bowel disease and other digestive tract**
**diseases, so these conditions need to be excluded firstly.**

\=====================

### JanSz — 2016-09-12T17:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-201350>

**If you have a Lyme, it is likely 2+ years old.**

[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/lyme-disease/basics/treatment/con-20019701](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/lyme-disease/basics/treatment/con-20019701)
**Lyme,**
**Tests and diagnosis**
[By Mayo Clinic Staff](http://www.mayoclinic.org/about-this-site/welcome)

The variable signs and symptoms of Lyme disease are nonspecific and often are found in other conditions, so diagnosis can be difficult. What's more, the ticks that transmit Lyme disease also can spread other diseases at the same time.

If you don't have the characteristic Lyme disease rash, your doctor might ask about your medical history, including whether you've been outdoors in the summer where Lyme disease is common, and do a physical exam.

Lab tests to identify antibodies to the bacteria can help confirm the diagnosis. These tests are most reliable a few weeks after an infection, after your body has had time to develop antibodies. They include:

-   **Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) test.** The test used most often to detect Lyme disease, ELISA detects antibodies to B. burgdorferi. But because it can sometimes provide false-positive results, it's not used as the sole basis for diagnosis. This test might not be positive during the early stage of Lyme disease, but the rash is distinctive enough to make the diagnosis without further testing in people who live in areas infested with ticks that transmit Lyme disease.
-   **Western blot test.** If the ELISA test is positive, this test is usually done to confirm the diagnosis. In this two-step approach, the Western blot detects antibodies to several proteins of B. burgdorferi.

///////////////////

**Treatments and drugs**
[By Mayo Clinic Staff](http://www.mayoclinic.org/about-this-site/welcome)

Antibiotics are used to treat Lyme disease. In general, recovery will be quicker and more complete the sooner treatment begins.

**Antibiotics**

-   **Oral antibiotics.** These are the standard treatment for early-stage Lyme disease. These usually include doxycycline for adults and children older than 8, or amoxicillin or cefuroxime for adults, younger children, and pregnant or breast-feeding women. A 14- to 21-day course of antibiotics is usually recommended, but some studies suggest that courses lasting 10 to 14 days are equally effective.
-   **Intravenous antibiotics.** If the disease involves the central nervous system, your doctor might recommend treatment with an intravenous antibiotic for 14 to 28 days. This is effective in eliminating infection, although it may take you some time to recover from your symptoms. Intravenous antibiotics can cause various side effects, including a lower white blood cell count, mild to severe diarrhea, or colonization or infection with other antibiotic-resistant organisms unrelated to Lyme.

After treatment, a small number of people still have some symptoms, such as muscle aches and fatigue. The cause of these continuing symptoms, known as post-treatment Lyme disease syndrome, is unknown, and treating with more antibiotics doesn't help. Some experts believe that certain people who get Lyme disease are predisposed to develop an autoimmune response that contributes to their symptoms. More research is needed.

### JanSz — 2016-09-13T21:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-be-better-than-the-rest.16795/post-201399>

We newer have enough of hands on advice. Here is one.

**Neurolipid-Membrane-Medicine-Conference-OCT-2016**

[http://www.neurolipid.org/conferenc...pid-Membrane-Medicine-Conference-OCT-2016.pdf](http://www.neurolipid.org/conference-emails/october2016/Neurolipid-Membrane-Medicine-Conference-OCT-2016.pdf)
//////////

//
**Abstract**
Maintaining the appropriate balance and content of lipids in cellular membranes is critical for normal neural processes.
Accumulating evidence suggests that even subtle perturbations in the lipid content of neurons and myelin can disrupt their function. The membrane and organelles within the cell are the primary focus of electrical discharge within the central nervous system and can be stabilized with phospholipid therapy and a Phospholipase A2 (PLA2) suppressive diet which is a targeted phospholipid, nutrient-dense, membrane stabilizing diet. The brain is 60% lipid, containing phospholipids, which comprise the plasma and organelle membranes, along with cardiolipin, locatedexclusively in the inner lipid membrane of mitochondria and myelin, and is a target for toxic exposure. Stabilization of cardiolipin is a primary therapeutic target in neurotoxic disorders ‘brain on fire’ which may be addressed using lipid therapy.
**Recent research has revealed that in the brain myelin acts as one enormous mitochondrion.**
Examination of red cell lipids at **Johns Hopkins Peroxisomal Diseases Laboratory** in subjects with **encephalopathy, psychiatricsyndromes, seizure disorders, super-refractory status epilepticus, rare diseases, Autism, Alzheimer’s, Post Stroke, neurolyme, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, neurofungal toxicity, Multiple Sclerosis, Motor Neuron and Parkinson’s Disease** over the past 15 years in 9000 analyses has revealed a characteristic accumulation of very long chain fatty acids (VLCFAs), which comprise lipid rafts, or ceramides, depicted as cell membrane derangement per disturbance in peroxisomal respiration, which has a vital role in cell membrane integrity and function. Membrane phospholipid abnormalities with elevation of VLCFAs may be indicative of exposure to neurotoxins resulting in suppressed peroxisomal beta oxidation of VLCFAs. Patients with psychiatric syndromes present with a very low total lipid content
and often overmyelination indicative of mitochondrial lipid disturbance and an increase in sphingomyelin with a marked decrease in phosphatidylcholine in the outer membrane leaflet of their cell membranes. Identification of nuclear and mitochondrial DNA adducts (toxins) has revealed a link between toxic exposure and the development of cell membrane derangement / dysfunction linking epigenetics to these neurometabolic syndromes. In our current studies we have captured visual images of distorted phospholipid membranes and have linked the impact
of the DNA adducts (toxins) altering gene expression to aberrations in lipid metabolism, cellular dysfunction and alteration of the structure of phospholipids in the cell membrane characteristic to the presenting diagnosis and symptoms. Our treatment protocol includes an oral targeted lipid therapy with and a membrane stabilizing diet with supplemental protocol (phospholipids and SR3 oil), and in some cases an intravenous infusion of phenylbutyrate, phosphatidylcholine/Essentiale, leucovorin, and glutathione to clear bioaccumulation of toxins impacting gene

**Methods**
Applying the Advanced Membrane Stabilizing protocol adult and pediatric patients are given weekly
one to two multigram bolus infusions of phosphatidylcholine,
followed by Growth Factors,
Leucovorin (folinic acid)
and rGSH Fast Push.
In addition, Sodium Phenylbutyrate as 3 to 5 grams is administered in an IV drip twice weekly.

Oral therapy includes unsaturated lower order fatty acids as
SR3 4:1 omega-6 to omega-3 oil,
Evening Primrose oil,
EPA,
intact phospholipids,
Fatty Alcohols,
Ca/Mg Butyrate or Na Phenylbutyrate,
Phosphatidylcholine (PC),
co-enzyme and methylation support-folinic acid,
5-MTHF,
tetrahydrobiopterin,
riboflavin,
NADH and methylcobalamin (by injection).

Targeted treatment protocols are utilized after red cell lipid analysis has been completed.

1) Phenylbutyrate per oral and IV to stimulate the peroxisomal beta oxidation and stimulate resolvins
2) Bolus intravenous Phosphatidylcholine as Lipostabil or Essentiale N
3) Methylation factors -folinic acid, riboflavin, methylcobalamin, 5-MTHF, tetrahydrobiopterin
4) Sulfation support - IV Glutathione and oral free form amino acids

WHO WE ARE
Microbiology DX is a CLIA certified and Massachusetts licensed microbiology laboratory founded to provide testing for patients and healthcare providers who are concerned about fungal and bacterial exposure. We specialize in MARCoNS testing per Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker’s protocol.
WHAT WE DO
We identify bacteria, fungi including mold and yeast in diagnostic, environmental and industrial samples. We perform susceptibility testing on various bacterial organisms to assist clinicians with treatment regimens.
WORKING WITH US
We work with clinicians including MD, DO, NDNP, PA, chiropractors and other healthcare professionals nationwide and internationally to help identify biological hazards and exposure.

You need to remind people that science is the process that takes us from confusion to understanding. In this sense science is the communication path man should be using to study how nature shapes us. We have to remind ourselves that the path is fallible because of the process of nature and the nature of man. Man always creates errors and often he fails to understand why his methodologies can create many scientific fallacies believed to be fact.
Much of what man has learned and come to believe is not true in nature, it just happens to bring us closer to the truths that nature weaves. This occurs because Mother Nature's hand is capable of erasing while she simultaneously write's more wisdom that builds things around us. You begin to realize in every experiment and theory there's no such thing as a complete lie or complete truth. There's always some truth in there and that is what science is trying to squeeze out from the pulp. Man's duty is to distill the juice of nature with experiment, but how something is studied effects the results and this can create new knowledge or dogmatic fallacy. Realizing what was once believed to be true, but no longer is true, is what creates man's wisdom.

But pretty much I'm a 28 year old male. Into health and fitness (I PT and coach people), competitive athlete, seeking optimal health and performance but I haven't got there yet (current lab tests show high cortisol, low T, low Estrogen, and a CRP of 2.0).

I want to learn as much as possible. I am just finishing Ben Greenfields Superhuman Coach course, and I think Dr Kruse will be the next step in my journey for health.

Oh, I was born in New Zealand, but have been in sydney for 5 years.

Finally, I'm pretty new in regards to Dr Kruses's methods (I have only heard a few of his podcasts on Ben's show), so excuse my ignorance. I'm reading as much as possible - but I must say, between the books, the blog and the forum there is a lot to read!

# Thread: Hot Tub Specs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-08-31T15:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176108>

We are looking to purchase a hot tub for me to use over the winter. DH is still sending out resume after resume to get us to FL, but it's just not happening . . . yet  We're still trying to be positive and keep our hopes up, but we cannot move without a job and health insurance when we have kids to support.

So in the meantime I'm trying to do the best I can to improve my health. I'm thinking a hot tub in the winter will be beneficial, but I do wonder about the EMF associated with the tub. What all should we be considering and looking for when purchasing one? I can't afford a large one. Right now we're just looking at a small 4-person unit on sale at Costco.

### Starfish Prime — 2015-09-02T15:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176259>

Are you planning on using it for CT? If you have any health issues, I don't think warm in winter will not be the best path to take. A poly stock tank can be had for $100ish, and works great.

Why do you think a hot tub would be beneficial?

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-03T15:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176323>

I was going for the effect that Jack has mentioned - I cannot for the life of me remember the name right now - but it's where half your body is cold and half is hot/warm. I thought that was supposed to be very beneficial. Ahh, why can't I remember the name?!?! Hopefully it will come to me and I'll re-post!

### fitness@home — 2015-09-03T15:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176324>

Fournier effect?

I will be using my CT Tub and infrared sauna during the next 5-6 months, alternating them. Have an ice vest and ice neck wrap and will incorporate then too.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-03T17:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176334>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=176324)
>
> Fournier effect?
>
> I will be using my CT Tub and infrared sauna during the next 5-6 months, alternating them. Have an ice vest and ice neck wrap and will incorporate then too.

YES! Thank you . . . that is what I was referring to. Ahh, so maybe I'm confused with how we actually are supposed to do this. I thought I could have my upper body freezing in the 20-degree winter temps and my lower body warmer in the hot tub at about 85 degrees or so. Or is it better to alternate hot/cold by taking a hot shower or using a sauna and then dipping into a cold tub?

### fitness@home — 2015-09-03T18:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176339>

Your way is correct.

Our spa heater is out, so I can't try it that way until we replace the heater. We don't want to heat the entire pool - - just the whirpool part.

I'm going to experiment with the options I have open to me currently.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-03T18:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176343>

Thanks for the clarification @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) ! I get so confused and sometimes I feel like I'm doing everything wrong. I'm glad to hear that maybe I am starting to understand something for once

### Da-mo — 2015-09-04T01:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176446>

-   Water itself is a good EMF attenuator

and

-   Think about what you will treat your hot tub water with . . . we went away from chlorine/bromine (for obvious reasons) and now use a product that is 595g/l hydrogen peroxide and 0.55g/l silver as silver nitrate.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-09-04T14:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176460>

Thanks for that info @[Da-mo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9712/) . I've been looking into water treatment options as I definitely don't want chlorine. I was reading about using hydrogen peroxide, but we have well water and most of what I read said you should only use that with municipal water. Can I ask where your water is sourced? And would you mind sharing the name of the product you use? Thanks again!

### Da-mo — 2015-09-04T22:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-tub-specs.16798/post-176494>

We have well/bore water too. Ours has a bit of iron in it so discolors at first as the iron oxidizes but that eventually gets taken out by the filter.

The peroxide acts as the oxidizer and the silver nitrate as the algacide. Residual peroxide levels may have some health benefits.

The product we use is Poppits [http://www.poolpoppits.com.au/about-poppits/chlorine-free-pool-spa-maintenance/](http://www.poolpoppits.com.au/about-poppits/chlorine-free-pool-spa-maintenance/)\- maybe you have something similar available locally.

The sky was changing color as the sun was rising behind Mt. Hood, the crescent moon was high in the sky, and Venus was shining brightly. It was gorgeous. I took a picture of it to share with you here but I'm having trouble uploading it. After I stood outside for a little while, I got back into bed and, almost immediately, found a creative solution to a problem that I've been pondering.

Although I can't say I felt a different between the sunrise and the sunshine later on in the day (which feels almost therapeutic to me) I'm totally going to keep experimenting and exploring

I just discovered the best new word **"rewilding."**

Definition:
/riˈ**wīld**/

verb

1.  to reverse the process of domestication.
2.  to return to a more wild or self-willed state.

**Rewilding** means restoring **ancestral ways** of living that create **greater health and well-being** for humans and the ecosystems that we belong to. Many things inspire people to rewild: ecological collapse, economic uncertainties, health problems, a sense of something missing from life, or a desire to “save the world.” Rewilding takes inspiration from the most modern interpretations of prehistory provided by anthropology, archaeology, and ethnobiology. It means returning to our senses, returning to ourselves, and **coming home to the world we belong to**

### PaulG — 2015-09-02T03:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ubiquitination-24-the-key-truth-bomb.16800/post-176216>

This morning as I stood absorbing the rays of the early morning sun, I looked up and noticed two galas perched on the outermost branches facing exactly the same direction looking at me as I looked at them..I think we shared a moment of natural connection mediated by photonic power.

Also found this 2014 paper which quite possibly has been mentioned before "[Differences in the reproductive hormone rhythm of tree sparrows (Passer montanus) from urban and rural sites in Beijing: The effect of anthropogenic light sources](http://www.svsu.edu/~gmlange/461LC8.pdf)"

**abstract** The pervasiveness of anthropogenic light in urban environments has increased the exposure to light of many animals. Since photoperiod is a regulator of the timing of reproduction in most temperate region birds, such light sources could potentially change the timing of reproduction. We compared the luteinizing hormone (LH), testosterone (T), and estradiol (E2) levels of tree sparrow (Passer montanus) populations sampled at two urban and two rural sites in China, and also performed a controlled photoperiod experiment to determine the influence of artificial light on the endocrine rhythm of these populations. LH levels of urban tree sparrows increased earlier than those of rural ones, but rural populations had higher LH peaks. A linear mixed model (LMM) indicates that increased exposure to light at night (LAN) significantly influenced the LH, T and E2 concentrations of free-living tree sparrows in urban environments compared to their rural counterparts. The results of the controlled photoperiod experiment showed that tree sparrows that were exposed to 6 lux of light during the dark phase of the artificial photoperiod began to secrete LH earlier, and had lower peak LH levels, than control birds. A LMM indicates that LAN had a significant effect on LH levels in this experiment. Although urban tree sparrows began to secrete LH earlier than their rural counterparts, we found no corresponding advance in T or E2 secretion. **On the contrary, peak T and E2 levels of urban birds were lower than those of rural birds**. These results suggest that although anthropogenic light sources appear to advance the onset of LH secretion in urban tree sparrow populations, they also lower peak LH, and consequently levels of T and E2. A possible explanation for these observations is that greater exposure to anthropogenic light in urban environments stimulates LH secretion and may influence photosensitivity, but further experimental work is required to test this hypothesis.

>
> Hemoglobin’s peak light absorption peaks at 280 mm 420nm 540nm and 580nm and has sharp cutoff at 600 nm since green and yellow wavelengths of krypton ion lasers peak at 531 mm and 568 mm respectively they almost perfectly match the absorption peaks of hemoglobin so these laser colors are used for coagulation of blood and blood vessels. These peaks ideally match the spectral absorptions of the aorta where atherosclerosis predominates.
>
> So what has Seneff et al. missed? Absorption spectra of the skin, aortic wall and cornea show us some interesting connections when we look at them all plotted together. In the visible range of light (sunlight). The absorption spectra of the skin is 20-30 times that of the cornea. The absorption spectra of the aortic wall exhibits VERY SIMILAR peaks as hemoglobin. This data has been published for years. No one seems to link it. Parrish and Anderson from 1983, Keijzer et al. in 1989 and Eichler and Seiler in 1991 have all shown this..........Seneff wants to suggest it is all about eating more foods with sulfur but what good is that when your skin and eye are constantly getting overwhelmed with blue light frequencies..........look again how the absorption spectra is in the cornea with respect to the skin........Blue light destroys the signaling optically. **Food can give you sulfur but you need the proper light signal to make use of it!**
> Seneff believes and has argued that an underlying deficiency in Ch-S, is due principally to insufficient dietary sulfur. I do not buy this. I think the largest components is a lack of the appropriate IR and UV signals from the sun in the AM that is the most critical component. Many papers have shown that inadequate sun exposure to the skin leads, over longer timescales, to severe depletion of cholesterol and sulfate in many tissues. This loss is ADVANCED by blue light signaling. This is the world we live in today. This is why I see heart attacks in young people and carotid artery occlusions in 20-and 30 year old commonly today. This sulfur deficiency in cholesterol, chondroitin, and heparin becomes life-threatening with age in tissues and the blood plasma to eventually lead to an atherosclerotic plaque and a lack of blood flow and the development of pseudohypoxia that cause a loss of triplet superoxide production. This fast forwards the disease further and the tissues ages faster and the patient dies sooner. It is a well-choreographed program for renewal of cholesterol that is sulfated. Seneff missed the point completely on superoxide. Singlet SO is a bad player but triplet superoxide is the ideal player cells need to run the sulfation program in our tissues to work with IR and UV light. The Russian data on the correlation of oxygen availability, UV light, and triplet state SO is crystal clear to those who go deeper than just food. Seneff in her own paper says the following, "Unfortunately, there is necessarily collateral damage from superoxide exposure". That alone tells me she does not understand light. She thinks it is a food story. Sorry..........it is not, in my opinion, for atherosclerosis a food story. Triplet state superoxide is required to oxidize the LDL and to catalyze the reaction that produces sulfate from homocysteine thiolactone.
>
> After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state. The last one, may also promote a reaction leading to the singlet ground state by either non radiative decay or by radiative triplet decay. The radiative decays are called fluorescence and phosphorescence, respectively. Life time for fluorescence are on the order of nanosecond time scales where phosphorescence may last up to milliseconds or even seconds. According to Foote (1968), two alternative reaction mechanisms exist for the decay of the excited triplet state which are called Type 1 or 2 reactions. They are characterized by either the generation of free radicals (Type 1) reactions, or the transfer of excitation energy to oxygen molecules directly in a Type 2 reaction.
>
> During Type 1 reactions the triplet state next interacts with a target molecule or protein, other than oxygen. Stop and think about mitochondria now at cytochrome 1 and what should happen seasonally for mitophagy. Type 1 reactions result in the release of free neutral or ionized radicals. We use these to signal. Further reaction with triplet oxygne may also lead to the formation of hydrogen dioxide or superoxide. Where have you heard that before?
> In Type 2 reactions, the triplet state of the photosensitizer directly interacts with molecular triplet oxygen (3O2) which is then transferred to an excited singlet state of (1O2). During this reaction the electronic spins are flipped of the electrons in oxygen.
>
> Both reactions are designed to occur simultaneously but when the environment around a mitochondria is altered by pseudohypoxia the Respiratory Quotient can be altered dramatically. The reason for this is because all electrons in ECT are designed to move to oxygen for reduction. Their flow is 100% dependent upon the electron density, size of the respiratory chain proteins, and the amount of oxygen present at the end of the respiratory chain. This makes pseudohypoxia within the mitochondria a very powerful environmental variable because it controls the pull of electrons in ECT. Pull in ECT is measured in voltage. So pseudohypoxia means that voltages in mitochodria are falling and that means the magnetic field developed in mitochondria have diminished.
>
> Excited singlet oxygen is very reactive and toxic. It leads to cellular oxidation, mitochondrial swelling and necrosis. Weishaupt in 1968 identified singlet oxygen as the TOXIC agent in photoactivation of tumor cells.
>
> Carotenoids promote the toxic singlet oxygen to triplet oxygen which is **harmless.**...............Does the skin and retina have carotenoids? Yep.
>
> Now.......did you know blue light increases singlet oxygen and decreases triplet? No bueno
>
>
> Did you know that diabetics, who all get atherosclerosis have no measurable superoxide?
>
> See why now SO and blue light and sulfur are all linked to atherosclerosis?
>
> With pseudohypoxia and blue light exposure you make singlet oxygen over triplet superoxide...........that is the reason why diabetes and most anterior eye diseases are blue light over exposures. This is why we see alterations in Vitamin A in the brain. We are using carotenoids up in tissues chronically exposed in blue light diseases.........and once it is used up there are severe downstream effects..............singlet oxygen predominates in a microwaved blue lit world and you never see triplet superoxide again until you get rid of this bad mitochondria.
