VO2 Max test completed...current score is 26.7, and I can't find the doc I'd located on the forum describing where to start exercising based on specific VO2 ranges! I have no idea which rabbit hole I stumbled down to get the info, and I guess I didn't save it as I thought I had..BUMMER.

\--fasting for the test was no bueno. I have to eat the BAB or I'm ravenous at all the wrong times.

\--Lunch: leftover rib eye, spoonful of coconut manna, small portion of smashed cauliflower..and I was hungry 4 hours later. So hungry, that I raided the cupboard as soon as I got home with the kids from school at 4 pm..

\--Dinner (4-5) an extended eating frenzy which seems to have subsisded: few bites of rib eye gnawed off the bone while bacon salmon finished warming up, followed my many pistachios, followed by bone broth, and then salmon, and then decaff coffee w/half & half. It felt like a binge because it was spurred by hunger and was not planned well. This is my fatigue eating for me. Oh! Also must add in the handful of gf chicken nuggets I ate off my daughter's plate.

\--Getting ready for tub CT. Fed the kids an early dinner and took a Bitter Melon capsule ..plan to get the water cold with ice from the freezer. The colder, the better I think based on what I've read from other CT threads.

\--Mind over matter. I'm so freaking tired all I want to do is sit on the couch until it's time to put the kids to bed. I'm going to do CT now. Maybe I'll sleep tonight!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-03T01:30:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178469>

Sleep: Ok. Had to take melatonin to fall asleep but was then awakened by wind and slamming doors in the house. Annoying. Weird cramping woke me towards the end of the night. I think something must be changing with my gut..never had that happen before and nothing I had for dinner explains it. Three activated charcoal took care of it in 10 minutes. I love that stuff.

BAB: Large ground chicken patty, 3 pastured eggs, coffee w/ccc. Took a capsure of bitter melon in anticipation of doing CT after dropping kids at school.

10 am: 30 min CT in bath. Got the water temp as low as 55-60 (surface thermometer kept changing its results) by adding ice. Added epsom salts and baking soda. Felt GREAT the entire time but could only fit to mid-torso in water. The tub is short and deep. I decided to leave the tub filled and then add more water tomorrow when I CT again to get it deeper. Hate to waste water, and I need to get my upper body in too! I definitely could have done more time but had a volunteer commitment. Skin surface temp was 65 when I got out and skin was rosy. The part that was submerged continued to have goose bumps and feel chilled for the next couple of hours, but I wasn't uncomfortable at all! As far as mood goes, I felt awake and happy. There was no fatigue at all this afternoon, despite a lackluster night's sleep.

Lunch: Chipotle salad

Dinner: mahi mahi patties w/mashed cauliflower, shrimp

Went to World Market today iso 99% dark chocolate that I'd seen referenced in a thread..no luck. Decided that I can add dark chocolate to breakfast but it is forbidden after dinner. I'm certain the caffeine affects my sleep.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-04T02:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178553>

Sleep: Not great. Was awakened by thunder and then later by child. I don't feel tired at all today, though. Very strange but very welcome!

Weight: 159.6 WOOHOO! Getting below 160 is a major milestone for me. I've been stuck there for two years.

BAB: Meatloaf wrapped in Bacon w/2 pastured eggs, coffee, ccc

NO LUNCH--not a problem except for watching the kids eat lunch made me really hungry, but the timing was all wrong (3 pm) so I talked myself into waiting. I do much better with grassfed ground beef for breakfast, not sure why, but it satiates me longest.

Great day--took the kids swimming, to the climbing wall at the gym, and to the playground (morning time, for the light!) They now come outside with me while I eat my breakfast.

Dinner: (4:45 pm) Salmon, shrimp, kale, smashed cauliflower, bacon grease as fat..Need to figure out where I can buy pastured bacon grease.. small decaff w/ccc. Was so full couldn't finish coffee or rest of dinner.

Planning a CT bath sometime this evening..if I can get the kids occupied. DH is still out of town making CT logistics on the weekend difficult.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-05T02:10:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178619>

Weight: 159.2 I'll take that .4 of a lb loss!

I don't want to fixate on weight BUT it is a priority. Also, I remember reading Mama Grok's journal and feeling so inspired as I watched the weight come off her slowly but surely.

Did CT at 6 pm last night. Lasted only 20 minutes. I couldn't get the water temp lower than 60, and I wore a compression shirt. Not sure why it was more difficult..I'm in the second half of the cycle so maybe that's it? I've read on other threads that it gets harder to deal with the cold after ovulation. It took me 4 hours to warm up! Had to take my sleep supp to fall asleep, though once I did fall asleep, I slept through the night.

BAB: bacon wrapped meatloaf with one egg and bubbie's sauerkraut

LUNCH/DINNER: I screwed up here. Was out with the kids until 2 pm, didn't pack a lunch for myself and didn't want to eat the crap at the fun park. Ended up eating macadamian nuts, kevita, European sharp cheddar, tuna melt.. I finished at 4:15 so I guess that was my last meal. Really need to plan better!

A note on the day's events: I dressed inappropriately for our destination in shorts and light pullover..we were in the foothills where the temp was 50 degrees, light wind, and mist. I WAS COLD. I shivered for 3 hours while the kids had fun on the rides. It took me an hour of sitting under a blanket when I got home to warm up.

WHAT GIVES?! I'm supposed to be becoming cold adapted, and I'm colder than ever! Generally speaking, I'm the one turning down the thermostat and not reaching for a blanket. Hope it's just the quick weather change from 80-50 degrees that's thrown me.

Going to do yard work and yoga in the am..will CT after lunch tomorrow and see what my recovery is like.

I've been researching the use of compression gear with CT but I can't find where JK talks about it aside from the rec to wear a compression shirt when spot ct-ing the torso.

I'm not planning additional CT today. That 3 hours of shivering and sniffling was enough for me!

EDIT: Dinner#2 happened at 6 pm as I was making hamburgers for the kids. I had a cheeseburger AND a generous helping of peanut butter. For some reason, I had an intense craving for bread and carbs in general, but I abstained...only had a few handfuls of potato chips cooked in coconut oil.

So, not optimal spacing today as far as the reset goes. Hoping it doesn't mess things up too much. At least I stopped before sunset!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-05T03:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178625>

Caroline, you read my mind (must be all that DHA)! I just went back and re-read the CT Protocol blog as well as FAQ...I think that explains why wearing the compression shirt for my last CT session made it seem colder in a shorter amount of time. Also, I was submerged almost to my clavicle. The "girls" hadn't experienced much coldness before then, nor had my arms been in the water either.

The CT FAQ on compression gear says that it will speed cold adaptation, so I'll keep wearing it even if it does shorten the length of time I can be in the water.

Finally, the outside temp dropped from 70's to 50's in a matter of hours (very typical for Denver) and my bedroom windows were open. The ambient air temp inside the house got chilly fast, and I don't think that helped. The CT FAQ & Protocol talk about starting indoors to control the air temp before heading outside. I understand why after yesterday.

Going to go dig out my hat for the next session. I had submerged my feet, and I will continue to do that because my right foot is still uncomfortable many days despite Neuroma surgery in May. I feel like it has not healed as quickly as expected, and I'm hoping CT can accelerate that process.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-05T03:42:54+0300

### caroline — 2015-10-05T06:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178631>

What I have is like a tank top made of lycra I suppose..... and I wear it a lot! way more comfortable than a bra! Mine are made by reebok and are meant to be exercise gear - and I bought them to fit tight ....

I am short waisted and always carry too much weight in my midsection so I love to wear these lycra tank tops .....no bra lines either!

Some folks also wear wool sox and wool gloves when they C/T.

I am lucky enuf to live near the ocean .... so that and cold showers are my C/T!

I now wear speedo swimsuits because they fit very snug and hold you in and are pretty inexpensive and look okay too!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-05T21:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178664>

Sounds like a modern girdle. I'll check them out for winter...I imagine I would be terribly hot wearing them any other season though.  I'm looking forward to getting back to CA where the ocean was a few blocks away and always cold. I can't believe I lived there for 3 years and missed the opportunity to CT in the real deal! In the meantime, I'll make due with ice in the tub and epsom salts!

It hadn't occurred to me to wear gloves, socks & hat but then after I re-read the CT protocol and FAQs, I understood why that might be helpful to maintaining core temp in the cold bath. I'll dig up my husband's surfing cap..

Today I will CT after lunch. I've been doing yard work this morning, trying to get the lawn ready for winter AND get my AM light/cold exposure. The day started at about 50 degrees, and I drove in my car with windows down and tank top on to run errands and work outside. Everyone else here is so bundled up--no doubt they think I'm crazy!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-07T04:27:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178776>

Ugh, not so awesome this evening as it turns out. My period is due any day, and I just fell into a cup of mint chocolate chip ice cream (hey, at least it was gluten free AND organic. Ugh) Plus more peanut butter. WHAT IS WITH THE PEANUT BUTTER ALREADY?? I think I need more fat, but I just end up feeling nauseated by BP coffee attempts or mouthfuls of coconut oil. What happened to my pledge to make palatable fat bombs? I need to do that.

I have to give myself points though.. I stopped at the cup of ice cream and tablespoonful of pb..in the past I would have binged on gluten and whatever else wasn't nailed down, all the while telling myself that I would start over tomorrow. So there, pat on the back for confining the bingish sugar craving to a reasonable portion!

Weight: 162.5...Up almost 3 lbs. Why did I weigh myself the week I'm supposed to get my period?!

BAB: Leftover bacon meatloaf with 2 fried eggs, coffee w/ccc

Did 60 min. hot yoga class..I'm a little sore and it was tough. I lack flexibility AND strength! Then came home and mowed the lawn, which was hard work because there were wet clods everywhere from the aerating and the grass was still wet.

Lunch: very rushed--salmon/tuna salad with bone broth. I probably needed to eat more but was talking a family member through a crisis (told him he needed to drop the vegetarianism and at least eat fish for God's sakes), gobbling down my food, and running out the door to help in my kid's class.

Dinner: Too late--6:30 pm because of soccer practices (in the rain and 50 degrees, not fun for me, the spectator) rotisserie chicken, kale, and a couple of raw veggies dipped in ranch. Not enough, too late, not satisfying. And then, the ice cream..I'll be happy when kids soccer ends. I hate the late dinner.

I got caught twice in rain storms this afternoon, in shorts and a t-shirt..the temp dropped 20 degrees in an hour. My cold adaptation has not caught up to the mercurial climate we have here. I was pretty chilly and unhappy about it. No CT today. I can't mentally do it. Maybe tomorrow...

I find coconut oil isn't great for me except during the summer. I do add a little for flavour sometimes, but my fats change with the seasons.

Right now I'm having a love affair with bacon fat!  I will eat it by the spoonful. And soon I will be looking to find some duck fat. Have you tried duck fat? So luscious! Yum!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-07T04:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178784>

I've been wondering about the coconut oil. I know it's pushed on the Leptin RX because of it's ketogenic properties, but now that we're into fall..it seems that I should be focused on animal fats not tropical fats. I've been meaning to google where I can buy duck fat. I really want to try it! Also, I'd love to find pastured bacon grease. Maybe I should start eating unsalted Kerrygold by the spoonful after each meal. Getting enough protein is really easy for me, and I cook with liberal amounts of fat, but I suspect, based on my cravings, that I'm still not eating enough. Hence this peanut butter love affair I've got going on in my weaker moments! That, and I've been feeling so much better these last couple weeks that my activity level has risen and my hunger seems to have gone up with the addition of CT.

Caroline, yes! Thanks for the reminder that I can bring jerky and hardboiled eggs. I need to plan my food better. I do well for a few days, and then tend to run out of steam or inspiration. I can't wait to get down to two meals a day as I'm tired of coming up with three ultra low carb meals daily. I feel like I'm at the grocery store every stinking day. I DID get to Costco today and stocked up on wild caught tuna, barramundi, and salmon. Wish they sold more wild caught fish. All their shrimp is farmed as is most of their other frozen fish. Now...need to find more fish recipes for variety!

### caroline — 2015-10-07T12:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178798>

Try the salmon patty recipe I posted in the recipe section - yummy and inexpensive. They are great cold and as a base for poached eggs and hollandaise. I have an easy recipe if you want.
don't worry so much! You can make little quiches in muffin cups that are great cold too. Make a big batch and freeze.

Do you use a crock pot? cook a leg of pork overnight and it is ready for breakfast! think outside the box ....dinner for breakfast!

Do the best you can - it isn't always possible to get wild caught fish.

Have you tried raw meat? That is my favourite ...it is much easier to digest and always in the fridge waiting for me! I have surf and turf = raw beef and shrimp!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-08T03:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178849>

So, last night's sleep was very bad. Shocker. I'm sure the late dinner followed by ice cream didn't to me any favors. I finished eating at 7 pm last night--right on the mark with sunset but too late for me. I'm seeing a pattern emerge of later meals followed by bad sleep. Tonight, I finished dinner by 6 pm. Sunset is 6:33..

Weight? Not weighing myself for a while. In fact, I think I'll put the scale away.

BAB: Rest of the bacon wrapped meatloaf, coffee w/half and half, 3 eggs

Lunch: Protein Shake--not great choice but all I had time for

Dinner: 5:40-6 pm Chili w/sour cream and shredded cheddar, Lemon-Cayenne Kevita (1/2) SPICY!

I took an Ashtanga Yoga class today, which was a challenge..but a good challenge. My balance and strength need work! Not to mention the flexibility angle..Followed it up with CT bath. Water temp 60 degrees...I made it 20 minutes with feet in and torso curled so girls were submerged too. My skin temp showed 65 degrees upon exit. It was hard but needed. I did it before lunch on an empty-ish stomach, which I know is not recommended, but it wouldn't have happened otherwise today. The first 10 minutes was fine, but after that it was a case of mind over matter to stay in! I was motivated to do CT to avoid excessive soreness from the yoga, and I think it worked.

Mulling over the change in sweating as a barometer of leptin sensitivity. Whereas I used to sweat a lot before with exercise, I wouldn't say I'm sweating excessively now. It's more of a light glow of sweat than dripping off of me. Even in the hot yoga class yesterday, where the room was 95, I didn't sweat profusely like I did in Bikram (which is only 10 degrees hotter). 2 years ago.

Unfortunately, I just don't think I can honestly call myself leptin sensitive until the sleep has improved consistently and reliably. Aargh. So frustrating.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-08T04:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178853>

Thisbirdhaswings..thank you for your thoughts on the exercise. I thought the yoga might be good for my cortisol levels and therefore sleep, which is why I started it. I haven't noticed that it has affected it one way or another. I purposely started it at the same time as CT to help with the adrenal fatigue... Mindfulness is not my strong suit, so I thought it might be helpful as a result...I'm staying away from any kind of cardio (despite pressure from my husband and friends). I know that it will deplete me at this point. As would lifting heavy things. My husband has been out of town for a few months. When he comes back, I may be able to integrate some kettlebells at night in the low light conditions recommended by JK, but, to be honest I'm not sure I'm ready for that yet.

The strange thing about the sleep issue is that I'm actually not that tired during the day anymore. In fact, my energy levels have picked up in the last couple of weeks DESPITE my sleep remaining a sore spot. Once I drag myself out of bed, get the morning light, eat my breakfast..I feel ok. Just being able to move through the day with some measure of enthusiasm is a win. I'm so frustrated because I know how important sleep is, and I've been working on it diligently and am just not seeing the gains I need (and desperately want). Tonight, I admit to being very tired. And I have a migraine developing over my right eye. My period will start tomorrow or the next, so this is not unexpected.. I need to get my recent labs up in the appropriate thread for feedback, but the hormones are looking good on paper..but they may need to be even higher because I live at such a high altitude. So many moving parts!! One thing I haven't done is listen to the Sleep Webinar, and I mean to schedule that for next week.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-08T21:57:17+0300

Just posted my labs in the Optimal Labs forum, though I'm thinking that I should have put it in the 101 Hormones forum...

Sleep: I SLEPT! Hallelujah. I'm thinking it's because my dinner was finished by 6 pm...But I also did a CT session...Great energy levels today, but still didn't want to get out of bed this morning.

BAB: Steak, 3 eggs, coffee w/cream

Lunch: Tuna melt, Half caff coffee w/cream

Dinner: Crockpot german sausage w/pickled red cabbage

Will try to fit in CT after dinner, but husband gets home from long trip tonight and not sure how the schedule will work out. He's actually interested in the CT protocol because he read about the effects of cold exposure in an Atlantic Article a few months ago. Of course, my knowledge is not enough! At least that won't seem so nuts to him. Plus, he'll do just about anything if he thinks it will get me into a size 8 again.

Back to the laundry.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-09T22:48:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178948>

No CT last night...Too hectic. Maybe tonight?

Sleep was fine until I was awakened at 4 am by DD and then at 6 am by DH..with little sleep in between. I'm having a hard time shaking the fatigue today but couldn't fall asleep for a nap. BAB was too late this am. I woke up late, had to get lunches made, kids dressed and one of to school an hour earlier, then I came back and made my breakfast and DD breakfast. Sigh. Every day can't be perfect, especially if the sleep sucks. Just got to keep going and believe it will sort its self out eventually.

BAB: 1/4 cheesy cauliflower chicken bake, coffee w/cream, leftover tuna salad

Lunch: Date with DH @ local Indian restaurant. Had white rice with butter chicken, tablespoon of dal, chicken vindaloo..no Naan, which I love, but has gluten and therefore doesn't love me back. This meal probably has the highest carb count of any for the last 8 weeks! I figure that's ok..

Dinner: Broiled fish of some sort with added fat

Making homemade beef jerky with a second hand dehydrator my neighbor just gave me . Can't wait--it smells delicious!!

Although it's 80 and sunny today, I feel like I need to move to making my LRx meals more ketogenic, outside of the breakfast, as we head towards November. I'm still eating about 30 carbs a day, but the best numbers I've had were .4 for blood ketones (I have a meter). That's not significant ketosis, likely because I am still eating a high volume of protein at every meal to keep the cravings at bay.

So far, no thoughts on my hormone posting from yesterday..that's ok, I can usually figure out solutions on my own and get my answers as I read and reread the threads and blogs here. Just takes some time to get through everything.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-10T02:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-178969>

Ack! What is happening to me?! I just ate several homemade rice krispie treats! I went in to give my daughter some and then I just started sampling...and it went downhill from there. Ok. Looking over possible triggers....1) Breakfast was too late and 2) my dairy intake has increased considerably the last few days. 3) Not enough water.

Action plan: Pay attention to water! Cut out dairy (after I finish this breakfast casserole I made) again. Bet I see the scale start moving when I do that!

Ok. Eating broiled barrimundi frosted with kerrygold butter. Let's stop this binge in its tracks!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-11T01:06:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179021>

Started the morning with BAB & 35 minutes CT bath at 56 degrees. Was able to get my surface temp down to 58 in most places. Only left the bath because family was home from playground and it was time to get to soccer games.

2.5 hours grounding in midday sun--HOT today for mid-October, almost 90 degrees. I'm home and wiped out. Is it the heat or the sun or the grounding or all of it that makes me so tired on these days? The sun is still so intense here. I think I need a 1/2 tsp of salt. My adrenals don't like tons of midday sun and heat.

Having dinner with family friends tonight. I've stayed away from social stuff because of the drinking for the last 2 months on the Leptin Rx, but I'm planning to have a glass of red wine tonight. We're having steaks and veggies so the food offerings are good for me.

I'm really struggling with cravings this afternoon. Breakfast was big, timed correctly. Lunch was a 5 Guys burger wrapped in lettuce. Not enough fat for sure. I shouldn't be thinking about food right now.

Off to get salt and to hydrate. I don't drink salt water..put the salt in my mouth and swallow it down. It tastes good, and I'm guessing that means my salt levels are low!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-11T17:36:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179085>

Drank more than one glass of red wine last night--ha--and ate/drank waaay too close to bedtime! No surprise there. BUT, I didn't eat anything that I shouldn't have. It really helped that dinner was my friend's house and she has celiac..I know there will never be gluten in any of her meals! We had steak, mashed parsnips (wow are they delicious), roasted veggies, salad. I'm a little slow getting to that BAB this morning, but it's going to happen. As will some CT, probably after a pumpkin patch trip...in 85 degrees. That's not seasonable at all!

I took 1/2 tsp of salt and a bunch of activated charcoal before bed. Had to take some Advil around 3:30 am, but no hangover from all that red wine after two months of abstaining. Usually I would get a migraine within the first few sips of a Cab--this time, nothing. Very, very interesting.

A talk yesterday with my neighbor who has worse adrenal fatigue than I reminded me about taking salt throughout the day. My sodium levels in August were about 135, which is at the bottom of the range, and I do feel better when I eat about 1/2 tsp of salt in the am. I should remember to do that before bed too. She said doing so reduced her night time bathroom trips?! Gonna do a little test on that one.

Back to my regularly scheduled programming..BAB, CT, no drinking for a couple of weeks (though I do not regret last night's indulgence at all). Next time I'll bring a Malbec!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-12T03:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179163>

BAB: Happened within one hour of waking up--Cheesy chicken & cauliflower casserole, 3 eggs fried in bacon fat, coffee w/cream

Lunch: Handful macadamian nuts, 2 squares dark chocolate, homemade beef jerky

Dinner: Leftover crockpot german sausage w/pickled red cabbage, added extra bacon fat, spoonful coconut manna

CT 25 mins @58 degrees in tub post BAB. Surface temp 61. Got water high enough to cover to top of waist. Just can't get surface temp down without colder water. It will come as tap water gets colder.

Nauseated throughout afternoon. Took a family trip to pumpkin patch and festival..it was 87 degrees and sun is so HOT. Very tiring. Stomach felt off so took more activated charcoal. Several trips to the bathroom later..not sure what's going on. Wine from last night?

Took a total of 1 tsp of sea salt today. Must remember to take some at bedtime.

Just received an array of new blue blockers..very exciting.

I avoid Advil, but I have a history of migraines...once the headaches are entrenched, they are very difficult to get rid of them. The Leptin Rx has basically rid me of my chronic headaches, but if a bad one is threatening, I do take some. Tylenol doesn't cut it, and I just don't have the option of taking to my bed for the day when a migraine hits since I have two young children.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-13T06:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179290>

Inger...ha, ha. I'm laughing about the chicken. I knew someone would eventually comment! I actually have a pork shoulder cooking for the remainder of the week's BAB, but it's not pastured. I just can't find pastured pork here, or one, I guess, whose price doesn't blow my budget out of the water. Not ideal, but I don't live by myself--two young kids to take care of plus a husband who is extremely skeptical. I'm just trying to find ways to get a BAB down, usually after very little sleep, with two kids hanging off of me while also fixing their food, their lunches, getting their teeth brushed, homework done, blah, blah, blah. There's perfection, and then there is my reality. Every single morning. Maybe if I could get some quality sleep, consistently, I could manage something better.. As it is, I'm just giving myself points for keeping on with the Leptin Reset even though it's taking a heck of a lot longer than I thought it would, I'm pretty tired most mornings, and I'm not seeing the results I want to see in my body comp or my sleep. I'm not sure I have patience AND perfection in me at this point on the road to better health.

I did decide to keep this journal and follow through on the reset because it all makes sense to me from an intellectual standpoint, and I'm desperate for better sleep. Plus, I've found such incredible inspiration from other members' journals, that I hope someone will get the same support from reading about my successes as they come.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-13T06:09:08+0300

### NadoRo — 2015-10-13T06:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179294>

Ugh, sleep last night was bad. Fell asleep by 10, woke up 40 minutes later. Couldn't fall asleep until after 3 am or so. Up at 7 for the day. That wake up really sucked. It's just so damn frustrating not to sleep well. I've been reading through the journal "My Bumpy Road to Optimal" and I can totally relate to how discouraging it is to try so hard, and still fail on the sleep. I haven't reached the end, but fingers crossed that she finds sleep. If she did, then I will. This is why I pore over the journal section of this forum!

Went for a long walk with husband who had the day off. Felt fine about it except for the two blisters I have on my feet. Stupid running shoes. The weather was glorious: 75-80, not a cloud in the sky.

No CT. No time today.

BAB: Rib eye, coffee

Lunch: (restaurant) antipasti salad (at least it wasn't chicken?)

Dinner: Tandoori chicken plus cucumber, onion, cherry tomato salad. Hey! The chicken marinated in turmeric for 6 hours..My family doesn't love seafood. I was too tired today to be creative, but next time, I'll marinate some shrimp too and make tandoori shrimp. Yum!

I promise to eat seafood tomorrow. BAB will be pork shoulder, Lunch will be salmon. I'll make hubs lasagna and I'll have shrimp. I can do this.

CT on the menu for tomorrow...need to pick up some ice to bring water temp down to a more comfortable 55 degrees or so.

I had cut back on 7-Keto DHEA because my provider told me I was too high at 371, but I'm going to keep going at 100 mg a day to see if it helps sleep. If I test above 400 next time, then I'll cut back. I really think my hormones have to be at the tip top of the high end to counteract the negative effects of living at altitude. Please, please, let us move back to SoCal in 1.5 years! All that fresh seafood, sunshine, and free CT..it's even more a paradise for me now than I realized when I lived there before!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-13T06:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179295>

Inger's right..why the heck did I make a chicken casserole? I could have put some mild white fish in it and been just fine with it. I really have to focus on seafood, seafood, seafood. Maybe that will equal sleep, sleep, sleep!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-13T16:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179336>

Ugh. Nothing but broken sleep last night. I fell asleep fine, which is usually the case, around 10:30..Woke up very thirsty twice, and with hot flashes. WTH?! This is the second night I've woken up sweating and had to throw off covers. Plus my stomach was a mess at around 4:45 am.. Hubs gets up at 5:40 am for work, so that was the end of any hope I had for sleeping. I just lay there until sunrise, about 6:40 am, and now I'm up and reheating BAB.

I started DIM again a few days ago to help with any CT detox. Cause of the hot flashes? Estrogen dipping too low? Other factor, yesterday was Day 3 of my cycle. I've noticed sleep is now worst the weeks after ovulation and during my period. It used to only be bad the second half of the cycle. I think I'm down to about 8 possible nights of ok sleep a month. That. Is. So. Depressing.

I'm tired today. Can't face any yoga or walking. Just going to take the kids to school, deal with the housework as best I can, and CT. And volunteer back at the school in the afternoon. I'm committed to being at the school almost every weekday afternoon through Christmas Break, but when I get back, I'm cutting it back to twice a week until I get some decent sleep. Some other parent will just have to pick up my slack..or not. I've got to find those boundaries everyone else seems to have (except for a handful of us). That's the tired, snarky, frustrated me talking..pay no mind. I'll be in a better mood after the protein hits. I do because I can, but damn I'm tired today!

Really annoying. I've contacted customer service, but it's a confusing set up. Oh well..The cold bath awaits. Guess I'll grab my book and read that instead. Walgreens was out of ice as well. Why are my attempts to do the right thing being frustrated today?

BAB: Tin of sardines plus pork shoulder, coffee

CT+sunshine grounding. CT only 20 minutes..got cold really fast today. Water temp: 59 degrees. Coldest surface temp: 61 degrees. Stayed outside in sun on grass to warm up, app. 45 mins.

I'm not getting much housework done today! But I'm putting myself first for once.

Lunch: 5 raw Oysters (at $1.50 per oyster, don't think I can have these more than once a week!) w/Frank's Red Hot sauce, and marinated salmon cooked in butter. That was a very tasty lunch!

Dinner: Will be shrimp cooked in butter since I don't have any bacon fat right now and forgot to get duck fat at WF today.

I feel very energized for 1 pm in the afternoon. I think the CT, done mid-morning, boosts afternoon energy for me. That is GREATLY appreciated.

I'm so thirsty. Must go drink more water..

### NadoRo — 2015-10-14T21:29:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179483>

Sleep: not so great, had a hard time falling asleep. I was tired when I turned off the light, but then...finally took a chewable sleep supplement I have that has melatonin and 5-htp in it, and I fell asleep. Only to then be awakened TWICE by my husband. He gets up early at 5:40 am, so that's when I woke up more or less for the day. Here's where it gets good...

I'm lying in bed and think, what the hell, I'm just getting up now. It was still dark, but I got my coffee and BAB, and headed outside as soon as I could see the sky lightening. I was out there for my first sunrise in a LONG time. And it was awesome! I beat my kids up, got tons of the first daylight, all while listening to JK's interview for a wellness summit that aired last week.

And that interview pulled it all together for me. As Oprah likes to say, it was my "Ah Ha" moment.

So, here I am having a lunch of canned wild tuna and canned oysters. Canned isn't ideal, but I'll take what I can get out here in Denver..I think it's still the right food group and has DHA so I should be good. And it's got to be better than chicken.

Also, did a CT session mid morning--with 10 lbs of ice got the water to 55 degrees. Stayed in 25 minutes. Got in all the triceps all the way and most of the chest was submerged with my whole torso and knees to waist. I think that's the best I'm going to do in the bathtub. Then, I warmed up by drying myself off in a sun patch with the window up in the bedroom. I live in a neighborhood...there's no way I can walk around in my birthday suit outside!

Then, I went to a Pilates class, which was so-so. Think I'll stick to yoga.

Lunch: oysters, tuna..needed some added fat, but didn't know what to do! I was hungry and having some cravings later but was volunteering in the library with only water, so no way to indulge them. Yay!

Ugh, trying some new recipes for the family and made a paleo pork chop recipe with smashed parsnips for dinner. Neither were good..Pork chops I undercooked because I was rushed and had a 5 year old underfoot..Kids had a smoothie instead, hubs had cereal, I had 1/2 decaf coffee w/handful of macadamian nuts. It was too late to do anything else for myself.

After listening to the interview, I'm no longer worried about my sleep. It will come. Gotta fix the mitochondria, from the inside out. Really want to be up for the sunrise again tomorrow!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-15T18:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179577>

Warning: potential rant/pity party ahead. I am not doing well today at all.

Day 5 of cycle. Mood and fatigue levels should be better, instead I woke up several times last night, then got up at 6:15 am for the day. It was still dark, but watched the sunrise, shivering outside with my BAB--temp was about 48 degrees.

If I don't sleep well the first part of my cycle, I'm done. There's no hope of sleep after ovulation.

I had a slight headache when I woke up, but I think I've got it under control, having drunk about 32 oz of water this morning.

I was, am so scattered right now. And angry, I feel like I'm going to meltdown at any minute. Absolutely no emotional control. It took everything I had this morning to get the kids to school (almost late again because they are SO SLOW), smile at everyone, pretend patience that I don't have with my children..

Made plans to go to yoga and broke them. I can't face wasting an hour doing something I don't want to do today. It's bad enough that I've obligated myself to be at the school every afternoon but one until Christmas Break. I don't know how to stop feeling guilty about wanting my space and not wanting to give up the only free time I've had in almost EIGHT years.

FUCK FUCK FUCK. I'm still trying to be the A student for everyone else but me.

Last night was bad too. Total meltdown after dinner with the family. I hate those days. I really want some emotional control. I feel like I live on a roller coaster of energy and mood. The end of the day is still really hard for me. I get so tired, having "Done" all day, and yet there is dinner, then cleaning up, then cleaning up the house, then getting the kids to bed, showers, teeth, then it's 8:30 pm and now it's time for my husband to get undivided attention from me. I'm fresh out. I have nothing to give to anyone, but, of course, that's not an option. So, on to Option B--keep doing the same shit you've been doing and hope something good comes out of it. Like sleep--uninterrupted, deep, restorative sleep. Where the fuck are you??

My rational mind says this is CT Detox talking. Do I stop the CT or continue on with it? Any decent sleep I had before starting it is a distant memory. And, yet, so many folks on the forum have found success with it.

I'm still waiting for the Kruse techs to get back to me with a link to the Sleep Webinar. I'm really frustrated right now.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-15T20:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179604>

Ok, chilling out after my morning pity party. Got some laundry done, cleaned up the house a bit, did some cooking. Feeling less panicked about appearing lazy to my husband who doesn't know why I'm tired and 20 pounds overweight. If only I would just start running...he says.

My neighbor stopped by with some "Happy" DoTerra oils and a crockpot beef/broccoli dish she had made. I just finished it and a tin of sardines for lunch. Drinking mint tea and eating a piece of dark chocolate on my deck outside. I'm feeling better now. Was it the oils? I'll never know. She swears by them but I have a hard time understanding how that would work...

Pretty sure I have some CT detox going on. Bad CT detox reactions were mentioned in another journal as a side effect of getting "the girls" all the way in. I did that yesterday in a 55 degree bath. Mine are big--too big--and have been bothering me since I started CT. I'm not sure if it's because I stopped supplementing iodine at the same time, progesterone issues, too much esterogen, bad E/PG ratio...All of the above. Hormones are mystery!

One thing is for sure..I'm in a bad environment. I just checked the side of my house and lo and behold! I have a Smart (Dumb) Meter. THAT was not there when I moved in a year and a half ago. I need to do some research on what I can do to mitigate the EMF from it. So that means everyone around me has one too. I'm going to have to eat a shit ton of seafood to replenish my electrons. I'm already in the hole, clearly, or I wouldn't look and feel the way I do. At least I don't work in an office and can get natural light all day, for the most part!

Gonna troll the forum looking for feedback on an earthing sheet. I can't buy a Magnetico right now, they're just too damn pricey.

Recount for today:

159.8 lbs (I'm only weighing Day 5 or so of cycle..but it's nice to see a number below 160)

BAB: Pork shoulder refried in coconut oil, 2 pastured eggs

I'm wondering about the eggs..do they aggravate my KP?

Lunch: Tin of sardines (gross, I could see backbones. I gulped them down in small bits..I'd rather eat oysters any day of the week), beef & broccoli crockpot dish my neighbor brought over. I think it had flecks of rice in it..Not ideal, but it was a quick addition to lunch protein. Dark chocolate (90% Lindt), peppermint tea

Dinner: Shrimp, marinated wild salmon, cooked in coconut oil, w/turmeric, onion powder, sea salt. Little lemon juice squeezed on for good meansure. Yum. Very small bit of coconut manna. It's so hard to get out of the jar that I'm naturally limited in how much I eat, which is a good thing.

I'm wondering about cooking oils and seafood? I know I've read about preferred oils..but then also read a note by JK saying that DHA takes precedence when the body is processing the food, so maybe it doesn't matter that much. No CT today. 1 hour of grounding at soccer practice. Lots of natural light all day.

CT will happen tomorrow! I found 10 lb bags of ice at Walmart for $2.

$1/oyster happy hour tomorrow at WF. I see some raw oysters in my future!

### NadoRo — 2015-10-16T23:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-179687>

Wow! I've been journaling here for almost a month. It's a great tool for staying accountable, I must say.

WF Oyster HH yielded 6 for $6. I can eat raw oysters all day. I noticed they're farmed, though. But there was no wild option, so that's what I've got!

Missed sunrise this morning because I was sleeping for once. Woke up one time last night...That's the best I've had in weeks!

Didn't take my high powered, anti-Sibo probiotic last night..wondering if that's what made the difference. I'm going to finish this pack and not re-up. Better to have Kevita, sauerkraut, raw oysters and LIGHT, if I've read JK's blogs correctly. More $$ for oysters.. Slowly I'm whittling down my supplement intake.

CT 20 mins. mid-morning. All I could stand and all I had time for. Water temp: 60 degrees out of tap. Threw in some hydrogen peroxide because read about it's help with water quality, but need to research further. Also, put in epsom salts. Gotta get my mag level up.

Plan on doing CT 30 mins. after BAB on Sat & Sun, when we don't have to rush out the door. Curious to see if I can stay in longer if I do it closer to my highest protein/fat of the day.

And speaking of fat, found duck fat AND lard at WF. Don't think the lard is pastured, but it's a start.

BAB: outside, temp was high 40s..chilly Tuna and pork shoulder in coconut oil, coffee w/ccc. I was too cold to take off my socks for grounding..next time I should suck it up. Harder to get into the cold as the temps drop.

Lunch: outside, grounding, 6 raw oysters w/a few drops of lemon, shrimp cooked in lard w/turmeric, onion powder, sea salt, over some arugala. Bit of darkest Lindt chocolate bar, peppermint tea.

Dinner: Flank steak w/chimichurri sauce, sauerkraut, 1/2 avocado, Cocktail: Kevita Lime Mojito, some macadamian nuts b/c I was starving fixing dinner

I'm wondering about the LRx as we move to winter. A key action is to get outside upon waking for light exposure, to link with meal timing. If cold makes light less relevant, I wonder if this action becomes less crucial too? Doesn't matter...I'll keep doing it because I like the way I feel, and need the dopamine boost! Just thinking out loud..

As usual, did all my driving with windows down. That was shiver inducing this morning, with the temp in the 40's! People in this neighborhood must think I'm bat shit crazy. Also, I showed up at school drop off with running shorts, t'shirt, lightweight jacket and Chacos...Everyone else was bundled up in winter jackets and pants. Definitely covered feet too.. I love it.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-29T13:43:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-180694>

It's been almost a week since I dropped in her. Unfortunately, the stomach virus was an especially pernicious strain of the Norovirus and it just hung on. My whole family ended up sick except for me.. I credit excessive activated charcoal, obsessive hand washing, and ACV for seeing me through. I think this round has finally broken my dryer, which was on its last legs anyway. I don't think it's any accident that there is a respiratory virus and a stomach virus making its way this time of year here. Even my kids' D levels and lots of outdoor time couldn't keep them from getting sick, not when they can't keep their fingers out of their mouths and noses!

So, last week was a total wash for me and my protocols. This week? The kids are on fall break. I am looking forward to Monday when they go back to school, and I can get back to my routine and some quiet space. And thank God Halloween is Saturday. I've been digging into the Reese's PB cups like it's my job. I'm sure that's perpetuating the horrible carb and sugar cravings I've been having.

When I could sleep (no one vomiting or waking me with hacking coughs), I actually have slept the past week and a half. That felt like a win, but now I'm on Day 20 and awoke for the day at 4:30 am. I haven't been consistent with BAB timing this week and haven't had enough seafood..Are my gains over the last few months this fragile?? They seem so hard fought. There has been no CT. I've been too tired and overwhelmed by taking care of sick kids to even contemplate how to do it with everyone around. Also, the temp here has dropped 30 degrees, is now freezing at night, and without the warmth of the sun and light exposure, I'm having a tough time getting in that bath. That it is the second half of my cycle doesn't help.

Two days ago, I had slept 9 hours (!!!) straight and felt like I lost 5 lbs over night. I was able to comfortably put on jeans I hadn't worn well in a year...I felt great. Fast forward one day, and there must have been a hormonal shift at Day 19, those same jeans are uncomfortable again and I'm up at 4:30 am with a very black, irritable mood. I just can't pinpoint with testing where the bad drop happens every month, but I think it happens around Day 19..that's when the cravings slam me, the mood becomes very bad, sleep (if there has been any) deteriorates. It's a cruel joke that fall break and the stomach virus had to fall the second half of my cycle!

I just want to be done with the Reset. I'm tired of the hassle of the BAB every morning and want to drop it so I can focus on becoming a fat burner. I'm sure that I'm not in ketosis--the tenacity of cravings suggests that.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-29T14:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-180701>

You know, I'm journaling here because it really helped me to see how other forum members experienced challenges and/or setbacks and carried on, ultimately to see success. Mamagrok is one person who comes to mind.

I pulled three all nighters last week with vomiting children. I've had to clean the same section of carpet TWICE in seven days because my kids didn't make it to the toilet. We won't talk about the diarrhea and stomach issues I battled all week trying to take care of everyone else and trying to avoid getting sick myself. Because, shit, who would do the laundry then?

Every time I post something here that is not up to par--I ate some chicken (!), I fell off the Leptin Reset wagon because my whole fucking family was vomiting everywhere for an entire WEEK (!), I'm criticized for not doing the right thing.

I don't get it. I've read through the MDA threads, I've explored this forum a lot. No one has a linear progression. I could have lied or just not posted, instead I chose to post what is REAL and what is challenging for me right now. Good for you if you get this shit 100% right every day. For the rest of us mere mortals, maybe reading my journal will help.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-29T15:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-180703>

Hey Nado,
Life sucks sometimes but it is a keeper at all costs.
Reason why people seem down on you is your own dopamine level taking a skydive with the change of seasons. A month ago you still had reasonable light levels. The mag ...tate has not been invented yet that can compensate for this.
Supplements or food will not get you out of the hole.
Have you followed the UV infrared,black light debate on here? It may be a worthwhile small investment especially as with Halloween and its accompanying special offers around the corner it need not be a bank breaker.

Sending you an icecold hug (Jack.Kruse.com the only place where you can say this and be understood )

Love this link! Thank you. We've been outside most of the weekend... I even got my husband to remove his sunglasses and take his shoes off. He made a few jokes about it...but he did it! I'm sitting outside now to type this message so I can get the last of the daylight before nightfall.

Am I correct in understanding that even though UV light is declining because of the season, I still need to seek out as much morning light as possible? This will be true even in the winter months? Or do I follow the stronger UV light to emphasize midday exposure?

### NadoRo — 2015-11-02T00:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-180935>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=180704)
>
> Ask yourself are you on the Leptin Reset or not? There's nothing wrong or unusual with going through ups and downs or challenges as we all do. We we are off track, though, we are not "on plan" and expecting our results to be what they would be if we're is wishful thinking.

Yes, I do see your point and I wholeheartedly agree. I was sidetracked by the lost sleep last week, and that plus some wicked PMS this month (seasonally caused maybe?) threw me. The sleep is such an issue for me..To have pulled three all nighters with sick kids killed my dopamine levels and motivation in a baaad way. Moreover, I'm sure all the time I spent inside taking care of them, cleaning, etc. did not help my mood or motivation at all. I need LIGHT.

YES, dammit. I'm all in. I've been doing this since mid-August, and I'm not going to stop until my sleep gets better. I think that is the marker of progress for me, and I know it will come. I got a glimpse of it last week, when I slept through the night TWICE. It was an amazing feeling..

I have to reconcile that the Reset is taking longer than I expected. That I am not in control of the length of the process but that I am in control of my level of participation! Kids are back in school tomorrow after a week's break. CT starts again tomorrow.

Oh, and my D-Ribose came! I've started putting a tsp in my coffee in the am. Energy has been very good last two days and motivation is up. Maybe my mitochondria appreciate the support?

Thanks for piping in, thisbirdhaswings. Your comment initially irritated me, but it was on point.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-02T05:33:31+0200

I can do both, just have to prioritize. Today, I started making a mental list of all the things I could do outside that I would normally do inside..like folding laundry, for instance. When I'm in the kitchen, I try to do all of my work by the sink with the window open..that's going to be interesting when the snow starts falling

Must take advantage of warmer sunnier days as we head into winter. Snow showers are forecasted for Thursday, but sunny and in the 50's until then!

Research this week to focus on the black light/reptile light uses and applications...

My 7 year old donned his red Dewalts as soon as the sun went down to watch Minecraft videos. I had explained to him a couple nights ago about blue light from computers and tvs suppressing melatonin production. He's a faster learner than his mom!

### NadoRo — 2015-11-03T04:07:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181036>

Lots of time outdoors today.. easy to do when it's 70 and sunny in November!

Back to CT today: 15 mins. w/58 degree tap water. Looking forward to the tap temp decreasing soon.. Focus will be on daily CTs of 15 mins at least..more if I have time and can stand it, but 15 mins. is doable even on busy days. I think consistency is important with cold adaptation efforts. It was tough to get in that water today, but I DID IT. Back on the horse!

Spent some time online watching diy fixes for mitigating SmartMeters..It was installed within the last year on the side of the house where our master and one child's bedroom is. Wonder if that's around the time he started sleeping in his sister's room.. Think I found a fairly simple way to make a Faraday Cage thanks to a video posted in the EMF RX forum by a helpful member. Will work on putting that together this week.

I'm craving crab cakes. Will be making some for lunch tomorrow. Yum.

Second day with D-Ribose in morning coffee--about a tsp worth. Need to do some research on dosing recs in the forum. Based on what I read, thought I should start slowly and see how I respond. Well, I can say that despite really crappy sleep last night..only about 3 hours due to a child waking, general anxiety about not being able to get back to sleep and some other issues, plus inexplicable hunger pangs..I had a great afternoon. The morning was tough, but I slogged through, got outside as much as possible and felt really energetic after lunch. I wonder how long it takes for the D-ribose to kick in?

COQ10 is on my shopping list for Costco this week. And, it's on special. Hooray!

Oh, and a woman I see occasionally around the neighborhood, stopped and asked me this afternoon what I've been doing to lose weight.. No one has really commented on my weight loss so far, which has only been about 5-10 lbs since starting the Leptin Reset, so it was motivating to know that something is showing externally from my efforts. (I just smiled and said that I was eating low carb and staying away from wine mostly, and doing some yoga here and there)

### NadoRo — 2015-11-04T22:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181164>

Well, someone else asked me what I've been doing...So funny, they all think it's because I joined a gym nearby. How to explain the simplicity of "what I'm doing".. Lots of natural light, seafood, water and a BAB?

I think the top part of my waist has thinned. I may even see the beginnings of that hour glass shape I fondly remember before I got my no-sleep mummy tummy.

Too much dark chocolate at lunch today. I need to figure out how to up my magnesium. I'm taking 144 mg Mag Threonate at breakfast and 400 mg Mag Asporotate at bedtime. More than that and I get diarrhea. I hate the sticky itchy feeling I get from mag oil, but may have to suck it up. I think these chocolate cravings and the occasional eye twitch and foot cramp suggest I'm still too low in mag.

I've CTed the last two days but just can't get myself in the tub today. I spent the morning cleaning up the yard in a tank top & shorts despite the temp only being mid-50's, but I've been freezing every since. My house temp is in the low 60's, which may be the issue. But I hate having the heat on.

There's a strong uptick in my hunger and thirst when I've spent a few hours grounding in sunlight and doing CT. Is it the battery charging and demanding replenishment?

I've procured the supplies I need for my Smart Meter DIY shield. I don't have a trifield meter so going on faith that it will work based on the You Tube video I watched. The guy at Home Depot was totally perplexed by my request for a foot of thin copper wire and aluminum mesh. I don't think he gets a lot of housewives asking for copper wire. I hope putting this up improves sleep quality for my son and me. The only issue is that our neighbors' smart meter is on the side of the house facing our bedrooms, so we're still affected by theirs. And then there's the issue of the large electrical box in our backyard. Power lines are buried in our neighborhood. I have no idea how to mitigate the effects of having that box so close to the house (about 15 feet away). I cannot WAIT to move in a year from this house, but I will do the best I can for the timebeing!

Time to construct my Faraday Cage. I just love writing that. I feel like a character out of Lost..or Better Call Saul.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-08T05:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181380>

15-20 min. CT baths yesterday and today. Water temp is now 57 degrees from tap and is perceptibly colder yet more comfortable. Just have to cross the mental hurdle of getting in a cold bath when it's chilly outside.

Grounded at two soccer games today. Lots of comments from fellow parents since it was mid 40's. I just shrugged and said that I love to be barefoot and I'm getting it in before the snow comes..One woman who eats paleo laughed and asked if I was earthing..She got it, no one else did. Funny. Noticed DH left off the sunglasses the whole game..Baby steps.

Weighed in at 157 lbs. today. I only weigh once a month now. Don't want to focus overly much on the scale and use lack of movement as an excuse to deviate from the protocol.

Blood testing scheduled for end of the month. Very curious to see results since August.

Sleep has improved. Am now sleeping through the night as long as I'm not awakened by external forces (aka the children or DH). I'm afraid to even write that I'm able to sleep through the night, lest it turn out to be a cruel hoax.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-09T01:01:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181443>

56 dg CT bath today 15 min. Kids were clammoring for playground. It was cold..not sure I could have stayed in much longer. Put both feet in this time and got the girls in, so maybe that's why?

Grounding and sunshine also. 60 degree day..easy to do.

I'm hungry. Just ate a bunch of butter and nuts..that's a snack, and I usually don't want snacks. Wondering if the spike in appetite is due to consistent CT? Hope the CT ultimately calms things down. Drinking peppermint tea and ignoring hunger until dinner.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-10T06:18:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181556>

55 deg F bath today. Brrr. In deep enough to cover most of my chest, and it was COLD. This was the first time I've really shivered in the bath. I kept the foot that had neuroma surgery in the whole time, and the other one out. Made it 20 mins. It was hard enough that I'm not looking forward to tomorrow. It's gotta happen though.

I did kettlebell swings w/15 lb weight to warm up before bath..so far zero soreness. Only downside is that I was really hungry at 3:30, having just eaten lunch at 11:30. My hunger is WAY up right now. I gave in and had some dark chocolate and pb with peppermint tea.

Do I need to up my BAB to compensate for the CT effects on appetite? Not sure what to do here..maybe I just ride it out and do the best I can? I remember reading other members' experiences with this and I think it eventually evens out. Just not sure about it in combo with the Leptin protocol.

Was re reading Leptin RX FAQ again.. I'm tired now by 9 or 9:30. My novels are piling up because I just can't read for very long before falling asleep. I'm still waking when kids get up to go to bathroom in the middle of the night, but it's not leading to hours of lost sleep. I'm able to fall back asleep before too long. Also, my nails are long and hard. I don't like long nails, but I'm curious to see how durable they are. My skin is a little on the dry side, but now that winter temps are here and the heat is on at night (thermostat at 63 deg), that's expected. My feet are much smoother than my friends'. I've always had a great complexion, clear skin, look younger than my age, so can't use that as a barometer.

I think these are signs pointing to increased Leptin sensitivity, but the uptick in my hunger and cravings says I'm not there yet. Maybe it's setting in at the brain level (sleep is improving, adrenal fatigue symptoms abating) but not at gut/liver (too much hunger still, overly reliant on protein for satiety). It's time to really push additional fat at every meal and see how that helps the cravings. I love cooking with CO, just hate the way it tastes off the spoon..Must continue to focus on spring water intake. With decreased humidity levels and heat on, I am waking up very thirsty. Not good. It is really tough to stay hydrated at this altitude, especially with colder temps and dryer air in play.

### Jonathin — 2015-11-10T18:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181588>

Omega-6 fats (ie- nuts) are known to make one more insulin sensitive and can drop your blood sugar. This might make one more inclined to snack. Saturated fats keep you more insulin resistance which is good in the winter.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-10T19:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181590>

Hmm..maybe I should try butter or coconut manna on the dark chocolate. Thanks, Jonathin!

Upped the BAB back to over 50 grams today--pork roast plus 3 eggs, extra coconut oil slathered on top. Looking to see what happens to cravings.

Outside grounding and sunning myself..6" of snow expected tonight and tomorrow!

Took a shower this am and discovered that anything more than lukewarm felt unbearably hot and uncomfortable. This is a great sign!

### NadoRo — 2015-11-11T18:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-181668>

First big snow of the season on Nov. 11 (6")!

I volunteered to do the shoveling..donned a hat, gloves, boots..long sleeve T shirt, capri leggings. No coat. No scarf. Absolutely no discomfort the whole time. Got some strange looks at school drop-off (it was mid- 30's). Cold adaptation is AWESOME.

20 min. in 55 deg. bath, post shoveling. Finally got belly area down to 58 degrees. Rest of my submerged part remain a stubborn 65 degrees. Don't know if that's good, bad or indifferent. Probably could have stayed in longer but ran out of reading material and got bored. Parts OUTSIDE of water were starting to get uncomfortably cold. Put socks on feet and hat on head. Definitely more comfortable that way. Trying to warm up now but house is only 63 degrees, so it's taking a while...

Addendum: followed up with a sledding session with kids after school. Jeans got really wet but wasn't uncomfortable until sun started going down 45 mins. in and temp really dropped. In general, I would say that I'm noticing an uptick in my ability to be cold and not feel it. Plus, my jeans are looser.

### NadoRo — 2015-11-24T20:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-182590>

Still here...major computer issues have kept me offline for the last couple weeks.

Some great news to report: Sleep is consistently improved, even through the second half of my cycle. This is a huge win for me. I now tell anyone who will listen to get OUTSIDE (dammit) as much as they can if they want to get better sleep. I can't believe it took me so long to figure out the simple equation that SLEEP=DAYLIGHT + NO NIGHT LIGHT. So simple.

With the better sleep, I've been able to cut back my breakfast to around 30 grams of protein. Watching my water intake helps with cravings. There's a direct correlation between hydration status and hunger for me.

I am finding that spring water is not cutting it however. I am really thirsty a lot and have been experiencing incipient migraines in the last few days. I have to drink more than a gallon of water to minimize them.. Just posted in the Beginner's area a question about RO vs. Spring and where in the blogs or webinars I can find the explanation of why RO might be better at improving hydration status. Getting the right water to speed up hydration is key for me in this environment. Just need to know if I should make the extra effort to find the RO sources here. Because we're close to the Rockies, spring water is so much easier to get in large quantities.

### Scompy — 2015-11-24T21:21:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-182591>

> [NadoRo said:](/goto/post?id=181590)
>
> Hmm..maybe I should try butter or coconut manna on the dark chocolate. Thanks, Jonathin!
>
> Upped the BAB back to over 50 grams today--pork roast plus 3 eggs, extra coconut oil slathered on top. Looking to see what happens to cravings.
>
> Outside grounding and sunning myself..6" of snow expected tonight and tomorrow!
>
> Took a shower this am and discovered that anything more than lukewarm felt unbearably hot and uncomfortable. This is a great sign!

Awesome update!

I wanted to share some of the insulin resistance issues I had in the past. My ravenous hunger is greatly reduced now by the Leptin RX and CT, plus keeping way more hydrated. I never consume any carbs in the morning and I get somewhere between 40 - 80 grams of protein depending on my hunger and previous day's workout, plus a lot of fats in the form of fish, meats, butter and coconut oil. Then at lunch, I often start some vege carbs (carrot, asparagus, garlic, celery, spinach, etc.). In the evening, I really back off of protein, and never go above about 10g.

In the past, I've found if I combine too much protein and carb for dinner, my blood glucose can spike higher back up past 90 - 100 in measurements, ESPECIALLY if i hadn't used up enough fuel in the day (more couch-potato/less active); anytime I get above 110, I get lethargic and ravenously hungry: this has a high correlation when I screw up my dinner specifically. I'm ketotic, so typically my blood glucose is in the 60 - 80 range because my blood ketones are supplementing in the 3.0 mmol range. But even saying this, at times, I simply eat food when I feel hungry.

Some lunches and dinners I bypass or minimize now that winter is coming on, and instead, get more hydrated. I do take some chocolate about every other day, but it's 85% cacao and only a little bit after lunch for the magnesium benefit. It's very easy to become over-addicted to chocolate, so if you're poor in the will-power deparement like i was in the past, I'd avoid chocolate altogether and get my magnesium from other sources like some pumpkin seeds. You've just made me want to post in my journal what foods are on my okay list and what foods are banned.

Thanks for sharing, Scompy! It's an interesting point you make about your blood sugar levels and how they relate to your hunger and activity levels. My bloodwork has never shown high fasting insulin levels, so I'd never considered the information I might get if I tested at various times of the day. I do have a blood glucose and ketone monitor I could use.

I was not able to get into ketosis this round of doing the leptin reset. This time I'm two years older and living at 5,000 feet, which I feel intuitively is not a good environment for me. My previous attempt was when I lived at sea level in SoCal, and I was two years younger. Hard to know if either one of those factors made a big difference. After Thursday (Thanksgiving), I'm going to change some daily macros and test nightly to see what I can do to impact the ketone levels. I think I eat too much protein and more carbs than I should be eating have crept in because I don't like to measure!

What does your dinner look like if you eat minimal protein?

### NadoRo — 2015-11-25T15:33:30+0200

The odd thing is that when I tell my friends/family members that THIS is how I've reversed by seemingly intractable insomnia, they are incredulous--even a bit dismissive. When you think about, it's just so obvious, simple and FREE. And no one wants to take off their designer sunglasses or bother to check out the rising sun. It's just too WEIRD and INCONVENIENT. ### Scompy — 2015-11-25T19:31:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-182664>

> [NadoRo said:](/goto/post?id=182646)
>
> Thanks for sharing, Scompy! It's an interesting point you make about your blood sugar levels and how they relate to your hunger and activity levels. My bloodwork has never shown high fasting insulin levels, so I'd never considered the information I might get if I tested at various times of the day. I do have a blood glucose and ketone monitor I could use.
>
> I was not able to get into ketosis this round of doing the leptin reset. This time I'm two years older and living at 5,000 feet, which I feel intuitively is not a good environment for me. My previous attempt was when I lived at sea level in SoCal, and I was two years younger. Hard to know if either one of those factors made a big difference. After Thursday (Thanksgiving), I'm going to change some daily macros and test nightly to see what I can do to impact the ketone levels. I think I eat too much protein and more carbs than I should be eating have crept in because I don't like to measure!
>
> What does your dinner look like if you eat minimal protein?

Typical dinner looks like 2 steamed eggs, or a slab of a higher fatty meat (like fish/salmon or bit of chicken thigh or maybe 5 frozen sausage links). Then for the side, I do steamed veges (chopped asparagus and/or bell pepper + little bit of purple onion + 2 wedges of garlic) and spiced up with hot-sauce like habenero (not salsa with tomato chunks/onions/etc.). I have this down to a science because I have these little glass dishes for my eggs for steaming. What I do is butter the inside of the glass cups, then put an egg into each one. Then I put celery salt, celtic salt, tumeric and a mixed seasoning of pepper and red pepper flakes on top. Then it only takes 6 mins to cook the eggs if the steamer is going or about 8-10mins for the veges. My steamer is just a pan with water in the bottom and a strainer-type container on top. The timing is such I can usually have my meal done in about 12mins from the time I start everything, and as this is going on, I'm often preparing my iced coffee, iced lemon water or herbal tea.

At dinner, if I go for a higher protein meat like chicken breast or even beef, I've gone into insulin lethargy 30-60mins after the meal with only sugars coming from steamed veges. Granted, that was about 6mo. ago when my redox was lower. I haven't had any nightly lethargy since about late last summer now, thankfully. Makes a great deal of sense that there is sliding scale between increasing redox and one's sensitivity to be able to increase protein/carbs later in the day. About 6 mo. ago, I wasn't consuming ENOUGH protein, so I think I had biochemically trained my processes to hit gluconeogenesis. I've heard other people complain about this eating keto as well. As a precaution, I limit myself to no more than 15 grams of protein in the evening because I'm consuming so much protein earlier in the day. But I'm thinking now that because of the leptin RX and more protein earlier in the day, my body might not want to trigger gluconeogenesis because there's enough available proteins/amino acids. It's the same reasoning if people are doing IF (Intermittent Fasting) along with eating Keto, I think it can PROMOTE gluconeogenesis.

I mentioned I won't do salsa at night either because the veges, especially onion, can turn back to sugar more readily. It's the same reasoning why I eat my carrots with my lunch rather than at dinner. I use a tea, decaf coffee or decaf iced coffee at night to wipe out any taste in my mouth and to suppress any potential appetite surge. As a note here, I'm really strict on myself and never use organic half and half . I only put butter/coconut oil in my coffee because I've found half and half can be a catalyst for an appetite swing.

### NadoRo — 2015-12-03T16:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-183168>

Checking in here..Nov/Dec are such busy months with holiday activities and commitments!

I'm seeking out daylight as possible, but it's getting so much more difficult now that winter temps are here. El Nino is bringing us a very cold, very snowy season this year..last week was multiple snow falls, single digit highs. Hard to be out for long at that temp..maybe I'll be cold adapted enough by next winter to get more exposure! We had a series of cloudy, cold & snowy days over the Thanksgiving holiday..and my mood plummeted. First sunny day was Tuesday, and I made a point to get out in it. There was an immediate lift to my mood and patience levels! I see now that my post partum depression after my daughter's birth mid-December several years ago was caused by a lack of light...If I had only known!

Sleep continues to be good most nights, regardless of where I am in my cycle. I credit light exposure/avoidance with this restoration. I still have some nights when I can't fall back asleep if awakened by children, but those are lessening too. I've lost 10 lbs.. but more important, but energy levels are stabilized. I feel good all day as long as I've slept through the night before.

Here's the interesting part..I just got back my labs. I test quarterly with my FMP. Insulin/glucose levels are optimal again. That's the Leptin Rx. Hormones, still stuck at suboptimal. HS-CRP? After 3 months of diligent gluten free eating and the Leptin Rx/vlc eating...NO DROP. It's been 1.4 consistently since I started testing a year ago, and now? 1.3!

Dairy has to GO. I've done a bunch of research but can't find anyone who has used the HS-CRP test to measure food sensitivity. Intuition tells me I must stop eating dairy..I've known this for a while but wanted to make sure that I was totally GF for a time to separate out the factors contributing to my chronic low grade inflammation. So now, I'm cutting dairy for a month. I'll go back and test my HS CRP to see what happens and report back. I know from my readings on this blog, that inflammation is more than diet..but I think dairy might be a bigger issue for me than gluten, based on my testing!

### NadoRo — 2016-04-28T20:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-193779>

Long winter, bad labs.. I'm back.

Restarted the Leptin Rx almost 3 weeks ago and seeing gains again. The protocol works, you just have to stick with it.

Recap: did the Leptin Rx last fall for 8 weeks, maybe more..felt great, sleep much improved, all labs moving in the right direction. I thought I was LS. I was not. It's been a LONG dark winter here in CO. My quarterly labs in February showed me as hypothyroid again, with low sex hormones AGAIN..literally every marker that had looked better in the fall had fallen to unacceptable levels along with my mood, energy & motivation.

Testing matters, people. Those numbers fired me up, got me off my complacent ass. I restarted the Leptin Rx in March, finally did an ASI, and found a new functional doc.

Today I walked for 30 minutes in a spring snowstorm to get whatever UV light there was to get. When there isn't snow on the ground, I focus on grounding some every day.

I see now the enormous effect light cycles have on me emotionally and physiologically. I need to re-read the Dopamine Rx as I'm sure to have suffered from low dopamine my whole life. Living at a higher latitude is not not a good choice for me. Looking to move within a year but that is dependent on my spouse's job search.

Cycloset--what's the deal? I'm reading through this forum, looking for information on how to apply it clinically to my situation. I've seen Wellbutrin mentioned here and there too.. I was on Zoloft for a bit for post-partum depression and do not like the idea of going back to ADs at all.

My March ASI showed below range AM cortisol and low for all other readings. I think the reason I don't show the usual pattern of high nighttime cortisol is that I am dedicated to low light after sunset and have been consistently for well over a year. But that first morning reading is a bitch. I am so tired when I get up..it takes all of my will power to get the BAB made and down, get the kids ready and fed..out the door to school. I look forward to the day when mornings are a pleasure, not a triumph of will.

### NadoRo — 2016-04-29T14:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-193814>

Thanks for the quick reply..

It's not enough light, for sure..but more illustrative of my efforts to get some light--any light--in a tough environment. Because of El Nino, our winter has lasted almost 6 months this year. Great for skiers, not so great for my labs. FWIW I do drive with my car windows down as much as possible, don't wear sunglasses..but it's not been enough.

I'm going for (delayed) quarterly testing in the next few days. I won't have my sex hormones at this draw, but I'll have thyroid, insulin, ACTH, and Vitamin D numbers soon. I have supplemented with high dose Vitamin K and D since November. This year, I intend to check labs early August to ensure I'm at a higher level moving into mid-September.

Question about aiming for 100 on the D test: I'd always read 70-100 in the blogs..Do we need to go for the high end of optimal ranges if we are in a crappy environment? In my case, I have minimal blue light exposure..some nnEMF like everyone else, but I don't work in an office..just live at too high a latitude and altitude right now.

My new doc is all about dosing on symptoms, so I've upped my Progesterone and am working on the thyroid. She's drawing a rT3 this time around and has no issue with using Cytomel to clear it if labs and symptoms show continued depressed T3 and high rT3. I'm just wondering how I use light to dig out of the depressed AM cortisol issue at my latitude? And if I'm successful this summer, what will happen in the fall as soon as we end up in diminished light cycles again?

That Sperti light is pricey. Too pricey for me at the moment, unfortunately. My doc and her testing plus my grocery bill is it for the moment.

For any newbie who hasn't taken the time to read the "Monster Thread" on MDA..I highly recommend it. It is motivating and chock full of useful information. I think Dr. Kruse has answered just about every question he can there about the Leptin Rx.
