About Sperti, think of Mother's day (Sunday, May 8/2016).
Happy Mother's Day!
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

With Sperti or without, I think it is good idea to have around you two or more SolarGlo 160W lamps. Closer is better, just do not burn your self with the (otherwise healthy IR)(heat).
Right when you get of the bed (in totally dark bedroom), look at one of those SolarGlo. For a moment directly, then on its edge. For a 2-3 minutes.
That should depress pineal gland from making melatonin, that should rise cortisol.
My SolarGlow are on for 14 hrs (designated as day, 365/7).
Above goes with garlic, onions, leeks, MSM, to provide sulfur for the (vit D,sulfate) we expect to make.

To raise cortisol, you have to have supply of it in the first place.
Good place to start is to fill up with DHEAs. Goal for Women (275-400) μg/dL
Remember, naturally we make pregnenolone from cholesterol, first.
Then progesterone from pregnenolone.
Cortisol is down the line.

You are supplementing with progesterone, that may be good but also less efficient, less natural.
Consider being more natural.
Supplement with pregnenolone, let your body decide the downward conversions.
And time when those conversions happens. Time may be important factor.

When I was doing this my self (supplementing with pregnenolone)
I was figuring my required daily dose by monitoring my 7:30AM cortisol,serum
with goal 20-21 ug/dL
laboratory range cortisol,serumAM(6.2-19.4)ug/dL
I was also monitoring my pregnenolone and progesterone, but no real action was taken based on test results.
My pregnenolone newer went significantly over the range, I would have cut down if it did, but newer need to do that.

I am a man, you are woman.
In first half of menstrual cycle woman have the same need for progesterone as man, 1.4 -1.5 ng/mL
In second half of menstrual cycle woman have larger desirable progesterone.

Supplementing with
Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix, 150mg tabs, from Nutricology
available from amazon.com
in the first year I had to use 900mg/wakeup (to get cortisol,7:30AM=~20ug/dL
then it was too much, eventually I stabilized at 300mg/wakeup for the next few years
Now
I do not need supplemental pregnenolone and my cortisol,7:30AM is in the upper range in the morning.
In the first year my progesterone was about 4ng/ml, now it is still highish but much closer to upper laboratory range.

Mind you, I was starting (from rather big hole)
with DHEAs below 100, pregnenolone and progesterone were not detectable, cortisol very low.

In case of a women, if you are menstrualing, it would be a good idea to (also) monitor progesterone around day #19-21
and (possibly) add some progesterone at that time or slightly before.
Prometrium 200mg right before bed time (for better sleep) may be good first try.
\-------------

About RT3.
I think of it as I would think of a gas gauge.
If you move the needle with your hand, would that make your gas tank more full?
I do not think so.
Same thing happens when you add Cytomel(T3).
RT3 indicates what body thinks about FreeT4.
If you have too much FreeT4 it tries to get rid of it by converting it to RT3 rather than T3.

Ideally we want TSH (0.5-2) and FreeT3 within upper 1/4 of laboratory range.
Between the two above, I think FT3 is first.

Reducing RT3 to some preconceived desirable range or almost to zero accomplishes nothing.
When person is not able to make sufficient amount of thyroid hormones,
preferable supplementation is with natural hormone from pigs.
Synthetic T4 or T3 lets say are bio-identical,
but natural product contains more of desirable products that just the two above.
Dr Kruse should sometime discuss role of T2 & T1.

Thyroid system depends on iodine/potassium iodide and selenium.
It also depends on bunch of minerals, vitamins, micronutrients.
This gives great opportunity to medical advisor to hold on to you for years without much helping you.
Similar situation exists with other hormonal systems,
Adrenal Fatigue label puts you in the same position.
The variables happens to be rather common, the amounts are always anybody's guess.
When I learned about Spectracell It was an eye opener (for me).
34 variables
each one evaluated according to individual needs of person who's blood is evaluated.
No generic laboratory ranges, instead individual assessment.
On the end of the report there is a list with what is missing and how to correct deficiency.

If you will be able to do this test every six months you should expect relatively quick improvement in deficient variables, and no deterioration of what was ok before.
When you reach steady state, testing once a year or less often is sufficient.

buy directly------->>>
[http://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/](http://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/)
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
$390.00 (if doc writes script insurance may cover some of it, but need to add doc's fee)
On a slide #12 there is a list of measured items:
[http://www.spectracell.com/media/up...itamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/s/0e2059361_slides-functional-vitamin-mineral-and-antioxidant-assessment.pdf)
Sample Report
[http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/m/0e2064041\_mnt-sample-test-report082312.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/m/0e2064041_mnt-sample-test-report082312.pdf)
\===================================

Other than having great test the people at Spectracell are doctors with relatively common views of BigPharma.
They do not have a problem advising use of statins,
so I would be careful with taking any other advice from them.

'''

### NadoRo — 2016-04-29T22:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-193852>

Wow. Thank you for such actionable advice! This is great.

I'm going to look into the Spectracell testing. I'm due to see my doc anyway at the end of May, if she writes the scrip for the test I may get some money back from insurance. It's worth a try anyway.

I've always tested hormones on 19-21 day and scrips are based on deficiencies at that point in the cycle. I think I'll test at the beginning on my own dime to see where progesterone is. Currently, I take 150 mg pre-ovulation and 300 mg post ovulation. I've had the scrip for a couple of months now. My new doc is the one who kicked up the levels based on my symptoms (bad sleep, mood issues, breast tenderness) at my request.

BUT, I had seen your previous responses on the forum and MDA thread about boosting downstream sex hormones through high dose pregnenalone. I like that idea a lot and had recently started taking divided doses (am/lunch) of 400 mg of Pregnenalone, along with 400 mg Keto-DHEA. My previous practitioner didn't like my estrogen levels and said I convert DHEA to estrogen, but my current provider is totally fine with my levels. My plan was to stay at this dosage level for both until I have my hormone blood draw in May. I think I'll bump up the Pregnenalone again even higher and see what happens.

Here were my February 16 labs (drawn Day 20 or 21):

TSH .98 U/mL (.27-4.20)

T4, Free .97 ng/dL (.93-1.70)

T3, Free 1.9 pg/mL (2.0-4.4) LOW

That was on approximately 96 g of Naturethroid. I am now taking app 165 g Naturethroid..but still suffer from significant daytime fatigue.

DHEA-s 104 ug/dL (61-337)

Estradiol 184.6 pg/mL (22.3-341)

Estrone 59 pg/mL (13-135)

Progesterone 16 ng/mL (1.7-27)

Testosterone 19 ng/dL (12-82)

Pregnenalone 76 ng/dL (Adult less than 151)

I don't think these lab ranges reflect optimal, but it's clear that I'm not even in the upper range for those references!

On the thyroid/rT3 issues: it's clear to me after re-reading several Leptin blogs and gathering information from the MDA thread that I'm still LR with these numbers. I had read about Cytomel as a boost, but my T4 &3 levels are too low across the board. I'll stick with the Naturethroid for now.

Yes, I've been diagnosed with Adrenal Fatigue..which changes nothing.

I made a huge mistake this winter--I joined a gym in the fall, caved to peer pressure and spent time exercising indoors. Excessive cardio, killer workout classes that ultimately left me exhausted and too sore. So classic. I've learned my lesson. NO exercise until I see the signs of Leptin Sensitivity. I'm so tired again that it doesn't matter. I've cancelled the gym membership.

Interestingly enough, I DID continue to lose weight throughout the winter--most likely due to the fact that I adhered pretty closely to a VLC eating template. I'm down 20 lbs since August, which is fantastic, but I would expect to feel better and I don't. The daytime fatigue is tough.

On with the Leptin RX. Leptin first. As soon as it stops snowing, I am cleaning off my deck and eating BAB outside. I am doing daily morning walks and focusin on outdoor tasks before Noon to maximize time in morning light. Off to finish reading the Dopamine RX. Off to order those lighbulbs. It's snowing today and I wish I had them set up already for a little basking!

Good point. After reading his DHEA blog, a couple years ago, I started with 7 Keto. I have no history of estrogen-dominant cancers, and I know from previous testing that the 7-Keto will raise my DHEA-S levels somewhat, but maybe not enough in that form. I'm comfortable with experimentation, just don't want to start too low and waste more valuable time. I'm re-reading the LEF info and starting at 75 mg this AM. Titrating up from there..I'm a tester and will pay for things myself when I can't leverage insurance.

I am very grateful for your quick and detailed responses, JanSz!

### NadoRo — 2016-05-02T21:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194034>

Snow has finally stopped (it's only May 2, for goodness sake) and the sun has come out. Spent an hour in full sunlight/bikini/50 degrees grounding. Over an hour and I start to burn. Got to work on being outside more, but it's a challenge when much of my work is inside like everyone else. Just listened to a podcast JK did with some guys in the UK on light..Very interesting. I got a lot out of it because my latitude and seasonal light challenges are similar. My take from it is that I need to be outside as much as possible, even in the shittiest of weather to get the light. I also need to sleep longer--which I'm not, because I can't. Leaving now to buy a UV bulb at home depot. Will start using that to read by at night instead of my red light in the hopes that it will help sleep duration and quality, which is my number one challenge right now. Just finished a meal of tuna, and probably having salmon for dinner. I've done a good job of keeping up with at least one seafood meal a day, so it must be the light issue that's holding me back.

Was just going to ask about where to find the best (cheapest) lamp base!

### JanSz — 2016-05-03T03:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194054>

Black light, that dr K used is Duracell lamp.
I think there should be a picture of it and place to buy on this thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-21#post-180657](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-21#post-180657)
That lamp emits UV light.

I will explain to you in few lines how I understand how light affects our health.

To raise cortisol we must first have it (be able to produce it),
then attempt to have it high in the morning may have a chance of success.

When we are able to make cortisol, we are making it
unless
melatonin prevents us from it
it is good to have lots of melatonin at night
when (blue) light shines on our skin or eyes, pineal gland learns about it
and stops melatonin production
this blue light better be only present from sunrise to sundown.
\------------
Two hours before solar noon till two hours after solar noon
sunlight contains UV frequency (invisible to human eyes).
\---
UV frequency is newer present after sundown.
Do not worry about using Duracell lamp at night,
some people (me included) got their sleep interrupted,
I suspect that could have been because small amount of blue light was (unintentionally) emitted by this lamp.
\============================
The ExoTerra SolarGlow 160W
are on in my place for 14hr/day every day
I divided my 24hrs into
14hr day
10hr night

/////

### NadoRo — 2016-05-03T03:48:30+0300

### NadoRo — 2016-05-05T17:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194231>

Update on the DHEA Supplementation.. Considering JanSZ's points on the subject of using high dose pregnenalone and DHEA supps to dig out of my adrenal/hormone/cortisol hole, I dropped my 400 g 7 Keto DHEA in favor of 50-75 mg regular DHEA. Along with that, I bumped up Pregnenalone to 500 grams upon waking. I'm on Day 19 of my cycle (of a 24-25 day cycle), and I'm experiencing significant increased breast tenderness. In the past, this has been a sign of PG/E2 imbalance. With increased progesterone (300 mg) at the second half of my last two cycles, (and the Leptin rx) this symptom had disappeared.

It's back. Plus, I'm bloated and up 3 lbs, which also has not happened the past couple of months. Instead, during the second half of my cycle I was still losing weight (albeit) slowly and not feeling any bloating in general. I think I'm converting the DHEA to estrogen only. Backing off the DHEA and going back to DHEA-S, which has shown to increase my levels in past testing. Continuing with the higher dose of Pregnenalone. I'm going in for testing on Saturday for an early June appointment.

For now here's how I'm addressing hormones/cortisol:

Morning/mid-day light exposure, with as much grounding as possible

Uvex glasses on at sunset. Reading under red light in bed.

500 mg Pregnenalone upon waking
400 mg 7 Keto DHEA
Low Dose Adrenal cortex in AM and at lunch
150 mg/300 mg Progesterone (not prometrium but from a compounding pharmacy)
Testosterone sublingual Troche (can't remember dosage..taken late afternoon/evening)
500-1000 Calcium Glucarate (to influence estrogen metabolism)

Leptin Reset with 1 seafood meal daily, Coconut oil is my main "gas" with avocado oil for salad dressing and bacon fat whenever I can get it nitrate free

### NadoRo — 2016-05-05T17:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194232>

An aside--I have a close friend who is my age, enjoys lots of wine and sweets after bed--all calorie controlled of course, gets up at 5 am to do hour long HIIT and weight lifting workouts under fluorescent lights at the gym, works all day in a school, wear sunglasses and sunscreen when she's outside...you get the picture. She's lost 20 lbs, looks amazing, seems to have plenty of energy.

Not fair at all.

I'm doing as much right as I can, and I've gained three pounds in one week..sleep like a baby, meaning I wake up repeatedly throughout the night, and feel a general deficit of energy.

In the face of her CW success story, it's challenging to explain what I'm doing. My friends see me not working out, losing some weight, but not drinking, not eating the same foods. It's clear a comparison is being made and I fall short. I have no intention of changing my path, but, like so many other newbies on this site, I can't help but wish to LOOD GOOD NAKED asap to prove my point!! I'm TEN POUNDS and some LHT/Sprinting workouts away from that goal. Just need to sleep and get the hormones and Leptin organized so I start working on my body comp.

I just keep telling myself that skinny people get AD, Parkinson's, cancer.. I'm on the road less traveled, but it WILL make a difference in the long run.

Gotta get the kids to school so I can sit in the sun for a while. I'm finished with my rant in the form of a pep talk.

### NadoRo — 2016-05-06T01:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194271>

Pregnenalone is from LEF bought via Amazon in 100 mg tablets. Just started higher dose, but energy levels are improved during the day even if sleep is not!

7 Keto DHEA is from LEF bought through Amazon in 100 mg tablets. Been taking this dose for a couple of months now.

Past experience with both supps based on quarterly blood work showed that these brands raise my levels, but my previous practitioner actively discouraged me from high dosing with either because her dogma said that diet alone would change my labs. It didn't and I fired her. My new practitioner is more of a partner, and I haven't discussed the higher levels with her.

You're pro DHEA, but I'm a perimenopausal 42 y/o woman with a pg/e2 ratio that isn't great. The Hormones 101 and DHEA blog both talk about using 7 Keto DHEA as an alternative. I test in a couple of days and will have results back in about a week.

### JanSz — 2016-05-06T03:58:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194277>

I am 76yo.
Not a doctor or any other medical worker.
My take.

pg/e2 ratio ---
please define what you mean by pg/e2 ratio---
I suspect to hear a story sort of similar to (omega6/omega3 ratio being good when 4:1)
just story without clear definition

Yes, I am pro-DHEA because when I tallied all major steroid hormones, I realized that DHEAs participation is 95%, see my table.
Cortisol's participation is about 4.25%.
Another way of saying that there is no chance to get anywhere until DHEAs is where it should be.
7ketoDHEA is not the pill to take when the goal is to raise DHEAs,serum.
Raising DHEAs,serum is a job #1.
Using supplemental pregnenolone in the absence of decent DHEAs levels is waste of time.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In the past I was heavy duty on frequent blood testing, got lucky with my doc, now that is in relatively past tense.
By making difference I mean: draw blood see where I am, take pill for at least a month, draw blood, see if make a difference.
I was using pregnenolone from lef.org for years, got nothing from it.
Latter I was using variety of other pregnenolone products, pills, creams, gels, got nothing from it.
Only pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs made difference.
I had to take 900mg/wakeup of those pills to get to the state that made desired difference.
By desired difference I mean that my
(cortisol,serum 7;30AM went from very low to about 20ug/dL which is slightly above top range)
On that 900mg/morning I was for a year, after that my cortisol started raising again
so
I lowered dose.
I got down to 300mg/wakeup and was there for couple years.
Now I do not use pregMLM and my morning cortisol stays high.

..
Since you are going to have blood test shortly, please do not make any changes in your protocol until you have results of your test.

..

### NadoRo — 2016-05-06T17:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194292>

An update on my Keratosis Pillaris for those of you out there with this issue..

I've noticed with my increased am/noon sun exposure, my KP is almost totally gone on my arms. There's a bit left on my upper thighs where I'm covered by shorts. I'm going out in a bikini when feasible but that doesn't happen on a daily basis. When it happens more regularly, I bet that area will clear too.

Woke up feeling less bloated after a full day without DHEA-S supps--only Pregnenalone and 7 Keto DHEA at the levels mentioned above. I take them before I get out of bed, go directly outside to sit (hopefully) in direct morning sunlight, and then make my BAB and drink coffee outside.

I have to say that the early morning sunshine feels so good. I could sit there forever, but I have to make my breakfast. :-(

This is good information, JanSZ..thank you for your continued input and support!

I would love to hear from another woman on the subject of high dosing DHEA, since I'm not in menopause and still have regular--albeit short cycles. I'm wary of doing anything that throws my (hard fought) weight loss and makes me feel uncomfortable. I noticed increased irritability, breast soreness, bloating on 50-75 mg of DHEA... The brand I was taking was drugstore--not the best, but didn't have any fillers that would matter to me based on prior experience. Maybe with the MRM brand I could take less and get a better result?

You say Keto DHEA will not raise levels, but then why is it referenced as an alternative in JK's blogs?

Moreover, I've been taking Keto DHEA off and on, and I've seen it raise my levels in bloodwork. I've been testing quarterly for two years. The reason my hormone panel doesn't look better is because I moved to a bad environment (high altitude, high latitude) already failing. I've realized that you have to be very aggressive and correct with light cycles, eating, DHA intake, etc...But it's taken me time and money to figure this out, unfortunately. In fact, at one point, I had DHEA levels that were approaching good to optimal on Keto DHEA, but my old doc told me to back off. My mistake was listening to her. I have learned after this winter that I have to keep everything high in this environment.

### NadoRo — 2016-05-08T17:24:22+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=194356)
>
> we have to pay close attention to our own n=1 ...

Totally agree with the N=1 perspective, especially because men seem to be simpler creatures when it comes to fixing hormone issues. I'm going to experiment with higher dose pregnenalone (500 mg to start) and see where that takes me. I'm battling below range morning cortisol, and since I moved it up from 100 mg in the am, I'm noticing better energy levels. With what I know about pregnenalone steal and the hormone cascade, this absolutely makes sense. If I were menopausal, I can see that DHEA would be a helpful adjunct because I would get estrogen from it..But I have to manage my estrogen levels carefully, because I have at least a decade-old issue with low progesterone that led to fertility issues when I was in my early 30's.

### NadoRo — 2016-05-08T17:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194371>

An update on my sleep..

Despite being at the very end of my cycle, I slept well last night. Why? The only (big) thing I changed was that I FINALLY got around to putting up temporary black out shades in my bathroom windows. Let's see if that makes a difference to night. I am extremely sensitive to light..fair skin, blue eyes, blonde hair--northern European heritage all around. I was also off my computer last night much earlier to watch a long movie with my husband. I always wear blue blockers at night, but still wondering about that. It's hard for me to put away the internet at night, as that is my longest stretch when I can read blogs or look at the forum.

Oh wait, I also did a magnesium foot soak because I've having muscle twitches and sciatica pain that keeps showing up despite no significant exercise.

Hmmm. I'll do all three again tonight and maybe that is the magic trifecta for a full night's sleep!

### NadoRo — 2016-05-09T01:42:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194390>

Wondering how you would deal with extra estrogen levels? DIM & Calcium Glucarate are what come to mind first to me? I want to be able to take DHEA without repercussions..just not sure how to do it. Think I'd better re-read the DHEA blog again. I know high levels are necessary for improved health.

I do think the Keto DHEA helps with weight loss..since I raised the levels my body comp seems to be improving more rapidly. Many folks have complimented me lately on how great I look and noted my "dedication" to working out. Lol. If only they knew. I just smile and tell them that it's more about what you eat than exercise and leave it at that.

Taking supplemental oral compounded progesterone has raised levels somewhat, but I need to be upper end of range to feel good. I'm no longer on prometrium, having been switched to a compounded form made in house by my pharmacy. Hoping this change means better labs to come.

I hear what you're saying on the pregnenalone brand and have ordered the Nutricology one. Next round of testing in three months will show how effective it's been. Gotta get those cortisol levels up before next winter.

I've been doing BHRT for almost two years now. My greatest success in getting levels up was the last time I did the Leptin Reset. Based on my quarterly labwork, I've been taking progesterone and testosterone from a compounding pharmacy. I recently did a salivary cortisol test that showed below range cortisol upon waking and low levels through out the day. In the past I was diagnosed with Adrenal fatigue, and I had all of the classic symptoms. Did the Reset last fall, felt good, dropped the big breakfast and strictures on exercising. Joined a gym, worked out a lot, lost a tiny bit of weight but CRASHED by February. Hormone panels back down, energy levels and sleep in the toilet. Now working to get everything back up and need to keep it up through next winter! No more gym workouts. Back to the reset, on week three or so. As much time outside as possible during the day...

Your advice to increase Pregnenalone was well taken. I'm up to 500 mg in the am upon waking and have good energy levels until mid afternoon. My sleep has not improved yet, but it will. I expect energy levels to do better after that. I've ordered the Nutricology Pregnenalone. I'm bumping up to higher amount of Pregnenalone after I test on day 19-21 of this cycle. I was supposed to test a couple of days ago, but my cycle ended up being only 20 days and I had scheduling issues.

I have to go back and read the DHEA blog. I'm not comfortable with the symptoms of bloating and breast tenderness that I saw with the DHEA supplementation. I've got to figure out how to keep those symptoms at bay while raising levels. It may be that topping out on Pregnenalone would do the trick.

I'm spending quite a bit on the BHRT, so it would be really great to be able to do just Pregnenalone and see a boost in my levels!

"

Selenium is also essential for the production of estrogen sulfotranserfase which is the enzyme which breaks down estrogen.
**A deficiency of selenium can thus lead to excessive amounts of estrogen,**
which may depress thyroid function, and also upset the progesterone-estrogen balance.

"

**1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day**

I can't thank you enough for your continued interest and input in my N=1! I've read a lot of your responses to hormone questions on the forum, and I've learned a lot from your knowledge.

The September 2015 weight has improved. According to the scale, I've lost anywhere from 15-20 lbs. I see hormonal fluctuations during my cycle, so I don't put too much stock in what the scale says. My clothes fit better, and I'm down 1/2 a size since last August, comfortably. Progress has been in fits and starts. I continued to lose weight VERY slowly over the winter by eating VLC, but I shot my adrenals by overdoing gym workouts and backing away from the BAB and meal timing rules of the reset. I stopped doing the reset in November because I thought my sleep had improved and was, therefore, LS. I was not and am not.

Low light levels, daily trips to the gym, insufficient BHRT tanked what were rising thyroid and hormone levels (November 15 testing). I live in CO, at 5,000 feet, and it is not a good environment for me. I expect to be moving next June to SoCal or NoVA. CA is preferable for the light levels, access to ocean CT, fresh seafood..sea level. That's where we want to be, but it is dependent on my spouse's work (military). At least NoVA doesn't have six months of winter and is at sea level, if that's where we go next.

The most recent labs, as of February 2016 are posted above.

Cholesterol levels remain high, but I credit that with the pregnenalone steal and altered hormone panel I've seen since the birth of my second child 6 years ago. I NEVER had high cholesterol before I had her.

The SIBO diagnosis came from my old practitioner whose dogma said that my hormones were bad because of my diet. She was not interested in hearing about my success with the Leptin RX. I don't believe the SIBO diagnosis is accurate as she based it on the lack of gut diversity shown by a Genova stool test 1.5 years ago. I was never able to verify through any research that SIBO can be diagnosed based on those stool test results!

After getting the Feb 2016 blood work results, I fired that practitioner. I found a new one, who is more expensive, but also more collaborative and open to learning.

All of these functional med docs focus on downstream hormone replacement..i.e. progesterone, testosterone. Why is that when the issues could be corrected with Pregnenalone and DHEA??

I'm going to try it your way. Not refilling my testosterone, since I've noticed no difference and it's a $25 copy per month.

Starting with 50 mg DHEA, per the LEF notes on DHEA replacement. I'll finish what I've got now and buy the LEF brand if that worked for you.

Starting with 900 mg Pregnenalone (Nutricology is on order, will finish off what I've got now in the meantime). Thinking I'll do a split dose of 500 mg in the am & 400 mg at noon as my ASI showed low cortisol levels all day long.

Continuing to supplement compounded progesterone tablets at 150 mg pre ovulation and 300 mg post ovulation.

Continuing with Selenium, Maca, Pantethine, Vitamin C, B 100 complex, fermented foods, Naturethroid

Thyroid question: Below range T3 & Low T4. Bloodwork shows depressed thyroid. New doc bumped me from 96 mg to 130 mg. Haven't noticed any difference in energy levels as a result, probably because my cortisol levels are so depressed.

I've been taking ACE twice a day for the cortisol levels per STTM, but I don't think it makes a difference and I don't like the jittery feeling I get when I take more than one tablet. It's like bad coffee for me.

My sleep is extremely inconsistent and unrefreshing. I dread mornings and middle of the night wakings, which happen too often for me. My productivity is way down as a result of bad sleep for more than 8 years now. I credit the poor sleeping habits of my babies with the way my bloodwork looks today! Chronic lack of sleep and the resulting physical and emotional stress put me in a state of adrenal fatigue several years ago. I'm convinced moving to CO from CA made it worse two years ago, and I'm digging myself out of a very deep hole in the context of a sub-optimal environment. I try not to dwell on the environment question because I can't immediately change it, and I don't need my stress levels to be any higher.

Sleep is the key. If raising DHEA helps my sleep, I will be eternally grateful to you.

**Do not use 900mg/day, that was my dose. You have to figure your own dose that will put your**
**cortisol,serum(7;30AM) at 20ug/dL**
**That can be had by trial and error. Excessive dose not advised.**
**But 600mg/wakeup may be ok, I guess. 600=4x150**

**Continuing to supplement compounded progesterone tablets at 150 mg pre ovulation and 300 mg post ovulation.**
**Do not use progesterone.**
**Do your blood draws at 2-4 days after starting cycle.**
**In first half you need progesterone=1.5ng/mL**
**I had up to 4ng/mL (more than enough)**
**There is a possibility that you may need some progesterone in second half of cycle,**
**but**
**lets go thru 2-4 cycles first to figure pregnenolone-MLM dose**

Continuing with Selenium, Maca, Pantethine, Vitamin C, B 100 complex, fermented foods, Naturethroid
**Other than fermented foods and selenium I would drop them all (preferred)**
**or**
**marry what you are taking and do not change dosing**
**do Spectracell, and follow test results (plus the list of stuff that you have married).**

Thyroid question: Below range T3 & Low T4. Bloodwork shows depressed thyroid. New doc bumped me from 96 mg to 130 mg. Haven't noticed any difference in energy levels as a result, probably because my cortisol levels are so depressed.
**I do not know what you are taking when saying 96 mg to 130mg.**
**I prefer Natural Thyroid from pigs,**
**dose sufficient to have**
**FreeT3 in upper 1/4 laboratory range, under tongue temperature about 36.6C**
**Good cortisol(7;30AM) is a must**
**I use product from orient. Thyroid-S, or THIROYD, or something new they have.**
**But it takes long to get it, so I have always 2+ years backup.**

I've been taking ACE twice a day for the cortisol levels per STTM, but I don't think it makes a difference and I don't like the jittery feeling I get when I take more than one tablet. It's like bad coffee for me.
**I do not know what is ACE.**

My sleep is extremely inconsistent and unrefreshing. I dread mornings and middle of the night wakings, which happen too often for me. My productivity is way down as a result of bad sleep for more than 8 years now. I credit the poor sleeping habits of my babies with the way my bloodwork looks today! Chronic lack of sleep and the resulting physical and emotional stress put me in a state of adrenal fatigue several years ago. I'm convinced moving to CO from CA made it worse two years ago, and I'm digging myself out of a very deep hole in the context of a sub-optimal environment. I try not to dwell on the environment question because I can't immediately change it, and I don't need my stress levels to be any higher.

Sleep is the key. If raising DHEA helps my sleep, I will be eternally grateful to you.\[/quote\]

**Sleep correlates highly with exposure to sun, specially within 2hrs before and two hours after solar noon.**
**It is ok to sleep under sunlight (after your skin is hardened to sunlight).**
**ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W 14hrs/day 365/year**
**If at wakeup there is no sun look at those lamps.**

### NadoRo — 2016-05-12T06:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194604>

A lot to digest here.

Spectracell is on my list to do..

NATURETHROID is the bio-identical dessicated pig thyroid that I take. I'll bet I see my thyroid numbers move with the higher dose pregnenalone and additional DHEA.

Just re-read the DHEA blog and glad I did. Every time I read these blogs again, I get new information! I'm so far behind on all of the information on this site.

Nutricology Pregnenalone came today. Starting 600 mg in the am.

Ordering sublingual DHEA 25 mg tomorrow

Will continue with 7 Keto DHEA at 100 mg. I do think it assists with weight loss.

No more testosterone. Continuing with progesterone. Understand now why it's not desirable to replace testosterone when progesterone/estrogen is not great.

Also going back to DIM & Indole 3 to help with estrogen metabolism. I've always had good success with Nature's Way DIM.

Also re-starting Iodoral at 50 mg. I took it faithfully for a year, ran out in the fall, and never refilled it. Never had any issues with it.

I look forward to paring down my supplement list. The cost is ridiculous, and I want to focus on the big hormones and stop spinning my wheels with BHRT.

I decided to try DHEA sublingually because (somewhere in the DHEA blog and comments) JK recommends trying that form if you have a leaky gut to improve absorption. I have a leaky gut. My labs will show whether or not that form works for my physiology.

Wondering why supplementing Progesterone down stream will disrupt my Pregnenalone N=1?

Many women on this forum supplement Progesterone, and I've read in multiple threads in the forum and in the Hormone blogs that high Progesterone levels (relative to estrogen) are essential to good health, especially for women. Men have less of an issue because your levels do not fluctuate cyclically. Mine were very low two years ago and have improved (though are not optimal yet) with supplementation. I've had low progesterone issues since my early 30's when I stopped taking the Pill to have children. Moreover, I recall reading that the more stressful (not optimal) your environment is, the more progesterone you'll need as a woman to counterbalance it. I live at 5,000 feet with almost 6 months of winter and low UVB for too much of the year. Not ideal at all!

I have not read all of the blogs nor have I had the opportunity (yet) to listen to the relevant webinars, but I know you have.. Just looking for more information on the progesterone supp question.

### NadoRo — 2016-05-13T16:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194686>

Hopeful sign with the Leptin Reset..

A full bladder woke me this morning at 5:30 am. The sun rises at that time here in CO, so my room was already bright.

While I wasn't dying to get out of bed so early, I did it and my BAB with very little effort. In other words, what is usually an act of will and self-discipline was routine and relatively simple today. This is a FIRST in recent memory. My morning cortisol is very low, and getting out of bed after what is usually a night of restless or unrefreshing sleep (at best) is an act of will. Tack on the BAB preparation and eating, and my reservoir of motivation & self-discipline has been used up for the day, LOL!

Also, my stomach is flatter and my waist is returning. Hooray!

Come on cortisol levels..come on..

### JanSz — 2016-05-14T15:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194769>

[http://www.endocrine-abstracts.org/ea/0011/ea0011p590.htm](http://www.endocrine-abstracts.org/ea/0011/ea0011p590.htm)
**The effect of salt on night sleep**
**F Heydarpour**
Author affiliations
Introduction: Salt in the principal supply of sodium. The needs body to salt changes according to life conditions. In normal conditions the average daily salt intake of Iranian adults is 5–15 gr.4–6 spoonfuls of salt in a day can be lethal, but for soldiers in equatorial regions 50 grams of salt in a day may be needed. Studying the different effects of salt needs separate research. In this study the effects of salt on night sleep was addressed.

Method: 20 volunteer students were chosen for the study. They had no problem sleeping before the study. They recorded the time of sleep and when they went to bed for five nights before salt eating, In the 6th day they took 0.5 gram of salt per 10 kilos of body weight at 8.00 PM at night We did record time of sleep, times of awakening at night, presence of any disturbances,depth of sleep, duration of fulfilling sleep. amount and times of drinking water at night, their general condition after sleep, effect on REM and NON-REM presence of nightmares. All these parameter about night sleep were studied.

Results: Salt delays time to go to bed, during sleep the individual awakens several times, Sleep disturbance is about 2–3 hours. The normal pattern is disrupted and deep sleep is decreased and it is superficial at best.REM and NON-REM cycles are influenced. NON-REM decreases and REM increases so that the individual dreams a lot and even sometimes has nightmares. The day after, they are not satisfied with the sleep. Subjects awaken earlier and during the night repeatedly got up to drink water. The day after they felt drowsy and fatigued.

Discussion: Chips, nuts and other foods normally contain lots of sodium (5–10%). Each 100 gram introduces 4–6 gram of salt to the body. **Taking these especially in the evenings can disrupt sleep. Thus it is recommended people with sleep problems must not take these in the last hours of the day**.

Thus it is recommended people with sleep problems must not take these in the last hours of the day**.

That's an interesting study, but without the context of where it was conducted and the hormonal status of the subjects..how could the same conclusions be drawn elsewhere for other folks?

For example, I have low aldosterone (2 points above bottom of range), and I eat a 1/4 tsp of salt in the am and at lunch. I don't mix it in water, as is typically recommended for adrenal fatigue, but actually EAT the salt. And it tastes good, every time. I don't eat processed foods...so it's not as if I'm eating the SAD with all of the sodium it includes. I drink RO water, which has no minerals in it.. I've even wondered if ADDING another 1/4 tsp of salt at bedtime would help with my night wakings. When I increase my water intake, I urinate even more. It's like I can't stay hydrated, no matter what. Thinking that salt at bedtime might help me hold on to water so I'm not waking up to go to the bathroom multiple times a night.

### JanSz — 2016-05-15T03:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-194798>

\[ quote="NadoRo, post: 194787, member: 14593"\]That's an interesting study, but without the context of where it was conducted and the hormonal status of the subjects..how could the same conclusions be drawn elsewhere for other folks?

For example, I have low aldosterone (2 points above bottom of range), and I eat a 1/4 tsp of salt in the am and at lunch. I don't mix it in water, as is typically recommended for adrenal fatigue, but actually EAT the salt. And it tastes good, every time. I don't eat processed foods...so it's not as if I'm eating the SAD with all of the sodium it includes. I drink RO water, which has no minerals in it.. I've even wondered if ADDING another 1/4 tsp of salt at bedtime would help with my night wakings. When I increase my water intake, I urinate even more. It's like I can't stay hydrated, no matter what. **Thinking that salt at bedtime might help me hold on to water so I'm not waking up to go to the bathroom multiple times a night.**\[/quote\]
\------------------------------------------------------------------
If you would end up using desmopressin,
using it at night should hold off or slow urine production for the night.

It all depends on grade of the problem you have.
And if you end up finding that your problem is light enough and using salt takes care of your needs, and you sleep whole night,
stop
worrying and be happy.
\=======================================
low aldosterone
low sodium
low vasopressin
\-----------

You may want to **ask your doctor to study** if you may possibly get some relief by using

Florinef (Fludrocortisone) pills
and/or

Desmopressin (nasal spray)
Clotting promoter and antidiuretic

\=============================
**Florinef (Fludrocortisone)** used in the treatment of cerebral salt wasting syndrome.
It is used primarily to replace the missing hormone aldosterone in various forms of adrenal insufficiency such as Addison's disease and the classic salt wasting (21-hydroxylase deficiency) form of congenital adrenal hyperplasia.
\==================================
**Desmopressin** (trade names: DDAVP, others) is a synthetic replacement for **vasopressin**, the hormone that reduces urine production. It may be taken nasally, intravenously, or as an oral or sublingual tablet.
\==================

You may want to add these tests to your other hormonal testing:

**Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) Pediatric Profile Six—Comprehensive Screen (Endocrine Sciences)**
**Test Number: 500175** CPT: 82157; 82533; 82626; 82633; 82634; 83498; 84143; 84144; 84403

**Test Includes**

17-α-hydroxyprogesterone;
androstenedione, serum;
cortisol, serum, LC/MS;
dehydroepiandrosterone, serum;
deoxycorticosterone (DOC), serum;
11-desoxycortisol;
17-OH pregnenolone, serum, MS;
progesterone, serum;
specific compound S;
testosterone, serum (total)

\======================================

**Antidiuretic Hormone (ADH) Profile**
**Test Number: 046557** CPT: 83930; 84588
**Test Includes**
ADH; osmolality

\=================

She's open to what I'm doing and sees a functional medicine doc. Unfortunately, she's down in the "weeds" with her N=1--essential oils, a laundry list of random supps, HC, too many carbs and eating all the time... I've sent her multiple links to JK's stuff, and I've given her step by step instructions on the Leptin Rx..but she's not willing to try it. I guess it's easier to spend all her $$ on supplements and drugs like HC, all the while wondering why her Hashimoto's antibodies are in the 400's. She prefers to follow the advice found on FB groups, which are giving some really bad or only half-truth advice. She'll have to see me heal first hand with the fairly simply stuff I'm doing here before she'll be convinced. She, and everyone else I know--ha, ha.

I know JK's way works. It doesn't always take effect on the timetable we want, but it does work because it is grounded in solid science (even if I don't understand it all--am looking forward to the brain fog lifting). I'm so eager to be Leptin Sensitive and regain optimal cortisol levels. Once I'm there, I am going to hit the HIIT and LHT HARD to change my body comp.

Got my MRM Pregnenalone and took the first dose this morning--600 mg!

### NadoRo — 2016-05-25T05:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-195300>

So far, so good with 600 mg of Pregnenalone and 50 mg DHEA in the am... I go in Thursday morning for fasting blood draw. Can't wait to get a snapshot of what's going on hormonally, thought it's too early to know how the Pregnenalone and DHEA increases are changing things.

I also got my high dose Vitamin K--15 mg--and started taking that. Don't know if it's a coincidence, but I've been in the bathroom a lot and my gut isn't feeling great..not that it usually feels fantastic, but I haven't felt this way in a while. Some diarrhea, loose stools, etc.. May be good, may be bad. I don't know. Posted a question about experiences with high dose K in the forum under Leaky Gut RX.. My reading of the Monster MDA thread on Vitamin K is that higher levels influence gut bacteria, which is what I'm looking to do. I recently ran out of my Bubbie's saurkraut, which I was eating every morning, and I'm wondering if that's an issue. I'm not taking a probiotic right now. Tired of scattershot supplementation and not knowing what is worth the money.

I'm also having issue moving up my bedtime dose of Mag Malate & Glycinate. Anything above 400 mg with either of them, and my bowels are waking me up at sunrise! I'm deficient in Mag and need to be able to raise it. Thinking of just going to nightly foot soaks. My guess is I'm not absorbing the mag well. $$ down the toilet.

Good news: my dreams are more vivid now. Makes me think melatonin production and/or serotonin production is increasing, which is very welcome.

Big push on my part to be outside as soon as I wake up, facing the direction of sunrise. Spending as much time outside or near an open window in the AM to garner light. Unfortunately, the sun here is very strong and I am very fair. Don't want to burn, want the sun exposure..what to do?!

Weight loss has stalled. Bummer. BUT, I'm suddenly considering the possiblity that I might start sprinting within the month in the evenings..or lifting weights again. After my February crash, I couldn't imagine having the energy or motivation to work out. I think the thoughts themselves are a sign that energy levels are picking up. Mornings are good now, even if I'm still pretty tired mid-afternoon! Come on, Cortisol!!

Also starting to eat my seafood lightly cooked or raw, depending on what it is. Had seared Ahi for dinner, and lightly cooked shrimp for lunch. Could this be the cause of my current gut issues? Hmmm.

Quite true in my case! Where I live is not optimal.. I'm doing what I can with what I've got since there's not much I can change outside of my own actions and choices at this point. My nnEMF exposure is high, but at least I manage my blue light exposure.--it's almost nothing, if that's possible in today's world. When I'm alone, I routinely turn off my wi fi router.. My location is bad--too high in latitude and altitude, but we can't move for another year. DH is deep CW in all ways, but I'm leading by example and trusting he'll eventually follow. Basically have decided to only focus on what I can change: what I eat, when I eat it, and how much light exposure I can get on any given day. Also restarting CT post breakfast next week. I will stay on the Leptin Rx as long as I have to this time to get it right.

### JanSz — 2016-05-25T17:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-195324>

From my notes:

[
](http://www.allthingsmale.com/community/threads/my-green-laser-prostate-surgery.13628/page-6#post-98129)
[http://www.allthingsmale.com/commun...aser-prostate-surgery.13628/page-6#post-98129](http://www.allthingsmale.com/community/threads/my-green-laser-prostate-surgery.13628/page-6#post-98129)

One newer know, lets keep this in mind.

**Magnesium Per Rectum**
From DoctorMyhill
[http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Magnesium\_Per\_Rectum](http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Magnesium_Per_Rectum)

Giving magnesium by injection is the quickest way of restoring normal blood and tissue levels of magnesium. However, for some patients the injections, whilst giving benefit, are too painful to be considered long term.

At a conference in Australia in 1999 I spoke to a doctor who had been trying magnesium sulphate given PR (per rectum - ie up the backside! - like a suppository) with some success. If this technique works, then it would be a cheap, safe, do-it-yourself at home technique which could replace uncomfortable injections. I have now tried magnesium PR with quite a few of my patients and it has been as effective as the injections in some of them.

To try this at home, you need some **Epsom salts and an enema syringe**. Epsom salts are virtually pure magnesium sulphate and are available from chemists. You can buy an enema syringe from the chemist and this can be re-used so long as sensible hygienic precautions are taken between doses.

Dissolve 250g of Epsom salts in 1 litre of warm water. This provides 5 grams in 20 ml, equivalent to 600mgs of elemental magnesium. This solution can be stored in the fridge for six months, but do not forget to warm up before use.

**Small bottles of spring water are 8fl oz=236ml**
**0.236\*250g=59g Epsom Salt.**

**1 US cup = 8 oz=236.588237 cc**
**I make my 10 oz coffee using round (measuring) tablespoon of Maxwell House Instant Coffee.**
**Coffee enemas are done with full strenght coffee.**
**For 20cc enema I would need 1 tablespoon coffee.**
**Lets try first half strenght.**

**236ml/20=11.8 portions=11.8/2=6 tablespoons(to get half strenght)**
**I am not giving up to purists who would insist on organic coffee.**

**Wonder if sleeping is going to be affected by "taking" 5oz of full strength coffee in the rear.**

**No, sleep is not disturbed.**

To load the syringe dip the tip into the magnesium solution, and draw 20ml back into the syringe. Some patients find it easier to hold the magnesium in by starting with a very tiny amount of the liquid and slowly increasing the dose, thus giving the back passage time to get used to the experience! Magnesium is well absorbed through the colon and 600mgs is a good dose. All electrolytes have potential toxicity, so do not use more than 20ml in 24 hours.

Insert the tip of the enema syringe into your bottom (perhaps using some KY jelly as a lubricant). Once the tip is in position, slowly squeeze the bulb and the contents will pass into the rectum. It is sensible to lie down for several minutes after! Hold on to the magnesium for as long as possible.

If the magnesium is being absorbed, then I would expect patients to get the same response as from a magnesium injection, but of course without the pain. It does work for a useful proportion of CFS patients so is well worth trying if you get benefit from the magnesium injections.

\====================

[http://www.firstoptionmedical.com/Coloplast-Cysto-Care-Coude-Tiemann-Red-Rubber-Late-p/60748.htm?sku=60748 0001&utm\_source=amazon&utm\_medium=versafeed&utm\_term=60748 0001&utm\_campaign=business industrial medical medical supplies&utm\_content=Coloplast&v\_traceback=c0215\_0500\_f0215\_0526&vfpr=0.5&Click=3044](http://www.firstoptionmedical.com/Coloplast-Cysto-Care-Coude-Tiemann-Red-Rubber-Late-p/60748.htm?sku=60748+0001&utm_source=amazon&utm_medium=versafeed&utm_term=60748+0001&utm_campaign=business+industrial+medical+medical+supplies&utm_content=Coloplast&v_traceback=c0215_0500_f0215_0526&vfpr=0.5&Click=3044)

Red Rubber Latex Urethral Intermittent Catheter 16 Fr Price: $1.49
[http://www.amazon.com/Mentor-Red-Ru...ef=sr\_1\_4?ie=UTF8&s=hpc&qid=1278630522&sr=1-4](http://www.amazon.com/Mentor-Red-Rubber-Urethral-Catheter/dp/B000O0COIC/ref=sr_1_4?ie=UTF8&s=hpc&qid=1278630522&sr=1-4)

Catheter Tip 60 CC Syringe Price: $0.72

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0002YFRBG/ref=oss\_product](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0002YFRBG/ref=oss_product)

Fast Weigh M-500 Digital Pocket Scale, 500 by 0.1 G Price: $5.70
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000P1NYE8/ref=oss\_product](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000P1NYE8/ref=oss_product)

Rite Aid Epsom Salt 64 oz (1.8 kg) Price: $6.99
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00008J2XP/ref=oss\_product](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00008J2XP/ref=oss_product)

\----------
[http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Magnesium\_-\_treating\_a\_deficiency](http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Magnesium_-_treating_a_deficiency)
**Can you hang on to magnesium?**

**For magnesium to be retained inside cells you need good cell membranes.** The two important facets of cell membranes are:
Have good antioxidant status - see Antioxidants. (I take them a lots for A1c management and other)
Have good levels of fats and Essential Fatty Acids in the diet. See GOOD FATS AND BAD FATS. (I take them)
**Boron** is necessary for normal calcium and magnesium metabolism.

I take 3mg of Boron with each Only Trace Minerals pill, I take 3 pills. Total 9mg of Boron
[http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Nutr...\_taking\_all\_the\_time\_even\_if\_nothing\_is\_wrong](http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/Nutritional_Supplements_-_what_everybody_should_be_taking_all_the_time_even_if_nothing_is_wrong)
Myhill's Magic Minerals would have me use 6\*2=12mg of boron
...

**All in post #1
Dr Patricia Kane thread
[http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942](http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942)
is meant to improve membranes.**

....

I think as my DHEA status improves, I should be able to get outside longer in the sun. Altitude here at 5,000 ft is a confounding factor.. most folks will burn here simply because of the elevation. Right now, I'm typing this post outside, in the shade on my patio--mitigates the blue light from the computer screen and exposes me to daylight without the sun exposure. I sit outside, in the sun, while I eat my breakfast and am out for most of the morning in it until around this time. I'll go out and eat my lunch in the sun with the intention of getting UVB exposure for about 20 minutes.

I think I'll make a ceviche with leftover tuna for lunch. Yum. I'm craving raw seafood like crazy today.

Just looked at my list.. I thought doc was testing progesterone.. Hmmm. Too late now, but I can test on my own on Days 2-4 as you've suggested.

I've been reading the MDA Monster Thread for weeks now, and finally got to your explanation of why we should be looking at downstream hormones Days 2-4. Totally makes sense! Of course, all the docs I've encountered only want to test Luteal Phase hormones if you're pre-menopausal. So, I'll take these test results to leverage my insurance $$, and test on my own for Days 2-4.

My current doc was satisfied with my estrogen numbers from February, so she didn't want to re-test them at this time. I'm fine with that because I don't want her hassling me if the DHEA is pushing up Estrogen levels temporarily. She doesn't know about my Pregnenalone/DHEA dosing, and I only plan to tell her after the fact when my levels are restored. I've found it much easier to do my thing (Leptin Rx, light management, supps) and tell the docs after when I've been successful. They're basically there to order the tests I want so that insurance will cover the bulk of it.

JanSZ, I want to thank you for advising the increase in Pregnenalone AND DHEA supplementation. I feel so much better already, and it makes a lot of sense to focus on getting those hormones right first. You've already saved me $$ in copays for the Testosterone and Progesterone, and that's great. More important is how much better I feel in the morning now!! I think the DHEA with the Leptin Reset is finally changing my sleep. Last night was Day 18, and I have very short cycles--21-25 days long. Usually, I can't stay asleep the second half of my cycle. This time, I'm sleeping. If I wake up, I am able to fall back asleep. While I still protest a little at getting up in the morning, IT DOESN'T FEEL SO HORRIBLE. I actually feel ok about it!! You know from having Adrenal Fatigue, just how difficult it is to take that first step out of bed! My sleep has been bad for almost 9 years.. NINE YEARS! I'm upping the DHEA to 75 mg tomorrow.

I'll get fasting insulin tested next time by my functional doc or my regular doc, who is the "Low Carb Diet Doctor" of Denver but is totally CW on hormones.

### JanSz — 2016-05-27T23:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-195495>

\[ QUOTE=Gene Devine;36948\]**DHEA Supplementation
Dehydroepiandrosterone: DHEA
As presented by Dr. Neal Rouzier, M.D.
Here's a great reason to add DHEA to your protocol.

DHEA is a hormone secreted primarily by the adrenal glands. It results in a shift of a catabolic state to an anabolic or protein building state.

· It reduces cardiovascular risks by increasing lipolyses (decrease visceral fat).
· It stimulates the immune system, restores sexual vitality, improves moods, decreases
cholesterol and body fat.
· It improves memory, increases energy, and has anti-cancer properties by enhancing the
immune system.
· It is an endocrine precursor to other hormones, prevents immuno-senescence, loss of sleep,
osteoporosis, atherosclerosis.
· DHEA reduces insulin requirement
· Adrenal hormone anabolic vs. catabolic metabolism
· Restores immunity
· Prevents osteoporosis, increases bone density
· Prevents cancer in lab animals
· Prevents diabetes & heart disease
· Decreases visceral fat
· Improves mood & well-being
· Improves energy & memory
· Slows aging process in lab animals
· Prevents lipid peroxidation = antioxidant
· Endocrine precursor to T.P.E.
· 7-keto DHEA is not a precursor to other HRT = avoid
· Neurotransmitter (recently discovered)
· Presently pending FDA approval for Lupus (Prasterone)

**Clinically substantiated uses of DHEA include replacement for:**
· Low DHEA levels
· Chronic disease
· Adrenal exhaustion or corticosteroid therapy
· SLE
· Improving bone density
· Improving depression & mood disorders
· Enhancing immune response by activating T-cells
· Improving well-being
· Decreasing cardiovascular risk
· Improving erectile dysfunction
· Anyone over 40
· DHEA has never been shown to reverse the aging process
· Nevertheless DHEA is important for preventive medicine
· DHEA inhibits synthesis of thromboxane A2, reduces plasminogen activator inhibitor, and
tissue plasminogen activator – all decreasing platelet aggregation and ischemia.

**Administration:**
Men<200lb: 50mg AM
Men>200lb: 75-100mg AM
Women <50yo: 10-15mg AM
Women >50yo: 25mg AM

**IMPORTANT:**
DHEA Serum Levels
MEN
· Range 100-600
· Optimal 500-600
WOMEN
· Range 50-300
· Optimal 200-250
Higher levels in women predispose them to side effects – therefore stay low.
· Monitor monthly until optimal
· Assure correct dose and compliance
· Measure DHEA-S and not DHEA
· Side effects: acne, hirsutism
· Tx: Lower dose or take QOD Spironolactone 100 mg/day
· Contraindicated in sex hormone responsive tumors – breast, ovarian, uterine, prostate
· Conversion to T.P.E.?
· DHEA raises testosterone levels in women slightly, yet not in men
· DHEA raises estradiol slightly in men
**Indications:**
· Over age 40 for health protection
· Preventive medicine and well-being
· Symptoms of aging, mood & depression
· Cause the medical literature suggests it if we want to live longer, happier, healthier**\[/QUOTE\]

Read more at: [http://www.excelmale.com/showthread.php?6657-DHEA-Supplementation](http://www.excelmale.com/showthread.php?6657-DHEA-Supplementation)

Farmer's markets are just starting here in CO for the season--We had snow through the beginning of May! (Longest, snowiest winter I've endured yet, thanks to El Nino) I have a Trader Joes, Whole Foods, Vitamin Cottage and several supermarket chains within a 15 minute drive of me. It's easy to get what I need as long as I don't mind paying for it and driving to 3 different stores to get it. I really don't eat much veggies except for a few time a week at dinner to provide variety for my spouse.

I've started adding 1/2 an apple to my lunch and that, plus the start of my cycle, has dampened the cravings. Thank goodness! I'm back to feeling totally in control again. Where I'm located in the state, we have longer windows of daylight than other areas in my time zone. The cravings started up again after Daylight Savings went into effect, so I do think they are a result of the increased light levels. I make every effort to be out side as much as possible, so my body is well aware of the change!

Getting sweet potatoes at the store today and will add a few slices of that to breakfast..

I do need to keep adding fat to my BAB. I'm great with the protein, because I am someone who needs and thrives on high protein. I do cook everything in coconut oil, as that is what I prefer. I've started adding a spoonful to my coffee, and I made some fat bombs. They're a little sweet for my taste, so I can only eat a bit at each meal!

I had bloodwork drawn last week, and I'm eagerly awaiting the results. I expect to see some improvement in my Pregnenalone and DHEA levels at least because my energy has been noticeably better since I significantly increased my dosage of both. I'm up to 600 mg Pregnenalone (divided into AM & Noon doses) and 100 mg sublingual DHEA+100 mg 7 Keto DHEA.

I take Naturethroid T4/T3 combo daily, along with Selenium and Iodine. To combat possible estrogen issues from increasing my DHEA supplementation, I take DIM and Indole 3. I've taken DIM and tested in the past, and I know it's effective in reducing undesirable estrogen levels for me.

On top of that, I'm outside for a substantial part of the morning (except when it's raining) and also get out during UVB hours for as long as possible without burning. I am tanned now, with a pinkish tinge, which may suggest overexposure! It's tough here because our elevation is 5,000 feet and the sun is STRONG. I'm trying to strike a balance between exposure and overexposure, but I do feel that my window for being in UVB sunlight will lengthen as my DHEA levels improve and inflammation resolves.

I have to say that I've seen some wins recently with what I'm doing. I'd swear that my skin looks better, softer even after a period of looking dull and too dry. My stomach is so much flatter now. When I lie down, nothing hangs over!! I'm SO happy about this. I'm back in a Medium top, even though my chest remains larger than before I had kids. I guess that's not a bad thing if my stomach is flat. Lol! I'm now between a size 8 & 10. I'm almost 5'8".. That is PROGRESS.

Will post lab results as soon as they come in..

### NadoRo — 2016-06-03T17:51:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-195939>

Labwork results are in! (from Labcorp, drawn Day 19 of a 22 day cycle, last week)

Well, either Progesterone & Pregnenalone weren't on my doc's list, or the Labcorp tech screwed up. Either way, I don't have those numbers.

Here are the results that matter, COMMENTS AND ADVICE WELCOME:

WIN:

FASTING GLUCOSE 83 mg/dl
That's the VLC Leptin Reset working..and I think that's a great FBG number, right??

HEMOGLOBIN A1c 5.0 (4.8-5.6 Non diabetic/normal)

VITAMIN D 25 HYDROXY 84.6 ng/ml
I plan to keep supplementing at 5,000 iu daily plus UVB exposure. Will retest mid August.

WORK IN PROGRESS:

BUN/CREATININE RATIO: 19 (19-23)

SERUM SODIUM: 136 mmol/L (134-144)
I eat Real Salt at least twice a day, on top of salting my food with it. This is classic end stage Adrenal Fatigue.. I have low blood pressure, feel like my RO water goes right through no matter what..always in the bathroom. What can I do to get this number up?

DHEA-s: 196.8 ug/dl (57.3-279.2)
Supplementing sublingual tablets 100mg DHEA in the AM at breakfast for previous week or so.. Had been relying on 200-400 7 Keto prior to that.

ACTH, plasma: 14.0 pg/mL (7.2-63.3)
I don't know much about this..

TSH: .170 LOW uIU/mL (.450-4.500)

T4, FREE: .92 ng/dL (.82-1.77)

T3, Free 2.1 pg/mL (2.0-4.4)
Holding fast at low thyroid numbers despite being on 196 mg of Naturethroid (T3/T4 dessicated thyroid) daily!!

Reverse T3: 16.3 ng/dL (9.2-24.1)

Ratio FT3 to RT3: 12.9 BAD

So is this where my Naturethroid is going?

Thoughts on asking for Cytomel--T3 only or is my T4 too low to warrant that?

Here's a quote from JK I lifted during my extensive blog/thread readings. This sounds a lot like what's going on with me..and I think he's referring to Colleen Coble's experience with T3 only dosing at the end:

@[Jane](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/746/) Regarding your NP action with your salivary cortisol level testing email: Stress response to your thyroid is huge and very misunderstood by many. Chronic physiologic, mental, or emotional stress results in decreased deiodinase 1 (D1) activity and an increase in D3 activity. **_This causes decreasing thyroid activity by converting T4 directly into reverse T3 instead of T3. Conversely, deiodinase 2 (D2) is stimulated, which results in increased T4 to T3 conversion in the pituitary only and results in reduced production of TSH!!!_** So when I see somebody with a lower TSH and other crazy hormone panels I know cortisol is raised somewhere and is causing the mixed signal from the activation of the D2. The increased cortisol levels seen with any stress also contribute to physiologic disconnect between the TSH and peripheral tissue T3 levels. This reduces intracellular T3 which is a cofactor in making all the peripheral hormones from LDL cholesterol. So when someone is stressed with a higher cortisol we a higher LDL and lower HDL and low hormone conversion. The easiest one to measure is Vitamin D and DHEA. I also use ferritin, HS CRP as proxies for cortisol too. This stress induced reduced tissue T3 level and increased reverse T3 results in tissue hypothyroidism and potential weight gain, low energy with fatigue (Fibromyalgia), and depression. **_This vicious cycle of weight gain, fatigue, and depression that is associated with stress can be prevented with supplementation with timed-released T3._** We have a lady over at MDA who struggled for 15 yrs before her docs got her right. If you know the biochemistry and apply it well you can help people.

I have experienced significant emotional & physical stress over the last 8 years, raising my children (basically as a single mother) with an active duty, military spouse who did not participate in their care when they were babies on up to recently. I experienced post partum depression with both children, most significantly with my youngest. Severe sleep deprivation with both children, multiple moves across country, little social or spousal support during these years.. My son has a clotting disorder, hemophilia, for which I am the primary caregiver emotionally & physically. My daughter can be very difficult to parent as she is emotionally overreactive and extremely sensitive, physically and emotionally too. Hemophilia makes the sibling dynamics difficult at times because of the attention my son receives when he is injured or managing an injury. I am now responsible for doing his intravenous factor infusions when he is injured. This has been a stressful and steep learning curve for me, as any parent (without medical training) can imagine!

My point: how to control my stress response when so many factors causing the stress seem out of my control?? Because moving is not an option until next May, I am stuck with this location and the negatives it entails. I don't want to focus on what I can't change! So, I focus on my food choices, on completing the Leptin Rx, on improving my hormones through supplementation and finding the right doc..

I was awake from 12-3 am last night after an exhausting day at a theme park with my children and friends. Unlike most people, I sleep worse after a day of physical exertion. Go figure!

Seeing my doc today with these lab results. Curious to hear what she has to say.

### NadoRo — 2016-06-08T12:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-196147>

Not so awesome tonight.. Yet another night of being awake from 12-?? I think I've slept through the night once this week.

I'm pretty sure my blood sugar is still dropping at night and adrenaline is waking me because my adrenals aren't making cortisol. At least that's the standard functional med explanation for my inability to sleep through the night. I have refused to eat a snack before bed, because I understand how that screws with circadian rhythms and leptin sensitivity. I just wonder how long it's going to take for my body to figure it out. It's 3: 15 am and my stomach is growling.

I've had three days of CT in the tub, but was too tired to get psyched for it tonight. My 6 year old has decided that CT time is mommy-daughter time, and now wants to be a part of it. I'd prefer to be on my own, with adult distractions, because the cold bath can be tedious enough..adding in entertaining child and trying to distract myself and I lose motivation fast.

I'm in the 8th week of the reset. It's odd that I had a few initial wins--sleep was getting better, skin was looking better, even libido might have been warming up. Now I just feel terrible..like everything I've done was pointless. My sleep is reliably bad again, libido is gone, energy is in the toilet. Every part of me is so squishy even though the scale shows I lost a pound in two days. Since I upped my thyroid meds per my doc, I'm having episodes of nausea from 3-5 pm, in addition to my usual fatigue. I had a total meltdown this afternoon..trying to fix dinner for everyone despite serious fatigue and nausea. I wonder if I would feel worse if I just stopped taking the Naturethroid.. How could I get any more tired or have levels any lower than what I already have?

I'm very frustrated to be trying so hard and seeing little progress in the areas where I need to see it. The insomnia is a killer. I'm tired of seeing my friends with their flat stomachs, their calorie counting and their daily workouts talking about "moderation" and discipline as keys to their success. Meanwhile I feel I'm the most disciplined of them all.. they have no idea what it takes to get out of bed in the morning, to pretend to function as well as everyone around me. It's plain exhausting.

I need a win. Something. 8 weeks in. Come on.

### NadoRo — 2016-06-16T03:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-196657>

Spent approximately 6 hours outside today! I clearly need a dopamine boost based on my last post.

Last night I slept through the night, which was awesome. I'm tinkering with my dinner..and I suspect it needs to be pretty big, almost like my breakfast. I've been told many times by practitioners that I have blood sugar issues (duh) and my night waking is because my body is trying to stabilize. Their answer is to have a snack before bedtime..I refuse. But I think I will make my dinner much bigger, force down some more protein and fat beyond what I eat. I'm so tired of fixing meals by dinner time that I tend to just eat the minimum to assuage hunger and move on. Let's see if my hunch is correct and my sleep improves.

Back in the cold tub last night. Told my kids story time was happening in the cold bath. DD got in (eventually) and DS got in up to his knees. Reading to them passed the time. I think we were in there for 45 minutes.

Hit tennis balls this morning in bright sun with some friends. I'm feeling a little sore this afternoon..looking forward to the cold tub to take care of that. Both friends were complaining about how bad they slept..I told them to take off their sunglasses. (They ignored me.)

### NadoRo — 2016-06-16T19:44:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-196714>

Another round of tub CT after dinner last night. Day 17 of my cycle and it was extremely difficult to get into the tub. It was also much colder to me and required a lot of distraction to keep me in there.

Sleep was terrible. I was awake from 12:30 pm-4:30 pm. Read half a novel, feel like I'm hungover today with none of the fun from an actual night of drinking. Maybe tonight will be better.

I am an emotional wreck these last two days. Very stressed out and depressed. I'm wondering if it's the CT. I'm not sure detox issues would show so quickly, but maybe that explains it. I'm trying not to get bogged down, but it's hard. Feeling extremely overwhelmed with several personal issues and unable to remain sanguine today. Looking for a light at the end of the tunnel with my sleep issues or resolution with some other stressors I'm dealing with currently.

### NadoRo — 2016-06-17T06:35:36+0300

I'd been supplementing haphazardly with glycinate and or malate, but I'm thinking I need to buckle down.

Ordering Mag Threonate and Malate tomorrow. If I've got Adrenal and Leptin Resistance, I've got an issue at the brain with my mag levels. I think that's right? So, Mag Threonate for the AR.

Malate..sleep. I think I will combine foot soaks as well to hasten absorption. Mag oil is SOOO itchy.

Let's see what a full court press on magnesium does for my sleep..

The bad mood has broken at least! Definitely hormones, detox..something going there.

Sadly, I was awake again for several hours in the middle of the night. I go to bed slightly chilly and fall asleep instantly, but I wake up hot and unable to return to sleep for a long time. I think I'll sleep in our basement tonight and see if the consistently cooler environment helps!

I've been outside about 2 hours total today--we're in the midst of a heat wave. Supposed to hit 100 degrees by Sunday and the sun is extremely hot. I swear it just stopped snowing!

### NadoRo — 2016-06-18T17:42:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nado-ros-journal.16883/post-196883>

Last night, I slept in the basement where there are black out shades (the only true ones in the house from previous owners, go figure) and it is ALWAYS 10-15 degrees cooler than the rest of the house. Guess who slept through the night, without waking once? Maybe this is why I was sleeping better in the fall than the winter? We turned on the heat mid-November, and my bed is on the side of the room with the one functioning vent. I wake up hot and gasping for air because the room is so dry December -May.

In the summer, 0ur AC is so anemic that the second floor, where the bedrooms are, is perpetually 10 degrees hotter minimum than the first floor thermostat that runs the system for the entire house. My kids' rooms are the worst--too warm to sleep in comfortably in my opinion and too bright despite my best efforts to darken the windows. I can't wait to get out of the rental market and into my own home again. Patience needed. 10 more months, I think at this location. In the meantime, I'm turning down the AC temp drastically every time DH is gone this summer.

DH hates it too cold and has no trouble sleeping in warmer temps in a room lit up with daylight. It's amazing. I'm 100% northern European ancestry on both sides, whereas he is a 1/4 southern Italian and looks it--more olive skin, dark eyes, dark hair. Makes me wonder if genetics governs things like ability to sleep well despite your environment. I need a cold, dark cave, year round. My mother is exactly the same.

Oh, and I lost another 1/2 pound last night despite it being the last week of my cycle, when very few women show a scale loss at all.
