There is no UVC because of the atmospheric chemistry.................
At the equatorial surface there is only 5% of sun light that is UVB........but the photons of UVB are very powerful so you do not need many to have an energy of momentum impact on skin or RPE of the eye. Here both are linked to DHA and Vitamin A and D. Read the first few paragraphs of BG 5 to see what fundamental idea you all missed.

Light and mass are linked to EMF2. When you reverse that equation this is how biology uses it. We dont use e=mc2 read left to right.......nuclear physics requires it and that is why it was expressed this way by Einstein because he described the equation from an nuclear physics/cosmologic perspective, but realize it is a math equation of multiplications which means implicitly that 2 x 3 still equals 3 x 2. Biology uses c2m = E. This means light alone can be used to create matter.............yes you read that correctly.

C2 is critical to biology and it is not arguable when you consider that photosynthesis forms the basis of all food webs on this planet. This means light creates matter with the help of water. The C2 is the most powerful part of the solar spectrum that comes to the Earth's surface naturally for all things living.

Eukaryotes like humans are designed to harness it and this is why they have massive cells that have numerous cell membranes loaded with DHA........when you have congenital atrophy or atrophy of any sort in any tissue with some storm left behind, or you come from an egg devoid of mass and momentum light can be used to rebuild that tissue. What draws light to a surface fundamentally? See the year 1919: Gravitational lensing of light is that answer. Purple light bends most around things with a mass. Mr Einstein yet again.

Gravitational lensing of light brings the UV light close to the surface of a planet so that beings on it can absorb the most powerful part of the solar spectrum that penetrates the ionosphere..........the issue is two kingdoms of life (bacteria and archea) cannot use UV light and only one can..........why? Two out of three did not have DHA in the beginning because when did it show up? Read the beginning of Brain gut 5 very carefully folks for the time line you all skipped by. Another law of physics........the photoelectric effect allowed eukaryotes to use UV light for the first time in evolutionary history because of what new compound's pi electron cloud?...........DHA. Time for you all to put it together............

### prAna303 — 2015-09-22T21:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-everyone.16886/post-177601>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=177414)
>
> you don't write, you don't call ......
>
> seems like everyone is busy living a life
>
> I think we have all taken the three legged stool on board and really working it......
>
> Would I be correct??????
>
> what protocols are you doing?? what is working for you???? Where have you screwed up?????
>
> what do you wish you could/would do better?
>
> What have you not implemented and need to??
>
> What do you need to do a better job of???
>
> since we all aren't perfect like Dr. K. ........ but we can try our very best! our optimal life depends on it.....
>
> I, for one, like french fries way too much ....at least if I have some - it is at the ocean with my feet and bum firmly planted on the ground...and I have seafood with!!
>
> But I have had my little fling with french fries ..... back to reality!
>
> Other flings are okay?????
>
> so please give us a run down on your oxy? magnetism? water? light? seafood???
>
> tell me......

The gap between me and the rest of modern society has grown... In many ways.

Almost put internet and computers to an end, only using it sometime, once or twice a week. Each time i sit down i feel an instant urge to run away. My senses scream that this is wrong so writing this has a price, and i will pay... Life is about that, do and learn and leave bad shit behind.

My only income has gone with the wind, it left with a different lover called bankruptcy. Well that is one of the good things i must say, now life can be lived in the moment to the fullest. Since i have always felt that planning, security and systems is dead wrong and breaks many of natures laws, time to live that idea to the fullest. My cells must now react to each little change i face, not just health but in any aspect...

These months i still paid my bills, always money comes in my way and i take each chance for what it is. This makes living vivid, sublime and almost erotic.

This summer i changed the idea of time for good, i live outside of time as we see it today. Not having so called must, so called have to, so called do this makes this guy a happy one. Old friends is just that, none of them i have on my list... I broke up with many of them because they live the same day again, again and again. Some have become so pale and so in love with technology and money that they can buy good drinks but still have nothing that shines in their eyes. They have grown older and i jumped backwards, still it is never to late to have the perfect childhood.

The social side has expanded... Been out and lived life. Just by showing up at places and be human makes connections. Being the only one without a phone in a bar today makes you one odd cookie.

Held some speeches, most said i changed their life but i claim that i can only be a virus...

Girls... Strange that when someone comes to a certain age, you should only dream about them. My "hack" of time gives proof the other way. Four different females have been my joy this period, all of them has gone on trips now so winter is open.

Art... My acting has been done a lot this summer, result will show up in the end of the year. This will be my future right now, nothing matters more because this is life, live.

My body had to be broken, fixed, broken again and constantly changed. All of my old wear and tear is gone, a mix of nature and thought... My eyes is the last piece, change is great.

The world is a wonderful place if you are willing to create your own reality. Using my ideas from nature together with how i see the world act, i live in a dream, this dream can not be bought at the supermarket.

In the end, to have a full life, perhaps we need some old fashioned... Just ripping of all of the technology, gears, clothes, mental states and force yourself into a state where you have to respond directly... IS LIFE!

And, being normal is committing an act of harm to oneself.

We use magnetism and the triplet state of oxygen to get the job done at nanoscales. Nano magnets have shown the ability to phase transition based on their temperature making them very capable of efficient information transfers. This is a secret built into your retina skin and brain.

### nicld — 2015-09-23T19:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-is-everyone.16886/post-177673>

So here’s my update.

Doing well, I think. Been trying to get as much morning sun as I can and recharging on the weekends. Using the nicotine lozenge 2mg 2X a day during the week and feel the brain function has greatly improved, ROCKSTAR at work again. Using the LED red light 3-4X a week for 15-25 minutes in the morning. Got some BluTech computer glasses and LOVE them. Not sure if the scale is going back down but I think it might. Not going to worry about it now. I think the money I spent on fixing the electrical issues in the house was a great choice.

Pounding the seafood and locally grown or home grown foods. Minding the light cycles. I have stopped coffee and switched to tea. It has not been bad at all but I miss the smell and taste of a great mug of coffee.

I have stopped trying to get the hubby to do anything. He says I was “too controlling” so I stopped. Hoping someday he will get it. The “in poor health” SIL is staying with us. I am not even going to try and get her to change. She sees what I am doing and has had no desire to inquire so I will not waste my energy. She has been losing weight since she showed up which is surprising since she just sits around all day and her sleep is effed up. It may be the change in altitude and not as much nnEMF’s. She will be staying in the area permanently now. Just need to figure out when she is going to get her own place and when we have to run out and get her stuff.

Oh and trying to book my flight out to AZ over Christmas to see the DD. Booking flights at the best rate is AWESOME……NOT!!!!

# Thread: congenital hypothyroidism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-hypothyroidism.16889/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2015-09-21T08:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-hypothyroidism.16889/post-177468>

so we had a baby on saturday the 12th. uneventful pregnancy and birth. baby was slightly jaundiced at birth but so was my last and many others i had seen. diet was decent during the pregnancy. Like to think we lead a moderately healthy life. anyway, 5 days later got a call from dept of health. the babys tsh was sky high (648, ref upper limit is 15!) and the t4 was low (3.7, ref lower limit is 4.5). They said not to worry it was probably a mistake but come in for a retest right away. Went in and the test came back at 411 and FREE t4 came back at .39ng/dl (ref lower limit .75). Sent me right to a pediatric endo who diagnosed my little girl with congenital hypothyroidism. Said if we didnt start synthroid right away SEVERE retardation would ensue, 100%. I had never heard of the disorder but am pretty familiar with the importance of thyroid hormones. as a parent to a newborn you just never think something will happen like this. i asked if theres a chance the thyroid gland will kick on, he said 100% no. he said there may not even be a gland. i said is there a chance she can get off the meds at some point he said 100% no. After basically forcing him he wrote a script for a sonogram to find out if theres a gland and if so if its in the right place, right size. we go tomorrow. I also asked about bio identical t4 instead of synthroid. he said synthroid is safe and there are problems w compounding. i asked about armour he said its not standardized and its too risky. After frantically reading online about what i could for a few hours we decided there was really no other choice and started our 5 day old baby on 75mcg(!) synthroid per day. when i arrived at the pharmacy the poor pharmacist was frantically trying to get hold of the doctor, she was sure he had made a mistake with the dosing for an 8.5lb newborn.

Has anyone dealt with anything like this?? We are absoluting reeling from this and would welcome any thoughts on what can be done.

Rates of this disorder in the 70's in NY was 1 in 3000. Now they are 1 in 1400. what do u guy think is the cause for the over 100% increase in this over the last few decades? If i know my stuff the thyroid gland is like the canary on the coal mine. all enviromental toxins sortof buildup there no?

also interesting to note that the rates in NY are DOUBLE that of the rest of the country! another reason to leave this dump

Almost all molecules encountered in daily life exist in a singlet state, but molecular oxygen is an exception. This is why pseudohypoxia is so dangerous for humans. Our modern world favors singlet over triplet O2 formation. The unusual electron configuration prevents molecular oxygen from reacting directly with many other molecules, which are often in the singlet state. Triplet oxygen will, however, readily react with molecules in a doublet state, such as radicals, to form a new radical. The triplet state for oxygen is most stable under the magnetic field of the Earth. If your local environment is altered you can no longer make triplet oxygen and tissue destruction is the result. However, if something local in the environment affects magnetic fields or flux the singlet state can replace the triplet state. This happens with blue light, nnEMF,and with microwaves which are all common in today's northeast corridor.

When electrical resistance rises in a cell membranes, cellular pH falls and so does the EZ, and temperatures rise to reduce quantum yield to light. When a regular wire in your house heats up, as an analogy, even more energy is lost. As energy is lost, we leak light, specifically ELF UV. This light must be recaptued from the environment to restore signaling. If you can't absorb it for the reasons mentioned about you continue to destroy tissues. This is how AI destruction happens. Heat loss also can happen. This is common in hypothyroidism. When cold thermogenesis acts on water, it increases water’s dielectric constant, increases the number and strength of the hydrogen bonding network and this allows for a greater amount of energy to be transferred to and fro on water networks in a cell to build the EZ. This is why surface CT should always be used when you are reversing a disease. cooling surfaces enhances UV assimilation and absorption. It works best when DHA is present because UV light and DHA work ideally via the photoelectric effect and this can rebuild tissues lost. Remember Cold is a synonym for magnetism and cold condenses water. **Cold increases the magnetic force in mitochondria and it also increase melatonin levels in the retina and brain in the eye and pineal while allowing more UV IR light absorption. The kid needs the full spectrum light and DHA badly to recover.** Excessive protons (pH) or inflammation destroy these things. _Lack of UV light and DHA demolish tissues. IR lights for energy

2\. You want to replicate:
light/darkness (sunrise ~7AM, sundown ~7PM)
temperature, mostly hot (75F ??), but also times in cold shower with mom or dad)
humidity (40-80%) (during heating season, ultrasonic humidifier that accepts aromatic oils)
eliminate electronics as much as you can

3\. You said that pregnancy and birth were uneventful.
I assume natural birth.
I recall discussions on this board about cesarean births or natural births that were very quick.

### JanSz — 2015-09-23T02:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-hypothyroidism.16889/post-177624>

New UV rating system

Reptile UVB 100
Reptile UVB 150
Reptile UVB 200

The numbers 100, 150 & 200 refer to the average UVB output at 4" or 10 cm from the bulb.
All our new UVB bulbs have been designed for maximum vitamin D3 photosynthesis, by concentrating the UVB output near the 294 nm within the UV output.
At this wavelength vitamin D3 synthesis is most active!

**About Ultraviolet Light**

Ultraviolet radiation only counts for about 3% of the solar energy that reaches the Earth’s surface. It is situated in the high energy portion of the electromagnetic spectrum, just beyond visible light.

Ultraviolet radiation is divided into three wavelength groups:

-   **UVA** - Long wave ultraviolet A, ranges from 320-400 nm and is significant for reptile vision and wellbeing
-   **UVB** - Medium wave ultraviolet B, ranges from 290-320 nm and is required for metabolic processes
-   **UVC** - Short wave ultraviolet C, ranges from 180-290 nm and is dangerous to all living organisms

### JanSz — 2015-09-23T03:18:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-hypothyroidism.16889/post-177627>

[http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/lighting.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/lighting.php)

Without the sun's energy, all life on earth would cease to exist. It is by the sun's energy that all life forces are driven. Without it, our planet would quickly radiate away its own energy in short order, making all life extinct.

Although light is much more complex, there are only three facets of light that are important for reptile husbandry: ultraviolet light, visible light and infrared light. In order to create these three biological aspects of sunlight artificially, Exo Terra has developed several specialized lights. Reptiles require appropriate and high quality lighting in order to meet certain different metabolic needs.

As it is almost impossible to accommodate all of these needs in a single light source, a combination of different light sources is necessary in most cases. The table above will help you in making the right lighting choice.

dr k thank u so much for time to respond. I have started half teaspoon daily of dha to the milk and have also started 4 x 10 min intervals per day of direct sunlight with her in a diaper (docs were very against this saying cancer blah blah blah). im going to test vit d and then supp accordingly. sorry havent read these posts earlier the kids bili spiked to 20 and they put her in the bili box for 10 hours. i hate that f\*\*king thing and asked if there was any other way like vitamin c therapy. they said combined w the CH (congenital hypothyroid) they needed to get the number down. SO i spent the last night from midnight until 10am staring at her under those blue lights. my eyes r killing me. no way that thign is good for u. i also think artifically reducing the bili w no regard to albumin is a mistake. but in the face of a team of docors telling me doomsday scenarios i had no choice in the matter. Anyway her bili dropped to 12 and we got discharged an hour ago. i asked them to retest her tsh and t4 (i asked about t3 too jansz bc i agree w u, they said too much flucuation for a stable reading, t4 more stable. have another doc appointment tomorrow will demand test ft3), tsh was 40 and her FT4 was 3.7 (ref .8 - 1.6). they went to give her another dose of 75mcg and i said no f\*\*king way. they said tsh was still double over range needed to get below 20 i said who gives a shit about tsh at this point. her level peaked at 648, just residual washout. t4 was the number to look at and its way over range. we argued for awhile but on this i wasnt caving. at the end i said get the endocrinologist and lets hear what they have to say. the endo came and was shocked at the dose she was on, said it was way too much and agreed t4 numbers were too high. lowered dose to 44mcgs and retest in a week.

i also went for a thyroid sono. the tech had no clue what to do w a week old baby so called in the radiologist. he said in his 20 years he never had to sono a baby thyroid. he could not conclusivley say one way or another whether there was any tissue to be found. one thing for sure its not where it should be. this was a blow bc i was hoping the thyroid was at least there and maybe w some hacking we might squeeze more action out of it.

guys it means the world that you took time out of your lives to try n help on this and i really appreciate it. i have come to terms w this, she will be a normal kid w the t4 meds and i will do what i can to help optimaize her life and thats just the best i can do. she is "normal" in every other way, eats like a champ, very alert, hearing is fine, lifts her head up at a week old and has a head of hair donald trump would be jealous of. so i believe in her. thank u guys again

[http://www.google.com/loon/](http://www.google.com/loon/)

After watching the video and seeing what's about to happen with this and what's going on with 4G, we're gonna have so much WiFi around us we cannot escape, it's gonna turn us all looney for sure. # Thread: Calcium challenges
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gayle Andrew — 2015-09-22T02:39:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/post-177529>

I know I have just started this journey. Most of my life I have had the privilege of being exceptionally healthy and an active athlete. My family lived in the country in western mass. My father grew organic vegetables before it was "cool". He hunted and fished regularly so most of our food was delicious and native. My mother was a stay at home mom who was into competitive sports and a healthy cook. We had little time for tv, but I did love to climb a tree and read, a lot!
Then came college and work, GE, (factory floor), back to school to learn to be a healer and had a beautiful son, Devin Shea, met my soul mate, stayed active, happy and healthy.
Then Devin and I got into a horrible car accident in 2001 and I am having all sorts of problems. I believe most of them relates to the accident in some way. I am less active due to being in a wheelchair half time. Most of the bones in my body were fractured and put back together with metal or cadaver bone.
I just found out I have osteoporosis. I seem to be peeing out my calcium, even though I take plenty of vit D (1500)iu in D3 form. And try to get a decent amount of sun, for a northern woman, and am careful to block blue light when the sun goes down. Devin is also continuing to educate us and make changes in our EMF exposure. The docs can give me no reasons for peeing calcium. They are relying on a med called hydroclorothiazide. I would prefer figuring out how to prevent this problem or figure out why my body is doing this. Any thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-22T05:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/post-177538>

You need AM UV and IR light and since you are in Mass you need to consider UVA or UVB/UVA bulbs. Talk to Devin about this stat. You live in a low quantum yied place. Best way to explain it to you........would a sick plant get well with a tarp over it blocking all its sun even if you watered and tended to the soil? Nope.......and neither will you.

### Celeste — 2015-09-22T09:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/post-177540>

Hi Gayle,
Nice to meet you and I hope to learn also from your experience. So sorry to hear about your accident. I'm also wanting to learn more about preventing the progression of osteoporosis.

### Celeste — 2015-09-22T09:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/post-177541>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=177538)
>
> You need AM UV and IR light and since you are in Mass you need to consider UVA or UVB/UVA bulbs. Talk to Devin about this stat. You live in a low quantum yied place. Best way to explain it to you........would a sick plant get well with a tarp over it blocking all its sun even if you watered and tended to the soil? Nope.......and neither will you.

So would you recommend tanning beds Jack? What is IR light?

### Optimalbound — 2015-09-22T09:44:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-challenges.16891/post-177542>

IR = Infrared light. Heat radiation, in other words.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-177570>

Hi all. It was suggested I post in this forum regarding labs. Below are my lab results grim June 2015. This was pretty Leptin RX which I started about a month ago. And pre- AM UV and tighter control on my environment. As the title of my post indicates I am looking for a precise location of point A on my health map so I can effectively get too point B. Part of that is to determine if I am really leptin resistant and get the best markers to track psoriasis and general inflammation improvement. You can read more about my background in my Optimal [Journal](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/diary-i-mean-journal-of-a-madman.16871/). As I mention at the end of this post my doc is willing to run whatever I need. TIA!

Bloodwork June 2015

I had a VAP cholesterol profile, thyroid panel(TSH+T4F-Hhyabs+TPO+T3F+T3 Reverse), CBC with differential/platelet,Comp metabolic panel, Lyme Western Blot serum, lipid panel, X-reactive protein(cardiac), Sedimentation rate-Westergen, and a Vitamin D 25-Hydroxy panel. Here are the full results.

-   HDL Cholesterol 54 mg/dL >

### FutureVision — 2015-10-14T17:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-179468>

About reptile lamps. I've held off getting one because I am still researching. From what I have read the Mega-Ray self ballasted onces seem to have the best record of reliability and longevity. They even have a 275w bulb . I've listed those specs below. I'm now trying to figure out what the appropriate amount of UVB would be for my N=1. The other thing I need to consider is my access to getting really inexpensive UVB and probably UVA and IR medical devices. I'm talking full body panels for a roughly 10% of the cost thanks to my insurance. If I were to use those panels predominantly for my UVB then I don't think I will need a 275w reptile lamp. I'm not sure what diagnosis I would need to qualify for UVA or IR lamps.

Specifications

Mega-Ray® SB 275-watt narrow-flood is a 275-watt Self Ballasted Par-38 True Frosted Flood Lamp. A true flood lamp, it will disperse usable UVB in a 30" circumference at a 24" distance.

MINIMUM distance setting of 24" will produce approximately 150-200 microwatts per square centimeter (uW/cm2) of UVB and 900-1200uW/cm2 of UVA.

MAXIMUM distance setting of 36" will produce approximately 50-75 microwatts per square centimeter (uW/cm2) of UVB. (Distance is measured from lamp face to basking spot or reptile for optimum UVB and heat.)

[http://www.reptileuv.com/shop/index.php?main\_page=product\_info&cPath=65\_67&products\_id=184](http://www.reptileuv.com/shop/index.php?main_page=product_info&cPath=65_67&products_id=184)

### JanSz — 2015-10-16T19:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-179670>

> [FutureVision said:](/goto/post?id=179669)
>
> And the Omega 6 ratio is part of the fatty acid profile right?

Omega6;3 ratio is bogus (non-descriptive value) when without additional explanation.
Basically we want to eat food that have omega 6: omega 3 ratio of about 4:1 (per Yehuda)
what we get as result in the body is different story.
**Specifically it is about eating (LA/ALA)=(4:1) O6/O3**
Every part of body tissue needs different ratio to be happy.
\-----
When you learn that EPA and other o3 fatty acids (highly) suppress AA and other o6 ,
and see the amount of EPA in fish food, it is easy to ask followup questions.
\-----
When you get results from fatty acids analysis,
the ideal values are in mid range per each type of analyzed fatty acid.
On bottom of post #20 I created table that attempt to recreate the post-processing of those results following dr Patricia Kane example.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

### JanSz — 2015-10-16T19:46:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-179671>

What many in the ancestral health community do not know is that EPA in the marine food chain limits the amount of AA that we can place in our tissues. It is like an internal control switch if our circadian signaling is working well. Since most modern humans have an altered circadian signaling today this switch is no longer operational.
....................
The brain makes up 3% of our body weight (BW) but has a 100 to 1 ratio of _**06 to 03 within it normally**_.
...................

[https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

Lifestyle changes since June labs.

Stopped IF and switched to Leptin RX diet in mid August
Stopped coffee all together over 3 weeks ago
Have not exercised since starting the Leptin RX mid August
Been out in the sun a lot more and wearing blueblockers at night and f.lux on screens all day
Tried to limit screen time as much as possible
Very little CT

### JanSz — 2015-10-28T20:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-180640>

> [FutureVision said:](/goto/post?id=180637)
>
> Stopping the vitamins and supps will do what for me? Will the throw off my results? Or maybe you are saying that I shouldn't be taking anything I might not need? Then, should I stop and wait to get the Spectracell test done so my system can clear out? Sorry for all the questions. Trying to learn as much as I can.

Spectracell Micronutrient test works of lymphocytes.
Lymphocytes have relatively long half-life.
Spectracell recommends doing this test not more often than 2x/year, so the next test is done from new generation of lymphocytes.
So,
I suggest that you throw out all supplements except selenium,
unless you have seriously good reason for taking them.

.

### FutureVision — 2015-10-28T20:14:51+0200

I started taking all of them for a good reason at one time or another. That's the quandry I face. For example, glutamine. I started taking that because my naturopath highly suspected that many of my issues were gut related. I'm not sure that is resolved. I'm not seeing improvements in my inflammation or AI issues so it is either not fixed or not my issue. Magnesium has been recommended over and over again since we have so little in our diet and you need zinc with it. The Vit C goes back a long ways when I was researching megadoses of vitamins for issues like ADD. As an added benefit I rarely get sick. At least not as sick as most people get. So I keep the Vit C around. Vit K was a recent add in. Also gut related. B-Complex was added within the last year as well by my naturopath. I forget the exact reason why. It looks like from my gentic data that I may have issues assimilating some of the B vitamins so it might be good to keep it around. Creatine was for pre workouts and is generally very safe but maybe not necessary. And the Gaia Thyroid support was for my thyroid issue. It contains iodine and, tyrosine and ashwaganda. Hasn't helped my numbers but I've been taking it in label recommended doses. Maybe I need more for a theraputic effect?

I see very good reason to dump all this.
The reason is:
You will be doing Spectracell Micronutrient test 2x/year for as long as next test gives significantly different results from the previous test.
Then
you will change to 1x/year
As you see from this discussion, the first test will not be the best no matter how you look at it,
but,
it should put you on solid road to some kind of stable path.

You possibly be working with numerous supplements, there is no sense in taking something that likely is not hurting you, if only because of bulk and cost.

### JanSz — 2015-11-09T01:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-181444>

ALA --------------- a-Linolenic Acid, C18w3 --------------- -65.00
EPA --------------- EPA, C20:5w3 --------------- 804.65
DPA --------------- DPA, C22:5w3 --------------- 85.79
DHA --------------- DHA, C22:6w3 --------------- 62.73
LA --------------- Linoleic Acid, C18:2w6 --------------- -40.57
GLA --------------- g-Linolenic Acid, C18w6 --------------- -49.25
DGLA --------------- h-g-Linolenic Acid, C20w6 --------------- -47.00
AA --------------- Arachidonic Acid, C20:4w6 --------------- -38.76
DTA --------------- DTA, C22:4w6 --------------- -55.71
DPA --------------- , C22:5w6 --------------- -60.00
\-------------------------------------------------

Ideal value for fatty Acid is when it is in mid range.
When it is in mid range, value=0% zero
When it is in top range, value=+50%
When it is in bottom range, value=-50%

You have EPA=804.65% (that is over 16x (sixteen) times more than proper level. 800/50=16
and high DHA
That is because you are eating fish oil or kril oil.
Stop eating them.
They are the reason for your suppressed AA.
For a (unspecified) amount of time, year or more, likely it would be a good idea to stay away from any seafood.
This can be treated as a transition phase.
Hopefully @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) will chime in with better idea.

You are low on ALA, LA, GLA & DGLA

I suggest to start with 1 tbsp/day
Nutiva Organic Hemp Oil, 24-Ounce Bottle
from [Nutiva](http://www.amazon.com/Nutiva/b/ref=bl_dp_s_web_2597575011?ie=UTF8&node=2597575011&field-lbr_brands_browse-bin=Nutiva)
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000GAO91K?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o00\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000GAO91K?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00)
and adjust latter.
Lots of eggs.

I probably eat roughly 1lb of grass fed ground beef daily too(.5lbs breakfast and .5lbs dinner).
>
> I have a food sensitivity to eggs per the test I took last December. Is it wise to eat eggs? Maybe my sensitivity has gotten better as I've worked on my gut over the last 10 months.

**I'm not taking any fish oil supplements.**
**\----------------------------------------------------**
This is information that I like to get (and bring it to attention) of those who may know more about it (and are willing to talk).
Look at the labels.
Most fish that we like to talk about have tremendous amounts of EPA (relatively to our needs),
It is not only that fish or krill oils have lots of EPA.
Fish have EPA in same excessive amounts.

Any Omega3 suppress Omega6.
(It does not work in reverse direction. Excess of O6 does not affect levels of O3.
Correct amount of any individual omega3 is (obviously) desirable.
Excess (of anything) is not desirable.
You have 16x more of EPA that you should ---->that is immediate reason for low AA.

\------
Not sure what to do with eggs, since you are allergic to them.
Eggs are good source of AA.
For now we can assume that when you reduce your intake of EPA, then the AA may raise without need for any other intervention.

I am not sure what to think about oysters as fatty acids source.
I think they are pretty good source of minerals.

/========================
**We need a ton of DHA but I have too much?**

Dr Jack emphasizes role of DHA in our health.
He talks a lot about it.
Every time he mentions DHA some may get impression that there is a need for another (additional) pound of salmon.
I think that is not the case.
We always need only the just right amount.
\-----

Jack does not talk much about other fatty acids,
but we still need them.

........

//////

Hopefully someone will chime in with good answer to your question.

For right now,
fatty acids (including EPA) are part of cell membranes and mitochondrial membranes
and triglycerides
likely
there are free floating fatty acids (specially during digestive process).

The way I deal (for my self) with this type of problems is by doing blood tests.
Consider making changes that you think are reasonable at the time
implement your idea (hack)
then
do blood test
to see how that is working for you.

If you in a hurry, you may test fatty acids 3-4x/year
Likely more practical would be to do 2x/year
Spectracell
Fatty Acids
Metabolic Panel (14)
Complete Blood Count (CBC) With Differential
Thyroid Panel
Steroid Panel

note lack of Lipid Panel
\-----------

Melasma-hyperpigmentation
fish among this:

Melanocyte-stimulating hormones (MSH)
low pregnenolone
low progesterone
high estradiol
low p/E ratio
adrenal fatigue
\---------------

Fatty Acids start with parental acids.
Yehuda figured out that 4:1 ratio is the sweet spot.
4:1 mean 4:1 of (LA:ALA)
to cloud the issue, people call it 4:1 (Omega6: Omega3)

To clarify further, it is about eating 4:1 (not testing it in the blood).

If you want to be precise (complicated and expensive), there is 4:1 oil available.
Hemp oil (see previous post) is not only close enough but it have other fatty acids that are generally beneficial,
and you are low on them.

Hi Dan,
to throw my 2 cents in - I saw that your homocysteine is elevated. In the US they end the "normal "range at 15, here everything over 10 ist already considered too high. This indicates a deficiency in B12, B6 or folic acid. Also gives a hint what might be going on epigenetically with the methylation.
You say you are taking a B complex supplement.......a B12 injection might be more beneficial (if it's the B12 level that is low)
Maybe check out your B Vitamin levels again- a deficiency would have implications in so many pathways

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-12T15:39:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-181767>

The near infrared portion of natural sunlight is clearly beneficial for the skin.
[https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/](https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/)
The sheer number of Low Level Light Therapy (LLLT) studies (all non-ablative, i.e. low-power) is staggering if you look in pub med. Interestingly, almost all these studies are using near infrared light (~ 600-1000 nm wavelengths) to heal the skin and the body.
This article posted today shows how near infrared light, the photobiologically active portion of the sunlight, positively impacts skin health and skin conditions. [https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/](https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/) So why did I post this here this AM. You need to understand how light and its momentum or lack of momentum works in your body. Where light hits our surfaces has many effects. These effects work by a sliding scale that is capable of going up and down based upon the redox of cells where light hits the surface. So if your redox is poor light cannot and won't do what you expect. That light either pushes or pulls cell membranes loaded with DHA. Can light push things like cell membranes? Could this result in phonons and solitons that get transmuted by the tensegrity system in a cell? You bet your ass it does. It has long been known that light carries both energy and momentum, but no one seems to know how momentum and energy get turned into quantum information for the cell. The momentum of light has not been so easy to describe for physics. New experimental results now suggest that the answer depends on whether or not the light can put fluid into motion: Since all cell membranes are fluid in humans this begins to make sense. If light can move a cell membrane, light slows and its momentum decreases and it exerts a pushing force on a cell membrane; This is how Vitamin D3 is made with the interaction of UVB light. Note that if the skin redox is poor you won't be able to make any D3 even in full spectrum sunlight. This should be an eye opener to people. It should help MD's realize why some people like the obese need whopping doses of D3 to get their level north. It is also why some people will need K2 with their D3 to get success. The darker one's skin is also is another factor in how the momentum of light works. Dark skinned people need more not less sun. Same is true with the obese and this with skin conditions like psoriasis. Clearly Vitamin D3 needs a lot of momentum to be made and this is why the most powerful frequency of light is the only frequency capable of making it. What happens if you have no UV light? Say you live indoors under an LED? What if you live in the UK? Light cannot move the cell membranes as well and lights momentum increases more and speeds up into a tissue and it exerts a pulling force on the lattice around it. This is similar to what a bullet tract does when you get shot by a gun. No bueno. If you live with a poor redox disease or in a poor quantum yield area you need sunlight and sauna. Consider a move and a change of behavior.

Here is the link to the physics research. [http://phys.org/news/2015-06-physicists-pressure.html](http://phys.org/news/2015-06-physicists-pressure.html)

When you start exercising think about recuperation time.
If it takes long time to recuperate, slow down on exercise.
If you recuperate quickly, exercise more.

### FutureVision — 2016-05-09T20:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-point-a.16893/post-194420>

I got my latest blood results back. I think there are some positives and some negatives. Just to give you an idea of my practices over the lat 6 months of so here they are.

1.  Roughly 7 month ago I stopped exercising completely
2.  Roughly around the same time I stopped all supplementation. Occasionally I'll take some magnesium, selenium, zinc and B complex but it is rare.
3.  December was a real tough month for me. Extra stress from knowing my job/contract was coming to an end and a poor light environment
4.  Full body UVB device for most of this year. There was a period of time in late March where I ran out of exposures and it took a few weeks to get a new prescription and get back to using the device.
5.  Stress has been fairly high since the beginning of the year. Making a go of running my own business.
6.  Eating habits have been less than stellar. There was a reduction in eating seafood daily but I try and get in a can of sardines every other day. I've stuck with eating mostly grass-fed beef and veggies most days. On some rare occasions I'll fall off the wagon and eat a bunch of carbs. Trying to get back to eating only 2 meals a day which are predominantly low carb.

With that background here are my most recent lab results from 4/27/16. The third number in parentheses are the newest numbers. The previous 2 numbers are for comparison from previous draws. Red indicates the number went down and green means the values went up. The colors are not indicative of good/bad/out of range results.

So here's what I am trying to figure out. Thyroid function seems to be tied to Vit D levels as I have read. My Vit D levels have gone down a little since my last draw but my TSH improved. Can TSH improve independently of Vit D levels? My other numbers moved too. I believe the increase in reverse T3 and Ferritin is definitely a sign of leptin resitance. I also went back to the Epi-Paleo RX book and found out that high ferritin could be a sign of metabolic stress. The book refers to an increase in HS-CRP and Ferritin as a result of an increase secretion of TSH. If my TSH numbers were historically high then it would appear with a lower TSH number that my thyroid is not sluggish anymore and producing more TSH which would be consistent with metabolic stress right?

I'm pretty sure we ordered a HS-CRP but I only say CRP, Cardiac on my lab results. Not sure if those are the same thing. I'll get that redrawn if they aren't.

Neurotransmitters look like they are a bit on the low side with the exception of maybe 5-HIAA, GABA and Epinephrine. What do you think?

> The sheer number of Low Level Light Therapy (LLLT) studies (all non-ablative, i.e. low-power) is staggering if you look in pub med. Interestingly, almost all these studies are using near infrared light (~ 600-1000 nm wavelengths) to heal the skin and the body.
> This article posted today shows how near infrared light, the photobiologically active portion of the sunlight, positively impacts skin health and skin conditions. [https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/](https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/) So why did I post this here this AM. You need to understand how light and its momentum or lack of momentum works in your body. Where light hits our surfaces has many effects. These effects work by a sliding scale that is capable of going up and down based upon the redox of cells where light hits the surface. So if your redox is poor light cannot and won't do what you expect. That light either pushes or pulls cell membranes loaded with DHA. Can light push things like cell membranes? Could this result in phonons and solitons that get transmuted by the tensegrity system in a cell? You bet your ass it does. It has long been known that light carries both energy and momentum, but no one seems to know how momentum and energy get turned into quantum information for the cell. The momentum of light has not been so easy to describe for physics. New experimental results now suggest that the answer depends on whether or not the light can put fluid into motion: Since all cell membranes are fluid in humans this begins to make sense. If light can move a cell membrane, light slows and its momentum decreases and it exerts a pushing force on a cell membrane; This is how Vitamin D3 is made with the interaction of UVB light. Note that if the skin redox is poor you won't be able to make any D3 even in full spectrum sunlight. This should be an eye opener to people. It should help MD's realize why some people like the obese need whopping doses of D3 to get their level north. It is also why some people will need K2 with their D3 to get success. The darker one's skin is also is another factor in how the momentum of light works. Dark skinned people need more not less sun. Same is true with the obese and this with skin conditions like psoriasis. Clearly Vitamin D3 needs a lot of momentum to be made and this is why the most powerful frequency of light is the only frequency capable of making it. What happens if you have no UV light? Say you live indoors under an LED? What if you live in the UK? Light cannot move the cell membranes as well and lights momentum increases more and speeds up into a tissue and it exerts a pulling force on the lattice around it. This is similar to what a bullet tract does when you get shot by a gun. No bueno. If you live with a poor redox disease or in a poor quantum yield area you need sunlight and sauna. Consider a move and a change of behavior.
>

# Thread: Posting photos online
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-photos-online.16897/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-09-23T20:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-photos-online.16897/post-177687>

I wonder does anyone here ever do that. I guess a few people have done that on this forum, and probably elsewhere as well.

The problem I see is the EXIF data. I'm not sure that everyone knows about that. It's a metadata (metadata = data about data) of a kind. Some devices insert it into image files. So a camera might do that, so might a internet-enabled phone with photographic capabilities (and far more likely), and so will some programs.

I see it as breach of privacy. Some forum software is sophisticated enough to scrub the EXIF data out of image files on upload. But most isn't.

What can it include? Quite a lot actually. Here is a list (not by any means exhaustive, and not meant to be): Information about the device the photo was taken on, information about the settings used (who cares?), a timestamp (getting more uncomfortable), GPS coordinates (i.e., latitude and longitude … more uncomfortable yet for those who value their privacy).

My advice: if you're uploading a photo you've taken anywhere, check what EXIF data is in the image file. If there's anything significant there, scrub that out before you upload it.

Here's an example of mine. On Mac OS X you can view this data simply by opening an image file in Preview.app and then using the keypress Command + I. (On Windows, IIRC, you'd want to right-click on the file and choose "Properties" and then go from there.)

Anyway, have a look. You can see where I took this, can't you? Not always a good idea ...

Ryan

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-24T16:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-has-lyme-here-i-would-like-to-connect-you.16899/post-177724>

### cantweight — 2015-09-24T17:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-has-lyme-here-i-would-like-to-connect-you.16899/post-177726>

Almost lost my life to it.

Ask any person with Lyme and they will all tell you their symptoms lessen in the summer....why? Uv light.

Lyme sufferers notoriously bundle up and stay indoors due to extreme fatigue, photosensitivity, pain...etc. And we wonder why we don't get better.

Print blogs for her. The entire Ubiquitination series is unreal. Anything on sleep. Anything on redox.

I do believe in supplementation when in such a poor state. Antibiotics have a place as well...and need to be used properly.

If your friends mental capacity is affected to where she is unable to read/comprehend blogs then you must do it for her and give her very simplified baby steps so to speak.

Get her away from mainstream Lyme bullshit...it will only confuse.

She needs every bit of sun she can get ...living in CT that will need to be augmented with artificial. She must hydrate well...with quality water. Small amounts of nutritionally dense seasonal food and obviously DHA....as much as possible and even an additional supplement could be useful if her current state warrants. Get electronics out of her hands....Lymies often resort to Internet when searching for answers and human connection when they are too debilitated to get it in real life....another reason they can't get well. Get her feet in the grass...wool socks OK if she can't tolerate cooler temps. Sleep/ wake cycle yoked with sunrise...drag her as out of bed and put bare eyes and bare skin in the sun. After dark no artificial light. Blueblockers if that is not possible.

I have been fully recovered from Lyme and Co infections for several years now. Dr. Kruse has opened my eyes to the why and how of it all when other docs could not. Your friend is very lucky to have you and is incredibly fortunate to have this information available...don't squander it or dismiss it because it is hard. I promise you being sick is harder than anything in these blogs.

### Penny — 2015-09-27T00:55:16+0300

My kid had 2 urease producers (h pylori type bugs) in his gut and when we went on a 12 national park-fest in 2009, he was asymptomatic - until we hit an RV park and plugged in - he was sleeping right above the generator in the motorhome - he immediately started to itch again after 12 days of nothing...

The UV light will kill Lyme and any other pathogen - you can shine it under the tongue/belly button -

My rabbit has even improved - he spends most of the day out in the sun and whereas before I would give him anything sweet, he would sneeze and get all snuffly, now, it doesn't faze him - he always slept in the sun 14 hours a day - but, he was indoors and I don't think he was getting enough -

I even have improvement just from being in the morning sun - fruit doesn't bother me - it is 90 degrees at the moment so that's bothering me, but truly, the sun is everything -

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-29T03:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-has-lyme-here-i-would-like-to-connect-you.16899/post-178094>

Ocular manifestations of Lyme disease have long been noted by me but not by Lyme docs. Ocular Lyme = low ocular melatonin = sleep disturbance = altered pineal melatonin = addiction tendency = alteration in VLPO neurons in hypothalamus that come from the central retinal pathways. This is why Lyme disease is often associated with sleep and addiction issues. The spirochete invades the eye early and remains dormant, accounting for both early and late ocular manifestations. It dramatically effects the RPE of the retina. The RPE of the retina needs UV light to work optimally. Lyme disease is destroyed by UV light. Do the mental gymnastics. Still think Lyme is the problem or a lack of UV light is the issue? A nonspecific follicular conjunctivitis occurs in approximately 10% of patients with early Lyme disease. Keratitis occurs often within a few months of onset of disease and is characterized by nummular nonstaining opacities. Inflammatory syndromes, such as vitritis and uveitis, have been reported; in some cases, a vitreous tap is required for diagnosis. Neuro-ophthalmic manifestations include neuroretinitis, involvement of multiple cranial nerves, optic atrophy, and disc edema. Seventh nerve paresis can lead to neurotrophic keratitis. In endemic areas where the quantum yield is low, Lyme disease may be responsible for approximately 25-50%% of new-onset Bell's palsy. I think that number is growing year by year. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9176782](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9176782)

Less UV exposure is required to kill bacteria in the human body than is necessary in the laboratory. When a small part of the infected bloodstream is exposed to UV light for less time than is required to kill bacteria in the laboratory, the pathogenic bacteria in the body are usually completely destroyed. In fact, many organisms are destroyed by an amount of irradiation that merely stimulates normal body cells.

Blood cells are much larger compared to bacteria and they are loaded with two UV absorbing proteins, namely hemoglobin and porphyrins. UV light stimulates RBC's to seek and destroy Lyme in the blood stream and in tissues with a large blood supply like the retina. They UV light stimulates the immune system and RBC's to destroy the spirochete while improving retinal function.

Full spectrum UV light exposure has the remarkable ability to allow the ill body to make rapid readjustments back to normal biologic and circadian functioning.

Also as mentioned in the Ubi 23-25 blogs, certain significant amounts of two photo-sensitive amino acids -- phenylalanine and tyrosine -- that are absent in eukaryotic cells are loaded in Lyme's spirochete. These amino acids absorb additional UV energy that kills the bacteria photonically.

There is also biophysical reasons to believe that by destroying the bacteria in the irradiated blood in the eye and skin is analogous to an "autogenous vaccine". It is produced on our surfaces where UV light is found and assimilated and can aid in rapid destruction of bloodstream bacteria in a way that is quantized by frequencies.

The first article that I know of on the benefit of artificial phototherapy was published in June 1934 by Hancock and Knott. By June 1942, 6,520 patients had been treated with UV therapy. The treatment was successful nearly every time. There was complete absence of any harmful effect according to the article.

The modern healthcare illusion is that we have “something” to lose by using UV light in our eye or skin when the reality is that when we bury the purple spectrum of the sun we get sick and die early.
Tissues with high energy demands, that are chronically light stressed suffer from a lower quantum yield. This is akin to placing a tarp over a tree to lower photosynthetic yields. This occurs in tissues such as the brain and the heart, contain a large number of mitochondria. They all rely on UV light exposure to drive RPE function of the eye clock and central retinal pathways that control all circadian regulators. These very active mitochondria-dense organs are more susceptible to reduction of the aerobic metabolism. Internal and external stressors, including cytokine up-regulation, pose a higher metabolic demand on the mitochondria to produce the necessary energy to maintain homeostasis. When the allostatic load becomes extreme due to extreme light stress, the energy demands increase exponentially, taxing the mitochondria and resulting in mitochondrial dysfunction, which creates inefficient energy production and leads to energy deficit. This in turn creates a disparity between very high metabolic energy demands in inflammatory states and the actual energy deficit resulting from mitochondrial dysfunction.

Keratitis occurs often within a few months of onset of disease and is characterized by nummular nonstaining opacities. Inflammatory syndromes, such as vitritis and uveitis, have been reported; in some cases, a vitreous tap is required for diagnosis. Neuro-ophthalmic manifestations include neuroretinitis, involvement of multiple cranial nerves, optic atrophy, and disc edema. Seventh nerve paresis can lead to neurotrophic keratitis. I have bartonella encephalopathy which i got from encephalitis so im in the lyme "community". Im also from CT. I recently moved myself out to the desert in california because its the only desert at a low enough altitude. Getting here was the best thing ive done for myself so far. I got rid of all my stuff and got some new basics that i needed, washed them thoroughly and headed southwest for better air quality. I went through northern florida and Louisiana to check lower latitudes but the heat, humidity and mold were killer. Felt worse than anywhere else in the country. You wont find any lyme patients doing well in the southeast, everyone migrates southwest (myself included) for the mold avoidance, better air quality and sun. Yes, UV kills mold but dead mold spores are still toxic- thats straight from dr shoemaker. I had a situation like this myself that took me finding that out to be able to improve. A dry, arid environment has far less mold from the little moisture and lots of sun. If i venture into a forest for even just a few minutes i already start to decline.

Im still pretty intolerant to the sun and very intolerant to cold (energy expenditure) but i get about an hour of sun in the early morning and a couple hours before sunset. I have to get on the abx soon but i wanted to have a good baseline of circadian rythems and ability to rest & detox in an area of good air quality. Diet has always been good.

I hope your friend is seeing some improvements, everyone has specific things they need but there also seems to be some stark similarities between almost all lyme patients. Hope this helps a little-

# Thread: Questions re: Mitigating wired PLC technology (TWACS)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-09-25T17:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/post-177868>

I recently learned my smart meter is a "pulsing" TWACS meter. The TWACS smart meters communicate with the utility by adding low frequency signals to the electrical lines. My utility company promotes such power line carrier (PLC) systems as a positive alternative to wireless smart meters.

The TWACS system creates powerful dirty electricity which is a constant presence, possibly 24/7.

-   **The dirty electricity turns all wires throughout the house into antennas.**
-   **Switching to an analog meter will not help much**
-   **The signals cannot be blocked or filtered (in the US)**

The issue with TWACS is referred to as "the antenna effect" which the utility company denies. The electrical wires inside a house and along the street were not designed to carry communication signals. When you put communication signals on them, it creates transients in the lines, effectively making the wires antenna. If wires on the poles are separated from each other, it enhances the antenna effect. The miles of electrical wires in a neighborhood can act as a very large antenna. It transforms all the wires in your home into antennas as well, so it no longer matters where you are in the house, you cannot reduce your exposure. Evil.

Here is an article describing all of this:
[http://www.eiwellspring.org/smartmeter/twacs.pdf](http://www.eiwellspring.org/smartmeter/twacs.pdf)

**LOTS OF QUESTIONS:** _Hopefully, someone out there has some experience they can share!_

1.  Has anyone out there who has this type of meter been able to measure the dirty electricity and mitigate it successfully? If so, what are the things you did?
2.  What EMF meter should I rent? I have heard the best is an oscilliscope with a filter to remove the 60Hz
3.  Does shutting off the breaker to the house at night mitigate things much?
4.  What is required to disconnect the house entirely at night?

### Scompy — 2015-09-25T23:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-mitigating-wired-plc-technology-twacs.16902/post-177891>

Wow, this is getting insideous now. That's gonna be the problem with this issue you brought up because it doesn't appear we have the correct types of meters to measure it at this time. Shutting off the breaker seems like a great idea. I'm wondering if using sound-EMFs could help determine intensity by the volume of the sound produced.

They do make products to reduce RF-EMF that you can plug into a wall here, but this sounds more like an electrical EMF issue rather than an RF one ([http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JM8POXE?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o03\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JM8POXE?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o03_s00))

# Thread: Nose running in the cold

### LisaLearning — 2015-09-26T22:49:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-running-in-the-cold.16904/post-177943>

I am I the only one whose nose runs in the cold? It doesn't run if I spot CT, but when I get in a cold enough bath for CT, it runs. It's getting to the point where the skin on my nose is getting slightly cracked from all the nose running due to CT.

Anyone know what causes this? I saw this otolaryngologist about something else back when I was at uni, and I mentioned it to her and she said something like it was akin to an allergic reaction, similar to people allergic to perfume, which sounded really odd. She gave me some ipratropium bromide nasal spray and that would keep me drip-free if I had to be out in the cold for a long time at an event or something, but is there anything else to be done to ameliorate this cold reaction?

### endless — 2015-10-15T03:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-running-in-the-cold.16904/post-179532>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-running-in-the-cold.16904/post-195691>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-31T18:53:08+0300

### LisaLearning — 2016-05-31T18:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nose-running-in-the-cold.16904/post-195696>

Hi Alla...wind makes my eyes water too (altho I don't have any pollen issues/hay fever), but I know this is a sign of dry eyes, the wind blows and my slightly dry eyes water, it's called epiphora.

Jack, if it's windy enough my eyes will water, but the cold majorly triggers the runny nose/eyes.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-31T19:21:23+0300

I'm posting this in the Leaky Gut area because I'm assuming it's a manifestation of leaky gut.. Keratosis Pilaris.

I've always had some form of it since I was a kid, but in the last few years it's gotten very bad on my upper arms and more acne like. I recently added desiccated liver pills in the hope that the increase in Vit A would help, but, while it initially decreased, it is now back with a vengeance. My dairy intake varies, and I can't tell if that's a catalyst, but it's typically limited to Kerrygold butter and heavy cream occasionally in coffee.

### Martin — 2015-09-27T16:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-177976>

Unless it's built to _exacting_ standards with NO way to allow ANY nEMF signals to enter, don't expand the time, money or energy trying. ### Scompy — 2015-09-27T22:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-177985>

However, if you can pinpoint a huge soure of RF-EMF outside the house, some of it can be mitigated/reduced down. For instance, I have a nearby house with WiFi that I can pinpoint, so blocking an inside wall of my house with silver-threaded curtains appears to back-reflect most of it in the sleeping area. Some people also use silver-threaded mosquito-like canopies over the bed while sleeping too like this image: [http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/1/1/36142-siam-bed-canopy-mosquito-net-ivory.html](http://www.bedbathandbeyond.com/1/1/36142-siam-bed-canopy-mosquito-net-ivory.html)

### Penny — 2015-09-28T04:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-tips-for-a-faraday-cage.16910/post-178005>

I prefer to sleep under the stars, lying just on a reindeer skin. The downside is that when I wake up in the middle of the night and open my eyes, the view is so stunningly beautiful that it's difficult to fall asleep again.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nf-kappa-beta-and-the-sun.16912/post-178011>

Hi,

I was designing some juice and soup recipes today. I own a health retreat in Thailand and thus am the head chef. I was looking at Indian herbs and spices to put into soups. I came across this blog when I looked up healing spices.

[http://www.anti-agingfirewalls.com/2009/06/14/spices-of-life/](http://www.anti-agingfirewalls.com/2009/06/14/spices-of-life/)

After looking at their reference to NF Kappa Beta, I thought I would search our blog here to see what Jack has said about it in respect to the latest blogs. I came up with this interesting tidbit that should stimulate some discussion. Can you name this blog?

"Most influenza outbreaks disproportionately kill juvenile, elderly or already weakened patients; in contrast the 1918 pandemic killed predominantly previously healthy young adults. Modern research, using virus’ taken from the bodies of frozen victims, has concluded that the virus kills through a cytokine storm (overreaction of the body’s immune system). The strong immune reactions of young adults ravaged the body, whereas the weaker immune systems of children and middle-aged adults resulted in fewer deaths among those groups. These cytokine storms are mediated via NF kappa beta and IL-6 levels in the brain and body. You learned about them in my [early leptin blogs](https://www.jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/). During the solar cycle 14 of 1918, there was a tremendous change in the Sun’s spots activity that dramatically altered the the Sun’s magnetic field, the solar wind, and the Earth’s magnetic field simultaneously. What was found when 1918 epidemiologic data was married with data NASA scientists found about the event later on, was that as the sun power decreased because less activity was seen those in **the coolest climate sustained the lowest death rates from influenza**. Those who lived in hotter climates died at much higher rate from the cytokine storm. This strongly pointed to a direct effect of the sun’s magnetic field in the pandemic. Physicists found the Earth had a weakened magnetic field in the solar maximum 14 as well. The pandemic of 1918 showed the direct alterations on human beings’ immune function worldwide. Why is this important for this blog? The exact same set of circumstance is now happening on the sun since 2009. In 2013, we should be at a solar maximum but the sun’s activity has been recently troubling NASA since March of 2013."

Mel

### Melanie Procter — 2015-09-28T06:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nf-kappa-beta-and-the-sun.16912/post-178012>

Actually, I feel the latest helps me to understand NF kappa beta more than anything else. Do you see the connection? [https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-25-uv-light-and-poop-plants/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-25-uv-light-and-poop-plants/)

Super-awesome-wonderful idea...! We need a Wikia of a running list of biohacks we're all doing, the reasons for doing them, and then list them in Alphabetical order or whatnot. Like you mentioned, coming up with broad-stroke categories is the first step and then being able to sort them would be amazing for anyone coming to the forums. This sort of stuff is second nature to me (my skill set at db work and in systems) to put something together actually if anyone needs help.

For me, I'm going to list a FEW of the biohacks I'm doing currently with the categories in-mind:

1\. Biohack DHA/Seafood (Oysters from PacNW, Shrimp from Florida, Salmon from Norway, grassfed foods/butter ~ 7x per week, staples in diet): For increase magnetism and mitrochondrial funneling of electrons for optimal energy use in body tissues
2\. Biohack Cysteine (Garlic, Asparagus, Bell Pepper, Egg, Chicken ~ staples in diet): Hardest to find amino acid in nature to help with energy management with Glutathione.
3\. Biohack Ketosis/Ketotic Diet/Epi-Paleo/Low-Carb (Eating around 40g-80g daily in the summer/fall, eating around 10g - 20g sugars in the winter) ATP impact, reduction of cytokines, etc.
4\. Biohack Gut flora boosting (Taking 'RestoreForLife' supplement which is touted to restore the gut biome) Widespread benefits
5\. Biohack Gaining Electrons (RO Water, Taking flights of stairs down holding metal-railing connected to ground, 30mins minimum Grounding/earthing, etc.) Replenishment of electrons to compensate for electrons suckage from environment
6\. Biohack nnEMF (Backreflecting RF-EMF with silver/copper threaded clothing & caps on brain, mitigating lots of tech, hardwiring connections in home instead of Wifi, etc.) Less overall suckage like the leech in the sky that it is
7\. Biohack Light (Morning light exposure, using certain types of indoor lights at various times, blue-blocking with amber glasses, F.lux, etc.) Controlling light for optimal use in the time of day

Etc., etc. This helps to identify the types of categoreis and what information could be sorted in a wiki.

### Scompy — 2015-10-26T22:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-protocols.16915/post-180511>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=180509)
>
> Thanks for sharing. I am a little apprehensive about putting anything on here since it seems everyone want to criticize what people are doing and being constantly told “your wrong” (quantum group). But everyone is an N=1.
>
> I will work on my light hack and maybe post it.

Yeah, sentiment understood. The way I view this topic is that, individually, we need specific tweaking. It's a conditional/variable issue, right? Since I live in Seattle vs. the next person that lives near the equator, the types of biohacks will be different. This may be another category altogether to lookup to see what higher frequency of biohack-categories we see people implementing in Los Angeles, CA vs. Seattle, WA. I mention these two places because I was under SEVERE nn-EMF in LA everywhere. In Seattle where I live right now, I've mitigated nearly all nn-EMF sources, but now I have a little access to morning light so I have to rely on DHA much more. And I've been trying natural UVA morning light with an IR machine as a possible hack. Maybe my hack is less optimal than just getting straight clouded-sunlight. It's why I posted my hack here.  In other words, these types of hacks are kind of begging the creation of a Wikia of us on the forum, and if we are willing, to post the biohacks we are doing for public response. We get some of them from the Forum Journals right now, but there's often no follow-up or specific, standardized data points. I've been reading a lot recently, and lotta good stuff. But it would also be cool if there was a go-to-place where we could post all of our current protocols, biohacks and changes we do that can be linked among all forum-users.

Then, a whole sweep of the forum-metadata could be collected to see what biohacks are the most common, what locations/environments people are living and working in, what benefits they are reporting, etc. as a go-to location for forum-users if they care about comparing what they are doing with other forum users. For practical use, any user could click on any keyword like "nn-EMF sources" and the website could show me all reported sources and how to biohack them. For instance, we might see 'Leaf Blower' and 'Airplane flight' on that list showing an intensity meter for using an active leaf-blower for an hour vs. flying on an airplane for an hour. Wouldn't you love to know that info all cataloged somewhere in an go-to-place about the data individuals have collected? Sure, some data is going to be off at times and may need to be corrected. But a forum-user could quickly find and compare all of the biohacks and protocols people are trying. You know, very easy-navigating Wikia-types of links and tables. Also, once there are enough Wikia-type pages established with a particular format, new forum-users will know what to mimic for their own biohacks.

Reporting biohacks in a central location will be risky for the individual because it's putting that person 'out-there' for embarrassment. (Personally I think my own health trumps any public embarassment if I'm doing something wrong). But that check-sum is what I'm looking for! If someone is thinking another person is biohacking something incorrectly or flat-out wrong, it could be identified quickly and other users could comment on the potential flaws of the biohack, make suggestions, etc. This is totally new territory for everyone--it's like diving into a swimming pool for the first time. For me personally, I love it when others critically analyze my biohacks/protocols and try to rip them a new outcome... 'cause it results in optimizing them even more. Ultimately, it's up to me to decide if I also alter that biohack or not. Confidence plays into this, huh?

### Scompy — 2015-10-27T00:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-protocols.16915/post-180519>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=180516)

> Excellent idea
> but
> I do not want to be embarrassed if someone find that I have made mistakes.
>
>

I'm not afraid of failure, at all. I used to be afraid of failure earlier in my life until I learned the power of failing. Now in my mid-40s, I view failure as the gateway to success. I've learned to 'suck it up' and move on when I fail. Also, I care less about what others think about my failures in the public eye. In my opinion, it is part of our humanity and why I give others the benefit of the doubt and actually admire when they try something to do the right thing. The analogy is like a parent looking at her child... like writing for the first time in preschool. We often accept the failures of children to be trivial and expected. We guide them to do right, to learn. Without failure, we wouldn't have much in the way of success. Instead, we would embrace our failures and live a lie. For this reason, I'm always willing to put myself on the line for public embarassment if it's an effort seeking truth. Truth trumps everything in my world, especially when I find I've failed at something because it usually reveals even more truth to me.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-27T14:42:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-protocols.16915/post-180550>

OK here is a gift from a future book:

Biohacking tip # 82: Do your biohacks in full spectrum light always. Man is attuned to natural sunlight via heliotropic accommodation. Doing hacks under artificial lights never yields the results in fake light conditions. This is often why we see men break bones easily when their muscles are fully hypertrophied by unmitigated blue frequencies that stimulate the pituitary while destroying the eye clock mechanism in the RPE. All artificial light has made man attuned to Hg resonance because of its strong blue signal. This standard was set by the CIE in 1931 because the eye camera has three cones in blue, green and red bands. Hg vapor fluorescent light have two light energy spikes in the blue (436nm), green (546nm) used to calculate the color temperature of light. The CIE added a red spike at 700 nm just as a convenience to cover the red cone found in the retina. These three bands became the ideal standard for an observer for manufactured light based upon the cones spectral responses in our eyes. In 1931, we had no idea about the opsin family of photoreceptors that are activated and regenerated by the RPE by dimming light in the 435-465 range. Today we know that artificial light causes the blue light hazard and destroys the eye clock, causes dry macular degeneration, and lowers ocular melatonin and decreases the pineal release of melatonin at night.

### Scompy — 2015-09-30T04:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-forum-category-for-things-in-the-news.16916/post-178214>

Not sure if this idea has been posted in the past, so posting it here. Thinking it would be nice to have a "go-to" place for posting stuff we see in the news. There's so much of a flood of information that relates to us, I've found posts from others here on the forum incredibly useful already.

For instance today, I saw that this Kickstarter just got funded: RF-EMF headphones. No one on the kickstarter site is aware of the risks plugged into the brain or even questions why headphones are usually hardwired. I have the same concerns if any company makes wireless gaming VR tech in the near future also, as it would also be on the brains of our mylennating youth.

[https://www.kickstarter.com/project...ireless-earbuds-you-charge-w?ref=fundedtoday#](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/pugz/pugz-worlds-smallest-wireless-earbuds-you-charge-w?ref=fundedtoday#)
