# Thread: Joseph's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josephs-journal.16917/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph Thia — 2015-09-30T12:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josephs-journal.16917/post-178234>

Hi!

I am 36 years old, Male, living in Singapore. My height is 1.73m and I weigh about 81kg at around 20% body fat (I weighed over 90kg a year ago).

The following are the blood tests results that I received yesterday:
High sensitive CRP: 0.3 mg/L
25-Hydroxy Vitamin D: 31.8 ug/L
DHEA-S: 5.52 umol/L (equivalent to 203.39 μg/dL)
Cortisol: 211 nmol/L (equivalent to 7.64 μg/dL) (blood sample taken at 9am)
Progesterone: 0.9 nmol/L (equivalent to 28.30 ng/dL)
Estradiol: <160 pmol/L (checking with the lab if they have the exact figures)

Sunlight: I am indoors for at least 8 hours a day during the weekdays. The low Vitamin D result means that I need to get creative on fitting my schedule around getting morning sunlight. I am currently using Reptile UVB 150 and a 8W sankyo-denki blacklight tube ([http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2\_08.html](http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2_08.html)) as a temporary fix.

Bluelight: I use blue blockers in office as I face the monitor for prolonged hours. I am currently trying to reduce my usage of electronics e.g. iPhone, iPads during the night (tough but I know it is necessary).

Sleep: I sleep at around 12 midnight after settling my kids and household chores (if I am not tired by then). This is a big No-No and I am definitely working on this.

nnEMF exposure: The greatest source of nnEMF exposure for me (based on the trimeter) is at my home's kitchen sink (the high tension power cables are located behind the wall). The other major source of nnEMF is at my office desk where I have taken note of the "danger zone".

CT: I use the Cool Fat Burner at the office about two to three times a week.

Health history: I suffered from asthma from 5 years old to around 19 years old. I have weak bowels (seems to have improved since starting on the Epi-Paleo diet) and I sneeze a lot (especially if there are dust particles in the air). I feel tired easily (seems to have improved recently). My dad suffers from high blood pressure, cataracts and recently had a pancreatic tumor removed while my sister suffers from hypothyroidism.

That's all for now!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-30T14:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josephs-journal.16917/post-178249>

Singapore is a massive quantum yield issue. This months webinar explains this in detail.
Smog = air pollution = lowered quantum yield = lowered solar power in all ranges = lowered UV exposure = Earth does not have its full spectrum of light = Tarp over a tree = trees dies no matter if it is watered and has access to CO2 and minerals = your modern world modeled in Singapore. It has both serious smog and terrible light stress........meaning life is always pseudohypoxic there and always has a low NAD+ level

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-30T14:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/josephs-journal.16917/post-178252>

_**The beliefs of modern man are the batteries that make them fuel for the matrix in which they live.**_
Do you look "at science" or do you look thru science through a lens? I submit that that most of us are prisoners to modern science perspectives because we see it via "their lenses".

The modern illusion is that we have “something” to lose by using UV light in our eye or skin when we exercise when the reality is that when we bury the purple spectrum of the sun we get sick and die early even when our bodies facade resembles something that looks amazingly well. LOOKS ARE DECEIVING in cities where people have brains and bodies built by singlet oxygen and not triplet oxygen.
Tissues with high energy demands, like brain, heart, and muscles, that are chronically light stressed suffer from a lower quantum yield. This is akin to placing a tarp over a tree to lower photosynthetic yields. This occurs in tissues such as the brain and the heart, contain a large number of mitochondria. They all rely on UV light exposure to drive RPE function of the eye clock and central retinal pathways that control all circadian regulators.
Light, **specifically purple light,** has a massive effect on blood flow within the eye. Flickering light tends to increase the metabolism of the inner central retina, while constant light reduces the metabolism in the outer retina.
These very active mitochondria-dense organs are more susceptible to reduction of the aerobic metabolism. Internal and external stressors, including cytokine up-regulation, pose a higher metabolic demand on the mitochondria to produce the necessary energy to maintain homeostasis. When the allostatic load becomes extreme due to extreme light stress, the energy demands increase exponentially, taxing the mitochondria and resulting in mitochondrial dysfunction, which creates inefficient energy production and leads to energy deficit. This in turn creates a disparity between very high metabolic energy demands in inflammatory states and the actual energy deficit resulting from mitochondrial dysfunction.

# Thread: Sleep reversed / Farady cage bed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-reversed-farady-cage-bed.16919/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2015-09-30T17:35:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-reversed-farady-cage-bed.16919/post-178278>

I've experimented with how I lie in beds.
Most people sleep with their bed touching a wall.
In that wall there's usually some version of low frequency nnemf.
My advice, is put your pillow on the other side of the bed. (opposite to the wall)
Sleep with your feet pointing at the wall.
Simple advice, but works as I constistently sleep better when I do that and there's electricity running..

\----

So somehow I've got the idea a building a farady cage around my bed. It's one of the simplest hacks to do.
I can't believe it's not something that's discussed much.
Costs less than 100$ and few hours of time, and pretty much destroys rf-emf.
What are the pros and cons, yay or nay?

### thomas — 2015-09-30T17:40:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-reversed-farady-cage-bed.16919/post-178279>

Seems there was a thread not long ago about it.. oops.

### Penny — 2015-10-01T23:38:03+0300

# Thread: Medicinal Mushroom Love
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medicinal-mushroom-love.16920/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-09-30T18:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/medicinal-mushroom-love.16920/post-178280>

I've been a fan of medicinal mushrooms for quite some time because of their profound affects on my body and mind. My favorite is probably Chaga, "The king of medicinal mushrooms". It has an incredible history of medicinal use, insanely high ORAC score (36567 per 1g), insanely high SOD enzyme (35000 per 1g), numerous studies showing efficacy against cancer, a wide range of phytonutrients and a delicious woody vanilla flavor that lends well to many uses.

What's unique about Chaga is that it is a perennial mushroom, meaning it stays on the tree all year round for several years before it is mature. I've seen theories about how this allows it to produce nanonutrients, gradually breaking nutrients down to smaller and smaller molecular weights allowing for higher permeability into cells. Who knows if this is true or relevant, but I think it's an interesting concept.

I had a very powerful experience with Chaga one time. After not using it for a few months, I made a strong batch of tea and drank a good amount of it. What happened was an almost immediate upgrade of my energy, I felt like I could effortlessly focus for long periods of time with clarity that I didn't even know I was missing. The boost wasn't a stimulating rush of something like caffeine, but an elevation of my consciousness. Along with this I had a greater sense of clarity and purpose in life and realignment with my goals and dreams. Ever since then I've made it a regular part of my diet.

Once we've gotten our DHA and light cycles managed, I think these medicinal mushrooms and other herbs are very interesting substances to consider adding to the repertoire. I think things like curcumin or gynostemma are just a more well-studied poster child for some of the potential medicines in the plant, fungal, insect (many ants are highly medicinal) and animal (i.e. antler velvet) kingdoms I was wondering if anyone else has gotten into herbalism or medicinal mushroom use?

[http://www.eartherbs.com/index.php?page=83](http://www.eartherbs.com/index.php?page=83)

Have you ever considered creating online courses or certification program? Have you ever considered an apprentice? I have a mild science background (studied materials engineering for 3 years before finishing with a psych degree, worked in thin film multiferroic and magnetic materials labs) and a good intuition for math and science. I have mixed feelings about institutional education, but have been considering getting a master's in quantum physics. Part of me feels like it would be a waste of time and money though.

### Joe Gavin — 2015-10-01T15:48:41+0300

Start with these, the blogs, and if you really want the education, become a paying member and listen to all the webinars and Q/A sessions available.

And you'll know more than most doctors.

Just by reading this very very cool shit.

### NadoRo — 2015-10-01T03:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moms-best-time-for-ct.16922/post-178328>

I'm just getting into CT after being on the Leptin Rx for 7 weeks.. This is a timing question: To the moms of younger children out there, how/when do you do it?? I have a tough enough time in the AM just knocking out the BAB and light exposure, there is no way I could fit in a CT bath too and get everyone fed, dressed, and dropped at school in time. At night, it's a challenge too with activities, dinner, homework.

My best (read, uninterrupted) times are after they go to bed..8:30 pm or after lunch during the school day...but I understand that this is not optimal. Please share your best practices.. thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-01T04:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moms-best-time-for-ct.16922/post-178331>

Best time = any time

### Penny — 2015-10-01T23:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moms-best-time-for-ct.16922/post-178407>

Cold showers - you have to do it anyway Then keep a bowl of ice water around for a face dunk or 2 - take your kids to a natural cold body of water and jump in with them

# Thread: Hello from Baltimore
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-baltimore.16926/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-02T03:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-baltimore.16926/post-178411>

\*\*\*After typing this, I realize this post may be more appropriate in the optimal journal section. If so, can an admin please move?

Hi, my name is Brad

I hail from Columbia, MD which is a suburb of Baltimore. Been following Jack since early 2013 but have been exploring all avenues of holistic health pretty intensely for the past several years.

Food is my passion. I worked as a chef in CO and have been feeding myself high quality foods ever since a transformative semester abroad in Costa Rica in fall of 2012. I started off paleo/bulletproof and eventually got into epi-paleo soon after. I love to experiment with everything from fermentation to sprouting to infusions and all sorts of my own processing techniques. I've also dabbled with foraging and wild foods and hope to pursue that as well as my growing my own foods over the next few years.

For the last year and a half or so, I've spent time living in CO, CA and China. This period of travel was a bit of a personal spiritual journey of trying to narrow my focus, connect to higher powers within and find what's really important.

Interestingly I found that my body moved away from animal proteins for about a six month period. I just gradually lost the desire to eat meat. For the first half of this period I continued to eat seafood, mostly raw salmon, but eventually even that didn't feel right. Then I was subsisting mostly on raw grass-fed, meditative goat milk (the goats lived in a yoga village and were actively loved every day), kefir products, eggs, fats, herbs, superfoods and vegetables as well as some grains.

The last two months of this period I had to let go of control of my food because I was participating in a work study program at a Qi Gong school in China. While the diet was horrible - mostly rice and vegetables cooked in peanut oil - the program was great for my awareness and control of qi. At least two hours of seated meditation every single day and two hours of various forms of qi gong on weekdays.

I also learned how to tune in various parts of my body and detect imbalances and address posture issues. My hips are tight and though I don't have pain in the lower back, that appears to be the weakest part of my body, though I'm still relatively fit and healthy by everyone else's standards.

I was fortunate to have the opportunity to meet with a Taoist Priest who did my Bazi (Taoist astrology) for me. He told me that I'm a golden mountain (strong in metal and wood), but I'm missing earth. Which means that I have trouble with keeping my energy down and grounded. Stomach and gall bladder are the organs associated with earth and he said that when I have health issues, it will likely be in these areas. He said I have the eat god in my stars which means I will always have good food in my life, an observation I had made myself several months before. Yellow is the color associated with earth and by having more yellow in my life I can help balance myself (I have since started wearing more yellow clothing and found that I do feel really good about it).

Unfortunately this wonderful spiritual awakening was paired with a work period of at least 4 hours a day inside staring at a computer screen in the EMF hell hole of a city where the school was located.

I ended up getting pretty severe food poisoning due to questionable kitchen cleanliness and my already comprimised gut function from a lifetime of overeating and the recent excessive carbohydrates. My gut was trashed pretty bad by the end of my trip.

When I got back to the states, in MD where I am now, I started eating meat again and got back into the epi paleo groove. I am also inspired a lot by the GAP diets as I'm focused on gut health currently. The food part is okay but I cant seem to catch a break with my light cycles.

I currently work in the evenings (until midnight) tutoring math online. So my job is staring into a blue screen for 7 hours straight 5 nights a week. I have longer term plans to address this by starting my own consulting business and I've already requested to shift my hours toward the daytime, but I will likely have to do this for at least the next couple months.

To mitigate some of the damage, I keep ice packs around and try to keep myself cool as much as I can. I've been wearing my orange goggles every night that I've had any artificial light for the past 2 years. I like to walk barefoot outside for a few minutes after my shift and I sleep with a homemade grounding mat. I know Jack's not a fan but I've found when the connections are secure and its plugged deep into moist earth outside that it really makes a noticeable difference. Will likely have enough saved up for a magnetico in a month or two.

The benefit of working in the evening is it frees up my time during the day and I am pretty good about getting outside barefoot and shirtless, sometimes naked in the morning sun, though I don't usually wake up till 8 or 80\. Here's a list of things I do during the day in the name of health:

Ice baths (3-5 x week)
IR sauna (4-7 x week)
Daily pranayama (breath work)
Qi gong/tai chi (2-4 x week)
Yoga (2-4 x week)
Seated stillness meditation (3-4 x week)
Stretching/mobility work (3-5 x week)
Long walks/jogs in nature (for jogs, I'm working more on efficiency and technique. Inspired by Danny Dreyer's ChiRunning system)
High-intensity Strength training (When I feel like it)
Sprints (1-2 x week)
Warm epsom salt baths (2-3 x week)
Reading JK and other health blogs, listening to podcasts (every day)

Some detox protocols I'm playing with:
1000mg niacin + 30 min exercise then IR sauna followed by CT
Saline enemas
Salt flushes
Abdominal massage
Xeolite clay
Activated charcoal
Microalgaes (spirulina and chlorella)
Apple cider vinegar
MSM
Dry skin brushing
EFT
Herbal teas and elixirs
Coconut oil

I'm really just experimenting with everything I can right now. I hope to teach this stuff one day so I want to be able to speak from experience about as much as I can when I do.

I guess I made this thread to introduce myself and give you a snapshot of where I am. I'm sick of hearing everyone in the health community say the same things that don't really ring true for me. When I listen to and read Jack's stuff, I hear that truth bell ring louder and I like the bottom up approach to understanding, as having worked in multiple scientific labs, I can see many ways studies can be flawed or corrupted, even by well-intentioned scientists.

I have a lot of respect for Jack's integrity, sincerity and conviction that he brings to everything he does. And I feel I have a lot to offer those who are ready to listen, especially on the mental-psycho-spiritual side of things. I'm happy to be part of a community to hear what other people are doing, as there are exactly 0 people on the same page as me where I am.

If you read all that (or even if you didn't), let me express my gratitude and humility in being here. Any comments, questions, critiques or questions are welcome and encouraged!

p.s. I recently started a baby website. You can check it out at [www.simplitarian.com](http://www.simplitarian.com)

### fitness@home — 2015-10-02T16:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-baltimore.16926/post-178435>

Welcome Brad!

Why don't you copy/paste your post and start a new thread in the Journal section?

### Chris Day — 2015-10-15T16:59:40+0300

Woah. I'm brand new here and you're way more advanced than me. I'm glad that you have so much self-awareness and knowledge to bring here. Reading this got me excited about this forum. Cheers.

### RobH — 2015-11-09T17:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-baltimore.16926/post-181498>

Finding that even late evenings suck more now that I've got my circadian rhythms better in sync. I used to be able to work any / all shifts and feel equally bad all the time, not realizing I could feel so good. Now I find when I work work late at night, I know I feel terrible -- way more in tune with my circadian biology than I ever was.

# Thread: NOLA EVENT THIS WEEKEND
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nola-event-this-weekend.16927/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-02T03:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nola-event-this-weekend.16927/post-178412>

This weekend the Cirque de Cuisine is coming to my house on Bourbon Street. You can buy the tickets at Tableau for the event. The chef and owner of Purloo will be cooking and serving at my place. Purloo's 20-seat exhibition kitchen and 50 seat restaurant gives diners an in-depth look and taste of Southern regional fare. Helmed by chef/owner Ryan Hughes, who has earned critical acclaim for his Southern cooking with French savvy at some of New Orleans’ most acclaimed restaurants including Brennan’s, Emeril’s Delmonico’s, Café Degas and more. Purloo serves as a place to both eat and learn. [http://www.nolapurloo.com](http://www.nolapurloo.com) SoFAB Presents Cirque de Cuisine: A Kitchen Tour

(New Orleans, LA) The Southern Food and Beverage Museum is happy to announce that the Cirque de Cuisine: A Kitchen Tour, will be taking place on Sunday, October 4, 2015 from Noon to 4:00PM.

Tour guests will enjoy a unique opportunity to view ten historic and beautiful private kitchens located throughout the French Quarter.

Kitchens on the tour range from Pirates Alley, Barracks Street, Bourbon Street and St. Philip Street, Governor Nicholls Street to Esplanade Avenue, and we invite you to stroll at your leisure.

Small tastes of delicious dishes will be provided in each home by restaurants such as Compère Lapin, Shaya, Cane & Table, Johnny Sanchez, eat, Grand Isle, Salon by Sucré, Primitivo and more. Brady's Wine Warehouse, Sonoma-Cutrer, Bin 428, Glazer's and others will serve wines and other beverages to complement the dishes. Guests are invited to browse Lucullus Culinary Antiques on Chartres Street where Patrick's Bar Vin will pour a complimentary sparkling wine.

The Tour begins at Tableau restaurant, 616 St. Peter Street where you may obtain your wristband and map and enjoy a special Cirque de Cuisine libation and a tasty bite. Tickets are $50 at the door, $45 in advance and $35 for SoFAB Members, and can be purchased at [http://sofabinstitute.org/events/cirque-de-cuisine](http://sofabinstitute.org/events/cirque-de-cuisine).

### Øyvind — 2015-10-02T15:29:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tktl1-og-apo-10-test.16928/post-178429>

I did an APO10 and TKTL1 test, to see if my ketogenic diet with alot of sun could decrease the TKTL1 in my brain tumour (astrocytoma).

I had very high TKTL1, that shows a resistance to apoptotic therapies but there could be also interfering factors like inflammation. So although I am doing this the very strict ketogenic diet there still seems to be a strong elevation in those 2 markers, so I think maybe I cannot really
influence the sugar dependence with my diet. It would make sense to have a control of this test done in about 6 months with my actual anticancer therapy to see if markers come down.

Anyone here knows stuff about TKTL1 and APO10? What kind of markers are this? Studies have shown that if they supress TKTL1 cancer cells grows 50% less or more..

What do those lab tests means? Is there anything to use here?

### lilly antic — 2015-10-03T00:10:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tktl1-og-apo-10-test.16928/post-178460>

I am familiar with both tests .
the Apo10 test is a score for proliferative anomalies, where the APO10 antigen is measured in macrophages. It shows deficiencies in apoptose, which occurs in tumors, but also in cysts and polyps, so not necessarily malignancies.
the TKTL-1 is measuring the activity of an enzyme and corresponding protein, which directs the metabolism away from oxidative phosphorylation in mitochondria to anaerobic metabolism from pyruvate to lactate, which is a strong indicator of cancer cells and Warburg mechanism. It also stands for the therapy resistance of the cancer cells in question.
Johannes Coy, the "inventor", propagates a ketogenic diet for good results.
I have seen this test positive in both markers when used as a screening tool, with the same levels as you have. And no detectable cancers!
When the affected persons changed to a ketogenic diet, there was no big change!
**Which again shows, it's not about food!!!**
Ketosis is a tool, not the manna.
Only positive in Apo10 indicates in most cases a cyst or similar.

Innovate new solutions and your life will fundamentally change.

The laws of physics dictate who we are.

Hexaganol shapes carry more light energy.

Ambivalence is due to low dopamine.

What we currently believe are actually opinions.

When you lose energy/light to the environment, the bigger you get (mass equivalence). \[too many protons, not enough electrons\]

Any water is better than no water.

Blood is 93% water; Water is the battery of life.

Hexagonal shapes carry more light energy.

Poryphrins are hexagonal shapes.

Sun is a cathode, the earth is the anode, our feet assess the current created.

Eat like a great white shark, live like a polar bear.

DHA takes sunlight and turns it into a DC current for energy.

Tissue levels of DHA correlate with health status.

Read Tensgrity 5.

Read Martin Chapman and water.

Eat seasonally.

NOT EVERYONE CAN BE SAVED. IF YOU CAN'T TURN OFF THE TECH, IT JUST AIN'T GOING TO WORK.

### John Smith — 2015-10-03T07:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178479>

hello, I am currently 20, male, 132 lbs, 6"2 and I am underweight. I am following the Leptin Rx at the moment.

My concerns are:
Should I be lifting so that I can gain muscle and not a lot of fat? Am I right in saying that I have to wait until I am leptin sensitive to start lifting to make more muscle gains?

How should I be eating if I am going into winter? Should I be decreasing carbs and increasing fats now? So in winter I have to eat a strict ketogenic diet? I will not be able to gain weight then?

I have been eating a high fat, moderate protein, low carb epi paleo diet since starting the protocol.
I would say that I am sill in an EMF and blue light toxic environment. I am slowly reducing this.

### Robert Haas — 2015-10-03T14:51:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178487>

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-03T16:57:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178500>

### John Smith — 2015-10-04T02:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178552>

### Jonathin — 2015-10-04T21:36:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178610>

John, I am somewhat similar as you regarding weight. It is not about eating per se. I would evaluate your daily and seasonal behaviors/environment to understand why you have the physique that you have. You are not that old so perhaps you can make changes fairly easily if you recognize some causes and effects. You have to put everything on the table and not think reductionistically. Is lack of muscle mass the only thing going on? Maybe not.

### Ian — 2015-10-05T00:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178617>

Hi John, are you genuinely underweight or just not a tubby like seems to be the "norm" now? You look pretty healthy in your photo. I'm always impressed how in amateur hill climbing in cycle races (here in the Alps) the winners are often incredibly skinny - for example their lycra racing shorts can look like baggy pants. Those guys are phenomenal climbers and exceptionally fit and fast - so as long as they don't have an unusual desire to emulate Arnold Schwarzenegger (heart valve surgery) they are doing fine!

I don't think that Leptin resistance leads to people being underweight - I think it makes them obese.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-05T04:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/underweight-and-want-to-gain-muscle-mass.16933/post-178628>

LR does make them anorexic............or obese. Underweight is a different issue.

# Thread: How to intelligently increase my times in cold baths/ how long should I be working up to?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### E_than — 2015-10-03T18:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-178538>

All,

I have been doing cold baths with temps ranging from 50-55 degrees. I started at 5 minutes and am up to 15 minutes. I always eat a huge fatty meal beforehand and do them when my wife is home so she can check on me.

Naturally I would like to avoid hypothermia/injury. I am curious about how others progressed their times. How long should I work up to?

Thanks in advance for any insights others may share.

Ethan

### caroline — 2015-10-06T02:46:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-178689>

Hi Ethan ....

There is a huge C/T thread here somewhere - and please re read the C/T protocol again.

Stay in as long as you can - monitor how you are feeling - use your intuition.

Sometimes we can stay in for longer than other times - do what feels right.

Do you drink lots of cold water before and after?

Did you start the protocol as written?

### CTforlife — 2015-11-18T05:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182250>

Until you think you are super human.

### seanb4 — 2015-11-18T15:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182260>

I'd say the most important thing is to stay disciplined with it and adaption will come with time, even if it's really slowly. If you stay at 15 mins for the next 2 months that's okay, as long as you keep doing it.

I personally think how fast you adapt depends on a lot of factors but a big one is how healthy you are to begin with. For me I am not healthy so it is taking much longer than others have reported however I am seeing benefits, use these to motivate you to stay disciplined.

For time reference I started pretty much daily CTing march this year and am now up to a point were shivering is minimal and it has stopped feeling like torture, however I am still far from being able to read a book whilst CTing.

Things that helped me: Making sure you eat enough the day before. A big one is also recently I have been sitting under a full spectrum reptile light in daytime whenever possible. This heats up my body thanks to IR. I then think (though I'm not sure) my body saves some of this IR and uses it when I CT, that or the full spectrum light is increasing my overall energy / hormones making CT easier. Either way this has had a big effect recently on making CT easier.

### caroline — 2015-11-19T03:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182302>

Sean - do you eat something high fat before C/T?

### seanb4 — 2015-11-19T09:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-intelligently-increase-my-times-in-cold-baths-how-long-should-i-be-working-up-to.16934/post-182314>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=182302)
>
> Sean - do you eat something high fat before C/T?

Not particularly, I like to eat fish meals before CT, usually boil in the bag kippers + can of mackerel / mussels.

If I eat high fat, when I lay in the tub I get reflux making the whole experience much more unpleasant...

# Thread: Feeling the way to optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-04T17:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-178590>

Hi, my name is Brad

I hail from Columbia, MD which is a suburb of Baltimore. Been following Jack since early 2013 but have been exploring all avenues of holistic health pretty intensely for the past several years.

Food is my passion. I worked as a chef in CO and have been feeding myself high quality foods ever since a transformative semester abroad in Costa Rica in fall of 2012. I started off paleo/bulletproof and eventually got into epi-paleo soon after. I love to experiment with everything from fermentation to sprouting to infusions and all sorts of my own processing techniques. I've also dabbled with foraging and wild foods and hope to pursue that as well as my growing my own foods over the next few years.

For the last year and a half or so, I've spent time living in CO, CA and China. This period of travel was a bit of a personal spiritual journey of trying to narrow my focus, connect to higher powers within and find what's really important.

Interestingly I found that my body moved away from animal proteins for about a six month period. I just gradually lost the desire to eat meat. For the first half of this period I continued to eat seafood, mostly raw salmon, but eventually even that didn't feel right. Then I was subsisting mostly on raw grass-fed, meditative goat milk (the goats lived in a yoga village and were actively loved every day), kefir products, eggs, fats, herbs, superfoods and vegetables as well as some grains.

The last two months of this period I had to let go of control of my food because I was participating in a work study program at a Qi Gong school in China. While the diet was horrible - mostly rice and vegetables cooked in peanut oil - the program was great for my awareness and control of qi. At least two hours of seated meditation every single day and two hours of various forms of qi gong on weekdays.

I also learned how to tune in various parts of my body and detect imbalances and address posture issues. My hips are tight and though I don't have pain in the lower back, that appears to be the weakest part of my body, though I'm still relatively fit and healthy by everyone else's standards.

I was fortunate to have the opportunity to meet with a Taoist Priest who did my Bazi (Taoist astrology) for me. He told me that I'm a golden mountain (strong in metal and wood), but I'm missing earth. Which means that I have trouble with keeping my energy down and grounded. Stomach and gall bladder are the organs associated with earth and he said that when I have health issues, it will likely be in these areas. He said I have the eat god in my stars which means I will always have good food in my life, an observation I had made myself several months before. Yellow is the color associated with earth and by having more yellow in my life I can help balance myself (I have since started wearing more yellow clothing and found that I do feel really good about it).

Unfortunately this wonderful spiritual awakening was paired with a work period of at least 4 hours a day inside staring at a computer screen in the EMF hell hole of a city where the school was located.

I ended up getting pretty severe food poisoning due to questionable kitchen cleanliness and my already comprimised gut function from a lifetime of overeating and the recent excessive carbohydrates. My gut was trashed pretty bad by the end of my trip.

When I got back to the states, in MD where I am now, I started eating meat again and got back into the epi paleo groove. I am also inspired a lot by the GAP diets as I'm focused on gut health currently. The food part is okay but I cant seem to catch a break with my light cycles.

I currently work in the evenings (until midnight) tutoring math online. So my job is staring into a blue screen for 7 hours straight 5 nights a week. I have longer term plans to address this by starting my own consulting business and I've already requested to shift my hours toward the daytime, but I will likely have to do this for at least the next couple months.

To mitigate some of the damage, I keep ice packs around and try to keep myself cool as much as I can. I've been wearing my orange goggles every night that I've had any artificial light for the past 2 years. I like to walk barefoot outside for a few minutes after my shift and I sleep with a homemade grounding mat. I know Jack's not a fan but I've found when the connections are secure and its plugged deep into moist earth outside that it really makes a noticeable difference. Will likely have enough saved up for a magnetico in a month or two.

The benefit of working in the evening is it frees up my time during the day and I am pretty good about getting outside barefoot and shirtless, sometimes naked in the morning sun, though I don't usually wake up till 8 or 8:30. Here's a list of things I do during the day in the name of health:

Ice baths (3-5 x week)
IR sauna (4-7 x week)
Daily pranayama (breath work)
Qi gong/tai chi (2-4 x week)
Yoga (2-4 x week)
Seated stillness meditation (3-4 x week)
Stretching/mobility work (3-5 x week)
Long walks/jogs in nature (for jogs, I'm working more on efficiency and technique. Inspired by Danny Dreyer's ChiRunning system)
High-intensity Strength training (When I feel like it)
Sprints (1-2 x week)
Warm epsom salt baths (2-3 x week)
Reading JK and other health blogs, listening to podcasts (every day)

Some detox protocols I'm playing with:
1000mg niacin + 30 min exercise then IR sauna followed by CT
Saline enemas
Salt flushes
Abdominal massage
Xeolite clay
Activated charcoal
Microalgaes (spirulina and chlorella)
Apple cider vinegar
MSM
Dry skin brushing
EFT
Herbal teas and elixirs
Coconut oil

I'm really just experimenting with everything I can right now. I hope to teach this stuff one day so I want to be able to speak from experience about as much as I can when I do.

I guess I made this thread to introduce myself and give you a snapshot of where I am. I'm sick of hearing everyone in the health community say the same things that don't really ring true for me. When I listen to and read Jack's stuff, I hear that truth bell ring louder and I like the bottom up approach to understanding, as having worked in multiple scientific labs, I can see many ways studies can be flawed or corrupted, even by well-intentioned scientists.

I have a lot of respect for Jack's integrity, sincerity and conviction that he brings to everything he does. And I feel I have a lot to offer those who are ready to listen, especially on the mental-psycho-spiritual side of things. I'm happy to be part of a community to hear what other people are doing, as there are exactly 0 people on the same page as me where I am.

If you read all that (or even if you didn't), let me express my gratitude and humility in being here. Any comments, questions, critiques or questions are welcome and encouraged!

p.s. I recently started a baby website. You can check it out at [www.simplitarian.com](http://www.simplitarian.com/)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-04T17:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-178598>

welcome...........Read books and unlearn to relearn. Begin.

### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-11T15:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179061>

One thing I forgot to mention above that is important is that I went through a course of Accutane when I was a teenager. As you can imagine I was super happy to see the lawyer commercials a few years later that said they could get me money for my IBS or death...

I was wondering what kinds of things besides AM UV light I can do to help speed a recovery. I don't know much about the mechanisms of Accutane aside from the fact that it fucks up Vitamin A cycling.

As we approach winter I've been shifting focus to grounding and CT for my electrons and I think I will start planning my trip next summer to a beach somewhere closer to the equator where I can just recharge my cells.

Any other advice is greatly appreciated!

### nicld — 2015-10-12T17:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179228>

Welcome Brad.

Where is Costa Rica were you? We visited there in April and absolutely LOVED the LaFortuna area. We felt the most as peace there and would go back and visit or move there. The volcano was the best part of our trip.

### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-13T01:26:58+0300

I lived on Puntarenas on the pacific side, but I traveled all around. Never made it to LaFortuna though. Costa Rica is definitely a magical place, I wouldn't mind spending a big chunk of my time there. The volcanic soil would be a great place for a quantum farm...

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-13T03:15:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179260>

You have a very interesting background and a pretty awesome weekly protocol. Looking forward to seeing how it all falls into place.

### nicld — 2015-10-13T16:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179339>

> [BradTheBreather said:](/goto/post?id=179256)
>
> I lived on Puntarenas on the pacific side, but I traveled all around. Never made it to LaFortuna though. Costa Rica is definitely a magical place, I wouldn't mind spending a big chunk of my time there. The volcanic soil would be a great place for a quantum farm...

We flew into San Jose then traveled over to the Caribbean side to Torteguero (nice place). The only way to get to our "hotel" was by boat and got to walk in the black sand of the Pacific. We then drove to LaFortuna for a couple of days then drove over to the Pacific side by Brasilitto. They hubby and I felt the best by the volcano. We had friends that lived there for a year and loved it. I would prefer to live by the volcano.

### alyson thiessen — 2015-10-13T16:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179359>

Welcome! I look forward to hearing about your journey....

### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-14T02:44:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-179413>

So I managed to get my hours switched to be more during daylight hours. It is still a lot of time on the computer, but I'm able to do my work sitting and moving around directly on the grass in full sun most of the time, and am loving my CT.

I've started weightlifting consistently and I think that combined with all the sunlight and grounding I've been doing is really great for my hormones. I've had multiple women give me compliments after I'm nice and sweaty and pheromones are in the air.

I just ordered some nicotine gum and a reptile bulb so I look forward to using those and seeing how that goes. I ate considerably less food and because of that drank much more of my hand collected spring water today after a couple days of consistent grounding and sun, so I'd like to keep that up and just focus on oysters and other seafood as well as some fats.

I've been integrating my pranayama (breath practice) into my day rather than setting aside time to do it separately. What I've noticed is that my lungs feel considerably cleaner today, probably after spending so much time outside. A very real feeling for just how stale, stagnant and polluted the air inside my house is. I'd like to open all the windows all the time, but everyone I live with probably wouldn't go for that. Good to know that the lungs gunk clears up so quickly though!

### BradTheBreather — 2015-12-16T20:59:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-184307>

Just an update after a long time away. I tend to get wrapped up and sucked into what I'm researching and can easily lose touch with my own sense of what's right. Though JK knows a lot about biochemistry and doctoring people up, he can't know what is best for me. So I'm focusing more on listening and processing the information here, then feeling how my own intuition wants to direct me. And looking to my own past for what I need to do.

For example: when I was 16, I went through a course of Accutane, which completely trashed my body in many ways and though my attention to clean living for the past few years has done a lot to mitigate some of that and begin to heal, but I never did any hardcore research into that issue specifically. I recently have started to dig a bit deeper into this and one thing I found that has been true for me is that gallbladder function and therefore fat digestion is compromised. So though my high fat diet over the past couple years has coincided with some improvements to my health, I still was facing many challenges particularly with my digestion and I always had trouble with very low carb for any extended period of time.

Here is a huge thread of a bunch of people working through their trouble with Accutane that I got a lot of info from if anyone is suffering similarly.
[http://www.acne.org/messageboard/topic/295030-repairing-the-long-term-damage-from-accutane/?page=307](http://www.acne.org/messageboard/topic/295030-repairing-the-long-term-damage-from-accutane/?page=307)

When I look back, it is obvious that the peak of my body's health was when I lived in Costa Rica one block from the beach. I had the habit of waking up with the sun and jogging along the ocean line, feeling the intensity of tropical sun as it rose. This was before I was really conscious of any sort of health stuff, but it was obviously one of the biggest steps I took toward reharmonizing my body.

I know that going back to the tropics to lay naked in the sun would be the optimal, but as I won't be able to do that for a while (planning a trip for summer or fall), I have to work with what I have now. Here's a summary of my current strategies:

Tanning salon membership - just upgraded so now I'm using the UVA beds regularly. One of the best decisions I made and I even got memberships for my mother and sister for xmas

Regular IR Sauna paired with cold showers - I have a sauna at home so I do that just about every day

Realignment of my posture and mindful movement - [http://gokhalemethod.com/](http://gokhalemethod.com/) and [http://movementmonk.xyz/](http://movementmonk.xyz/) are two amazing resources for really optimizing the collagen and fascia structures, releasing tension and developing real flexibility and openness

Liver flushes - I just did my first flush last month according to [this protocol](http://www.amazon.com/The-Amazing-Liver-Gallbladder-Flush/dp/0976571501). I released a ton of gallstones and oxidized cholesterol chunks (multiple hundreds) and I'm looking forward to continuing them over the next few months. I think this will really cause a cascade of improvements and really allow me to start expelling the accutane from my body and healing the damage its done.

Fasting - As my meditation practices deepens, I really start to feel the power of nothing. Often times we try to solve problems by throwing more stuff into the mix when the best thing is really just to stop. I've done some one and two day fasts over the past few months along with occasional intermittent fasting. With my next liver flush, I will see if it feels good to do a couple days fasting before and after. I think training the body to really run without food is my ultimate goal. And I feel that if I can find a place where I can do an extended fast (10+ days) with sunlight, that will get to the real deep healing and tapping into cosmic energy.

Breath awareness and training - I like to try out various breath exercises inspired by [pranayama](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HFkui4Hl5AY), [EWOT](http://www.breathing.com/articles/ewot.htm), [buteyko](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buteyko_method), freediving/breath holding, Wim Hof method, qi gong, etc. Usually I combine the breathing with movement and I find this is incredibly powerful for mindset and just clarity of mind. I think breathing is the foundation for the rest of what I do.

Herbal protocols - I buy whole herbs and make teas and I'm going to start playing around with making my own tinctures and using essential oils as well. I think these tools are seriously overlooked by super-sciency people.

Other stuff..... you get the idea

**One thing in particular I'd like to draw attention to is _attention_**. When you read this right now, you are devoting your attention to the screen. But what happens when you devote that attention inwards into the body? What electrically/physically is going on when you pay attention to how you're or how your muscles or organs feel? My qi gong master would talk about how when he looks into his abdomen during meditation, he sees light smiling back at him. And many meditations involve pay attention to a specific area of the body (i.e. third eye, pineal gland, abdomen, etc) until you see/feel light. _Is this really the key to packing UV light into the body_, by directing our attention inward and improving our connection to the various parts of our body? Are emotions and thoughts just different flutterings and symphonies of light passing through our antenna (body)? Obviously they are, but maybe instead of trying to eat things to get better light, we can just bring this direct intention of packing awareness/light into the body as we do everything else.

I'm still working to understand the physics and biochemistry to explain all this, but my intuition tells me I am really onto something. And I think this community could learn a lot by looking at the meditation/spiritual community with some open minds. Just a thought...

### BradTheBreather — 2016-03-02T01:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-190056>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=190053)
>
> Hi  I just searched 'liver flush' and found this post. Love this post! I'm going to do a liver flush protocol again too... Have you done any more since this post? How are you doing?

Wow I just checked this forum for the first time in a while and saw you had just posted this!

I did a couple more liver flushes with some success, but my perspective now is that they don't address the underlying thing that causes the liver to need to be flushed. I've seen people online who have done tons of flushes and they keep releasing gallstones, which tells me that there is some sort of liver dysfunction that can't be completely solved by flushing. For some people, a couple flushes may be enough relief to the liver to allow it to start improving it's function on its own, but my own journey has brought me to optimizing methylation.

I recently met with Josh Ferber who did some biotensor testing on me that showed I was lacking some of the key supporters of methylation. I talked to him at length about many things and he said the majority of what he does now is focused on supporting methylation, particularly with these four supplements:

Methyl-folate
Lithium
Molybdenum
Manganese

I did some mudpacking and I took some lithium and molybdenum, as per Josh's recommendations, for the first time yesterday and experienced some major detox symptoms, which I'm hoping means things are moving around. It's too early to make any grand conclusions.

I'm much more humble with my use of supplements and foods these days. I don't like to pretend that I always know (with my head from reading whatever online), what is best. I hope to explore medical qi gong a lot more as this is the foundation for the precision needed for biotensor testing, but I've basically stopped all my supplements and herbs and am certainly not eating epi-paleo. I don't think carbs are the devil and I most certainly know that the stress I put myself through over my food choices in the past negated a good chunk of the benefits I might have gotten from eating those foods.

Every person has their own story and radically individual needs that vary second to second. To try and make a template for everyone to follow will simply not work.

Though health is still a primary value of mine, I find that I am shifting more toward creativity and happiness as my primary motivators. It can make sense to sacrifice or let go of certain things in order to improve your health and wellbeing, but I took this way too far and ended up being quite isolated because of how uniquely I live my life. I've lightened up considerably over the past few months and place much more value on helping others feel good, even if I have to spend some time under artificial light or eat something I wouldn't normally eat.

Now I focus on connection.
Connection to the sun - I feel best when I'm deeply tanned and warmed by the sun
Connection to the earth - I walk barefoot and almost naked in nature often and sleep on the floor. I practice static and dynamic postures (yoga, qi gong, etc) where I focus on aligning my body to move with minimal muscle use, feeling how I can sink all my energy into the ground without it getting stuck somewhere in my body.
Connection to breath - Noticing the flow from one moment to the next. The breath always offers the opportunity to find stillness in the movement from the in breath (inspiration) to the out breath (expiration). Synchronize breath with movement
Connection to people - Nothing feeds the soul more than real, intimate connection with fellow humans.

For me, the point of being healthy is so I can better share my love and creativity with the world. What is the point of being healthy if you have no one to share it with? We get so caught up in things that don't matter that we forget what does: contributing to the world. Most of what the focus is within the health community is what we can _take_ _for our own wellbeing_, however, I think the times are shifting where we need to focus on _how we can best support the sharing of our gifts._

I hope this helps you feel relieved about things. You don't need to beat yourself up every time you eat some sugar. Just do what makes you feel good!

Haha it feels exciting reading this  That is awesome! Awesome and weird because it reflects so much of where I'm at too... haha  it's like you're a guy Me right now, with just a couple things switched around !

I too have been realizing I don't want to isolate, going back to creativity and happiness. And letting go of the stress - SO much stress I had, I can relate to you on that... trying to get things 'right' and 'forcing' myself when I know better, that for me it's not really about being uncomfortable but about babysteps to what feels good, being plenty uncomfortable is what illness feels like  haha... but when one is healthy, sure you can challenge yourself  and maybe not everyone is tuned like me, I know I used to live a hard and pushing life, until I did a lot of emotional healing and learning to get to that trust in myself to Not push. But then I lost my trust in myself, got sick, and I got confused...

Just recently again, I'm coming out of 'freak out' mode and I can again trust my intuition on food and light - the twist for me is that getting my health back on this issue will let me do more of that lovely people connecting, nature connecting stuff! Yipee  Being sick had me feeling disconnected both from people and from nature... I just felt 'off' from nature, it was so strange! I'm already much better now and I can tell it's a two way street, being disconnected from nature can make you sick, but also being sick will make me feel disconnected from nature. When one feels well you easily feel 'in tune' with nature... but when ill, I wanted to get in tune, but just felt and noticed mainly the stressors...

And another reflection: I'm two days away from finishing a 30day protocol I did suggested by Josh  I felt the mudding detoxing me too...

And my intuition started kicking so hard last week that I should now do a liver flushes... I had done just a couple last winter but stopped hoping flushing would just happen on it's own with keto and CT... and I got confused (and strict) and keto-ed two summers in a row, and then found out that's not even the recommendation...

(in a way it was good to stop then because the flush method I used was not compatible with winter (neither by Dr. K's recs, nor my own body desires). And Dr. K's Leptin Rx DID help me much by get me eating again - because I wasn't... couldn't figure out how to eat adequate food on a regular daily schedule anymore, and was in a bad state, probably hypoglycemic and starved - I didn't know not to keto in the summer, and had big problems because of it and no resolution on decongesting my liver, but I did fix my mental degeneration even just with DHA and high fats at that point and stopped going downhill with skipping food for days and on and off carb-only binges - whew... Thank you to Dr. Kruse for that! ).

So after this 30day Josh program I AM going to do some liver flushes, because my intuition and body is going nuts for it. I can feel the tightness when I do Chi Gong postures, and the energy stuckness, not getting to sink all the way to the ground the way it used to - mmmmm...I used to flow with health.

But for me, I don't think I have methylation issues very much. Even Josh has tested me a needing just Molybdenum for methylation. In fact I KNOW what happened to cause the extra stones for me - I starved myself trying to extend a very successful fast... (my environment switched from peaceful/spiritual to stressful/busy and intuition screamed at me then too - don't fast when stressed and busy!!! - but I ignored it, probably the first time ever ignoring it on a health issue - knowing my body was Very strong... so just to see if I could assert with just my head what would happen... well I didn't know it would be THIS bad!!  ).

This time I'm not going to ignore my intuition  I think it can take quite a few flushes - like over 10 - to deeply flush out the liver, and it may not even need to be 'deeply' flushed and work just fine with some old stones in it...

I actually know quite a bit about how not to create gallstones in myself (when I pay attention to what my body knows - but just to throw some out there, sunflower seeds, and apples... )... I also think immersion in cold water (especially natural water) CT when one has an already healthy liver can be really beneficial to keep it that way, like exercise for it... and many other liver-health people are missing out on that thought as constant warmth is recommended to not over-tax it... maybe over a long period of time CT on it's own along with plentiful seeds and fruits and herbs in the spring summers and fall would decongest the liver as Dr. K says ... I don't know since I keto-ed the last two summers...

I think 'flushing' at season's change is natural, and healthy! Maybe that's why my body is calling for it. I think for me I just have to flush the ones I have out, and I'll be set!

I don't know since I keto-ed the last two summers...
>
> I think 'flushing' at season's change is natural, and healthy! Maybe that's why my body is calling for it. I think for me I just have to flush the ones I have out, and I'll be set!

We are approaching the spring equinox, a time that is natural to do some spring cleaning, both externally and internally. I am in the middle of a transition and will be starting a new season of my life just around the time of the equinox and was planning on doing some sort of fasting or other thing to mark the transition.

I would be curious to know more about how you do your liver flushes. I follow the moritz protocol, trying to have as little food in my body as possible when I do the actual flush. If my next living environment has low enough stress, I would love to continue with them. Also what do you think is special about sunflower seeds and apples? Lecithin, malic acid and pectin perhaps? I go through periods where I will go through serious amounts of soaked/sprouted sunflower seeds in the form of a kind of hummus-like spread and I really enjoy that, but have never been super into apples. What are some other things that you think prevent the formation of gallstones?

I went through a long period of very high fat, consistent seafood kind of diet, but recently have been eating much less meat, focusing on shellfish when I do eat animals, and more carbs (still pretty high fat). I'm a mushroom fiend now. One of my favorite things is a mushroom soup made with tons of mushrooms, onions, potatoes, seaweed, and whatever other vegetables I have around, tons of salt, a bit of apple cider vinegar, extra collagen powder and a healthy pad of butter. Or simply bread (GF or not; I was strict GF for 2+years, but now I will eat good wheat bread from unbromated flour if I have it), with tons of butter and salt, maybe topped with dried fruit or a jelly. I also make some pretty awesome coconut-based treats! I like eating fruit, but I think this is more of a treat than helpful unless the fruit is fresh and is very well grown (i.e. beyond organic) or has close to wild genetics and nutrition. Commonly available fruit doesn't feel as sacred to me.

This kind of diet really makes me happy and feels more right than forcing myself to eat fish and fear carbs all the time. At the beginning I really craved fish and wanted to eat it, but perhaps I reached a point of relative equilibrium where I can hold onto my DHA a bit better. I do a pretty good job with my circadian rhythms and light/EMF stress and I've consumed exclusively spring water for more than 3 years.

I think the most important thing to make sure happens during summer is to really let the sun heat you to the core consistently and repeatedly. UV is great for the surfaces and signalling, but I think the IR is key for letting your insides know that it is summer. To the point where it is uncomfortable and your body flips some switches to adapt to the heat. I failed to do this the past two summers and I think I suffered because of it.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T03:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-190116>

> [BradTheBreather said:](/goto/post?id=190110)
>
> We are approaching the spring equinox, a time that is natural to do some spring cleaning, both externally and internally. >
> I think the most important thing to make sure happens during summer is to really let the sun heat you to the core consistently and repeatedly. UV is great for the surfaces and signalling, but I think the IR is key for letting your insides know that it is summer. To the point where it is uncomfortable and your body flips some switches to adapt to the heat. I failed to do this the past two summers and I think I suffered because of it.

Yup, you're right on re: sunflower seeds and apples, I'm thinking sunflower seeds for the lecithin (I have some sunflower seed lechitin I just got) and apples for the malic acid. Sour cherry juice also has malic acid, and cranberry juice. Clark store sells some Sour cherry Concentrate (their stuff is really pure) and I know it affects gallbladder because I gave myself gallbladder attacks with drinking it cold a couple years ago, but I didn't know what that horrible pain was then (I've never otherwise had gallbladder attacks, just when taking that concentrate cold on a starvation type diet ) I also know of Black Cumin oil (seems a really good one because of it also boosting immunity - so less toxins - though I think is somewhat estrogenic). Also the herbs Gold Coin Grass, and Chanca Piedra. I am using Chanca Piedra tincture right now, and it should break up calcified stones, but not sure that it would melt cholesterol ones... but it Does help liver function as well not just break stones and I can really feel that effect.

The lecithin I am trying for 3 days now works a little too well, it seems to have started melting my cholesterol stones and releasing toxins into my liver... and now I have an under skin rash on my liver and under breast area. I'm concerned about toxins getting trapped in breast fat - girl problems lol! - so I'm going to drop the lecithin until later on when my liver is less stuffed. I might get some Black Cumin oil and try that.

I think the liver actually makes these stones to PROTECT me from the toxins it doesn't have time to neutralize. So I'm going to flush them out as they are, that is hoping they get a move on when I do...

I did the Hulda Clark liver flush before, which involved doing her parasite cleanse by taking a schedule of green black-walnut hull herb, her wormwood containing blend, and cloves for awhile before, then drinking her "liver tea" of mixed herbs for a couple weeks before flush. The tea was really sweet and didn't feel good to me last winter while also keto and I was getting really bloated - at that time sweet stuff would consistently bloat me and feel inflammatory to me. I'm more sweet tolerant now. It's pretty similar to Andrew Moritz + Epsom as far as flush night goes.

I'm going to do a combo this time, I will do the parasite cleanse with the herbs before hand, then do the Clark Liver Tea and also Chanca Piedra, but I will also add in L-malic acid + water (instead of apple juice, I got L-Malic acid not D-malic, I think that actually matters for me as far as preventing kidney irritation and other toxicity) the six days before, and even fast from food a day before the flush.

I'm a little concerned reading Andrew Moritz about stones can get stuck in one's colon and then make you more toxic, and I can kind of see how that could happen. I will also do Colosan + the lemon juice/ACV tintestinal "cleanses" the week before the flush, and some after... because I don't want to do colonics in town and I don't know how to do enemas myself and think it may take a long time to learn how to do a self enema that flushes the entire colon... So I'm hoping doing the Clark herbal parasite cleanse before and doing maintenance doses after will help with preventing after-infections, and doing some Colosan assisted purges help with making sure no toxic metals or whatever else may be in the stones remains in my intestines to seep into me. This is actually my biggest concern, though Hulda Clark doesn't mention this concern, other than making sure you're on a parasite maintenance. Colosan is in the mail and I will start testing it out.

Later along the flushes at some point and for prevention I plan on using lecithin again and Black Cumin Oil.

I also think prevention is also about not eating at night, not binge eating, or mixing foods that don't seem to go together, something I used to do all the time. And also again cold water dips will probably do a lot to strengthen a healthy liver.

I've actually gotten a lot of sun the past few summers, not morning but full midday sun and afternoon. Tons of it, I was hoping it would help me, well it probably Did help me somewhat, but didn't resolve my condition... but I was messing up doing keto in summer. (I should say, unlike you I believe, I can FEEL my liver being stuck during Chi Gong, to the point that energy doesn't flow well along my right side. It used to be much worse, my entire right arm and leg being 'stiff feeling' and unable to ground my right foot... now it's mostly just the liver and gallbladder that I can still feel 'stuck' and it tenses and affects my neck and the right side of my head along my Gallbladder and Liver meridians. So if you're able to sink your energy into the ground and feel the flow, you're probably right to focus on methylation rather than "unclogging" right now. I'm in the other boat).

Your mushroom soup sounds really good! I'm not really into mushrooms, meaning I like them a lot but haven't been craving them (ok now I'm craving shiitake and other rather uncommon ones but I would want a LOT and they're expensive), but soup sounds really good especially with seaweed... I think I'm doing well with my DHA level now also. I started taking a whole BUNCH of Kelp granules on intuition about 6 weeks ago, and I seem to want less fish, but I think a lot of the granules aren't completely absorbed and my body is using them as insoluble fiber to detox my intestines. They actually really help me, I'm taking about two doses a day of 1.5oz granules mixed with yoghurt. This is supposedly a huge amount but I don't think I'm fully digesting them all.

What kind of extra collagen are you using? I just tried taking a small spoonfull of Knox gelatin yesterday... but it was morning and made me really sleepy. I used to eat menudo almost every weekend a long time ago, and didn't notice the tiredness, but I just had it again recently and: really sleepy and bloated! I must have really been missing gelatin and now it was too much at once - it makes sense I was mostly all steak and salmon and eggs, as I didn't add in bone broths or anything. So I'm thinking to titer up with the gelatin thing, probably before bed or regularly do some jello  and have menudo on the weekends again.

I can tolerate bread now pretty well too, though I hardly ever have it. I wonder if bread was in the past included for fiber content, I will research and experiment. I also got some amaranth and will start to see about making bread or crackers... I have some info from a starchless vegan type diet with foods that are supposed to really energize the body and amaranth, spelt and some others grains are included. I don't follow it but I think some of the ideas are useful to me, and can point to some especially energizing foods (that diet also is supposed to avoid hybridized food, so much of the recommendations would be foods that are still similar to their wild form. I didn't realize a lot of cultivated plants are hybridized (I don't mean GMO) and are not able to reproduce in the wild. This diet considers that they don't have the right vibration and not the right balance of nutrients anymore. It also uses a lot of seaweed.) I still eat hybridized plants though.

ex: "
The banana plant is a hybrid, originating from the mismatched pairing of two South Asian wild plant species:_Musa acuminata_ and _Musa balbisiana_. Between these two products of nature, the former produces unpalatable fruit flesh, and the latter is far too seedy for enjoyable consumption. Nonetheless, these closely related plants occasionally cross-pollinate and spawn seedlings which grow into sterile, half-breed banana plants. Some ten thousand years ago, early human experimenters noted that some of these hybridized Musa bore unexpectedly tasty, seedless fruit with an unheard-of yellowness and inexplicably amusing shape. They also proved an excellent source of carbohydrates and other important nutrients.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

A seed-packed wild musa (banana)
Despite the hybrid’s unfortunate sexual impotence, shrewd would-be agriculturalists realised that the plants could be cultivated from suckering shoots and cuttings taken from the underground stem. The genetically identical progeny produced this way remained sterile, yet the new plant could be widely propagated with human help. An intensive and prolonged process of selective breeding—aided by the variety of hybrids and occasional random genetic mutations—eventually evolved the banana into its present familiar form." [http://www.damninteresting.com/the-unfortunate-sex-life-of-the-banana/](http://www.damninteresting.com/the-unfortunate-sex-life-of-the-banana/)

Green banana species are supposedly not hybrid though...

Fruit fruit fruit... Maybe that's why you don't feel the sacredness.  I don't have a lot but will have a lot more as it's getting hotter. I like berries a lot. I get Wild Boreal Blueberries frozen at Trader Joes and really like those, they're small and not too sweet... more 'real' feeling than big organic ones.

### Celeste — 2016-03-03T07:35:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feeling-the-way-to-optimal.16936/post-190128>

> [BradTheBreather said:](/goto/post?id=184307)
>
> For example: when I was 16, I went through a course of Accutane, which completely trashed my body in many ways and though my attention to clean living for the past few years has done a lot to mitigate some of that and begin to heal, but I never did any hardcore research into that issue specifically. I recently have started to dig a bit deeper into this and one thing I found that has been true for me is that gallbladder function and therefore fat digestion is compromised. So though my high fat diet over the past couple years has coincided with some improvements to my health, I still was facing many challenges particularly with my digestion and I always had trouble with very low carb for any extended period

# Thread: Non-traditional ways of raising testosterone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-traditional-ways-of-raising-testosterone.16937/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-04T17:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-traditional-ways-of-raising-testosterone.16937/post-178594>

So I had this idea....

I tend to naturally be on the lower side with my testosterone levels (not confirmed with blood work, I can just tell from feeling), but I've noticed there are certain things I can do beyond the standard lifting heavy things and meal timing methods to raise my testosterone levels.

If while I sprint or lift weights, I get angry, yell and grunt like hell, I can feel the pump getting more intense. I've also played around with visualizing going into fierce battle or competition, or that I'm a wolf hunting a sheep or an elk clashing antlers with another elk. I feel like intense competition or exertion is the way to get things flowing.

Maybe a little tmi but I can also apply the same kinds of grunts to masturbation to pump myself up. But to feel the benefit of the higher testosterone levels, I don't ejaculate but instead use Taoist sexual practices to reabsorb, circulate and store the energy. I remember hearing somewhere that the lower testosterone levels of today's young men could be attributed to internet porn addiction and too frequent ejaculation. In the Taoist sexual practices, the seed represents the man's jing, or vital life force and one of the first steps to the practice is to reduce or eliminate ejaculation and practice orgasm without ejaculation (possible but difficult).

Maybe this is my ego reading into things, but I notice when I go long periods without ejaculating, I do certainly get more female attention. This is especially true if I am sexually stimulated and can control myself rather than just pacified and unstimulated. I haven't worn deoderant in a long time and I'm very careful with soap use, so I wonder if this has to do with pheromones.

I'm not well-versed at searching through the the literature on this sort of thing, but I was wondering if anyone has any feelings or ideas of this, or maybe a quantum perspective? I know it's a man-centered post, but I think female opinions are especially valuable here; are there just men that are irresistibly attractive for no obvious or logical reason?

# Thread: Keto breath
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-breath.16939/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flawed — 2015-10-05T16:08:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-breath.16939/post-178644>

Hey,
When i'm in ketosis (haven't actually done a blood/ketone test but think i'm in) i can get bad breath.

Also i get something that i've heard Tim Ferriss describe as "metal mouth" where there's this weird (not exactly metallic) taste in my mouth.

Could it be from excreting heavy metals stored in fat?

### nonchalant — 2015-10-05T18:28:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-breath.16939/post-178654>

Flawed, are you eating daily seafood with that diet?

### Scompy — 2015-10-06T02:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-breath.16939/post-178687>

I used to get bad breath when I combined Keto + IF (Intermittent fasting) in particular. After getting 15+ hours into a fast, the breath would have a high amount of ketones and stay super-potent until I ate again. Take note, the bad breath was especially bad when I was overweight compared to today at 10% total bodyfat. Once I got down to around 12% Bodyfat, the bad breath lessened to the point of being almost non-existent. I suspect (but don't hold me to this) that in people with more fat to lose, there is simply more volume of ketones that can be produced during the fatloss period. I don't think it has anything to do with stored heavy metals in adipose tissues. Those would probably make their way out of the body in other ways. When fat breaks down, I've read it converts to about 83% carbon dioxide and 17% water, so this would lead one to believe we're mostly exhaling the fat we break down and peeing out the rest.

By the way, I've ditched IF with my keto these days because I was draining my liver glycogen too strong/too much. I found the optimal daily window is 12 hours of an eating window and 12 hours off.

Pretty much daily, however atm i am only eating tilapia which i know is't as low down the food chain as we would like for DHA levels. Also i supplement with DHA/EPA (i know supplementing is considered somewhat of a no-go but i feel an effect).

I'm gonna remedy this by sourcing some dope cockles/mussels or ideally oysters if i can get them. I live by the sea so shouldn't be too tough
