# Thread: Heliophobics transitioning into The Unliving
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heliophobics-transitioning-into-the-unliving.16940/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-05T21:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heliophobics-transitioning-into-the-unliving.16940/post-178665>

Do you know or have known a heliophobic? Someone with a real phobia to the sun? I do. Many actually. I think we have a large segment of the population that believes: 'UV light is bad.' And it's worse for many women that have been told by the mainstream, "UV light = wrinkles." We now have a generation that fears the sun, slathers on sunscreen and hasn't seen a sunrise or sunset in years. Sad, I know.

But they need serious education. They have little to no understanding that daily timing matters greatly as well as seasonal patterns, the latitude where someone lives, etc. So have any of you watched the behaviors of people that omit and mitigate as much UV light in their lives as possible? Then, watch what happens to them over the next several years?

I could write a book about a 'Jane Doe' in my life on this subject. The phobia is like this as a past example:

1\. Shove material into door cracks that had morning light coming through it.
2\. Put up rugs or other material up over windows to prevent light coming in.
3\. Do all activities at night when the sun is down.

The Jane Doe I noticed doing this was tired/sleep all day long and never wanted to get out of bed. It's usually starts with depression that manifests, then into something else...Seeing is believing. I've seen the lack of UV + circadian mismatches in-person and seen the damage up-close and personal. Then disease manifests along with the behaviors that literally converts them slowly into the walking dead: the unliving.

# Thread: What happens when we lose electron spin?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-we-lose-electron-spin.16950/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-07T04:53:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-we-lose-electron-spin.16950/post-178783>

Is this a mitochondrial phenomena.

When an electron loses its spin it also loses its magnetism. When this occurs it seem to disappear in a magnetic field. This is a big issue in the sun and might be a bigger deal in mitochondria.

Today, all electronic devices use transistors to work. Today we have smaller newer versions that use both electric and magnetic forces to do the things they do. They are found in computers, TV’s and your cell phone. In these modern devices, the magnetically sensitive transistor is also known as the _spin transistor or spintronic transistor_. This was named for spintronics, which is a technology which this development spawned in 1990. Spintronics emerged from discoveries in the 1980’s concerning spin-dependent electron transport phenomena in solid-state devices. Every electron in every atom has four quantum numbers that organizes the periodic table of elements. Spin number is one of those numbers.

The spins of electrons are not only manipulated by magnetic fields, but also by electrical fields. Both exist in mitochondria and both are powerful. They can be used to collect and store information from electrons or the photons they carry. Mitochondria are masters at controlling the spin of electrons to do the amazing things life can do. Few people understand why this is true because they do not understand quantum spin or radical chemistry.

Most of the time in an atom electrons are paired. One spins up an the other spins down. This is called their singlet state. When electrons are in their "singlet state" in atoms their electron spins are paired and they cancel each other out. This is how the Pauli exclusion principle works. This fundamental quantum rule dictates how the periodic table is set up by stating that two electrons can not exist in the same quantum state. But what happens when you create chemicals called free radicals that have electrons that spin in the same direction? All mitochondria create free radicals that have electrons that spin in the same directions. _**Free radicals have unusual spin characteristics.**_
_**
Did you know on Earth, its magnetic field favors triplet state oxygen radicals and not singlet state?

So the question you should have now is what happens when these two same spinning electrons are excited by electric fields or magnetic fields?

This becomes the incredibly important issue in chloroplasts and mitochondria. All life on this planet uses one or the other to make energy from the sun using electrons and protons. It is why all cytochromes use Iron- sulfur (Fe-S) redox complexes where quantum mechanisms dominate on electron spins.

The mitochondrial complexes are like electromagnetic compasses, that tell the mitochondria what the environment is calling for. In this sense, they are _**quantum heat pumps**_ because they all generate some amount of **infrared light from the amount of UV light frequencies that are fed into it. The amount of UV light fed into it is also a factor in how much infrared light is released**. The free radical chemical signal a mitochondria makes determines how much light or energy can and should be released to the MINOS water layer around the mitochondria to drive signaling and physiologic functioning. This is a critical step in mito-nuclear coaptation. Since water normally has a high dielectric point this means that the charges can affect its electric abilities. The dielectric constant measures how effectively water can shield the negative and positive charges from one another and hence reduces the force between them. This is why water fundamentally breaks symmetry in nature because it is very effective in separating charges and forces. I would remind you that heat shrinks or condenses water when it interacts with infra-red light.**_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-07T05:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-we-lose-electron-spin.16950/post-178785>

In this way, when you study the water molecules subsequent motions you can deduce something about the particles that hit water as they exit the cytochromes. Water is H20 and has oxygen within it. Water, actually the oxygen in it with its added electrons from mitochondria, is how a cell deciphers environmental signals. Water is not a homogenous fluid because of oxygen. Water can act like an ionic plasma that can imprint information and energies of things that interact with it when it contains more triplet oxygen. This is how a mitochondria works at its smallest scale. The light fed into a mitochondria has the ability to affect time. Evolution deals with time on several scales. Evolution happens by natural genetic engineering of light and water, and not by random mutation and natural selection.

It turns out oxygen in water and in cells are linked by spin or de coupled by a lack of spin.

Velocity with light in a vacuum is fixed. Velocity in matter is not fixed. Within mitochondria, velocity depends on measuring BOTH time and distance on the inner mitochondrial membrane where the cytochromes exist. In fact, two measurements must be made here. One is the distance measurement in Angstroms between cytochromes and the Q cycle built into them. This velocity measurement concerns itself with how fast electrons can move from NAD+ to oxygen. The second Both of these measurements are fundamental to evolutionary progress or extinction.

In the SCN and peripheral circadian clocks we are using atomic optical light clocks, with negligible margins of error. This misses the point of how time in circadian biology is actually measured. Even if the clocks have zero margin of error themselves, the clocks are being used to measure a distance gap between the clocks. The distance gap has the margin of error exclusive of any margin of error in the timepieces. Light and or electrons travel between the clocks and that velocity is subject to their masses.

The clocks can be altered by the light in the AM that fuels the regeneration of rods and the eye clock in daylight or by excessive blue light at night destroying cones or eye camera and the eye clock. As the destruction of the clocks occurs it increases the size of the respiratory proteins. This also increases the distance between the mitochondria and the nucleus.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-07T05:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-we-lose-electron-spin.16950/post-178787>

Here is the irony, 99.9% of doctors think that the cornea and lens block UV light. It is ridiculous idea when you consider data from the 1960's that show UV light gets into the retina. And in 1996 we find this: The retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) contains an abundant opsin that is distinct from rhodopsin and cone visual pigments and is able to bind the retinaldehyde chromophore. The putative retinal G protein-coupled receptor (RGR) was isolated in digitonin solution from bovine RPE microsomes and copurified consistently with a minor 34-kDa protein. The absorption spectrum of RGR revealed endogenous pH-sensitive absorbance in the blue and near-ultraviolet regions of light. Membrane-bound RGR was incubated with exogenously added all-trans-retinal and formed two long-lived pH-dependent photopigments with absorption maxima of 469 +/- 2.4 and 370 +/- 7.3 nm. The effects of hydrogen ion concentration suggest that the blue and near-UV photopigments are tautomeric forms of RGR, in which an all-trans-retinal Schiff base is protonated or unprotonated, respectively. The RPE pigment was also demonstrable by its reactivity to hydroxylamine in the dark. The retinaldehyde-RGR conjugate at neutral pH favors the "near-UV pigment" and is a novel light-absorbing opsin in the vertebrate eye. If we block blues and purples why would we have melanopisin in our eyes? I smell bullshit. Have a look at the paper "Spectral Transmission of the Eye to Ultraviolet Radiations by Everett Kinsey. It is pure bullshit. But that BS lead to all safety glasses, lenses, and eye glasses blocking UV parts of the spectrum. Not good at all. If you wear sunglasses by term insurance and get a long term policy..........you likely will wind up with a low dopamine style disease like obesity or sleep apnea.

The measurement made determines the reality we get. Singlet free radicals make life filled with disease and triplet free radicals build a life of optimal wellness. Mitochondria also release _monochromatic infrared light_ because you can only **entangle electrons** and protons with light at **one specific frequency**. Live in the morning sunlight and harvest it. Sleep in the dark. Heal in the red. Repeat.

Red light slows time, hence why our mitochondria emit it. Cold slows time, which is why ETC speeds up in cold as cytochromes and the MINOS are condensed. Blue light speeds up time and your life is lived like a blue straggler star, because ETC speeds slow, and respiratory proteins are stretched so far they can't make triplet free radicals. Quick and bright is your life. Over supplemented fro pills or foods comes from being undereducated about light and not food. All food is broken down to electrons. All electrons have spin.
So instead of "You are what you eat", it's more like, "You are what you absorb, repel and emit.

Light also has weight. As it falls to anything with gravity it gets heavier because light has momentum. So AM UV light tends to fall to things with mass like mitochondria. What is in mitochondria that UV light is drawn too? NADH. Light moving away from mass allows light to "get lighter" because it loses its purple power and shifts to red. NAD+ does not draw purple light to it. Gravitational lensing is a redshift effect in stars and in our mitochondria.

The double slit experiment in QED has been done thousands of times proving quantum entanglement only works if light is monochromatic. This is why your skin has to begin the filtering process of sunlight for a long time in our tissues before our mitochondria can process the electromagnetic wave of light. You should be reminded that water in our blood plasma absorbs electromagnetic radiation best at 270 nm directly in the ultraviolet range. When sunlight hits our skin blood flow is increased to the skin surface. UV/IR light can extend the EZ in blood plasma because blood is 93% water. Everything is coupled by quantum design when you understand nature's plan.

# Thread: Newbie here - question about past CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### earthwalker — 2015-10-07T18:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178823>

I am recovering from severe environmental illness which I had for about 15 years. During that time, there was a period of a few years where I could barely tolerate clothing, could not use any bedding, could not tolerate heating systems, etc. So I essentially lived a CT lifestyle but without the water part. I was living in northern Arizona where the nights were cold and winters could go down to -20 deg Fahrenheit at times. I lived through 5 winters wearing just a very thin long sleeved t-shirt and thin leggings, sometimes without socks, and flip flops. I never wore a winter coat or used a sheet or blanket. The result of this, I discovered after some time, was that my body had adjusted to the cold temperatures quite well. I remember standing outside at a party one day (I lived in a community of others with environmental illness) in my thin t-shirt and leggings. It was cold and windy but I felt quite fine temperature wise. A couple that had just moved there had their winter coats on and had to leave the party because they were too cold to be outside. That was the moment I realized how well my body had adjusted to the cold temperatures.

My question is... does that type of adaptation stay over time even if you stop exposing yourself to cold temps like that? I no longer have to live that way because I have recovered enough to wear clothes and use bedding and heat. Although in the winter here in Sedona, Az I don't use heat while sleeping as I am quite warm with my down comforter - many times I wake up sweating and have to push the covers off. I don't wear a winter coat normally either. I feel that the adaptation has stayed with me but I am wondering if I still need to do CT. I take hot showers nowadays but always finish with a burst of really cold water at the end and get my torso chilled, front and back.

Any comments appreciated. Thank you!

### nonchalant — 2015-10-07T18:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178825>

Hi Earthwalker, welcome. Adaptation to cold is good, but one of the main purposes of CT is to actually get your skin chilled, so you can benefit from more ultraviolet light.

### earthwalker — 2015-10-07T23:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-question-about-past-ct.16955/post-178840>

Ah, thank you so much for clarifying that. Very helpful! So would one want to do CT and then directly go into the sun?

### nonchalant — 2015-10-08T01:37:44+0300

# Thread: NadoRo's High Altitude Labs

### NadoRo — 2015-10-08T21:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nadoros-high-altitude-labs.16957/post-178881>

I couldn't decide if I should post in the labs thread or Hormones 101 thread, so please advise if I need to repost elsewhere.

I have a journal for context, but here's a rough sketch:

\--Live at 5,000 ft near Denver

\--Have been working on bringing up sex hormones since last July w/Functional Medicine Practitioner who is governed by diet dogma. Diagnosed w/Stage 2-3 Adrenal Fatigue a year ago...Hormones were in the toilet all around, with elevated cholesterol and high LDL. Classic leptin resistance. She seems to think the magic of a gluten free diet will cure all issues...my experience as a perimenopausal 42 year old suggests otherwise. What you see below is the result of aggressive supplementation and experimentation on my part, which she's (mostly) gone along with.

\--Diagnosed w/SIBO Spring 15 by Genova Stool test. Practitioner cited excess growth of one type of bacteria and HS CRP levels as reason. I'm not convinced...but I do feel better on a very low carb diet.

\--7 years of bad sleep caused by 2 babies then toddlers then preschoolers who didn't sleep, stress from multiple moves due to husband's job (military) , little to no support system because of said moves..Both kids are in full time school as of late August and stress levels are lower as a result.

\--started leptin rx two months ago. Have lost 8 lbs (20 more to go to a 20-24% body fat range rec. by JK), eat at least one seafood meal a day, get lots of natural daylight, eat BAB outside, never wear sunglasses, red blockers go on at sunset. Sleep still very inconsistent. I wrestle mostly with night wakings and being awake for long periods of time during the night, despite fatigue and a strong desire to fall asleep. My current body fat is around 34-38% based on a Dexa scan last February, and subsequent measurements at my doctor's office. I HAVE NO HISTORY OF OBESITY OR HIGH CHOLESTEROL. This all started after the birth of my second child at age 36. That's why I know Leptin Resistance is a real state of being!

The labs: (drawn 8/18/15)

Vit D: 41 (so bad! that's what happens when you stop supplementing at altitude, even in the summer)

HS CRP: 1.4

LDL-P 2501

Small LDL-P 911

HDL-P 44.3-67 (I had two cholesterol tests performed with in a week of each other)

TRG: 63

Fasting Glucose: 82

Fasting Insulin: 13 (Optimal range 3-9)

A1C: 4.8

BUN/Creatinine ratio 18 ( I know! I'm deydrated)

DHEA-S: 371 (provider's range is 200-300)

Estradiol pg/ml: 75-125 (her range is 75-125)

Estrone pg/ml: 45 (13-135)

Progesterone: 23.38 ng/ml (her optimal range is 10-20???)

Testosterone: 26 ng/dl (her optimal range is 12-82) Still waiting for that sex drive to come back..

Pregnenalone: 174 ng/dl (her optimal range is 100-150)

If you've made it this far, here are my questions:

1) If my hormones are high, too high for DHEA-S & Pregnenalone according to my provider, WHY IS MY SLEEP STILL BAD?

2) Is my Progesterone high enough for living at altitude?? Should I press for it to go higher? Should all my hormones be higher, living at altitude?

I'm not going to post thyroid, except to say that I'm on 90 Naturethroid daily to raise a low T3 of 78 ng/dl (80-200). My TSH is 1.84, and I know that needs to be at 1.00 or lower. My Reverse T3 ratio was somewhere around 18 when I calculated it.

I have started the CT protocol and am taking cold baths every day or so as I can fit it in. I also started yoga classes and Relora to work on cortisol levels along with continuing the Leptin RX. I know I've got pregnenalone steal going on, and I'm trying to contain my stress levels. Haven't done an ASI in a couple of years. Thanks to Leptin Rx and light exposure, I've got the best energy levels I've had in a looong time. Just not sure why bad sleep persists and feel that it's linked to my hormones.

Thoughts and input are greatly appreciated!!

when indoor

extends from 38th parallel to 66degree 33minutes in each hemisphere

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-12T05:03:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-best-uv-lights-for-inside.16959/post-179174>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=179136)
>
> this is everyones question. we need spectrometer pics of all the different globes.

You actually don't because of **Fraunhofer lines. These lines vary second to second in our sun so this means the solar spectrum is NEVER the same. That means neither do your indoor bulbs. I think varying frequencies in both UV and IR ranges is more than wise. This is why I dont get why people want to be precise in nm frequencies for light used artificially. It means that you need to look at mixes of spectrums as a symphony. The red and purple bands work in unison. Red can offset the massive singlet ROS of blues but not without UV. UV quiets CM and the interior of the cell because it can refill what we are using in ELF UV bio photon release for signaling. This is also why humans do not see UV or IR light. You can't use light in your camera you are using to signal other things.

UV and IR are always coupled because nitrogen and oxygen radical need to be balanced in cells. Full solar light releases massive amounts of RNS in nitric oxide. Blue spectrum in light is what releases massive singlet ROS. UV is what makes mitochondria make triplet state ROS. The real problem is the lack of solar exposure to make proper RNS and ROS in the modern world. IRA and NIR can balance bad light to a degree but you need a touch of UVA if you are not getting proper daily sun in your eye. I**n astronomical spectroscopy, any of the dark absorption **lines** in the spectrum of the Sun are caused by selective absorption of the Sun's or star's radiation at specific wavelengths by the various elements existing as gases in its atmosphere.

### JanSz — 2015-10-14T19:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-the-best-uv-lights-for-inside.16959/post-179480>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=179174)
>
> You actually don't because of **Fraunhofer lines. These lines vary second to second in our sun so this means the solar spectrum is NEVER the same. That means neither do your indoor bulbs. I think varying frequencies in both UV and IR ranges is more than wise. This is why I dont get why people want to be precise in nm frequencies for light used artificially. It means that you need to look at mixes of spectrums as a symphony. The red and purple bands work in unison. Red can offset the massive singlet ROS of blues but not without UV. UV quiets CM and the interior of the cell because it can refill what we are using in ELF UV bio photon release for signaling. This is also why humans do not see UV or IR light. You can't use light in your camera you are using to signal other things.
>
> UV and IR are always coupled because nitrogen and oxygen radical need to be balanced in cells. Full solar light releases massive amounts of RNS in nitric oxide. Blue spectrum in light is what releases massive singlet ROS. UV is what makes mitochondria make triplet state ROS. The real problem is the lack of solar exposure to make proper RNS and ROS in the modern world. IRA and NIR can balance bad light to a degree but you need a touch of UVA if you are not getting proper daily sun in your eye. I**n astronomical spectroscopy, any of the dark absorption **lines** in the spectrum of the Sun are caused by selective absorption of the Sun's or star's radiation at specific wavelengths by the various elements existing as gases in its atmosphere.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fraunhofer\_lines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fraunhofer_lines)

Solar spectrum with Fraunhofer lines as it appears visually.

Because of their well defined wavelengths, Fraunhofer lines are often used to characterize the [refractive index](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Refractive_index) and [dispersion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dispersion_%28optics%29) properties of optical materials.

.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-24T11:18:43+0200

But the frequencies of the sun change throughout the day which turns on and turns off hormones at the right time .....
From an actinotherapy paper from 1929:
_“At the London Zoological Gardens gas-filled incandescent tungsten lamps were, at Dr. Leonard Hill's suggestion, installed in the lizard house. In order that the lizards might get ultra-violet light these lamps were made with vitaglass bulbs. The effect on the lizards was very great. Instead of being lethargic and refusing to eat, and dying, they woke up, took an interest in their food and lived. This effect was at first thought to be due to such ultra-violet radiation as the lamps emitted. It is now known that these lamps give very little more ultra-violet radiation than ordinary domestic lamps, and it is nearly certain that this improvement in the lizards was due to the visible and infra-red radiations.”
_
I've intuitively been suspicious of my UVB containing reptile lamp, its always been a very occasional midday last resort. Now I'm avoiding it, its a treatment, not a lifestyle choice, because apart from any flicker, mercury resonance issues etc, JK:
"UVB turns off testosterone when it irradiates the skin and blood".
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/non-traditional-ways-of-raising-testosterone.16937/#post-210904](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/non-traditional-ways-of-raising-testosterone.16937/#post-210904)
I still need a normal female testosterone pulse at the right time .... and I sure as hell don't want to be screwing with DH or DS's testosterone levels

So,
I assume that any UVa & UVb light is better than nothing.
Also,
in his residence, as described in one of blogs by visitor, there is many black light bulbs of this type:
[https://www.batteriesplus.com/content/images/product/large/825283.jpg](https://www.batteriesplus.com/content/images/product/large/825283.jpg)
Jack, in one of his blogs shows picture of similar lamp in his daughter bedroom.

@[Dia](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11720/) in consultation with Jack was advised to use Sperti.
She and her daughter used it and had great results in raising vit D.

I had discussion with Dia, she was able to provide frequency charts for Red and white Sperti.
I compared both and it turns out that
Red Sperti contains all that White Serti have but additionally contains UVa.
Since we need both UVa and UVb I concluded that
I should use Red Sperti.

Eventually I have bought Red Sperti, and since was able to do hack.

I did not make any modifications to Red Sperti.
That is how it comes.

The sequence of events IIRC was that Dia started posting that she and her daughter used Sperti on dr Jack advice (with great results).

I have found that there is White and red Sperti.

White is more expensive I guess because it is about Vit D, and vit D and tan are in vogue.
Upon investigation I found that light given by White is included in Red Sperti and more.

Now, 6-8 months latter, I see that my red Sperti at 16 min/day gives decent vit D (and likely helps also with vit A).
\=================

ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W is designed as a full daylight for a lizard.
Lizzard is usually few inches from that light.
Lizzard needs also much more heat that people can stand.
So ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W is good light for people but when used additional UV light source is need.

///////
I would not buy "full spectrum" lights" from dr Mercola.
I have not seen reasonable research about those lights.
ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W was designed for a living creature and many lizards live under them giving us some reason to believe that they may be good for us.

In Europe there are lighting companies that work research and production of healthy lights.
I copied some info on them on this thread,.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-68](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-68)
but I do not have direct link to particular post there.
If interested look up "quantum dots".

/

...

Edit 3/24/2017
I just finished my hack with Red Sperti.
Got my Red Sperti in Sep 2016.
Since I got it I use it every day 16 min/day. (one full timer's turn=16min)
My navel is at the middle of the lamp level. I am very close to lamp, sometime touching it.
I started with heavy duty sun exposure last summer, plus some vit D supplements.
Over fall and specially winter not much sun exposure.
Do not used vit D supplements since I got Red Sperti.
5/17/2016 vit D=130.0 ng/mL(30-100) (winter, low sun 5drops Bio-D-Mulsion Forte)
8/16/2016 vit D=80.3 ng/mL(30-100) (lots of sun, (3-4)drops Bio-D-Mulsion Forte)
2/27/2017 vit D=63.0 ng/mL(30-100) (16min Red Sperti, no supplemental vit D)

I think from now on I will
use sun as best as I can
keep using Red Sperti 16min/day
add 4 drops/day of Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D
\---------------------------------------------

I was trying nicotine gum, but overall hated nicotine.
Other than estradiol there is estrone.
For whatever reason I started reacting differently to my DHEA supplementation.
It was surprising, I was on that dose for close to 10 years.
I still do not know the reason,
but I was getting high estrone. That is my dose need adjustment. As it turned out very big adjustment.
In man it is known that high estradiol interferes with erections.
Estrone is less known. Few monitor E1, good that I am doing that.
Anyhow, Happy Days are here again.

In my case, estradiol(E2) is ok, but estrone(E1) is high.
Both E1 & E2 are converted by aromatase.
I was trying AI, aromatase inhibitor (Anastrozole).
It worked, but it also lowered my Estradiol.
For short time it was ok, long term would weaken my bones, so not the right way to deal with it.
Give boner, take away bones, bad deal.

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), here's an answer to your question:

[https://www.jackkruse.com/time-10-can-you-supplement-sunlight/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-10-can-you-supplement-sunlight/)

"Our vitamin D3 plasma levels are markers for the light stress we face and the amount we get from our environment. This is why everyone’s Vitamin D3 levels are low when physicians check them. It is also why oral pills often do not budge a plasma vitamin D3 level when the patient goes right back to the same light stressed environment they acquired it in."

There must be more to it.
I have doubts about the word **OFTEN**.

So there must be another (actual) reason.

I was mostly naked, living mostly outside. One 40 watts light bulb downstairs, the other upstairs. Before TV, just after crystal radios, tube radios started appearing (in Poland)(in 1940's, in remote villages).
Mom would give me a spoon full of fish oil that she got from UNRA. (Thank you USA).
Kids who hated that oil ended with rickets.

# Thread: Circuit breaker off = Dreams on
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-10-09T17:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178928>

I've been cutting the circuit breaker to the house at night and I can say for sure at this point there is a huge difference in my sleep. The thing I notice the most is how incredibly detailed and vivid my dreams are, and how I remember them well in to the next day. I also feel like the quality of sleep is better, like I'm getting sleep that is more restorative.

Has anyone else experienced this as well?

### caroline — 2015-10-10T00:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178964>

What about your fridge? I found better sleep when i turned off the power to my bedroom.

### Scompy — 2015-10-10T02:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178974>

I just got myself a Stetzerizer Microsurge Meter and discovered my computer room had a 1000+ coming in off my power. The recommended is 50 and under. Bedroom plug on one side was around 250 and with a stetzer plug adaptor, I got it down to 45

I'll be posting some picutres or a video of my new Stetzerizer and how I'm mitigating in my journal some point this weekend. Making this a hobby these days

### Psyche — 2015-10-10T18:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-178994>

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/). Yes, power to everything shut down at night, not just bedroom. For one thing our house has everything tied to one master breaker. But really I would do it anyways given how the smart meter acts to make all the house wires an antennae(power line communication-TWACS). All power off is the best I can do and still be sleeping inside. Still getting the smart meter and if line voltage is unbalanced it could be an issue. However the fact I have dramatically improved sleep says my little rural house is probably not getting ground voltages back inside. Even talking about this shit totally pisses me off.

### RobH — 2015-10-11T07:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circuit-breaker-off-dreams-on.16960/post-179053>

had switch installed for bedroom, work likes a charm. Tell her about my two trees. I have an oak tree with power line running to light mounted on tree -- almost never turned on. Tree is dead. Tree next to it, same water, same sun, same soil, etc, is very much alive. Just the presence of the potential voltage -- not even current flowing, has killed the tree after 15 years. Every oak tree around my property (4 total) with conduit running up it and light on it (nearly never on, like twice year for a few hours) is dead compared the the thriving ones. I will try to post picture of it soon. It made a huge impression on my wife and kids when I pointed it out and now my wife switches off electricity in bedroom even if I'm in bed first. Looking at getting switching installed for kids bedrooms also. It wasn't that expensive, and avoids me having to run outside and around the house to breaker box.

RobH

# Thread: When you cannot implement many of the suggestions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-cannot-implement-many-of-the-suggestions.16961/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### earthwalker — 2015-10-09T19:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-cannot-implement-many-of-the-suggestions.16961/post-178942>

Just read Epi-Paleo Rx book. I have 35+ years of Crohn's disease that has recently morphed into full body inflammation showing as rheumatoid like arthritis in fingers, wrists, elbows, knees, ribs, feet, toes and more recently in skin manifestation - ulcerating open wound that won't heal in 3 months now. Previously I had 15 years of severe environmental illness and sensitivities to foods, chemicals, EMFs, mold, sun, vibration, sound, energy. I am much improved from the severe EI but still dealing with sensitivities to some things.

My question is.... I have reacted to whey, colostrum, coconut oil, and do not do great with bone broths. I have trouble digesting all fats and oils - comes out on my face as pustules. I ate oysters for the first time the last 2 days and my face is horribly broken out right now. Also, many years ago I did a liver detox test that showed high cysteine plasma levels and was told not to use NAC.

So I am at a loss as to how to proceed since the Leaky Gut Rx is suggesting all these things I cannot ingest. I've taken copious amounts of L-Glutamine in the past and never noticed a difference. Wondering if there is a specific kind/brand that is suggested.

I did the HLA-DR test years ago and am mold illness susceptible as well as post lyme disease susceptible. I tested positive for IgM lyme in 2005 and again in 2014 but was never treated. I had a mold infestation in my apartment in 2005 and did 6 weeks of CSM with good results but then had a life threatening reaction to the filler ingredients and had to stop.

I was very low in leptin when I did the Shoemaker tests in 2005. My CRP was above 80 a couple years ago and is now down around 38 or so. Big improvement but still a long way to go. I am very anemic and supplementing does not help. I tried chlorophyll, Flora-Dix, Angstrom Iron, and iron capsules.

Ironically, I used to eat wild salmon 2x day and grassfed beef 1x day. I got the fatty acids profile test done and my omega 3 was way too high according to the test. I spent time reevaluating my diet so I could balance out all my fatty acids to the proper ratios. That was the beginning of the end for me in terms of my inflammation levels. I went from very low inflammation to very high. I also was recovering from severe mercury poisoning so I did not want to eat too much seafood.

I just ordered a bunch of salmon, shrimp and oysters from Vital Choice and plan on increasing my salmon intake considerably now.

I am new to this forum and just started implementing the 20 min of sun before 10am and organic sulfur in water in the mornings. I use flux on my laptop, wear blue blockers at night, sleep on a grounding sheet, have a landline phone, DSL computer, no smart meter. I take low dose naltrexone to manage inflammation and depression. It helps a lot... but not enough. I did the Specific Carbohydrate Diet (SCD) for 6 years but in recent years I have added back in rice and wild rice at times as well as gluten. I know - not good - but I was experimenting to see how it would affect me. I also ate a lot of nightshades for a couple years right before the arthritis exploded in my body. Learned my lesson.

Any advice on what else to do considering that colostrum, whey and other suggested things in Leaky Gut Rx are not viable for me right now? Or are there brands that are recommended that people with cow dairy allergy can typically tolerate? THANK YOU.

### caroline — 2015-10-10T02:26:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-cannot-implement-many-of-the-suggestions.16961/post-178971>

This week is mental Health week In Australia. The radio station we listen to has been playing interviews all week with people who have depression.

The remarkable young woman this morning talked about swimming. After having severe depression come back and not wanting to leave the house .... she decided to force herself to go swimming. It was very hard for her at first - but now she appreciates the little bit of interaction with near naked people \[nowhere to hide!\] and the water and sun are having enormous benefits for her.

Please trust your own instincts and intuition. Dr. K. always tells us that the best Doctor is the one in our own head!

Are you doing C/T?
grounding?
are you sure that your grounding mat is okay? there can be dirty electricity......Some here have had serious issues with a grounding sheet.

How do you feel when you have a little rice?

Have you thought of a fecal transplant?

everyone's n=1 is different......

baby steps ....and keep notes every day - especially after meals

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-10T02:54:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-cannot-implement-many-of-the-suggestions.16961/post-178977>

Light in the AM is the key......increase DHA to use the purple light to create matter from light in your mitochondria to heal your gut.

### RobH — 2015-10-11T07:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-you-cannot-implement-many-of-the-suggestions.16961/post-179051>

# Thread: Monica's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 45
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Monica W. — 2015-10-10T00:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-178950>

Hey ya'll! This is the first time I've actually posted in the Forum proper but I've been fairly active on the FB group. I'm not typically one to "journal" but after reading Alyson's journal I feel a little inspired.

I found Jack via my other half, Gabe. We've both been "paleo" for a few years now and have been doing a lot to get healthier and happier and via the Circadian FB group he found Jack Kruse and we haven't looked back! Gabe has more serious health issues than I do and he suffers from insomnia, sleep apnea and Adrenal Fatigue. He started on this path to wellness before I did but I have totally embraced it and I feel so incredibly lucky to have such an awesome, optimal man to share my life with. But enough about him...I'm sure I'll talk about him plenty in future posts.

Since discovering this Optimal tribe I've embraced/done the following:

-   Daily CT via Gut Buster, Cryohelmet and/or Cool Fat Burner. We don't have a CT tank yet, but we're working on it.

-   Daily BAB
-   bought a 20 gauss Magnetico.

-   Daily UV/grounding/general nature time. I have 2 dogs so the daily walks are done barefoot. My neighbors know I'm a bit weird so only 1 has actually asked me why I don't wear shoes.

-   reduced nnEMF in my home by hard-wiring our computer and turning off the wifi at night. I do have a smart phone but I almost never talk on it. I've never been a big phone talker so cutting back on it wasn't hard!

-   removed all electronics from the bedroom
-   replaced all the bulbs in our home with yellow, red or orange bulbs. I also installed dimmers on our overhead lights and pendant fixtures so we can adjust them. I'm very much a DIY gal so I love doing stuff like this ;-)
-   Bought Blutech specs for work. I'm a web designer at Hamilton Beach and I'm on a computer all day at work so I'm doing everything I can to mitigate blue light issues.
-   I wear blue blockers (usually Uvex Skypers) every night and even wear them out in public \*gasp\*. My knitting group thinks I'm funny ;-)
-   I eat a shit ton of seafood. Gabe does most of the cooking and we eat shrimp or salmon most nights for dinner and I try and eat seafood a few times a week for lunch. We have a ton of great lunch places near where I work so there's lots of options for optimal lunches if I don't bring it from home.
-   I eat oysters when I can, but not as often as I'd like.
-   I've always drunk a ton of water, but I installed an under-sink 2-stage water filter under my kitchen sink so we can drink good filtered water. I also drink Pellegrino for dinner and when I eat out. I'd like to start buying RO water from the local Good Foods Grocery.

-   There's probably a lot of other things that I'm missing but I can talk about them at a later time.

Since starting down this Optimal path wonderful things have happened:

-   My period is now very regular with no spotting!

-   My myopia is improving. I've worn glasses since I was 29 and I find that I don't need to wear them most of the time now. The only time I want to wear them is when I'm tired and I really want the latest episode of Doctor Who to be crystal clear ;-)
-   I've lost weight and gained strength
-   My Rosacea has cleared up a lot
-   My energy level has become very consistent
-   I sleep like a log and wake up a few minutes before my alarm goes off on most mornings.
-   I don't crave ice cream as much as I used to. But I still crave it sometimes ;-)
-   My stomach/GI tract is much less volatile. I had my Gallbladder evicted back in 2001 and I struggled with a number of GI and IBS-style issues as a result. I still have to take a digestive enzyme with most meals but I'm so happy that I can eat a big meal and not have to run to the toilet shortly thereafter.
-   I'm becoming much more tolerant of the August heat here in Virginia. The CT really does amazing things.
-   My mind feels clearer/sharper most days. I still have foggy days, but overall I think the DHA has really helped improve my brain function.
-   The neck and back pain that I suffered from for YEARS has abated. I feel like the Magnetico paired with an excellent DNS doctor has fixed it!

Some things I'm still working on that need improvement:

-   My libido is very irregular, but thank goodness Gabe is very patient and very sweet.
-   My Vit D is low
-   My CRP number is higher than I'd like it to be.
-   My weight is not optimal
-   My myopia is annoying, but improving
-   My energy levels are more consistent but I'd like to have more energy on a daily basis

Anyway...that's about all I want to say for now. Thanks for listening!

Thanks to everyone I've met and interacted with on the FB group. You're all a bunch of weirdos and I feel incredibly lucky to have found my new tribe. I've rarely met such a welcoming, supportive group of people and I want you to all know I love you all! Well, maybe not Bee Brah...I didn't love him that much...

Jack, you're right about my job being the culprit. But for now I'm doing the best I can to mitigate the artificial light that I'm forced to endure here. BUT there is hope! My manager is very open to me working from home from time to time AND she said I can take my laptop outside and work out there when I like, which is a huge SCORE! My office building sits right on a lovely little man-man lake that I walk around every day between 10 and 11am, so sitting outside by it will be great.

Things are going pretty well but starting yesterday I had a very unexpected Rosacea flair-up. I can't for the life of me figure out the trigger and my face is very flushed and feels hot. I've been using Finacea just at night for many, many months and that's kept it nicely under control so I can't figure out why the fair-up. I'm also feeling generally a little unwell and I'm thirsty as the dickens and I'm sitting here at work with a huge pitcher of water so I can drink my weight in filtered water. Thank goodness we have a good 2-stage filter here at work! Yesterday my face was so flushed and irritated that I went home 2 hours early, put on my Cryohelmet put 2 small ice packs on my cheeks and laid down in the yard in the late afternoon sun. The cold on my head/face and the warm sun on the rest of my skin was bliss! I'm going to do it again today.

Thanks again for the feedback and encouragement and please let me know if you have any input on my Rosacea issue. It's so frustrating and uncomfortable.

### fitness@home — 2015-10-20T03:44:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-179918>

Genova Diagnostics Complete Hormone test is what I do annually. Need a doctor to order if you want medical insurance to cover most of the cost.

I currently have a CIGNA medical policy. My portion, or the "Patient Pay" amount, is about $159 or $169.

I've had the test done with BC/BS as my provider and the Patient Pay amount was comparable.

If you go this route, make sure you have a doctor that can read the results. Bad experience with the first test I had done...the doctor couldn't explain the results to me. Had to fire her...

Love my current BHRT doc.

### Monica W. — 2015-10-22T19:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-180154>

My apologies if you already read this post on the FB page, but I'm posting here too because I really appreciate the feedback on my situation. At this point I'm particularly interested in any medical/legal input I can get. I want to be well-armed in case my request for my co-worker to stop wearing her horrible perfume doesn't work out like I want it to. So here it is! Thanks in advance for your input.

Warning....long post.

Okay, so I did something yesterday at work that I've been wanting to do for a long time and I hope will improve my work environment. I went to HR to ask them to step in and talk to a co-worker who BATHES in stinky perfume. I am not the only one in my area who is aware of how strong it is, but to this point I'm the only one having any sort of physical reaction to it.

I work closely with this woman and I've always been very sensitive to her overwhelming perfume. We are good work friends and have typically had lunch together 3-4 days a week and there were days that I'd have to roll down the car windows and open the sunroof if she was riding in the car with me. I've had conversations with her in the past about how strong her scent is but her reaction was always "I don't care what other people think". Needless to say I was very disappointed in her disregard for me.

This week everything came to a head and it finally reached the point where her perfume triggered instant headaches, nausea, dizziness and face-flushing. So on Monday when she came into my cube and commented on how I didn't look well I took the opportunity to ask her to do me a favor and cut WAY back on the perfume. I explained to her that I've always been very sensitive to fragrances since I was young and I told her that her scent gives me headaches and nausea. She looked at me and said "Well...I'll cut back a little." I was very disappointed. I tried to handle the situation on my own but she didn't take me seriously.

Tuesday and Wednesday I talked to my manager AND her manager (who I'm very good friends with) about the situation and explained how I'm now having a severe reaction to the olfactory irritant. Both days I had to leave the office and work from home due to the headaches. Luckily my manager is very cool and totally understands.

Yesterday (Wed.) I went to HR and asked them to intervene and then I went home. HR talked to her around 2pm and said that if I have any further problems to let them know.

I JUST walked by her area on the way to the printer and I smelled her perfume. I don't know if it has permeated the cube fabric to the point that it's still lingering, but I'm terrified to actually come within 10 feet of her.

Sooo...I'm trying my best to optimize my work environment and I've been doing really well until this week when the perfume stench got to be too much and my body said "FUCK THIS SHIT" and launched into full reaction mode to the chemical irritant. This is really stressing me out and the sad thing is that perfume woman is awesome and talented and I honestly do like her company, but I can't come within 10 feet of her without feeling physically ill.

I want to give a shout out to [John Scott Major](https://www.facebook.com/john.s.major.7) for giving me some good medical info and advice. You are a Rock Star...may the Force be with you sir!

So to end this ramble...I'm sitting here stressing out that she said FUCK YOU to HR and is still wearing the scent. GAAAHH! What is a girl to do when she wants to have good, friendly work relationships AND optimize her work environment?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T21:16:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-180171>

Wear surgical masks. Buy them on line and keep them in your office. If you get flack about it let me know I'll send you a doctors note.

### Monica W. — 2015-10-22T21:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-180174>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=180171)
>
> Wear surgical masks. Buy them on line and keep them in your office. If you get flack about it let me know I'll send you a doctors note.

Thanks Jack! Wow...I never expected to get that response. I do have a pack of disposable masks with the over-the-ear loops that I wear when spreading organic fertilizer on my lawn (the airborne chicken poop can make me wheeze) or when I'm processing the green tripe I give my dogs. They are comfortable enough but I don't think I should have to wear them all day at work just because one woman bathes in eau de Disgusting.

As an update, I emailed HR an hour ago basically asking how the meeting with L. went and this is what the HR rep said: "I asked L. if she would please stop wearing the perfume and without hesitation she said she would and the tone of our meeting was professional and positive.
I have no doubt the issue is resolved."

I haven't physically seen L. today but I'm not smelling her so I'm hoping that she has stopped wearing it. I will give her a few days to let things settle down and then I may swing by her area to say hello. I just hope she doesn't get really hurt over this whole deal and can realize that it's the perfume that makes me ill and not her. She may have taken it very personally, but in my mind it's not personal...it's about a noxious liquid that she sprays herself with and not at all about her as a person. It's not like I'm asking her to shave her head, stop wearing ALL THE MAKEUP, or stop wearing black ;-)

It's just weird that this has happened to me because typically I'm pretty tolerant of all kinds of scents, but in the last few years it seems like EVERYTHING is hyper-scented and fake fragrances are everywhere. My current pet peeve is when I walk the dogs I smell the dryer sheets that my neighbors are using (the scent blows out from their dryer vents onto the street). Also, car deodorizers drive me nuts as does the ubiquitous Febreeze spray in the bathrooms here at work. Personally, I'd rather smell shit than the floral/shit miasma that I have to deal with. A few months ago a coworker and I asked that one of the restrooms be make scent-free and our request was denied. Maybe we will revisit that request in the future. Maybe I need to compile some "science" to back up the request.

### Monica W. — 2015-11-04T22:18:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-181162>

Update on the perfume issue: I'm still smelling the offending perfume on the co-worker but she says "...it's been almost two weeks since it's been in the building." But I swear I still smell it on her. Maybe her clothing is just really saturated with the stuff after wearing it for so many years.

Today I had a very nice follow-up conversation with our head HR manager. She just wanted to check in and see if I was feeling better. I told her I'm still smelling it but it's definitely less noticeable than before, but after talking with perfume girl this morning I've felt a bit headachy and stuffy in my sinuses.

So my new question is this: could I be suffering from some sort of rhinitis or even MCS? I just read up on Multiple Chemical Sensitivity and a lot of folks think it's total woo, and many doctors claim it isn't real. Something had occurred with me in the last month or so to make me super sensitive here in my office and I find myself reacting poorly to perfume and the Febreeze spray in the bathroom.

Another co-worker was having headaches and other symptoms and back in March she requested an air quality test be done here in the office. It was done by Hayes Microbial Consulting and the report (which I have a copy of) said "So basically the air quality inside is better than the air we breathe outside." The results I have are from a Spore Trap Analysis and I've attached a photo of one page from the report.

I've consulted with John Scott Major and he suggested I see and allergist. I live in the pollen capital of the country and I've always suffered from seasonal pollen allergies (Birch tree pollen is my Kryptonite), so I currently use Flonase to help with my nasal/sinus issues, but it's not helping me with the fragrance sensitivities.

Does anyone here know anything about MCS and if it's really a real thing? I read this page and [http://arizonaadvancedmedicine.com/multiple-chemical-sensitivity-mcs/](http://arizonaadvancedmedicine.com/multiple-chemical-sensitivity-mcs/)

### Monica W. — 2015-11-17T22:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monicas-journal.16964/post-182219>

So it's November here in Virginia and while it's been in the 60s and low 70s the last couple of weeks it still gets so darn and chilly at night and I'm just not craving CT in the evenings like I usually do. I've been doing my CT in the evenings with the Gut Buster and Cryohelmet because we don't have a CT tank yet, but we hope to pick up a 70 gal. Rubbermaid stock tank very soon. This past weekend I spent as much time as I could in my little backyard knitting in the sunshine and if we had a CT tank I would have been in it for part of the time. I wonder if I can knit while IN the CT tank. I'll let ya'll know how that works out ;-)

I work from 8am to 5pm so during the week I have to grab my D while I'm at work. I installed the D-Minder app on my phone (thanks Jena for the recommendation) and it's been working brilliantly. The last 2 weeks I've been going outside and taking a walk around the lake here at work for at least 30 minutes in a sleeveless top. All the other sheeple from the surrounding offices are out walking too but they're all wearing sunglasses and sweaters or even coats and I feel sorry for them. They all have their step counters of various types and I can't help but chuckle as they power-walk past me. Oh, and let's not forget the idiots out there in their sunglasses looking at their dumb phones while walking. I keep hoping I'll see one of them trip and fall into the water while looking at their screen. Why the hell can't they just look at the freakin' nature and enjoy their outside time. They are completely incapable of being in the moment. They think they're doing the right thing but they're oh so wrong and I feel sorry for them.

Oh, I got a membership to Palm Beach Tans and plan on hitting the tanning bed as soon as my Vit D opportunities start to wane. According to D-Minder I'll be able to get natural D until Dec 3. After that I won't be able to get it from the sun again until early January. The app doesn't give me the date for 2016, but the first day in 2015 was Jan 7, so I'm guessing it'll be about the same for 2016.

I'm also concerned about Gabe's inability to use the Gut Buster without getting cryroburn. Poor guy. We really need to get the CT tank for him. I've never gotten cryoburn but he seems to get it so easily on his stomach. He says it's because his redox is worse than mine. I feel bad for him because the GB/CFB are so damn convenient.

I've been bugging Gabe to start a journal but he's so swamped with homework and the D&D campaign he's DMing. Hopefully he will start one soon. He has a LOT to say/write about and he really wants Jack's feedback.

I'm trying oil pulling for the very first time right now! I've been at it for about 25 minutes now while sitting here at my desk and it's not as disgusting and I thought it would be. I have a sore spot on my gum where I whacked myself with my toothbrush so it feels very soothing.

That's it for now. Thanks for your time!

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Life has a redox sequence and that sequence is tied to light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-has-a-redox-sequence-and-that-sequence-is-tied-to-light.16965/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-10T02:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-has-a-redox-sequence-and-that-sequence-is-tied-to-light.16965/post-178970>

Redox potentials were important because only certain redox reactions occur at a given redox level in an environment. If redox reactions occurred in the order of their thermodynamic possibility, reductants (e.g., organic material—say CH2O) would transfer electrons to the lowest unoccupied electron level, which is oxygen (O2). When the number of electrons are greater than the number of unoccupied electron levels of oxygen, the higher unoccupied electron level will be filled up, namely, NO3-, NO2-, MnO2(s), and so on (Stumm and Morgan, 1996). The sequence of microbially mediated redox processes may be seen in the schematic shown in Figure 2-2 reproduced from Sigg (2000). The oxidant with the highest redox potential will be utilized first, followed by the one with the next lower potential.

This sequence has been prominently observed in the vertical distribution of components in a nutrient-enriched (eutrophied) lake and also in closed systems containing excess organic matter, such as batch digesters (anaerobic fermentation unit) (Stumm and Morgan, 1996). Most of the reactions are microbially mediated (except for the reduction of MnO2 and FeOOH(s)), and they are, therefore, paralleled by an ecological succession of microorganisms: aerobic heterotrophs, denitrifiers, fermenters, sulfate reducers, and methane bacteria. It has also been observed that higher energy-yielding reactions take precedence over lower energy-yielding processes (Stumm and Morgan, 1996). The aerobic oxidation of CH2O yields the highest energy (125 kJ per equivalent), followed by the CH2O oxidation with nitrate (119 kJ per equivalent), and so on.

Redox potentials in natural waters range from about –400 mV to +800 mV at pH 7 to 8. They are bounded in the negative range by the reduction of H2O to hydrogen gas (H2(g)) and in the positive range by the oxidation of H2O to O2(g) are keys from Sigg, 2000 and Stumm and Morgan (1966), shows four representative ranges of redox potentials in water and sediment. Water saturated with oxygen may have a redox potential within the first range (710 to 800 mV at pH 7 to 8). In systems where some oxygen has been consumed, the redox potentials may range between –100 to 710 mV (Range 2) at pH 7 to 8 (Stumm and Morgan, 1996). The potential is not, however, **_sensitive to oxygen concentrations and has been observed to remain nearly constant down to values of 0.1% O2 saturation_** (Greatz et al, 1973). Within the second range, solid iron three (Fe(III)), and manganese three and four (Mn(III,IV) are reduced to soluble iron two (Fe(II)) and manganese two (Mn(II)) when organic matter is mineralized. Phosphorus, which is initially bound to Fe(III) hydroxides, is released to the overlying water as a result of the reduction of Fe(III) solid phases. Under even more reduced conditions (Range 3, -240 to –100 mV at pH 7 to 8), the concentration of Fe(II) and Mn(II) rises further; SO42- begins to be reduced, accompanied by the precipitation of Manganous Sulfide (MnS) and Ferrous Sulfide (FeS), and the formation of pyrite.

This was a made a big deal in the Vital Question by Nick Lane.

At oxic-anoxic interfaces, which could be in the deeper parts of a lake, at the sediment- water interfaces, or within sediments, Fe and Mn may transform between the dissolved and solid forms. Fe and Mn may be observed to go through a sequence of upward diffusional transport of their reduced forms, oxidation to the insoluble forms, and subsequent settling and reduction back their soluble forms. This sequence normally occurs within a relatively narrow redox cline (Stumm and Morgan, 1996).

Many researchers have studied the relationships between oxidation-reduction potential and the physical, chemical, and biological processes in soil-water systems. One of the pioneers in this field was Mortimer (1941, 1942). Mortimer studied the factors which control the rate of nutrient supply to phytoplankton in systems of lake water and sediment deposits.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-10T02:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/life-has-a-redox-sequence-and-that-sequence-is-tied-to-light.16965/post-178972>

[http://scholar.lib.vt.edu/theses/available/etd-01102003-162857/unrestricted/(08)Lit\_Rev\_2.pdf](http://scholar.lib.vt.edu/theses/available/etd-01102003-162857/unrestricted/\(08\)Lit_Rev_2.pdf)

# Thread: D-Ribose Ok for Leptin Rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NadoRo — 2015-10-10T03:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-178979>

I'm wondering if D-Ribose is ok while on the Leptin Rx?

If I take it 2-3 times a day, that averages out to an extra 10-15 grams of carbs in addition to what's in my food..likely bringing my carb count higher than the recommended 30 grams.

Does its benefits for mitochondrial function outweigh the carb concerns in this case?

### NadoRo — 2015-10-10T04:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-178981>

After getting further along in my reading of the D-Ribose thread...I'm going to answer my own question and say that, Yes, it is OK. Consensus seems to be that the body doesn't treat D-Ribose as it does other sugars, so not a concern.

### DavidL — 2015-10-26T17:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-ok-for-leptin-rx.16966/post-180490>

D-Ribose is great after a workout in the afternoon outdoors.

# Thread: Patty Cakes at the Beach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 42
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-10T19:34:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-178997>

I am finally going to take the plunge (no pun intended) and start a journal. The time is right!

I'm in my late 50s, and have had asthma since I was a kid but it's very mild and really only exercise induced. I also have Reynauds which really only affects my toes but I do have to keep the buggers out of the cold water or they turn white! About eight years ago I was diagnosed with Hashimotos and that started my focus more on health as an adult. I was raised by my dad and my "health nut" mom who was always trying whacky things when we were kids. She tried things to "cure" my asthma like getting a Chihuahua that I would sleep with so that I could breathe the oils from the dogs skin. I loved that dog but she did nothing for my asthma! Other crazy stuff happened but she was always reading and trying to do the next right thing for the health of her family. We now know that some of the craziness was just that...CRAZY! Everyone wanted my mom to be their mom because she was fun, adventurous, and smart. Most people who knew her saw that I have grown up to be her. Now if they only knew some of the stuff I have done following these protocols they would say I have surpassed her craziness. I am proud of it.

I started following Dr. Kruse back during his days on MDA. I continued to be amazed by his ingeniousness concepts for optimal living and instituted most if not all of them. I dove in and tried everything I could. I had more energy, felt fantastic, lost weight, and the bonus was that my face even changed to soften up and not look so intense, maybe even a bit younger.

Then I started working full time again! BAM! With one fell swoop I was overwhelmed by new concepts, pushed by my boss to learn more, and slowly I couldn't keep up with everything that I was doing outside of my job. I made a choice to put the job first. Today I have learned a lot, put in my time, and received my professional certification but in the process I have gained 15# back, feel just OK, have less energy, and I realize I let too many of the protocols slip. But in the past month I have climbed aboard and started to implement all over again.

For the past two weeks I get as much sun as I can. I get about 10 minutes in the early morning and around 11am I go to the beach and walk in the ocean above my knees to get some magnesium, sun, and a bit of exercise. This has proven to be the best way for me to absorb magnesium. The water isn't very cool right now so I really can't count it as CT but it's something. I get about 30-40 minutes during weekdays. I'm lucky I work about 5 blocks from the beautiful So Cal beach. On weekends I get about 60-90 minutes in the early morning and some during the rest of the day depending on what I need to be doing.

I'm behind on a couple of webinars and way behind on blogs. But I have already started reading and trying to understand. I am not a sciency person and a lot of this is hard to understand but I put forth the best effort I can to understand the overall concepts and use the info to help my husband and I. In the beginning he wasn't as on board as he is now. The guy goes and sits in the sun now every morning (he works from home) for almost 2 hours. I am so jealous! He is lucky and has been healthy, thin, with no present disease factors all of his life. But he is willing to work the protocols to keep himself that way.

We eat epi-paleo , although I feel we need more seafood. Currently we eat it about 4x a week. I have a great source for oysters and know I should be eating more of those! YAH!!! But we both hate shucking them.

Although I have been absent from the forums for the past year I have been able to lurk in the background every few months just to get a little bit of info. I feel truly blessed to be a part of such a community with wide-ranging thinkers and just nice people. Thanks Dr. Kruse for your wisdom and the bat shit craziness you have provided to us all!

Today I am going to get some oysters, buy my Magnetico, do Pilates, and read more blogs. A good day has started....

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-10T21:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-179013>

I work on the second floor of my office which I know is not good. But my office is very cold so I feel like maybe I am mitigating the non-grounding a bit. In the morning it's so cold in there that it's almost intolerable without a jacket on. I share my office with a male co-worker and he even dons a jacket some mornings due to the temp.

Right now I think my adrenals have taken a beating and I will be testing them as well as melatonin (which I have never tested before). I have the kits so I'll be doing this next week during the work week. My thryoid and labs seem to be on a good swing right now. I take a Cytomel in the morning and WP Thyroid +Cytomel in the afternoon and they seem to be doing the trick. I want to do as much as possible to help support my thyroid as time goes on.

I am post menopausal so hormones are shot. I take 200 mgs Progesterone at night but estrogen is an issue as I can't seem to detox and gain weight when I take it. I work out with weights 3x a week and cannot seem to gain any muscle or much strength and since my testosterone is practically nil that's probably why. The recent labs show this all to be true.

Vit D is in the mid 60s so hopefully all this sun will raise it more.

Sleep is OK but I wake usually 2-4x/day. My DHEA is low (80s) and need to deal with that as well.

### caroline — 2015-10-11T04:17:43+0300

welcome to journal land Patty Cakes!

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-13T05:12:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-179286>

Thanks! I've read UB 22 and 23 so on to UB24. I need to print the blog out and hopefully get a chance tomorrow. I've put myself on restriction - no computer after 7pm and as we loose light even more, the timing will get even tighter.

I bought the Magnetico on Saturday and can't wait for it to arrive. I'm watching Craig's List for the other piece. I also had some oysters that were just plain too big! Sounds weird but they were so gigantic that we had to chew them into pieces to even swallow them. Did a better job getting the shells open this time. Probably all the practice over the past year or so. We sure did feel good the next morning!

I can't wait for the Nov Light webinar. I think with all my reading I have figured it out which will make the webinar easier to follow and I can take notes on the things I need to change. It's rather ironic about the morning UV exposure: I had a basal cell cancer found last year and treated it and now it is gone. I'm a little concerned about this and getting sun but not enough to keep me from sun. If the morning sun helps mitigate blue light exposure and basal cell carcinoma is not deadly (or so I'm told) then I choose sun. But there is still a little devil on my shoulder whispering words of caution. More reading obviously...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T06:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-179289>

basal cell cancer has zero to do with the sun. that is just a modern precept. Most BCC occur in people who spend more time inside out of the sun. [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1390243/Sun-CAN-protect-skin-cancer.html#](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1390243/Sun-CAN-protect-skin-cancer.html#)

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-15T04:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-179533>

My Magnetico came today. I got the 10 gauss and will watch on craigslist for another 10. I'm sure I can use it. It's still sitting outside waiting for my guy to come home so we can put it on the bed. I'll report more on how my sleep goes. I wake up 2-3 times a night now but some of that is the temperature (too hot). I'm quite curious to see how I do on the Magnetico.

So far this week I've had fish everyday. lots of salmon, fresh oysters, and canned to supplement. I feel pretty good.

I haven't been out to the beach this week. We are having horrible humidity and 90s temps. We are down an AC compressor in our building and I have so much to do that I talked myself out of heading for my beach/water walk. I feel so good when I am outside by the beautiful beach walkin gin the water. It's my happy place so I hope to get out there tomorrow.

My curfew of 7pm to be off the computer is here and I need to catch up on a couple of blogs I printed out.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-18T22:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-179800>

I still wake up a couple of times a night and it's not to go to the bathroom. I don't use an alarm clock and wake up naturally around 530am most days. But today I slept until 7am!

Investigating some other light sources since it's hard for me to get more than 10-15 min before 10am. I have the SolarGlo light that I want to put in my office. Hopefully I won't get flack for it. And looking into an intranasal light device. I read most of what is on the forum and still curious to find out more.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-24T04:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-180312>

Friday night dinner...the best ever! Fresh raw salmon, ahi tuna, and of course, oysters. My guy now has the hang of shucking and knows the value of them so is eating them. The fish monger even suggested some raw live shrimp he had in the back. I got four never having them and not knowing if I could peel them alive. While I was slicing the salmon my guy went ahead and cut the heads off and peeled them. OMGosh these shrimpies were amazing with a little lemon juice on them. So filling and rich! YUM! I don't think he enjoyed the fact that they were live and he wanted to be humane but it had to be done somehow. I told him I wanted to save the shells, head, and whatever to make some broth/soup. Somehow he didn't hear me and as I was getting the pot with some celery and onion ready he promptly pushed all the shells into the garbage disposal and GRRRRRRRR...they were gone! So no soup for us.  I wanted to post a pic of the feast but I have to look up how to do that. Aargh!

Thursday I was able to get the reptile lamp setup at work. I had it on for three-four hours but not in a good spot. I tried clamping it to my double monitors but then that bright light was shining in my eyes, which I know is good but maybe a bit too much for me right now. My eyes are very light pale green and my right eye has Adie pupil so bright light can be painful. I deal with it when I am outside sans sunglasses getting my outdoor AM UV but being two feet away from that bulb is a bit much. SO I had to take the lamp off my monitor. I have to have a setup that I can easily take down so that I am not "caught" putting my own electrical items in the office. This is against company policy. I have an idea to get an expanding pole and put it near my desk and clamp the light there. Easy to setup and take down after a few hours of UV. My boss could care less and finds my "weird healthy things" amusing. However, he believes the the light issue and how it is a helpful treatment. Ohhhh he doesn't know the half of it.

The reptile light is the only step I have taken toward getting the right light. I feel like I need more knowledge and am keeping notes on all things "light" so I can pull it all together. I want to do the red and the black but still not exactly sure how to implement it the right way without creating any havoc. I do have red lights in the bathrooms for night time and orange bulbs in the bedrooms also for evenings. We have been avid blue-blocker wearers since they were first mentioned a few years ago. That prootcol never slipped.

The best thing I can say for this week is that my guy has gone outside every morning (he works from home) and stayed outside for over an hour. I think he probably could use more but this is a huge start. He sits at the computer most of the day so I am going to get him setup with a light as well. He has actually asked for my help in getting him the light he needs. This is more than he would do a couple of years ago so we are making progress with this stuff! He is the healthy one so maybe he needs less than I do but I want to keep him healthy. If I don't who will take care of me when I am old??? LOL

The other piece I forgot to mention in my first journal entry was that we both had the telomere testing done a couple of years ago. He scored in the 99% and I scored 98%. It sounds like we both may live a long life but I don't want to be cranky and crotchety when I get older. Mostly I don't want to groan when getting in and out of the car!

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-24T18:43:34+0300
