Great thoughts Caroline. He uses two desks and two computers with only one being a laptop for portability so I don't think that will happen. We do have tile in our house with concrete below on sand or dirt so I think there is grounding in the house. Maybe that's wishful thinking. I'm curious to see if he continues throughout the winter because he is definitely not going to partake in CT. Already tried that and he won't do it.

I stopped typing this and have been outside for the last hour +. There's the guy talking about taking his laptop outside to do his research.May miracles never cease. He also said he wants me to figure out what lights to put in the entire house: red, reptile, black, whatever is best and we will start to change them out. YAH...a big win for me!

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T04:01:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-180759>

I noticed the last couple of days I have been sans sunglasses and blinking a lot to cool my eyes. This works! My car is 3-1/2 years old and I haven't opened the sun roof since the first month because the brightness of the sun hurt my large pupil. Now I drive home with the sunroof open, sans sunglasses, and blinking like a crazy person!

I've been sleeping quite well on the Magnetico and I'm happy I got it. Something went wrong today but I was super exhausted most of the day even though I only woke up once last night. Solar flares...maybe? Not sure.

I'm continuing with the sun (not as much as I'd like), UV light in my office at work which I try to keep on most of the day but I move about a lot at work due to meetings (including drive-by meetings) so I'm not with my light the whole time.

Today I got the book Light in Shaping Life and so excited to spend some time with it this weekend in the sun! I'm hoping it will give me a deeper understanding of using light for the health of my body.

I realized this week I need to do a better job on weekends of planning my fish intake throughout the week. If I stop by the fish monger on the way home from work it takes me an extra 45 minutes. I've done it before and will do it again but if I plan head I won't have to.

### fitness@home — 2015-10-30T15:00:59+0200

My commute home is 60-90 minutes (depending on traffic conditions). That part sucks but at least I get some sunshine...

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-31T04:10:44+0200

### Patty Cakes — 2015-11-01T19:15:08+0200

### Patty Cakes — 2015-11-02T19:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-180998>

It's Monday and I have the day off! Had a couple of appointments scheduled and was going to get my sunshine in. I am one of the unfortunate souls who own a "clean" diesel VW. I have this additive that has 4.5 gallon tank that has to be kept fairly full. If you run it down too low the car won't start. Well, already being cursed with a "defective" vehicle I don't really trust much of anything on this car. I had issues with the battery just dying after 3 years so I put a new one in. A week later the car won't start and took two sets of jumper cables hooked to two batteries to get it started. Defective battery!!! Got the new battery installed and all is well until the diesel additive gets low and my guy puts in around 2.5 gallons of it. Followed the VW protocol on how to start after filling up with additive and BAM! nothing happens. It has been programmed not to start if the additive is not kept filled. So this morning the car is being towed to the dealer just to reset the code so I can start the damn thing!. On the flipside I get to sit out here in the sun and collect more rays! The is always a way to twist the bad into something good.

Sorry if there are any weird typos...I'm sitting in the sun and cannot see my keyboard too well.

### Destiny — 2015-11-12T12:50:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-181760>

No, believe me, Caroline, one move was plenty ! It was actually 3 moves in one- had to sell a house, move into an apartment before getting a new house. The job things was not easy either but it all can be done. My family is glad that we got out of the NYC area. We live in much less populated area with much less of everything and close to the beach. I feel really good and going by Dr. Kruse's basics every day- sun, water, magnetism, a little computer time, in bed by 8 PM and out at 6 AM and still eating no sugar, no fruit, etc. . For all of that I getting back this - less stressed out, hopefully healthier and getting much more out of my life.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-11-14T19:03:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-181977>

I've been doing the Vielight now every night for an hour in each nostril. I seem to sleep better but that is all I notice to this point. It's only been a couple of weeks so I am anticipating more changes.

I upped my fish intake. It's almost every day. I just need to plan and I can do it. Canned salmon and canned smoked oysters are my fallback and I have a lot of those in the house.

Today I woke up with what I think is a bladder infection. Aargh! I've had two in my lifetime and it's been over 15 years since the last one. I am peeing blood and clots so it's pretty gross. Sorry TMI. Cranberry juice?, wait and see?, flush out with lots of water?, just not sure what I'm supposed to do as this isn't something I've really had to deal with much before.

Still getting sun on weekends. Been hectic at work and haven't been able to get out in the sun much during the day so I've turned on my lizard lamp and hoping that will help. I do notice when I am out in the real sun for at least two hours I get excellent sleep at night. I wish I could get out every day. Going out right now to get some purple (my fav color since I was a tiny child)!

### Patty Cakes — 2018-12-19T22:08:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-258472>

It's been a long time but I am still here listening to all the webinars and reading as many blogs as I can. I find I do best with the blogs when I print them and read them in the sun. I have missed many blogs but I am working toward catching up.

My current action plan is wear my blue blockers at night as well as since July 2018 I wear them at work, keep the fluorescent lights in my office off all day, get the morning sun, take a walk at lunch for more sun, get 1-2 hours each weekend day or more if I can, and eat as much fish as I can. I currently don't take any supplements. I've started wearing scarves around my thyroid lately as well. I am the freak at work and I really do not care at all. I am strong in my beliefs that all this crap is going on can harm us and I want to mitigate it as best I can.

I got a cold and decided to take the day off. So I will be spending at least 3 hours out in the sun today. There are so many great podcasts that I need to catch up on from this past weekend where Dr. Kruse was in LA.

I tested my sun intake with D Minder to see how much vit D I was getting over a six month period. I have VDR issues and know I need more sun than all. Plus I had laser eye surgery years ago before I knew how bad it was for my health. Ultimately, it was fairly close and I recognize that I need more sun. I drive with my moon roof open every time I get in the car to help that and it just plain feels good. I am doing what I can with my knowledge and will get better the more info I garner.

### Patty Cakes — 2018-12-21T03:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-258611>

Yah, a record for something (although not a great thing). My posting has suffered because I decided something had to give or I would be on this darn computer 14-16 hours a day. So I made an executive decision and stopped posting although kept reading and listening. Currently I am working out a system of how I will make it all work but I won't sacrifice my time in gaining more knowledge. And of course I work on a computer most of the day....hurry up retirement...you cannot get here soon enough.

I may be known as the whacky gal at work but each little comment I make to my cohorts will make an impact sooner or later. They will have heard me comment so much that it will start to sound familiar and I have already seen two come around and ask me more.

I felt so good yesterday and then today I woke up feeling like I fell of the wrong side of cliff with this cold. I may take tomorrow off as well to beat this monster with some sunshine. Three hours of sun was just amazing. I want to do that everyday (or more). I played outside all the time as a kid since my mom made us go outside to do anything it didn't matter to her. She knew the value of nature. She took us on so many adventures to the beach, the mountains, etc. where ever we could pack a lunch and only be gone a day. It was a great childhood.

I'm getting off the computer to get away from the blue light for now.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-01-06T04:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-259542>

I started 2019 out with a morning walk and an afternoon walk on the beach. The afternoon one was probably 3 hours long. It was a beautiful day to be out in nature.

I've seen every sunrise since Christmas even though today was gray and cloudy. I still drove to the top of the hills to see the sun rise even though I didn't see the sun just something brighter in behind the gray.

On my agenda is to order more salmon roe. This is a great little lunch to bring to work that doesn't take any preparation. Also need to replace out a couple of light bulbs in my bathroom. I have an organized routine for the morning since I am out of the house by 6am and I don't have to turn the lights on much at all and when I do I don the orange glasses. I keep them on during my drive since most folks are driving with their lights on at that time of morning.

I'm making some little changes that I can continue and not get overwhelmed with everything to do and remember.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-02-11T04:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-262825>

I have been doing quite well chasing sunrises. The valley is too low from the hilltops and the sun takes a long time to get to me. This morning I found the BEST place to get my dose of sunrise. The tope of a little mountain near (15 min) me. There were a few jokers there blasting musica in their cars iwth all their windows rolled up. I felt very priveldged and grateful to have the info on the sun that I do. Sunsets are my next little thing to conquer in the light arena.

I've also made some other changes. I have orange glasses that I wear for work since I am on the conputer A lot. But I finally invested in some Ra Optics red glassess for evening. I LOVE THEM! They are so much darker and do make a difference. Now we need to get my partner a pair. He is really good about following my lead on these things. We have fish/sushi/salmon roe almost every night and since I love fish this is an enjoyable modification for us.

Catching up on the backlog of webinars, Q&As, and blogs is happening slowly. Honestly, I spend time reading and searching almost every evening. But I'm sure I am making a little headway it jsut doesn't feel like it sometimes.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-04-07T05:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-267522>

I'm still seeing the sunrise adn some sunsets and getting sun int he middle of the day. Starting to build the D back up again. I really do like the D Minder app as a mechanism for tracking. It may be off due my my own N-1 but at least there is some guidance. Loving these longer days as it's easier to get everything done on the weekends and still have sun time. My BP is totally normal now after all the sun I got last summer. It really didn't go up this weinter as I ontined to be out during the lunch hour. Now my focus is learning more on water and how this will help my conditions.

I have five autoimmune diseases starting with Asthma (which as I have said earlier is exercized induced or if I have a cold. I got Reynauds in my 30s, Hashimotos in my 40s, another skin one is my 50s (can't remember the name of it), and I don't want to continue this trend.

I feel like I still need to learn a lot more about light but I really need to gather info about the water. My goal for the next few months.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-06-26T06:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-273291>

I have been doing some reading but not catching up very fast.

My vit D is up and the D Minder app seems to be quite spot on. I am currently at 55 which I did get down to a low of 29 in the middle of winter. I get out for a walk everyday around 12:30 for an hour and then race home to get a 30-60 minute session in. Sometimes longer at night but the D is gone. I stay out until sunset or whenever I need to head inside. I like jsut sitting outside and doing stuff out there. I have the best tan I have had in my entire life. I'm not dark by any means with my Fitzpatrick II or III, but still dark for me! My goal is to be close to 100 by October 1st.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-07-14T23:03:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-274291>

I am re-listening to old webinars because I don't seem to have a lot of basic knowledge. I'm learning a lot more than I did the first time around. Most of these are over my head and the science is not my thing. But when I know a little and go back and listen again I get so much more. I have really wondered about my eye clock since I have a few eye issues. I have aides pupil (enlarged non-contracting pupil) and I had RK on my eyes in 1990 and in 2009ish I had PRK. The surgeries have had zero complications and I don't even get dry eyes. But it dawned on me that I must be taking in more blue light in my office because of the size of the pupil. But again maybe the science doesn't show that. I have been wearing orange glasses at work for over a year now so I feel like I am protecting it as best I can for now. However I have thought maybe I need a red light panel in my office to help balance the light. I keep the fluorescents off and although there are windows they have a smoked film on them and do not open. On the flip side the same reasoning would lead me to believe that the size of my pupil contributes to more sunlight going into that pupil. I'm sure more reading and relistening will help me understand that.

My Vit D is currently reading 79 on D Minder so I am moving in the right direction. I'm getting 3-4 hours of sun each weekend day and about two hours on weekdays. I feel as if I am living my life to get sun and it feels great! I am trying to make the connections of all the things the sun does to processes inside our bodies. That takes longer than getting sun!

### Patty Cakes — 2019-07-21T05:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-274700>

Last weekend I had an amazing day that just seemed to not end. I felt like time was on my side and everything I needed to do, which was a lot I was able to complete and I still had time to spare. I want days like this all the time.

I discovered this morning while walking onthe beach and listening to the April 2016 webinar that some of the info seemed to make sense. But I remember listing to this one back at the time it was pur out and it was pretty deep stuff. There is a lot I may not remember but in all the info I am taking in some of it and it makes more sense.I think this webinar is one that will take more than two listenings for me to understand it all the way.

I'm continuing to make changes. I'm bored with the people who think I'm a nut. I've been made fun of a lot of my life because I'm not afraid to fly my freak flag high. Especially at my age now...I just don't care. However, I don't waste my breath on those I know won't "hear" me. It's just too exhausting. I walk at lunch every day with a co-worker who is very mainstream and I say some things to him and he listens. After a while he took his glasses off when we went walking to get sun in his eyes. Then he started rolling his shirt sleeves up to get more sun on his skin. He never calls me a nut or coo-coo beans but I have realized that he is listening and implementing in his own way. I believe there will be more changes as time goes on.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-08-02T05:00:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-275351>

My Farm visit was fun yet interesting. I specifically learned about myself and of course that I need to leave California. Yep, knew that was coming.

I really want to move to Mexico and have wanted to for some time. But that is only after I toured around the USA a bit to see if there is a good place for me to move to as Sprinter Van Patty. The Mexico move may not be the first move but as I figure this out I will know where to go. In the meantime, I'll mitigate what I can and spend every waking moment in the sun except when I'm at work.I have the whole naked sun soak down now.

I have no personal attachment to this state. I'm a minimalist and don't have much "stuff" so it's easy. I actually spoke to someone at work today to see if she is interested in applying for my position when I leave so I don't leave my boss high and dry. Funny thing is now that I'm talking about leaving so is he!

### Patty Cakes — 2019-08-14T06:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-275936>

Dr Kruse said I would need 3-4 bottles of DDW/day to help combat the horrible San Diego environment. I live almost an hour from SD but I think that probably doesn't matter much. So I am doing a bio hack on one bottle of DDW/day and will test after six weeks to see if that makes any difference. If not I will probably try two and test again. I have hope that I can mitigate what I can for now.

I'm also orking on getting some kind of red light in my office. I have alwyas kept the flouresents off and have been wearing the oragne glasses for almost two years at work. Now it's time to up the game with some red light to balance all the blue in there. I had a red (660nm) light I brought in from home and no one seemed to care that my office looked like Satan's Den. The light adds a bit more brightness to the room. Unfortunately I couldn't get it setup correctly and I feel I need more in there. A small panel maybe. Researching the forun and other places to find a decent one for now that is small enough to use in a 10x10 space.

The good news is that my friend I brought to the Farm has been blasting her naked body with sun and has brought her vit D up (according to D Minder which was a little off for me). She has taken what she learned and is telling her friends. I really felt she was the best person so come with me for her ability to move a lot of people to action.

### Patty Cakes — 2019-09-26T04:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-278920>

I'm still getting a couple of hours of sun every day and more on weekends. It's quite a habit and really an obcession. I plan my life around how I can get the most sun and not get fired. With the sun change I have to take a break and get out to see the morning rising sun after I have started work. I basically now keep the orange glasses on all day. I'm going to see if I can get my IT people to put IRIS on my laptop. For home I just received my big red light so I have been suing that 3-4 times a week. Still making some new changes.

### Inger — 2019-09-26T19:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-278959>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=278920)
>
> I'm still getting a couple of hours of sun every day and more on weekends. **It's quite a habit and really an obcession. I plan my life around how I can get the most sun and not get fired.** With the sun change I have to take a break and get out to see the morning rising sun after I have started work. I basically now keep the orange glasses on all day. I'm going to see if I can get my IT people to put IRIS on my laptop. For home I just received my big red light so I have been suing that 3-4 times a week. Still making some new changes.

LOL Patty Cakes... are you my sister?  this is exactly how I do it too

### Patty Cakes — 2019-10-22T07:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-280937>

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) I think we have to do what we have to do and just make it work.I finagle a lot in my life to get sun. I found myself making a choice the other day should I race home and get a couple of hours of sun or get groceries and run errands. You know I chose sun! I was so happy just driving home because I knew I made the right decision. Afterwards I ran my errands. Yes it took more time because I had to drive back into civilization but I felt like the decision, albeit super simplistic was the right one.

I have always enjoyed the out of doors and activities around that. But now it's pretty great just hanging out int he sun. I'm still driving to the beach on weekends and walking in the water for 1.5 hours. That's just my appetizer. I go grocery shopping and get home just to catch some good early afternoon sun. I take a break after about two hours to get some cooking done for my lunches during the week, then back outside. My sleep is great and I feel good.

I am using the red light about 4-5 times a week. I keep my thyroid close to it and the rest of my body a little further away. My sleep is usually pretty good (because of my sun habits) but this has taken the sleep to the next level on the nights that I use it. My cat sits by it if I don't turn it off. She knows what's good for her!!

### Inger — 2019-10-22T10:14:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-280941>

I love this Patty!  That is how I live and it is so fun to read you describing it...lol

I even sometimes tell my second boss (the Vet, I work there about once a week in addition to my full time work), I cant come work for her because I am so busy/I have an appointment, only because I am spending the whole day naked in the sun....
But I need it! I know what my body needs. And I do have an appointment. With the sun!

And if the Vet does not get everything super clean because I do not come, no one will die from that  she just get very stressed out (she could take another housekeeper but she is so complicated no one seems to want to stay, they all leave... I can take it because I do love her, but only when I know I have the extra energy).
But that is her choice. I do not clean my home either if I need to be outside. I do not get stressed because of that. Outside always comes first!
That s where I get my life-force.

and yes, I often do not go grocery shopping at all for days even if I really should need it, only because I am too busy spending time with my lover... The Sun
I just use whatever foods I have in the freezer  and oysters. Works too!

### Inger — 2019-10-22T10:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patty-cakes-at-the-beach.16967/post-280942>

we are so lucky to be able to choose how we spend our day
Sure I do work a lot but all my time off I can do whatever I want. That feels just sooo good

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-10-23T18:48:12+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-is-the-key-to-redox-potential-in-cells.16969/post-179046>

Like the strings on a violin or the pipes of an organ, the proteins in the human body vibrate in different patterns. The symphony of life is played by the momentum and energy contained within light frequencies from the sun. Light interacts with electrons in the lattice but the momentum is conserved in phonons that make the atomic lattice of the cell membrane and tensegrity system. All phonons are controlled by magnetic fields generated in mitochondria. Mitochondria is where matter is created or destroyed using light as its raw material.

Phonons are controlled by magnetic fields............and someone with a history of cancer has to be concerned about poor magnetic fields and poor quantum yields from a lack of AM UV and IR light. Why? Molecular vibrations or oscillations are the short answer. They can leads to distortions of cell size and shape that can lead to altered autophagy and apoptosis ala Tensegrity 9 blog. This limits the randomness or entropy in cell membranes and that "control" or quietness, is transmitted to the Tensegrity system to all proteins, to the nucleus and to the mitochondria. Any thing that gives stability of oscillations lowers entropy and controls the speed limit of how proteins are replaced by ubiquitin. You need purple light and DHA to interact to give that stability. The stability is built by the DC electric current to make a huge EZ around the proteins, mitochondria, and nucleus. This is what mito-nuclear coaptation is fundamentally all about that I laid out in Ubiquitination 5. What lowers molecular vibrations in living systems like us? UVB light does it best (not the only type of UV that can do it) and the Earth's native magnetic field. Non native fields destroy these relationships. How? It decreases Vitamin D3 levels, triplet state oxygen and nitrogen radicals by lowering the quantum yield to light. What does Vitamin D3 do fundamentally ? It is a calcium channel blocker on our surfaces or our eye,skin, and gut and lung. What did I tell you calcium does in eukaryotic cell membranes when I spoke about their antenna function of the Cell Membrane in Ubi 14? Calcium is the concrete of the lipid bilayer in all eukaryotic cell membranes. When you efflux calcium you have no concrete and your cell membranes cannot store the momentum energy of light within its atomic lattice. When you cannot store energy, you radiate to your environment. You sweat light. This is the story of EMF 2.

I wrote an entire blog in the Ubi 14 series about that antenna function. Now think about what impact that has when UVB light hits your surfaces...........it lowers molecular vibrations of the atoms there when sunlight hits it. This contains light while improving optical signaling. What if you live in a low UV environment? What if you live in a city with a low quantum yield? It means you have a lower threshold to handle electromagnetic non native energies. You will radiate energy to your environment and lose the ability to make matter from this stored light within your mitochondria.

Disease like MS destroy the AQA4 gates in the CNS and you lose control of water flows between glial cells and neurons. This destroys optical signaling and results in loss of neurologic function. You need light to regenerate the components of AQA 4 gates to make MS vanish. This is why MS is explosive in its incidence and prevalence when people are further away from the equator where light assimilation is more stable and less variable to environmental conditions. It is not a food story, and never was. foods that absorb UV light improve it to a degree. MS however, is a story of light and QED physics and the destruction and creation of matter in the nervous system. WiFi and microwaves limit your ability to recreate and regenerate the matter destroyed in MS. Microwaves cause CM and the Tensegrity system inside a cell to vibrate and oscillate. This also causes us to lose light to our environment. The more microwaves and mobile technology you use or allow in your life the less Vitamin D3 you can make even when you are in full spectrum sun light. The reason for this is microwaves are EMF's that form waves that can create interference patterns for UVB/UVA/IR light. This occurs on all surfaces where light strikes us on our skin, retina, gut, and lung. Sunlight is capable of quieting the vibration of atoms in your cell membranes and inside your cells to collect and direct light flows to and fro things capable of making the energy in cells. Mitochondria being one such cellular part. The mitochondria must be in close coaptation with the nucleus to signal properly. The environmental signals are what turn on or off epigenetic expression by deciphering environmental signals from the Schumann resonance/magnetic field or from the solar spectrum. Genes are quantum computers and the environment they exist in is their measuring devices. The act of measurement gives us the reality of our life. Measurement and observation effects their epigenetic expression. The genome is an ‘organ of the cell’, not its dictator. Light not only become matter it becomes ideas. Thoughts also become things. Plain and simple. This is why people get ill fundamentally. This is how life organizes in mitochondria to create the reality we get called a life. [http://www.sciencedaily.com/rele.../2014/01/140116084838.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2014/01/140116084838.htm)

Surface dwellers are what society calls hip or the in crowd. Being earnest comes from our depths and our core. It stops only when the job is complete. Being hip only lasts til the next fashion season. Time begins and ends with a light meter in your eye. So if you have implanted lenses all hope is not lost.......be earnest and not hip. Surface dwellers are what society calls hip or the in crowd. Being earnest comes from our depths and our core. It stops only when the job is complete. ### Penny — 2016-01-12T06:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/post-186053>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-14T15:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/post-186261>

The synthesis and timing of melatonin production begins in the eye because it requires an afferent signal from light in the SCN which projects to the pineal gland via the paraventricular nucleus and the superior cervical ganglion.

### Penny — 2016-01-15T22:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/post-186404>

> [RobH said:](/goto/post?id=179068)
>
> Very good to know. Was wondering about the patient we saw with implanted lenses already.
>
> Just had a long discussion re contact with local optometrist. They gleefully advertise the "SPF" of contact lenses now. Apparently there are a few brands available without UV blocking but he seemed puzzled why anybody should want. About to hand Ubi 24 to him. For now I wear them only a few times a month so I can wear blue blockers to work evenings / nights. Soon Rx blue blockers and it won't be an issue.
>
> RobH

I talked to someone at lenscrafters and there are no glasses that let in UV light either - you would think there would be a plastic that would be easily able to do this - although then it would be more easily scratched - I guess I'll just keep being blind as a bat when I'm outdoors... if one is severely myopic, and they do get sunshine, unless they get enough to change to shape of their eyes to be less elongated, will the photoelectric effect still happen because it's not hitting the retina at the right place? And is it even worse if you have atigmatism? It seems the answer to this would be \*more\* sunshine and seafood... and if you add age to that equation, would more sunshine be better as long as there was enough DHA in you eye? I guess Melanie Procter's eye hack wouldn't have happened if that were not true...

Thanks for the cataract response

### TerrierMom — 2016-01-15T23:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/post-186415>

I had astigmatism in one eye, and myopia in both. and Lasik surgery around 2000. So I have changed the shape of my eyeballs but no lens in there other than my cornea put back over the cut. I am getting UV in there though on a regular basis (even though Jack told me to in 2012 in my first consult, I only now GET that I really need to, ahem, DO it.). Wondering how badly messed up I am in my ability to assimilate UV because of that. I have not exactly figured out WHY this is an issue though I understand it IS an issue (I know it has been addressed in blogs but I can't quite grasp it YET. More DHA and sunlight needed first, I will get there).

I wear reading glasses now that are progressive because I hated taking them off and on all the time. But guess what. Now I DO take them off whenever I am outside or inside and not reading. I've come to detest looking through them unless I HAVE to. My new reading glasses will be for reading only and will be bluetechs or 550 nm amber dipped..... The bonus of taking my glasses off, is it REALLY makes me not want to bother looking at my phone because I will have to put glasses on to do that. Double win, helping me break the addiction of looking at it (even if it does have its orange filter on).

### cmegan — 2015-10-11T17:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179084>

Hi!

What is the best timing to use a NIR sauna? I'm assuming after reading Jack's recent blogs that it would be in the morning hours...

I get sun and earth every morning, then have been using the NIR in the evening before CT...any suggestions?

Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T18:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179091>

### cmegan — 2015-10-11T21:38:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179123>

So, if I am understanding it correctly, red lights are best from sun down to bedtime?
And CT should be done before the red lights for the cold to prepare your skin for optimal absorption?
Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T22:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179124>

Red works 24/7.......so does purple.......you need them both together when you are using them..........for optimal function.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T22:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179125>

purple via you eye at night allows you to catch up for an indoor life..........but if you add an red light at night you lose nothing to melatonin because red and purple light do not affect melatonin or melanopsin.

### cmegan — 2015-10-11T22:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179126>

Ok got it now...thank you very much Jack!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T22:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179127>

melanopsin actually use UV light at night stored in the RPE to regenerate cones. UV regenerates melanopsin and rods during daytime when you dont use either........so this is why UV light at night appears to be something we can use without biologic penalty. But I always recommend collecting red light when using it........so if you get some some IR during the day.......you can use purple at night indoors.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T16:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-near-infrared-sauna.16973/post-179347>

The cell is a play room for photons. Life is based upon the entangled activity of photobiochemical and photobiophysical processes. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Biological processes notably seem to be able to live on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics to some extent by remaining metastable and far from equilibrium to take full advantage of the probabilities that quantum mechanics gives nature. Electromagnetic radiation or light in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction chains and their outcome. Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) IRA, NIR, IR and red light are all stored deeply in water to make an EZ. An EZ = battery. Water inside a cell is fundamentally a repository for light. If you look at a cell's spectrum of color........it stores most of its purple light inside the nucleus.........and most of the red light toward the periphery of the cell. This is why humans radiate light around 900 nm when you use GDV cameras or IR camera with spectroscopic analysis. Ubi 5 talks about mito-nuclear coaptation..........it is a function of how much purple is 'stored' in the nucleus (DNA/RNA) and how much red is made in mitochondria and below the Cell Membranes at carbonyl groups.
When batteries are discharging cells are dying. They die by releasing too much ELF-UV light. To divide cells use small pulses of ELF-UV to stimulate mitosis. Too much release = oncogenesis due to too many mitosis. Cells are looking to survive rather than reproduce in this case. No reproduction = death and disease = modern world's neolithic disease and infertility issues.

I have to think about this math. If we assume the light source is an expanding sphere, the surface area is 4 times pi r-squared. So as the distance from the source increases you can calculate the intensity of the beam. But, as Jack says, at short distances, not much affect. Either increase the time of exposure, or add sources.

Would it have a similar effect?

Only if it make connection to the Earth........

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T19:39:55+0300

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-02T23:50:01+0200

Yes, the "ground" in electrical outlets has stray electricity, which is unsafe to ground to.

# Thread: levonorgestrel detox
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/levonorgestrel-detox.16978/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sunny1966 — 2015-10-12T15:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/levonorgestrel-detox.16978/post-179197>

I have been reading on the forum now for a few months and I think the personal stories are really helpful connecting the dots, also for people with no medical background like me. Therefore I decided to start my own journal. Since 2003 I have had many health issues after two pregnancies and inserting the Mirena IUD.

I live in the south west of Holland, 6 kilometres from the beach. Currently 160 lb, but came from 222 lb in 2011. 1,73 m (shrunk already 1cm from 1,74m)

History:

1966: born on a farm in the north of Holland, not breast feed, because GP told my mom that cow milk from the farm was fine. During childhood a lot of eczema and bronchitis (many times antibiotics and hydrocortisone cream for eczema), always a bit bloated and very carb addicted even then (wheat). Spending a lot of time outdoors, but addicted to reading at night….

1977 Menarche and needed glasses minus 2.75 first prescription

1978: move to another village to a mold toxic home. Started to have many problems: severe hay fever and eczema, struggling with weight. Started to eat margarine instead of butter (in hindsight this was also start of a lot of problems). Always reading until midnight. Vision declined during the years and allergy tests confirmed allergies: chocolate, pork, strong flavours (I think it must have been e621, because of current problems). Weight did not exceed 132 lb during these years. But struggling with carb addiction and bloating. 1981 starting with contact lenses, I remember asking myself if these contacts were really proven safe....

1987 move to a dorm house. Allergy/inflammation was less as long as I did not go home a lot. Struggling with weight, but still not exceeding 132 lb. Quit using the strong version hydrocortisone cream beneath my eyes for eczema.

1989 moved to Toronto, Canada for 1 year. Gained about 12 lb the first few months, because of salted butter, muffins, etc. Was able to lose the weight with diet and lot of physical activities. Started with Birth control pill

1990 moved back to Holland to the area where I am still live. Started to get white spots on my upper belly and back in the summer (half a year after starting BCP). Fungus, according to the GP. A few bladder inflammations since starting BCP.

1991 After starting with soft contact lenses which could be in day and night for 14 days, my vision went from minus 4 to minus 6 and I went back to rigid contact lenses

1992-1999 Struggling with weight (Weight Watchers almost continuously, always hungry), but never exceeded 154 lb. Office job, but cycling to work a lot of the time and working out indoors at night. 1993-1998 lived on the 10th floor

1999 Export Manager job and quite a lot of flying around the world. Health started to decline with migraines and further struggle with weight (160 lb max)

Summer 2000 finally I blamed the BPC and got pregnant in the same month at age 33. Had quite some vaccinations at the start of pregnancy because of a trip to South Africa. During pregnancy I could not stop snacking carbs (tested for diabetes but tested negative). Gained a lot of weight and gave birth after 38 weeks in the hospital in 2001. Started BCP again after a few months, but migraines returned and I quit the BCP, got pregnant again in January 2002. Gained a lot of weight again, fluid retention and higher BP by end of pregnancy, pregnancy mask spots, Carpal Tunnel Syndrome during pregnancy. Difficulties with sleep and snacking carbs at night. Struggle to lose weight after the 2nd baby, 198 lb after the pregnancy. In both cases I was determined to breast feed, but after trying a week the babies lost a lot of weight and I was forced to give up. I must have had thyroid problems by then I think.

End of 2002 Move to a ground floor house in the area where I am still today. New GP inserted Mirena in January 2003. She told me that my concerns about hormones weren’t necessary, because Mirena hormones stay in the uterus. Totally safe form of birth control even for people with hormone sensivities she insisted. I had informed her that there is a lot of Diabetes in my family (grandmother, aunts and father).

Feb. 2003 Immediately after inserting the Mirena I got pelvic instability. Was sent to a physical therapist, but no connection with Mirena was made. The pregnancies without pelvic instability….

March 2003 more problems: extreme hair loss, acne. Struggling very hard at weight watchers to lose weight, finally I was at 176 lb in May 2003. Got the flu and did not recover very easily (hindsight: all my life I got sick (excessive mucous and sore throat) when trying very hard to lose weight with Weight Watchers). Health decline steadily until 2nd Mirena in 2008, always tired, got sensitive to perfume, shampoo, body wash, washing powder, etc. Problems with lymph inflammation regularly, sometimes size of a ping pong ball. Itchy vagina, constipation, GERD, problems with finding words, difficulties to swallow, vision started to decline/blurry vision, starting to get hirsutism. I went to the GP a lot to discuss these problems. But was sent home with medication (antibiotics, reflux medication). Went to see a dietist, but practically no weight loss. Body composition started to change to large breasts, bloated painful belly. I got a lymphoma in my arm pit (size 0,5 cm and still there today). Always stiff neck and back problems made me see a physical therapist, manual therapist or chiropractor often, but this resulted never in a permanent solution. My 2nd baby (son) was put on Salbutamol after a few months, because of excessive mucous problems at night. Very weak health and he also got quite some antibiotics and even prednisone sometimes. Also he had eczema which runs a lot in my family.

After the 2nd Mirena in 2008 my health started to decline much faster. Severe molluscum contagiosum for half a year, GP told me that this does only happen to adults with severe health problems such as AIDS or cancer. But furthermore no solution or further investigation to my weak health. Weight and fatigue started to be a big problem and continuous trying a Weight Watchers diet and working out indoors did not help. I started crashing at night and binge eating. GP had already prescribed my DH (now ex husband) in 2006 sleeping pills and had been generous to him with the prescription pad, resulting in a severe addiction to various medication in the end. So continuous stress at home and a busy full time job as a purchasing manager with 2 small children to take care of was really taking it’s toll on my health. At that time I blamed the intercontinental flights to Asia for my job and not sleeping enough, because of the health of my son. In 2010 I went on a protein shake diet, very helpful for weight loss, but I stopped this because I could not imagine this to be a healthy diet. I started to get continuous problems with my ears and got antibiotics on a regular base. In March 2011 I was bleeding heavily from my gut after bad constipation, also no reason for my GP to dig any further in my problems. In June 2011 I could not work anymore. I was exhausted and at my top weight of 222 lb. GP still did not order any blood tests and I had no clue. Assumed the problems were caused by addiction stress of husband and busy job. Went back to work after a few months, but still felt exhausted. Went to the company GP in February 2012 because I could work full time, he told me that I would never get better in this job and told me I would be best to find a new job, which I did find very quickly. Between the 2 jobs I went back to Weight Watchers, because this time I was determined to succeed. Started again on the program, but got sick immediately with acute sinusitis, but continued to eat the WW diet (low fat dairy) even when I felt so sick. Had not experienced so much mucous ever in my life before. Got antibiotics and was told by the GP that a flight to Italy was no problem, I was concerned because of my new job, which I would start after the holiday. Flight was no success, I could hardly hear anymore because the ears were full with fluid. Got more antibiotics during the holiday and started the new job quite sick. Needed to have a tube inserted in one ear in the 2nd week and another tube in the other ear after 4 weeks in the job. Had quite some antibiotics and dexamethasone in 2012. None of it did help and after a few months I was told I needed sinus surgery. Which I doubted very much, because I could not imagine this would be my solution and this kind of surgery did not make any sense in an evolutionary point of view to me, but I could not find any other solutions and I was just exhausted. In the spring of 2012 I also had got a 0,5 cm melasma spot on my forehead. GP told me not to worry, it was no cancer she told me. My belly is still covered with a lot of cherry angiomas and every summer I get the white spots on my shoulders. Right now they are covered with white spots.

Finally I approved to have the surgery in January. My gut health was devastated at that moment after so many antibiotics, I could not stop farting and had either diarrhea or constipation. My contract got terminated. Terrible stressful time, because of the loss of the job, husband with a severe addiction to benzodiazepines and the upcoming surgery. After the surgery when the wounds were healed inside my head and after I had to stop the nose spray, my problem with mucous got back immediately in the spring of 2013. At that time I ask the doctor again what could be the cause (had asked this question before the surgery to several doctors as well….), then he admitted it could be allergy. So I assumed it was the hay fever as I had had in my teens, but now in a different form. He told me to get back when I needed surgery again. I tried myself not to panic because of the whole mess. Was determined to help my husband to get through this and then find a new job. I started to read more about the benzo addiction and was literally in shock because of the scale of this problem worldwide. Withdrawal was not successful and I gave up on the marriage by then. Just finding the right time to quit. In the meantime I had started Weight Watchers again and the problems re occurred with the mucous. I started to drink fresh mint tea and suddenly my facial hair started to get better. I started to question why and then it suddenly hit me that it could be the fresh mint tea (which is very popular in Turkey and Marocco, also dark haired women). After a bit of googling I found the connection and that was also the moment I realized what a disaster the mirena could be for my health. Another google session confirmed my assumptions were correct about the side effects. Asked my GP to take it out and asked for a detox with a NP. She told me it was not possible because holistic health and regular health care are 2 different kinds of sport. I was shocked and thought how can you describe health care like playing a game of sports. She was still convinced that the hormones in Mirena only stay in the Uterus. I was sent away without any tests and found myself a NP.

Recovery until now:

After listening to my history the NP told me within 15 minutes that dairy could be a problem for me. Which it indeed turned out to be after some testing at home. I found my way through quite some testing, first Weston Price, then Paleo, and since this summer additional information from the Jack Kruse website. I gradually have lost the weight and never experience the severe cravings (and hunger) anymore as long as I do not eat a lot of carbs. According to the ophthalmologist in the hospital I need prescription for minus 10,5 and minus 11,5 last April, but I started to wear my 20 year old glasses of minus 6,5 since last week. I feels like a real relieve and I can sense improvement already, whenever I can I put them off. I think my biohack with soft lenses in a weaker prescription from 2014-2015 had adverse outcome for my health (lowered dopamine I assume and I feel it destroyed my vitamin A cycle). I started to regularly put icepack in my neck and will now also introduce cold baths very soon. Since February 2015 I regularly use Epsom salts for my baths (respond very well to this, it is very helpful for detoxing I think). Since removing the Mirena and starting the LCHF diary I have almost not back pain/stiff neck anymore. Also dental calculus on my teeth is almost non existing, I still can feel it sometimes, but it will go away without any dentist help. I still wonder about this dental calculus thing, before lifestyle changes it was terrible for my and also for my son. Gluten, dairy (apart from ghee), fructose, sulphites, lectins and oxalates are a problem for me. Hay fever is completely gone, also in my son. I eat way more fresh salmon, sardines and shrimps than before. Oysters are hard to find and I have never tried them. Mussels have got me really sick a few times when I tried them about 20 years ago. I have not taken a lot of supplements during my recovery, because I did not trust this either and also it does not seem correct in an evolutionary point of view to me. My periods (very light) are regular at 26 days, I will be 49 by the end of the month. I still have my hair dyed with natural dye (as far as possible in case of dye) and I realize I should also quit this. I try to get AM sun without my glasses and I can feel the difference now. At night and at the computer my son and I wear blueblockers and we only use a few dim lights. I am very sensitive to carbs and also I have not tried working out again because of a bad response to a few times of running training in January. Afterward each training I started to feel real bad and I regained in total about 10 lb in a few weeks then. Last month I have lost another 6 lb and now I am at 160 lb. My body composition has tremendously improved, but I feel that inside my body things are not yet as they should be. I sometimes have severe sleeping legs or arms during the night. Although this problem has improved a lot, it was really bad when I was still on Mirena, occurring several times during the night. Since starting with Epsom salts my fungus nails on my toes and my severe callus on my feet has much improved, also my bunion on my right foot has become much smaller. My mom of almost 92 years old is still swimming 3 times a week in hour in a rather cold swimming pool (CT), she has told us all her life that her body needs sunshine (UV), I remember we had an Infrared light and UVB light at home in the 1970s. The last few years her health has suddenly declined after cataract surgery and her D3 levels are very low these days. I should have watched my mom more closely instead asking the GP....When I found the Kruse site I knew this was the missing puzzle, because of my mum , myself and also because I had observed my ex husband in the past. I knew always there had to be a biological explanation to his problems, too bad medicine is not interested in finding the truth. He was very sensitive to light (always wearing sunglasses), addicted but sensitive to carbs, all of his problems could have been solved by the Kruse lifestyle, I think. But we are hardly in contact anymore. I think he cannot be rescued from the ever prescribing psychiatrists and withdrawal efforts without further lifestyle suggestions. My children have to grow up without their father, my son is also on my diet/lifestyle and responds very well, at the moment we still have to finish the divorce financially and I am without a job, so a lot of things going on (chronically high cortisol I assume). I am trying to find a new job and wonder what would be the best option for me now, considering I got very sick working full time in offices and flying around the world. I have always been sensitive to mobile phones, I can feel the nnEMF before the phone starts to ring when a message comes in. Still a lot of improvements to be made. And also I need to deal with my anger towards my GP (and other health care practitioners) for years of not hearing me and therefore ruining my life, only try to describe meds, hiding the truth and not thinking for themselves (even though many times I asked the correct questions (which are answered in this website)). Last week I had some blood testing done and based on these results we will start to dig further, results will be in on Wednesday. Thank you very much for providing all this information, dr. Kruse!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T15:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/levonorgestrel-detox.16978/post-179331>

The cell is a play room for photons. Life is based upon the entangled activity of photobiochemical and photobiophysical processes. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Biological processes notably seem to be able to live on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics to some extent by remaining metastable and far from equilibrium to take full advantage of the probabilities that quantum mechanics gives nature. Electromagnetic radiation or light in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction chains and their outcome. Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) IRA, NIR, IR and red light are all stored deeply in water to make an EZ. An EZ = battery. Water inside a cell is fundamentally a repository for light. If you look at a cell's spectrum of color........it stores most of its purple light inside the nucleus.........and most of the red light toward the periphery of the cell. This is why humans radiate light around 900 nm when you use GDV cameras or IR camera with spectroscopic analysis. Ubi 5 talks about mito-nuclear coaptation..........it is a function of how much purple is 'stored' in the nucleus (DNA/RNA) and how much red is made in mitochondria and below the Cell Membranes at carbonyl groups.
When batteries are discharging cells are dying. They die by releasing too much ELF-UV light. To divide cells use small pulses of ELF-UV to stimulate mitosis. Too much release = oncogenesis due to too many mitosis. Cells are looking to survive rather than reproduce in this case. No reproduction = death and disease = modern world's neolithic disease and infertility issues.

### Sunny1966 — 2015-10-14T09:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/levonorgestrel-detox.16978/post-179439>

Dear all, thank you for your replies. Right now is a very rough time in my divorce and it is indeed very very costly and time consuming. I will get back with more replies/connecting the dots, but need to focus on my divorce now.
I do not have a medical background, so I need to google a lot. My perineum has been badly damaged giving birth to my first baby. Small baby girl but her head was heading towards the sun  instead of chin towards the chest. After 24 hours the baby was stuck and with a lot of effort and devices she was taken out with a pump. Whole perineum area was badly damaged and afterwards it became a real mess with the wound healing out of control. So at that time I had to stay with the perineum in the open air for weeks.... It was corrected more or less after the second child, but still does not feel as it was before. For years I was thinking that all the spotting which I had was caused by the damage of giving birth, I did not know anything about leaky guts. These days I know when my gut is leaky, it does not happen a lot, only when I try to eat something new like a teaspoon of peanut butter last week.

I have been thinking about light a lot....
During my frequent business trips to China I always felt horrible in the factories. They are fully illuminated with white light also during night time I always felt sorry for the workers to be in this terrible light. Meetings took mostly place in show rooms without any daylight. Driving from factory to factory or to the hotel you can see the dorms for the workers and the small shops lighted all night with horrible white light. During the years I noticed that the sunshine was only visible in the early morning, because of the air pollution. Now I know why I always told everyone that the Japanese food and traditional chinese food was the only good thing about these trips. I literally craved the sashimi during the trips. At that time I did not understand the chinese eating the animal eyes and serving chicken feet with skin and nails. However I did wonder why I could not estimate how old a chinese woman is most of the time. Women of 45 seems to have no wrinkles at all and almost no gray hair. During the last years this seemed to change very fast with the fast food chains exploding accross China.

### Sunny1966 — 2015-10-14T18:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/levonorgestrel-detox.16978/post-179474>

This afternoon I went to the GP and first tests came out real good. Things look good and I will give it a try to do some running training tomorrow. Hopefully my body will respond better than in January.
Results:
Blood sedimentation (bezinking) 5
Leukocytes 7.9
Erythrocytes 4.5
Folic Acid 21.2
Sodium (natrium) 141
Potassium (kalium) 4.5
Vitamin B1 109
Vitamin B6 91
**Vitamin D 25-OH 122** (Dutch weather and 2 sunny weeks in the South of France, no sun screen at all and only a few times prescription sun glasses, furthermore rigid contact lenses). I have never used a lot of sun screen and sun glasses, this just felt never correct to me and I always remembered the sunny summer days in my youth when sun screen protection did not exist yet.
I translated the above the other tests are in the attachments. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: Pinguecula

### JediMindTricks — 2015-10-12T16:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-179211>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinguecula](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinguecula)

Can anyone offer any insight into why I have pingueculae? Wiki says:

'they are not uncommon in 20 and 30 years old adults who spend significant time in the sun'.

That doesn't make sense, as I've avoided the sun for years. I have spend a lot of that time in front of a computer though.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-12T16:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-179212>

That doesn't make seen as i've avoided the sun for years. I have spend a lot of that time in front of a computer though.
I will rephrase that for you.
" I have avoided full spectrum light for years and have spent a lot of those years in a reduced light spectrum environment"

### JediMindTricks — 2016-01-23T15:24:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-187164>

My eyes have been forcing shut (reflex action) lately when I've been sunbathing and I'm worried about this. I'm in the UK and the sun is at its weakest. I've been following the advice on the forum but the info here totally contradicts everything I read about pinguecula. The modern consensus is that they are caused by UV exposure.

Is this reflex action normal? or a signal to stop? It happens after only a few seconds of the light hitting my eyes (while not directly looking at the sun).
Any advice is welcome. Many thanks.

### JediMindTricks — 2016-01-23T15:26:04+0200

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-24T00:24:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-187215>

> [JediMindTricks said:](/goto/post?id=187164)
>
> My eyes have been forcing shut (reflex action) lately when I've been sunbathing and I'm worried about this. I'm in the UK and the sun is at its weakest. I've been following the advice on the forum but the info here totally contradicts everything I read about pinguecula. The modern consensus is that they are caused by UV exposure.
>
> Is this reflex action normal? or a signal to stop? It happens after only a few seconds of the light hitting my eyes (while not directly looking at the sun).
> Any advice is welcome. Many thanks.

I understood it was important to **rapidly blink** even at very weak sun, not stare without blinking... it causes a different effect. If eyes are watering looking near sun this is good as allows more UV absorption where you want it, keep blinking. I and others also sometimes have iced our eyelids which for me again induces even more blinking.

### JediMindTricks — 2016-01-24T16:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-187278>

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=187215)
>
> I understood it was important to **rapidly blink** even at very weak sun, not stare without blinking... it causes a different effect. If eyes are watering looking near sun this is good as allows more UV absorption where you want it, keep blinking. I and others also sometimes have iced our eyelids which for me again induces even more blinking.

Hi lilreddgirl. I've read on this forum that blinking is the way we cool our eyes surfaces but what I'm describing isn't plain blinking.

I just done some more digging around the net and what I'm experiencing is called Blepharospasm. This makes a little more sense now. Heres a quote from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blepharospasm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blepharospasm)

> In addition to blepharospasm being a [benzodiazepine withdrawal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benzodiazepine_withdrawal) symptom, prolonged use of [benzodiazepines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benzodiazepines) can induce blepharospasm and is a known risk factor for the development of blepharospasm.

Is there no end to withdrawal symptoms from these crazy drugs!

My question is, should I continue getting sunlight on my eye surfaces regardless of the blepharospasms?

### lilreddgirl — 2016-01-24T23:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-187300>

Oh man... keep asking and I hope Jack helps you. My guess would be yes keep at it but I feel concerned for you. @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) blepharospasms...

### JediMindTricks — 2016-02-21T10:46:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pinguecula.16980/post-189505>

I went for an eye test yesterday and of course the optician said that pingueculae are caused by exposure to UV light. She said that they are very common amongst africans and that the buildup of fatty tissue is to reflect the UV light away from the eye. Basically a complete regurgitation of what is stated on [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinguecula](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinguecula)

She nearly had a fit when I told her that I was exposing my eyes to direct sunlight as often as possible. She said I would only cause cataracts by doing that.

Other than the pingueculae, my eye health is exceptional and my vision is beyond 20/20

### JediMindTricks — 2016-09-21T12:54:31+0300

### Eilee — 2015-10-12T16:19:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-from-a-newbie.16981/post-179213>

1) If I am outside much of the day, don't I risk photoaging of the skin and wrinkles, etc , if I don't use sunscreen for at least part of the day?

2) When I go outside in the mornings, should I looks straight at the sun? (Doesnt' that increase risk of cataracts or is that a myth?) What is the minimum amount of time necessary

3) Is it fine to wear contacts outside if they are not UV tinted?

### Lahelada — 2015-10-12T16:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-from-a-newbie.16981/post-179223>

1: be outside but don't burn
2\. Not straight at the sun but at an 35 degreeangle towards the east.
3\. Nope

Before you do anything read ubi 23-25 so that you get the basic contexts.

Oh and welcome !

### Joe Gavin — 2015-10-13T14:46:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-from-a-newbie.16981/post-179319>

Hi Eilee! Have fun here!

My take based on results this summer in NJ:
I'm Irish German White boy.
I eat a pretty high fat every day, lots of seafood. Ate carbs during summer when they are appropriate.
I spent more time outdoors this summer than almost any other.
I didn't use sunblock.
I didn't burn. Got red once I think.
The more good fats you have in your diet, the fewer shitty carbs, the better your skin will adapt to the sunlight.
Be smart: get your sun from 6-11 am and 3-7pm while you're there. Siestas were created for a reason.
Enjoy!

Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolic chaos. Biological processes notably "**_seem"_** to be able to live on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics. That "sight" is a mirage of nature. Reality is more odd. The power of light i sburied and transformed in the collision of sunlight and water in the cytosol to make a circular thermodynamics. In this way light energy can be pumped in chronically and stored for use in the system to be used at a later date without every being thermalized. In this way, the second law is never broken and it just appears that life lives on thi sfine edge. It turns out the physics of cell builds this huge circular stage using water as its muse. This allows the living cell, to remain by some extent, metastable and far from equilibrium to take full advantage of the probabilities that quantum mechanics of light gives nature to use. Electromagnetic radiation (light) in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction pathways in biochemistry and their **_various_** outcomes. Light controls the outcomes. The pathways have no deterministic ability in understanding life. Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. IR-A and B light move things in a cell with mass. The most important thing they move is the water that surrounds the side chains of aromatic amino acids that generate the circadian mechanism in a cell. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/). IR-A, NIR, IR-B red light are all stored deeply in water to make an EZ. Part of the EZ forms coherent domains (CD) within the EZ. An EZ = battery, but it is more than that. EZ becomes a CD and a CD is critical in making the ELF-UV bio-photon field that makes light to activate melatonin during darkness. Water inside a cell is fundamentally a repository for light. If you look at a cell's spectrum of color........it stores most of its purple light inside the nucleus where DNA/RNA is.........and most of the red light toward the periphery of the cell where water and mitochondria exist. The nucleus is where matter is changed because UV light has that type of kinetic power. The cytosol is where things are moved to create energy for a cell. This is why humans radiate light around 900 nm when you use GDV cameras or IR cameras with spectroscopic analysis. My ubiquitination 5 blog talks about mito-nuclear coaptation. It is a function of how much purple light is 'stored' in the nucleus (DNA/RNA) and how much red is made in mitochondria and below the cell membranes at carbonyl groups.
When batteries inside cells are discharging energy cells are dying in the living state. Energy is being lost. They die by releasing too much ELF-UV light. Energy, light energy is lost from a cell. To divide themselves, cells use small pulses of ELF-UV to stimulate mitosis. We've kown this since the 1920's, but modern science has ignored the data. Too much light release = oncogenesis due to too many mitosis??? Could too much light release = mitochondrial disease generation? Cells losing light are looking to survive rather than reproduce in this case. No reproduction = death and disease = modern world's neolithic disease and infertility issues plus mitochondrial disease explosion.

### LisaLearning — 2015-10-29T11:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-from-a-newbie.16981/post-180692>

Hi Eilee, contacts are bad for your eyes, period. There is no good blood supply to your cornea, and they get their oxygen from the ambient air. When you put contacts on your cornea, they inhibit the capacity of the cornea to get oxygen (all eye doctors know this, and in fact, contact lenses are marketed to eye docs with information about their oxygen transmission rates, some higher, some lower, but all lenses inhibit the passage of oxygen to your corneas). This basically means that your corneas don't get all the oxygen they need, and starving body tissue of oxygen is not good. Jack says this causes eyes to elongate (presumably in an attempt to get more oxygen?) and makes people more myopic, and they continue to wear contacts, and basically it's a self-defeating circle with vision and eyes deteriorating along the way.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-29T13:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quick-questions-from-a-newbie.16981/post-180695>

Contacts filter out purple light and this atrophies the eye muscles of the globe elongate. This makes vision less sharp because blue light enters and it bends most through the anterior eye where the prisming of light occurs. Blue light falls anterior to the macula to cause visual blur. The more visual blur with a lack of purple light one gets the more chance dry macular degeneration occurs because we need purple light to hit the retina and specifically the macula to stimulate blood flow to the surface of the retina just as we do in the skin.

Dan Oren, MD, PhD, researcher for Yale University and the National Institutes of Health (NIH) indicated that 50 percent of the entire blood volume in the body passes through the eyes in 40 minutes, and that there is a biochemical mechanism of light involving hemoglobin within the blood (280 nm light) , allowing the eyes to be an appropriate portal for phototherapy treatment. Nature uses this effect to maintain vision.

Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.jackkruse.com%2F&h=QAQHtYV6tAQFEwOnujSEyWMrxPFf7PB1LR-dpXXPXeM-nTQ&enc=AZPR9Wf3HDvcpFcS2rz5dGFgUBKvLrsOFBkgNE6eRPeUbU-L5zF5cy7agaEaOF-0QNYI-qZv-oArEjkdvj69knrng04rKZsfDifTV4AqE41hb8mXvklsO4CkNsxn710ECTNMbHsw0vJq-rYNECOiZZ_svEitqr63cZXHn9CmgenOomh2VKriLLyp17E9QLsTGWuV7qyb1ysY-uVbZeDFkG3c&s=1)

The first day I wore those orange shorts my life changed dramatically ..... I remember that day and all the detail very vividly - I felt like I was lit from within and it was very noticeable to strangers and those I knew. I was meeting a special someone at the ocean and I was late because strangers kept stopping me and talking. I became vey conscious of what I wanted and there were consequences if I chose that path . There was nothing heavy or onerous ....I was just seeing things very clearly.

From that time forward I began making choices and doing things that were seemingly totally out of character for me ..... and I haven't looked back since.

I suppose I was on a path that led me to buy orange shorts .....and now the most incredible journey of self discovery and entanglement.

I can remember loving the colour orange but I never had the courage to buy it ........ so it was a learning curve to get there - to step into my life! I have done some very outrageous things since then ... I couldn't be happier about that!

orange closely related to red is about breath......and ATP and AMPK pathways.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sses-dr-kruse-is-wearing-in-this-video.16987/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads%2Fcan-anyone-identify-the-glasses-dr-kruse-is-wearing-in-this-video.16987%2F)

The cell is a play room for photons. Life is based upon the entangled activity of photobiochemical and photobiophysical processes. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Biological processes notably seem to be able to live on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics to some extent by remaining metastable and far from equilibrium to take full advantage of the probabilities that quantum mechanics gives nature. Electromagnetic radiation or light in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction chains and their outcome. Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) IRA, NIR, IR and red light are all stored deeply in water to make an EZ. An EZ = battery. Water inside a cell is fundamentally a repository for light. If you look at a cell's spectrum of color........it stores most of its purple light inside the nucleus.........and most of the red light toward the periphery of the cell. This is why humans radiate light around 900 nm when you use GDV cameras or IR camera with spectroscopic analysis. Ubi 5 talks about mito-nuclear coaptation..........it is a function of how much purple is 'stored' in the nucleus (DNA/RNA) and how much red is made in mitochondria and below the Cell Membranes at carbonyl groups.
When batteries are discharging cells are dying. They die by releasing too much ELF-UV light. To divide cells use small pulses of ELF-UV to stimulate mitosis. Too much release = oncogenesis due to too many mitosis. Cells are looking to survive rather than reproduce in this case. No reproduction = death and disease = modern world's neolithic disease and infertility issues.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-how-do-i-post-a-picture-of-myself.16984/post-179262>

Look in the right hand upper corner of your screen. You will see your screen name.
