# Thread: Understanding Black Light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 129
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Prall — 2015-10-13T05:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-179280>

From what I can tell, there is a lot of confusion and assumptions that are occurring regarding the UV (black) light hacks as of late. I'm hoping we can start to clear that up in this thread.

As I understand it, the ideal spectrum to hack is 290-390 nm light (UVA + UVB) that is missing from virtually all indoor lighting. While old Edison style incandescent bulbs do contain a small portion of their light in the <400 nm spectrum, they primarily give off light in the yellow, orange, red, IR spectrum. Not good, but better than CFL and LED.

Standard CFLs (which give off x-ray light) do not emit UV light at all. Not good.

Standard LED lights are becoming more white and give off mostly yellow, orange, red with a spike in the blue spectrum. Not good.

What I'm wondering is what type of bulb is optimal to supplement an office space during the day. It seems UV LED would be as it is absent of x-ray EMF. As I understand, incandescent black lights do not produce sufficient UV light.

As such, I'm considering this LED black light that sits at the 365 nm range. This would be entirely in the UVA spectrum (320 nm - 400 nm) and absent of UVB (290-320). I don't want to play with light at night until I am more comfortable in my understanding of this topic.

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0...ack light&qid=1444697044&ref\_=sr\_1\_14&sr=8-14](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B013W6SJEM?keywords=led%20black%20light&qid=1444697044&ref_=sr_1_14&sr=8-14)

Any thoughts? Is there a better option out there for the A19 bulbs (standard screw-in type)?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T05:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-179281>

we'll clear it up on a webinar. Decided that yesterday.

### Jason Prall — 2015-10-13T05:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-179282>

Ok great. Appreciate that. Any chance you can give me a thumbs up or thumbs down on the light bulb in the link above?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T05:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-179285>

365 nm LED would give you a 20 nm spread either way..........an incandescent with a indigo/violet polyethylene filter would be best but much harder to find.

### Joseph Thia — 2015-10-13T07:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-179298>

I am only able to find blacklight blue tubes in Singapore. The one that I have is an 8W from sankyo-denki and I am using it at my office desk. I am wondering what wattage should I get (i.e. is 8W enough?) and if there is any safety distance that I should keep it away from me (it is about 35 inches away from me)? Many thanks!

Information extracted from [http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2\_06.html](http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2_06.html)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

These lamps effciently emit near ultraviolet rays at 315nm- 400nm which have strong photochemical and fluorescent effects.
They are special deep blue filter glass which absorbs visible rays and transmits near ultraviolet rays and two types ofphosphor are used with radiation peak at 352nm and 368nm, respectively.
They are used for tests, inspections, examinations, and investigations in various fields, as well as for stage illumination in theaters and nightclubs, advertisements and sign boards.

### JanSz — 2015-12-02T21:32:47+0200

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=183081)
>
> Amazon has a 125-Watt version of this bulb. Would this be okay for a regular screw in socket, JanSz?
> [http://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Sol...p/B00101JII8/ref=cm\_cr\_pr\_product\_top?ie=UTF8](http://www.amazon.com/Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-Intensity-Self-Ballasted/dp/B00101JII8/ref=cm_cr_pr_product_top?ie=UTF8)

Same link have 160W version for $2 more.
Lizard will fit under one light bulb, and be close to the bulb. That makes use of higher intensity.

Humans are larger, light must be farther away. Light intensity drops (very fast) with distance.
Consult available table on bulb packing.

The general idea is to replicate full spectrum sunlight at equator (but no more).
Frequencies and intensities.
Consider distance (light to skin), initial light intensity and exposure time.

..
When your skin and eyes are currently sun deprived,
it takes at least a month to accustom gradually to more light.

..

Also I thought that I be better of with at least two lamps or more.
.

### Penny — 2015-12-03T01:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-183105>

Why not just get the ones Dr. Kruse uses from Batteries and Bulbs?
[https://www.batteriesplus.com/light-bulb/cfl/spiral/screw/cfl10028](https://www.batteriesplus.com/light-bulb/cfl/spiral/screw/cfl10028)

If you use a flourescent light, it will emit x-rays from the end - you have 2 choices, move it at least 3 ft away from you or cover the ends with lead tape or go with a CFL... less power so buy more of them - the fluorescent tubes say to be within 2 ft so you are losing therapeutic benefits when you are further away - but at least there are no x-rays that way -

Would love to know what IR light he is using... the ones I've seen require a special socket... and they suck down power... You need the IR light plus the UV light or the IR light first, then the UV light plus enough DHA for the paramagnetic function of DHA to work... you also need to be fully hydrated and probably being grounded is a great idea... if you make the surface cool that the light is hitting, even more effective...

If you're using an LED setup like for instance this:
[http://www.amazon.com/RubyLux-Red-B...?srs=9286491011&ie=UTF8&qid=1449098924&sr=8-1](http://www.amazon.com/RubyLux-Red-Blue-LED-Bulb/dp/B00IYSQIPG/ref=lp_9286491011_1_1?srs=9286491011&ie=UTF8&qid=1449098924&sr=8-1)
cover the leds in plastic wrap and put it against your skin - inside of wrist where you take your pulse - cool it off first with an ice pack - sort of a nonportable poor man's quantlet...
This is because the leds don't have enough power when they're far away from you -

But truly, the morning sun is the way to go if at all possible - and if you have enough DHA onboard, I would think you could be outside all day with no problems as long as your eyes were exposed to the same frequencies as your skin -

Of course, I could be wrong...

Artificial light can be used for plant growth in three different ways: 1. To provide all the light a plant needs to grow, 2. To supplement sunlight, especially in winter months when daylight hours are short. 3. To increase the length of the "day" in order to trigger specific growth and flowering.

So if you have a bad gut or a bad pupil can we help a human grow and thrive without their version of a soil? Yes. This is where the idea of the Quantlet came from in my mind. I put this all down on paper and Ruben built it.

Yes, full spectrum light is our best imitation of sunlight.
Use it only in day time.
For convenience it is good idea to define what you mean by day time.
I call 14hrs as my day and 10hrs as night.
Last two hrs of "day" light there is less light.
That is about description of my longest day where I live and I try to replicate it 365 times.
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
For the night light I use:
RubyLux ALL RED High Intensity LED Bulb 640 to 660nm

When I sleep there is no light.

### nicld — 2015-12-09T16:21:35+0200

My N=1 is that I have the UVB200 at work (I work in an office with no windows). I do shut it off around 3:30 – 4 pm when the sun would normally start setting. I have a UVB150 and a Solar Glo in my basement that I use on the weekends when I am downstairs sewing but these are on only when the sun would be out and they do go off when the sun’s UVB would start to diminish as well. If I am still sewing later in the day, I use only a black light and orange.

I have the third black light upstairs and use it in the evenings but never when I am sleeping. I try to get as much real sun as I can. Just had my vitamin D levels tested as well as my hsCRP and will be retesting in abut 4-5 months. I am not expecting the lights to raise my Vitamin D levels since it is winter where I live and the levels would normally drop. Also flying out to a sunny location in 2 weeks and plan on spending as much time outside as I can to harvest natural UVB.

### JanSz — 2015-12-09T17:39:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-183800>

@[Daniel](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12609/)
Short answer.
I use only two types of light bulbs.
1\. Full spectrum light, Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W (day light)
2\. RubyLux ALL RED High Intensity LED Bulb 640 to 660nm (night light)
\------------------------------------------------------------------------

Longer answer.
Initially I bought number of different light bulbs.
Variety of black ligts
Variety of Exo Terra UV lights
Variety of red lights

Tried black lights at day time or night when not sleeping, ok
Tried black light during sleep, bothers me.
I see too many lights, each dedicated for specific function.

Naturally we evolved at equator with just one sun.
For me important when trying to replicate natural sunlight condition is:
1\. timing
2\. frequency content
3\. intensity of light
4\. there are practically no seasons at equator
5\. I lived full year real close to equator, short (all 12hr) days bother me, otherwise love it
6\. light intensity at equator is likely as high as I ever want to replicate
specialty light that produces super high vit D under 5 min/day exposure, seems unnatural.
Yes, it saves time, may be good to get over the hump,
but I do not see it as desirable for long term use.
7\. keep in mind that animals on equator (or other places) seek shade when they feel need for it
8\. The Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W is supposed to shine only on part of the terrarium (basking spot). When lizard got enough basking he will see other cooler more in the shade place.
\------
Taking above under consideration
I use my natural sunlight to the practical max
I divide my 24hr day as follows
day=14 hrs
night=10 hrs

I have two Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W on for the first 12hrs then only one for remaining 2 hrs
Night time starts with RubyLux ALL RED for two hrs then total darkness for remaining 8hrs
\-------------
I do not have information on light intensity at equator (please help).
From this chart:
[https://fluxometer.com/rainbow/#!id=Outdoors/10AM](https://fluxometer.com/rainbow/#!id=Outdoors/10AM) LA sunny
Los Angeles Latitude 34.0500° N
Note that chart for sunny day in LA 10AM have top light density at 152 μW/cm^2
LA is rather far up north when good light is concerned.

\------------

I am a bit confused about one issue with black lights. I have a black light that emits light at 365nm and it is labeled as a BLB black light. 365nm ------ that is just one frequency and it is in UV-A range.

Single frequency light is usually made for some unique purpose.

I would use my farmers logic.
When you use Spetri UV light
you should use it with a goal of getting vit D somewhat over 100 without any additional vit D supplementation.
Starting dose of light is 5minutes twice/day
Adjust time per results.

Now cfl black lights or any other source of UV light should be used with similar goal of vit D level.
The other sources produce usually UV light of much lower density.
That will increase the required time.

//
If one is after visual effects, one cfl bulb at any distance will do.
If one is after certain vit D level, blood testing is need.

I am concerned about the blue spike too in Solar Glo also as being counterproductive to UV basking... I can say that I have gained significant fat within a week of starting using it. (This was however something I personally am happy with, as I was feeling 'too skinny' before.). The gain stabilized after the first week. I was unsure why UV light would do that for me, other than boosting my appetite and slight carb intake - but similar dietary changes even in winter did not cause that type of weight gain - maybe it was the blue.

I am also now interested in Vitamin D production... unfortunately it looks like high Vitamin D production is a bit challenging to achieve, even with the most powerful 20Watt Exo Terra 200...

at a distance of **8 inches it charts 20 Vitamin D IU's per minute**... (assuming it would be similar for humans)...

that would mean 1200 IU's per hour, so in 2 hours one would get approx... 2400 IU's...

that's similar amount to an 'average' oral supplement dose... (though since we're looking at endogenous production due to light exposure let's say it would be 'better' than oral ingestion both health wise and at keeping D levels increased)

4 hours of use would yield 4800IU's...

that would mean 2 hours of it only 8 inches away! That is a close distance, though doable... (Warning that there are possible EMF concerns with using the lamps close, not sure if the issues span across all types of bulbs, perhaps someone can test). There is also UVA in the light so that is a built in benefit.

at 12 inches which may be an easier set up it charts 15 Vitamin D IU's per minute... that means 900 IU's per hour, so in 2 hours one would get 1800, in 3 hours 2700 IUs , in 4 hours 3600IU's

please let us know if you try the Exo Terra bulbs, how you feel and if you decide to test your levels!

I would really like to hack a Sperti Vitamin D lamp like Dia used in her successful hack story, where 5 or 10 minutes of exposure significantly boosts levels. However it's also significantly more expensive (over $400) than the Exo Terra bulb which seems to be under 20$.

Anyone already using the Exo Terra 150 or 200 here? I read at the company site that the Exo Terra 150 was previously called the Repti Glo 10...

You need the IR light plus the UV light or the IR light first, then the UV light plus enough DHA for the paramagnetic function of DHA to work... you also need to be fully hydrated and probably being grounded is a great idea... if you make the surface cool that the light is hitting, even more effective...
>
> If you're using an LED setup like for instance this:
> [http://www.amazon.com/RubyLux-Red-B...?srs=9286491011&ie=UTF8&qid=1449098924&sr=8-1](http://www.amazon.com/RubyLux-Red-Blue-LED-Bulb/dp/B00IYSQIPG/ref=lp_9286491011_1_1?srs=9286491011&ie=UTF8&qid=1449098924&sr=8-1)
> cover the leds in plastic wrap and put it against your skin - inside of wrist where you take your pulse - cool it off first with an ice pack - sort of a nonportable poor man's quantlet...
> This is because the leds don't have enough power when they're far away from you -
>
> But truly, the morning sun is the way to go if at all possible - and if you have enough DHA onboard, I would think you could be outside all day with no problems as long as your eyes were exposed to the same frequencies as your skin -
>
> Of course, I could be wrong...

what wattage does jk use?

>
> I remember reading in Ott's book about the Chicago restaurant that used black lights and how good people felt there and no one called in any sick days. I always wondered how that could be if they were using the black lights at night. I was under the impression that UV lights were only meant to be used between sunrise and sunset.

Probably a mix of factors, one being that most people get way too little UV light and any supplementation would make them feel better, even if it was at night.
Another factor is that it's mostly the blue light spectrum that harms circadian rhythms, and blacklights don't have much of it.

### JmanGigHarbor — 2018-11-01T16:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-black-light.16985/post-254719>

I am new to this forum, But I thought I would add to this, as I have been researching this lately and would like to make it simpler for some new people.
So the overall problem with "LED's" is that when they run on "AC" current, it causes them to flicker from the way AC current runs through a normal US house. This AC current will pulse as it is used. In order to get around this issue, you can use LED's that run directly from "DC" current. Because this is a direct line of current and does not pulse like AC current does. This can be easily tested using your smart phone on video record, setting it to "slow motion" mode you are able to see the LED light pulse on AC current. Now, if you start looking around there are actually AC to DC wall adapters for running off a standard US home power supply. What I have found is on amazon you can find a UV 12v Led flood light, that is 10w. this can then be ran off an AC-DC power supply. And by doing this it has a perfect, constant light output with no flickering.
There are also easier to test devices that are 5v DC UV light bars, that come with there own wall adapter. I have also found a red light LED that uses this same 5v DC adapter method. I will provide links below to all the devices, I hope this helps someone out

UV Flood Light (395-400nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o02\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

AC to DC power supply
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B073QTNF9F/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B073QTNF9F/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Low power 5v UV Light Bar (395-400nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B071CQF7V5/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B071CQF7V5/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

5v 20w Red Light Bar (650nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07D16NQMV/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07D16NQMV/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Standard Light for lighting room (2700k) "These are powered off the same AC to DC power supply as above"
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B077Q2G7NZ/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o01\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B077Q2G7NZ/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

# Thread: The 1997 Japanese Seizure Event
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-1997-japanese-seizure-event.16986/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-13T05:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-1997-japanese-seizure-event.16986/post-179283>

"At this point, viewers started to complain of blurred vision, headaches, dizziness and [nausea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nausea).[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-banned-1)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-csi-5)[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-reuters-10) Some experienced seizures, [blindness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blindness), convulsions and loss of consciousness.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-banned-1)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-csi-5) Japan's Fire Defense Agency reported that a total of 685 viewers – 310 boys and 375 girls – were taken to hospitals by ambulances.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-csi-5)[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-washington-11) Although many victims recovered during the ambulance trip, more than 150 of them were admitted to hospitals.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-csi-5)[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-washington-11) Two people remained hospitalized for more than two weeks.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-washington-11) Some other people had seizures when parts of the scene were rebroadcast during news reports on the seizures.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-reuters-10) Only a small fraction of the 685 children treated were diagnosed with[photosensitive epilepsy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosensitive_epilepsy).[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-12) This phenomenon was later called "Pokémon Shock".[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-banned-1)[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-Mental-13)"

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dennō\_Senshi\_Porygon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon)

They established a series of guidelines for future animated programs,[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-washington-11)[\[22\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denn%C5%8D_Senshi_Porygon#cite_note-24) including:

-   Flashing images, especially those with red, should not flicker faster than three times per second. If the image does not have red, it still should not flicker faster than five times per second.
-   Flashing images should not be displayed for a total duration of more than two seconds.
-   Stripes, whirls and concentric circles should not take up a large part of the television screen.

Scompy believes, "This event only scratches the surface of what's really going on."

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-13T05:13:48+0300

it does.............pulsing light is for experts

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-identify-the-glasses-dr-kruse-is-wearing-in-this-video.16987/post-179353>

The cell is a play room for photons. Life is based upon the entangled activity of photobiochemical and photobiophysical processes. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Biological processes notably seem to be able to live on the edge of the second law of thermodynamics to some extent by remaining metastable and far from equilibrium to take full advantage of the probabilities that quantum mechanics gives nature. Electromagnetic radiation or light in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction chains and their outcome. Thermal management in mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to heat but also the keeper of biochemical reproducibility and constancy of reaction kinetics. The learner will want to explore the realm of biophotonics, addressing a number of fundamental mechanisms and hypotheses related to life, light and health. We do just that at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) IRA, NIR, IR and red light are all stored deeply in water to make an EZ. An EZ = battery. Water inside a cell is fundamentally a repository for light. If you look at a cell's spectrum of color........it stores most of its purple light inside the nucleus.........and most of the red light toward the periphery of the cell. This is why humans radiate light around 900 nm when you use GDV cameras or IR camera with spectroscopic analysis. Ubi 5 talks about mito-nuclear coaptation..........it is a function of how much purple is 'stored' in the nucleus (DNA/RNA) and how much red is made in mitochondria and below the Cell Membranes at carbonyl groups.
When batteries are discharging cells are dying. They die by releasing too much ELF-UV light. To divide cells use small pulses of ELF-UV to stimulate mitosis. Too much release = oncogenesis due to too many mitosis. Cells are looking to survive rather than reproduce in this case. No reproduction = death and disease = modern world's neolithic disease and infertility issues.

# Thread: Google Trends: Albert Einstein vs. Britney Spears
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-trends-albert-einstein-vs-britney-spears.16989/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-13T21:21:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-trends-albert-einstein-vs-britney-spears.16989/post-179398>

If you don't know about this feature on Google, it's a way to track popularity about various people and topics. I did a few on "Jack Kruse" and "DHA" to see what people know about it and general public interest based on searches.

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=Jack Kruse&date=3/2011 56m&cmpt=date&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=Jack%20Kruse&date=3%2F2011%2056m&cmpt=date&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=DHA&cmpt=date&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=DHA&cmpt=date&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

You're able to enter in time-ranges and such, and also overlay the results to see comparisons of searched-terms against each other like this one with Einstein and Britney Spears:

[https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=/m/0jcx, /m/015f7&cmpt=q&tz=Etc/GMT+7](https://www.google.com/trends/explore#q=%2Fm%2F0jcx%2C%20%2Fm%2F015f7&cmpt=q&tz=Etc%2FGMT%2B7)

As expected, Britney is the Hare doomed to lose to Einstein--the Turtle that will win the race.

### John Smith — 2015-10-13T23:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-directly-at-the-sun.16990/post-179409>

I look directly at the sun in AM when I get up and before my BAB.

Is it bad to look directly at the sun for too long and during midday, afternoon. Many times I think that I am damaging my eyes by doing this.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-14T03:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-directly-at-the-sun.16990/post-179423>

I dont think this is wise

### thomas — 2015-10-14T14:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-directly-at-the-sun.16990/post-179447>

It's totally safe to look when the sun is at a low altitude: less than 2-5 degrees high. the sun will be orange/red then.
When High in the sky it's dangerous. And it's painful (big clue!!) so you shouldn't do it.

# Thread: Sleeping directly on the magnetico
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2015-10-14T03:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179417>

I'm planning on purchasing a magnetico soon and I was wondering if its safe to sleep directly on the magnetico, I'm assuming this will be great, but just wanted to ask to be sure. I've slept on the floor without a mattress for a couple years now, usually with just a couple blankets under me or a thin pad. When I start using the magnetico I'm not planning on buying a mattress, so I'm guessing being 8in closer to the pad than the design intend will mean a much bigger magnetic field because of the inverse square law.

Just wondering if anyone has tried this or would recommend against it for any reason?

### JMO — 2015-10-14T04:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179425>

I don't think they recommend sleeping directly on it.

### endless — 2015-10-14T04:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179426>

They recommend being a minimum of 4 inches above the magnetico to avoid the positive return field spikes. This is pretty clearly diagrammed on their website.

> [https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/comparison-diagram/](https://magneticosleep.com/why-were-better/comparison-diagram/)

### JMO — 2015-10-14T04:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179427>

Don't forget to ask for the kruse discount. Hopefully they still give it.

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-14T05:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179429>

They do still offer the discount. I just used it!

### Just Breathe — 2015-10-15T17:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-directly-on-the-magnetico.16994/post-179563>

I used the discount in August and it was still valid. You need a mattress with at least 4 in of thickness between you and the magnetico.

# Thread: What the sun is messaging you in frequencies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-sun-is-messaging-you-in-frequencies.16995/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-14T17:58:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-the-sun-is-messaging-you-in-frequencies.16995/post-179470>

Note the red part of the curve below CAREFULLY.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Those are the wavelengths that come to your surfaces. Those are the ones you need to concern yourself with. Note the eye camera only uses the visible spectra.......and other cells in your eye and in your body use the two portions of sunlight not used in the eye camera, namely UV and IR light. If you look at every cell in the body each cell is topographically organized with respect to light frequencies. Purple UV light is stored in the nucleus because of DNA and RNA and out close to the periphery is where red or IR light dominates. Humans radiate mostly 800-900 nm light. Get any IR FLIR camera to check this FACT. Every cell radiates small amounts of ELF UV to signal one another to divide and communicate coherently to make cells form organs and organs forms synctiums. Now stop and think why Mother Nature was crafty. She was building a playground for photons to limit entropy and randomness to make life occur, recharge, and regenerate. Nothing in physics really regenerates. It degenerates. Think of snubbers fabrics. They are UV resistant but kept in sun long enough they will fade. There is no program in "tangible items" to regenerate. In biology we use UV and IR light to build those regeneration programs. It's time to elevate your game on light. It is the most powerful drug and tool a doctor or patient can wield in the war on disease.

Hey guys.

I've been following the epi paleo diet, blue blockers as soon as the sun goes down, turning all electrics off at 8pm, and I'm out in the morning sun for as long as possible for the last few months (took my first ice bath yesterday). Lately (for the last 3 weeks) I've been getting a lot of static shocks (a few times a day) when I touch something metal. I remember getting these occasionally when I was a child but haven't experienced any for years. Is there any relevance? Or just coincidence? Good, bad, indifferent? Maybe I'm not grounding enough?

# Thread: The Quantlet.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.16999/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-10-15T01:45:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quantlet.16999/post-179500>

This Quantlet stuff is extremely fascinating ...

I must admit I haven't been paying as much attention as I should be - and am behind. I also thought that very likely, I wouldn't be able to afford it. Although I don't have the remotest idea of the cost!.

My guiding principles have been light, water, and magnetism - or as Dr. K. refers to these ...The three legged stool.

So - I can see that the Quantlet is the three legged stool that we can wear on our wrist!

# Thread: Shielded power cable for eliminating monitor electric fields ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-15T06:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179543>

In the EMF bootcamp series Michael Neaurt said that you can remove a lot of the computer monitor electric fields with a shielded power cord. I couldn't find one anywhere local so I just wrapped my existing one in aluminum foil and grounded it. Bad mistake. The fields INCREASED dramatically.

I tried more and more foil to increase the mass of the shielding so it would start to absorb and not reflect. I tried grounding on different locations on the cord, all kinds of stuff.

My best result was just clipping the grounding cable straight to the cord rubber casing (?) It reduced the fields by almost half just doing that. Adding a second grounding cable made it worse again.

Interesting that the best absorber of electric fields is the human body

Anybody else had luck with shielding a power cable? I don't want to order one and find out it doesn't work.
This stuff can be really finicky, even for an experienced electrician like me!

### Scompy — 2015-10-15T07:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179544>

Yes, you have to constantly monitor with a meter, then remonitor again to take measurements. Same with Faraday cages and RF. You may think everything works or works better, but only the meter reveals the truth.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-16T04:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179631>

Today I went to Home Depot, bought a few feet of metal clad (armored) house wiring and a three pronged 120v plug. I snipped off the connector on the monitor side of the existing cable and soldered it to the new wiring and plug. I tried to ground the armor to the ground wire but it just would not stick to the solder no matter how much I buffed up the clad.

So I just plugged it in and grounded the clad with my alligator to ground plug cable.

Well.. it totally did not work..

I am hesitant to purchase a shielded AC power cable for the monitor because I don't want to end up with the same result.

I kind of got the impression from Michael Neaurt's bootcamp that it was an easy fix - like you could just go down to Radio Shack and buy a shielded AC power cable for a computer monitor. I went to three different computer stores and they all looked at me like I was from outer space!

I dunno...I guess the tinfoil hat stays until I can figure something else out

I found one site online that was raving about a cord that they have but I did not see any information on how to buy it anywhere:[http://www.lowemfoffice.com/shielded\_power\_cord.htm](http://www.lowemfoffice.com/shielded_power_cord.htm)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-10-16T05:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-179632>

I just found a place that sells them. I'm not sure about the quality but its something -
[http://www.slt.co/products/ShieldedPowerCords/ShieldedPowerCords-6Foot-12Foot.aspx](http://www.slt.co/products/ShieldedPowerCords/ShieldedPowerCords-6Foot-12Foot.aspx)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-01T04:14:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-182935>

Just updating- I got myself a shielded power cable for my monitor but it really didn't do much. The readings came down a little bit but hardly worth the hassle. But who knows...maybe my house has a weak ground.

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-08-03T15:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-247379>

> [Cpt.Tired said:](/goto/post?id=179543)
>
> In the EMF bootcamp series Michael Neaurt said that you can remove a lot of the computer monitor electric fields with a shielded power cord. I couldn't find one anywhere local so I just wrapped my existing one in aluminum foil and grounded it. Bad mistake. The fields INCREASED dramatically.
>
> I tried more and more foil to increase the mass of the shielding so it would start to absorb and not reflect. I tried grounding on different locations on the cord, all kinds of stuff.
>

>
> Interesting that the best absorber of electric fields is the human body
>
> Anybody else had luck with shielding a power cable? I don't want to order one and find out it doesn't work.
> This stuff can be really finicky, even for an experienced electrician like me!

### Christina Gagnon — 2018-08-03T15:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielded-power-cable-for-eliminating-monitor-electric-fields.17002/post-247380>

# Thread: Hello from Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ho-chi-minh-city-vietnam.17004/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Day — 2015-10-15T16:53:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ho-chi-minh-city-vietnam.17004/post-179559>

I'm in a pretty specific situation. I suffer from chronic post nasal drip. I was tested for allergies in Houston, TX a couple of years ago. The test yielded impressive allergies to pollen and dust mites. I began allergy shots. According to my ENT, the post nasal drip is the last allergy symptom to go away. I was about 1.5-2 years into the allergy shots when I decided to move to HCMC, VN. I cannot afford to do allergy shots at this time. While taking the allergy shots, I noticed some improvement in my symptoms, but not much. I believe my doctor that if I stay the course with the shots, my symptoms would go away, but is there another way for me to heal my post nasal drip? I don't have access to a kitchen at the moment, so restrictive diets are tough here, but can fixing my diet fix the post nasal drip despite the fact that I'm allergic to stuff in the air? I ate a paleo diet for over 2 weeks at one point in the states and the improvement was negligible. I also ate a vegetarian diet for 10 days and didn't notice much improvement either. Sorry, if it's a silly question, but I'm new to these approaches to health.

Cheers,
Chris

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-15T17:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ho-chi-minh-city-vietnam.17004/post-179566>

welcome. Get a vie light and use it as directed. Add DHA daily and full spectrum sun of Vietnam. Make sure the DHA is not farmed.

Pretty easy to fix.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-15T17:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ho-chi-minh-city-vietnam.17004/post-179567>

being vegan will worsen it.

### Chris Day — 2015-10-17T17:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-ho-chi-minh-city-vietnam.17004/post-179724>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=179566)
>
> welcome. Get a vie light and use it as directed. Add DHA daily and full spectrum sun of Vietnam. Make sure the DHA is not farmed.
>
> Pretty easy to fix.

[http://vielight.com/vielight-products/](http://vielight.com/vielight-products/)

Thanks you very much for the reply. Will the cheapest one, the Vielight 633 Red, do what I need it to do? I'm going to visit Houston in a couple of months and have one waiting for me there.

# Thread: Dream control and redox potential
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-10-15T17:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/post-179564>

During my childhood and teenage years, I remember having the ability to "rewind" my dreams if something bad ever happened, inserting an alternate positive outcome. Usually this involved the death of a loved one or sometimes even myself. As an adult I lost that ability. Actually, until the past few years, I hardly ever dreamed, and on the rare occasion I did, it was generally some sort of nightmare with something dreadful happening to my children. Awful.

Well, recently (since reducing carbs due to season, and actually about a week ago cutting out carbs except for those present in fibrous veggies like green beans and broccoli, also increasing fat intake), I have been having very vivid dreams. Coincidentally, for the past week my recall of them when awake is excellent. I can literally push "play" in my brain and replay the significant part (generally the climax of the dream), visualizing it in my head just like I was watching it on a screen. Very cool.

Last night, however, something bad happened in my dream and I was able to rewind it and insert a positive outcome once again. My father was shot in the back seat of a car, so I rewound and inserted dialog that would change his positioning in the car (and that of myself, in the passenger seat, and my children in the rear seat with my dad -- getting on the floor instead of just ducking down) so THE DRIVER (hilariously it was O-ren Ishii from Kill Bill 2) was killed instead. Athough as a child I could alter events my dreams, I don't ever remember altering dialog which then altered an event. In any case, it's interesting.

So I know that Dr. Kruse has mentioned that dream recall is a sign of good redox potential. Is it possible that being able to rewind and alter your dreams is a sign of excellent redox potential? Is it possible to have a surplus?

### Dennis Clark — 2015-10-18T20:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/post-179791>

Hi, Hansen...

This sounds like the childhood ability for lucid dreaming, which adults often lose. Your ability apparently didn't get too far away from you. I hope you develop it further, since lucid dreaming is a great way to make things happen that can transfer into real life. No personal experience yet (as an adult). Still working on recovering it.

### CTforlife — 2015-10-18T22:29:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/post-179802>

DMT, vibration, nitrogen can pull electrons while leaving the rest behind.

### Inger — 2015-10-19T08:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/post-179850>

I had a vivid dream last night....... and in the evening before I lied nude in front of the fireplace in purple light earthing on a lamb skin on my stone floor...
dipping in my rain barrel in between
it was beautiful
I want every evening to be like that.......

### Dennis Clark — 2015-10-19T23:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dream-control-and-redox-potential.17005/post-179896>

JK and all...right on...getting more AM UV every day, plus wearing blue blockers at night, have helped me get more good sleep (less interrupted) AND more vivid dreams. It almost seems miraculous. Looking forward to getting the reptile light used by Jansz for my home office when I am working indoors. It will supplement my outdoor breaks in the Arizona sun. Already stopped wearing sunglasses while golfing or outdoors for any reason. All are simple changes for moving closer to optimum. What a ride!

### Scompy — 2015-10-15T19:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/virtual-reality-coming-soon.17007/post-179582>

I personally see that we are on the doorstep of having easy-access to the replacement of reality. No one questions the physics behind the technology they place on their bodies and how it negatively interacts and intersects with their biology.

First 2mins...

Strapping Cellphone onto your head...
[https://www.kickstarter.com/project...rtual-reality-headset-for-sma?ref=fundedtoday](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/1040005785/dscvr-a-roadworthy-virtual-reality-headset-for-sma?ref=fundedtoday)

Completely 'untethered' = No hardwiring to mitigate RF and magnetic EMF

UGH!!!!

The public has 0% awareness about RF-EMF, magnetic EMF and how this impacts the eyes. For one, someone needs to champion EMF reduction and blue-light blocking settings into these devices. The reality is when a person gets ahold of VR and has a typical lifestyle of gaming, that person will spend hours on end each day using this tech on their bodies.

I don't know the answer to this question yet, but the primary one that's hitting me is:

**What poison is worse:**
1\. Playing on VR tech for an hour?
2\. Talking on a cell phone up to the head for an hour?"

**The next question to ask is about exposure time when combining tech:**
Play on your VR tech for an hour. Then go talk on the cell phone with your friends for an hour. 1+1=2.

### Scompy — 2018-02-22T08:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/virtual-reality-coming-soon.17007/post-235354>

This is about all of the biology they understand about the human eye...

"A surprise inclusion on the system is the addition of eye-tracking, styled for the sake of utilizing a technology that focuses high-resolution graphics on the center of your vision to account for the biology of how your eyes work. Foveated rendering will be included in the specs for the reference design and will allow a pair of cameras to track where your eyes are looking and will adjust the resolution to lighten the load on the graphics processing. This can allow for increased battery life or higher resolution displays."

[https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/21/q...-its-vision-for-the-near-future-of-mobile-vr/](https://techcrunch.com/2018/02/21/qualcomm-shares-details-on-its-vision-for-the-near-future-of-mobile-vr/)

# Thread: UV Bulbs to biohack awakening? / Magnetic sleeping mat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daniel — 2015-10-15T22:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-179614>

Hi all, since winter is coming, and I live in Budapest, Hungary, we will soon have very short days, when the sun comes up after 8am. I usually wake up around 5-6am to work or exercise, and I was thinking if I buy one of those UV lightbulbs what they use for reptilians living in captivity might help to wake up. What do you think?

It's not possible to sleep with the sunset, cause it goes down at 5-6pm in the winter, but I usually go to bed around 8:30-9pm, and I have a fairly good sleep.

Also cuould you link me a few stuff to read about magnetic sleeping mat, and why is it good for? I heard Jack Kruse talking about it in a podcast, but couldn't manage to find information on the website. Thanks a lot!

### Daniel — 2015-10-19T10:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-179853>

Ok, so I found the following lightbulbs available over here:
\- Mercury UV lightbulb called SYLVANIA HSW 125W E27 BLACKLIGHT 0023970
\- PHILIPS IR RED R95 100W 230V E27 (this one is red)
\- TUNGSRAM INFRASEC 150R/IR/CL/E27 245V TU 509699 (transparent)

Are these good? Which of the IR do I need, the red or the transparent?

I work in office / on business trip, but mostly driving in car... and it's almost winter over here, which means less and less sunlight

### Monica W. — 2015-10-30T21:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-180804>

Many of us have Magnetico sleep pads. They're made here in the US so I'm not sure if you'd even be able to get one in Hungary, but you can probably find something similar where you are. I have a 20 gauss Magnetico and I LOVE it! I sleep like a baby and within 2 weeks of sleeping on it my chronic neck and back pain was gone.

Here's the Magnetico website for reference. Lots of great info there. I also suggest you search for "Magnetico" on the forum and you should find more info: [https://magneticosleep.com/](https://magneticosleep.com/)

### Daniel — 2015-10-30T21:51:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-180805>

>
> Here's the Magnetico website for reference. Lots of great info there. I also suggest you search for "Magnetico" on the forum and you should find more info: [https://magneticosleep.com/](https://magneticosleep.com/)

Hi Monica, thanks for your feedback. Well we don't have Magnetico, but we do have some magnetic sheets from Magbett and Billerback, however they don't state the gauss values, only talking about how nice the wool cover of it is. Not sure if it would have the same effect. They say it has magnetic stripes in them. I think you have to put these right under your bedsheet.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-bulbs-to-biohack-awakening-magnetic-sleeping-mat.17008/post-180806>

Are those sheets grounding sheets? Is there a cord/wire attached to them that you would stick into the Earth? I Googled "Magbett and Billerback" and didn't get any results. Do you have a web site for them that I can look at?

Do not use a grounding mat if your house is not grounded. I tried using one last year and experienced all sorts of weird feelings and I was grounded to the outside. Turned out my house had old old wiring and nothing was grounded. By me grounding myself to the outside I was a magnet for all of the bad electricity from the house. All of the nnEMF;s were going though me. We just had the house rewired and it is much much better but still will not ground myself inside. I either go outside or sit in my basement which is bare concrete.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-climate-change.17009/post-179624>

nnEMF = lowers the quantum yield of the sun and magnetic field = decreased photosynthetic capacity of all things that rely on it.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-10-16T06:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-climate-change.17009/post-179639>

Yup. Incoming Solar EMF is the strongest driver of our climate.

Solar Radiations drives the Earth's Magnetic field. Less solar radiation, less magnetism here on earth, compromised brain function.

\- [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2003JA010019/full](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2003JA010019/full)
\- [http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/stor...pi&s=718cd133e1f57d3410a35007444373dee242c1c5](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/store/10.1029/2006RS003478/asset/rds5349.pdf?v=1&t=ift3gxpi&s=718cd133e1f57d3410a35007444373dee242c1c5)
\- [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306987703000276](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306987703000276)

Decreased magnetic field strength means more cosmic rays into earth. Major implications include immune dysfunction in biological organisms, and increased cloud cover with less useful solar input like UV and IR light ([https://scholar.google.com.au/scholar?q=cosmic+rays+cloud+author:Svensmark&btnG=&hl=en&as\_sdt=0,5](https://scholar.google.com.au/scholar?q=cosmic+rays+cloud+author%3ASvensmark&btnG=&hl=en&as_sdt=0%2C5))

# Thread: Dancing and red dresses and hugging and loving and looking hot and being a girl!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-10-16T11:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179643>

Awhile back we had a great thread about "dresses" ..... I learned so much about life and living and we had so much fun and lots of guys contributed too!

This is partly in response to something JK said ..... He thinks it is pretty easy to do it all if you get enuf lite and DHA. ....... I don't disagree - Of course lite and DHA are huge - no argument there.

It is hard work being a woman! We have to look good - preferably really hot, be a great cook, be sensitive, supportive and caring and loving, be great in bed , take care of the kids, the house, the shopping, contribute financially ..... and lets not forget to try to keep up with Jack and the blogs and the webinars and FB and all the reading etc. et. etc.

Holy smokes!

Being a woman is not for the feint hearted! We need to be tough and strong and at the same time gentle and caring...... and take care of our family and their needs as well as everything else.

My guy will sometimes remark that a woman we pass when out walking is "high maintenance". That ruffles my feathers a bit!

Guys love looking at a woman who is well dressed and in great shape and manicured and pedicured and coiffed within an inch of her life........ and why not???

But .... that takes a lot of money and time and self focus - well, unless you are young and a natural beauty.

How are all you girls juggling all of this?????

tell me.....

### Inger — 2015-10-16T15:10:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179647>

Well... I have no issues dressing like a girl... I love that  I almost always wear skirts and stuff.....
but.....
My nails are so not cute!!! OMG as I dig oysters and open them with my hands.. it makes for no pretty nails!!! That is my issue.........
I need to make me manicure and pedicure way more often! And I have no money to let someone else do it.....
My hands and feet (as I go barefoot a lot in dirt) are so not pretty..........
what to do

### fitness@home — 2015-10-16T16:51:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dancing-and-red-dresses-and-hugging-and-loving-and-looking-hot-and-being-a-girl.17011/post-179657>

Haircut and pedi/mani every 5-6 weeks. Since I am rough on my hands (like Inger) I only get fingernails trimmed & buffed.

Have to wear dress clothes all week for work, so no problem there with my feminine side. Nights my husband really likes my attire of just a simple tank top and panties .

_Love_ wearing sundresses on the weekends.

### thomas — 2015-10-16T20:45:18+0300

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-17T03:23:34+0300

### Inger — 2015-10-17T10:37:44+0300

he told me he thinks it is sexy when women have hands looking like they are doing something... like dirty..... he said he does not care one bit about that women have so manicured nails.. he said he loves wild looking women...
maybe he said it only to make me happy... because he loves me.. but I do think he is like that. He is very special for sure.. too bad he is my brother so I cant marry him...!

and the dirt is so hard to get off

One day one will come in my way

### caroline — 2015-10-17T11:00:05+0300

# Thread: Hello from London
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### omegajosh — 2015-10-17T09:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179706>

Hello all,

Having first been introduced to Jack's work via Ben Greenfield - one of the few popular health enthusiasts who seems to appreciate Jack's ideas - I have been getting increasingly interested in Mr Kruse's theories. They have become more and more relevant to me as I deal with worsening symptoms of adrenal fatigue/hypothyroidism, as confirmed by ASI (low cortisol and drastically low DHEA).

I have just this week quit my dream job in the forestry industry to dedicate some time to recovering. Fortunately my company has been very understanding and promised to give me back my job when/if this recovery is completed.

As I mentioned, I live in London, so am not in exactly the best spot in the world to enact Dr Kruse's prescriptions i.e. low EMF and morning Sun. However, and here is where I am looking for some advice, I have some money in the bank and, as I mentioned, a lot of free time. I am willing to temporarily relocate to anywhere in Europe and dedicate myself to returning my HPA axis to normal function. My criteria so far are: low EMF zone; lots of available natural light; source of good quality; affordable food.

It is slightly frustrating that I am having to take this time (and money) out of my life but I am looking at my situation positively: I have always wanted to spend some time living independently in a remote setting and by optimising my environment I can truly put Jack's theories to the test.

I will post my blood tests (Standard blood panel, ASI, Vit D, Thyroid panel inc. Reverse t3, testosterone) as soon as I get the last few back and would love to hear peoples opinions on them.

Thanks for reading, I look forward to many more posts here!
J

### caroline — 2015-10-17T10:21:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179707>

welcome Josh....

Please start a journal in the journal section and give Dr. Kruse your health history so he can direct you.

How is your Mom's health? Grandma? siblings? were you breast fed?

Describe your home environment......

Are you doing C/T? getting outside first thing in the morning barefoot and little clothes?

What are you eating? drinking?

Seafood should absolutely be a priority.

You need lots of sunshine and an ocean and low nnEMF..... maybe you could go camping in a forest and on a lake. Go fishing and catch dinner!

Have you read the last two guest blogs? one by Clayton and one by RobH...

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX

Are you listening to JK's podcasts and reading the blogs?

Exercise?

### omegajosh — 2015-10-17T10:44:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179709>

Wow, speedy reply. Many thanks, Caroline.

I will absolutely start a journal - I'll do that today

**Describe your home environment......**Have recently found out that the whole house needs rewiring as, due to very old wiring, the plug sockets are not grounded. I'm not sure if this makes a difference to EMF exposure or not. I use the computer far too much - i suspect it is my biggest source of EMF exposure.

**Are you doing C/T? getting outside first thing in the morning barefoot and little clothes?**I have just started CT. I tried once before but think I got too eager by diving into a cold bath. It took me 2 hours to warm up again - a truly horrible experience. I get up in the morning and go outside. The trouble is, I wake up at 6-6.30am and can't get back to sleep. So when I get up it is completely dark outside! A horrible feeling. I have bought a sun lamp (lumie arabica) but it just feels unnatural to use it at that hour of the day.

**What are you eating? drinking?**Eating - high saturated fat from coconut oil and butter, also plenty of olive oil and the odd avocado. I get about 100-150 carb from the so called safe starches and am trying to include more seafood, but oysters are difficult to get for me. I started the leptin reset today with BAB because Jack said that it can also help adrenal issues due to Leptin's interaction with Cortisol (i think). Having said this, I am not fat. In fact, looking at my physique you would think I am in very good shape - 6 pack, decent muscle tone. Knowing my ideal bodyshape though, I am underweight. I will be getting the blood tests back for reverse T3 next week which will tell me once and for all whether I am Leptin resistant or not.

**You need lots of sunshine and an ocean and low nnEMF..... maybe you could go camping in a forest and on a lake. Go fishing and catch dinner!**This sounds like a plan although I'm not sure I have a feeling that I would need a a good few months to get back on track and I'm not sure camping would be suitable for a long term stay.
**
Have you read the last two guest blogs? one by Clayton and one by RobH...**No, I will do these today.

**Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX**No, I have ordered the book today if you think there is info in there that is more in depth than the blogs.

**Are you listening to JK's podcasts and reading the blogs?**
Yes, working through them.

**Exercise?**
Not at the moment. The only exercise I seem to tolerate is walking and very heavy, low rep lifting. Anything more intense and I get pounding heart rate and flush a bright, unhealthy red, followed by exhaustion for the rest of the day.

### caroline — 2015-10-17T11:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179711>

Dr. K's book is the first two years of the blog ....an easy, fast way to get info. The podcasts are great - Jack has a very earthy way of speaking ...he speaks our language on a podcast!

It is all about the three legged stool ...... light, water, magnetism....

You didn't say how old you are ..... come to Oz and back pack around. This country loves backpackers! - especially In Queensland. Yew \[on the forum\] moved to Cannes and his health improved greatly. He had lived in Melbourne.

safe starches???

don't forget about canned sardines and mackerel - very inexpensive...

what kind of water are you drinking?

your computer ..... are you wired in?

Get all electronics out of your bedroom.

You NEED blue blocker glasses!

how is your sleep?

### omegajosh — 2015-10-17T11:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179712>

I am 23 - too young for these health issues! I could come to Oz and mooch around a bit. The trouble is, backpackers don't exactly lead the most Krusean existence. Eating healthy is hard when you're on the road and hostels don't lend themselves to a good night's sleep. If I was going to come over to Australia I think I'd have to sort out some accommodation with someone who 'gets' this way of life.

**safe starches???**I know, I know. Apparently there is no such thing. But I am just overwhelmed by the amount of people who claim to have ruined their adrenals by low carbing or IFing - we are talking thousands of people. Far more than are claiming to have healed their adrenals through ketosis. I am just playing it safe at the moment but would be happy for someone to change my mind on this. I tried ketosis a few months ago around the same time I attempted CT and my blood sugars went through the roof. My fasting and post-prandial blood sugars basically doubled in just a few days. I figured this much be a stress response so I stopped immediately. Not going to lie, It scared me.

**don't forget about canned sardines and mackerel - very inexpensive...**I love canned sardines and eat them regularly. One issue though is histamine. I have an intolerance to too much histamine - which I suspect is due to producing too little of it's anti-inflammatory antagonist, cortisol.

**what kind of water are you drinking?**
Good question. Mostly tap water. Although I have been buying highland spring water (sparkling and in plastic bottle :0) recently.

**your computer ..... are you wired in?**I try to only use the internet when wired into the router via ethernet cable. I use wireless on the odd occasion.

**Get all electronics out of your bedroom.**Already done

**You NEED blue blocker glasses!**Already done

**how is your sleep?**
I get to sleep fine but often wake up at 2 or 3 with pounding heart (adrenaline?). As I mentioned, I wake up at 6AM, when I would much rather wake up two hours later when the sun is up.

### fitness@home — 2015-10-17T15:51:19+0300

Welcome Josh. Caroline has already given you great advice!

### omegajosh — 2015-10-17T16:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17015/post-179717>

> [thisbirdhaswings said:](/goto/post?id=179716)

> Welcome. I'm struck by your courage to become aware and then willing make dramatic changes at a relatively young age. Light makes a huge difference. I moved to the Gulf Coast based in large part on what I was hearing from Jack, and it was well worth it. As you are seeking a place to heal, keep that in mind. Best of luck.

Thanks for the encouragement. I don't see myself as brave. I have been so silly getting myself into this state. Stressful life, staring at computer (certainly no f-lux) until 2am. I have know realised that the truly important thing in life is your health. Without that you cannot enjoy relationships, food, sport, anything! This is why I have decided to dedicate myself 100% to recovery.

I will keep searching for sunny places to relocate. Ideally I'd find somewhere I could share with likeminded people but I think that's unlikely. Keep the suggestions coming people.

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eilee — 2015-10-17T20:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ct.17016/post-179745>

I have very cold extremities (lower arms, legs, tip of nose). I remember reading someplace that I shouldn't do CT if this is the case. Would love to know if this is the case.

### seanb4 — 2015-10-18T13:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ct.17016/post-179776>

I have cold extremities too. I think it depends on if they are caused by a high omega 6 ratio or not. If they are caused by high O6 low DHA then possibly better to wait until your ratio gets better. If it is caused by low thyroid, like I think mine is, then CT should help.

You can always try doing it with your head legs and arms out of the water...bearing in mind it will take quite a few sessions before getting adapted. If it is to much you can always get out and put your extremities in warm water or next to a radiator or something...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-19T02:44:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ct.17016/post-179831>

cold legs = low DC current = low UV light assimilation or excessive loss of ELF-UV light

The rest is all me....They do have a member rate for everything else so I don't pay full price, but considering what I'm paying in I think they could pay a bit more.

If possible I'd like to get them paying more. They are getting 4,872.60 a year from me and if they paid for everything I've done this year it would amount to only 400 dollars total.

Some of it is subsidy from my employer, but they are getting paid regardless. I'd like to get more out of this if possible. How do I go about getting them to cover more. I want them to cover all my labs, and the doctors visits. They'd still be making a profit.

Sure insurance is also about the possibility of a catastrophic happening..and if that happened than the 8,000 dollars I've put in over 2 years would be nothing, but I don't see that happening..anyway I'd like to get more out of this.

Also insurance renewal is soon...I'm betting my out of pocket next year will go up...it went from 0 I was paying to 432 dollars...I can only imagine what it's going to increase to.

# Thread: Jan Baalsrud
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-10-18T16:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179780>

Has anyone heard about this man before?

His seems to be yet another remarkable survival story. He was a member of the Norwegian Resistance in WWII and while chased by the Nazis survived swimming through icy water, snow blindness - and even frostbite, amputating nine of his own toes after gangrene set in.

[http://www.scotsman.com/news/jan-baalsrud-the-other-great-escape-1-786223](http://www.scotsman.com/news/jan-baalsrud-the-other-great-escape-1-786223)

This seems to be a book about him:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/We-Die-Alone-David-Howarth/dp/1847678459/](http://www.amazon.co.uk/We-Die-Alone-David-Howarth/dp/1847678459/)

### Optimalbound — 2015-10-18T19:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jan-baalsrud.17018/post-179786>

Definitely heard of [Baalsrud](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jan_Baalsrud). I remember that his incredible story made quite an impression on me when I read it in my early teens. Especially the part where he amputates his own gangrene-ridden toes... (Yes, you CAN do too much CT! )

There's a movie from 1957 that depicts his espace from the German army. Not exactly an Oscar-winning production, but I believe it is historically quite accurate.
[https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5017C6434C63875C)

# Thread: Fire Therapy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-therapy.17019/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Prall — 2015-10-18T18:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-therapy.17019/post-179785>

"Cool" and gloomy day in Santa Monica got me thinking about fire.

Where, when, why we historically used standard wood-burning fire: Mostly used at night. Contains primarily orange, red, IR light (aka heat). Most needed/used in low-light cycle seasons/environments. Used more in cold winter than in hot summer.

So...it would seem that there is a very elegant anthropological quantum health history of the use of fire as it pertains to quantum principles.

Red and IR light at night in the eyes and on the skin. Used in a cold environment where that light has higher quantum yield and better electron flow...where the cold air has higher oxygen density for both our skin, eyes, lungs, and the fire itself. Usually associated with cultures that typically eat a seafood and fat rich diet.

Could this be another example of the quantum story of our entangled biology with nature??? Might we consider using this ancient "bio-hack" from a spectrum of light generated from natural elements like wood as a fuel source?

Perhaps this is not an original thought or question as I have not combed the forum. But just throwing this out into Universe.

# Thread: Warren Found Hope...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Narrew — 2015-10-19T00:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-179810>

History:
Last 10 years...fighting weight issues, had some big losses but gained it all back plus more. Cycled through bouts of head in the sand because I gave up trying to lose weight the conventional way, then eventually would read and try again...but got pretty beat down over time.

April 2015...@475lbs, inflammation pain in the lower leg (skin rupture) and feet got so bad that the pain motivated me to learn about inflammation which led me to LCHF. I Thought I had been eating "fairly" good though it still kept my insulin levels to high to lose weight. At the end of April, I cut total carbs to 20g total per day max, upped saturated fats and added better proteins. In 2 weeks pain in feet were gone and skin rupture healed. I had 4+ weeks of keto flu during the transition, but was feeling so damn good mentally I was convinced I was on the right path.

The first 8 weeks or so, each time I thought I couldn't feel any better (mood wise), the next week would be better. It has taken time for my body to catch up, I had a lot muscle atrophy and it wasn't till month 4 or so, my leg strength started returning. In 26 weeks, am down 41 lbs, and feeling good.

4 weeks ago I came across Jack. I had been been doing good, but I my sleep was still messed up (probably other things I am sure). Read Epi-Paleo RX book, added more seafood, got blublocker clip ons for night time at home. I already blackened bedroom for sleep, flux on the pc. I did get an earthing mat, started drinking only bottled spring water. I had been skipping breakfast until I read about the big ass breakfast, started eating the large breakfast 2 weeks ago.

My sleep is starting to get better with the things I have been adding since reading Jacks material, instead of waking up at 1-2 am and not going back to sleep, now I wake at 3 am and can get back to sleep. I have the early shift, I get up at 430 am to start work at 6 am.

Been reading the Biohacking 101 e-mail course and added my 23andme profile. I also added my recent blood test. I have a ways to go, but am getting better. But I want to learn more. I think I am on the right path food wise, now to understand other things I have no clue about!

Warren

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-19T00:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-179811>

Welcome Warren! There is a lot to read and learn on this site. Take the time to digest what you can and implement some protocols. Good luck!

### JanSz — 2015-10-19T05:24:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-179839>

Narrew
April 2015...@475lbs
Location: Renton, WA ------>**47° 28' 59" N**
Occupation: I.T. Support
\=========================
Washington state, -------->dark, not much light, up North
IT support------->> lots of blue light and nnEMF
\=========================
Humans ---->> creatures dependent on, developed in (equatorial) light

According to your schedule
pick
14 hours and designate it as a day, must be same hours 365days/year
10 hours and designate it as a night

During day time have around you (on top of what you already have)
3 lights, Exoterra Solar Glo 160 W, they should be at about 3 feet distance from you.
Expose as much skin as possible.
Supplement daily with
10000iu/day vit D
onions, garlic, MSM
200mcg/day selenium
45mg/day iodine/iodide (one full dropper full of lugols)
3 tbspoon/day or more coconut oil, more is ok if tolerated (spread over many hours to tolerate more.)
Eat lots of butter.
\===========
Sleep in totally dark room.
At wakeup look at (under)sun or Solar Glo for awhile (that wakes up your cortisol)
Within 30 min of wakeup eat 50% of your daily food intake.

Your hemoglobin is low, possibly low testosterone (check it, also check DHEAs,serum)
Post your underarm temperature in mid day.
.....................................
Have those Solar Glo lamps at your residence and at your work, maximize their use.
No computer screens, TV, cell phones, light other than red at night time.
\--------------------------------
Look over top of your glasses on Solar Glo lights, as often as you can.
Preferably, hold your glasses in hand all the time and put them on only if you must read something.

.................
.

### Narrew — 2015-10-20T04:22:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-179924>

Thank you for the warm welcome!

re: Caroline, is there another journal thread? I did sign up for a silver membership, is there another one?

re: Jack, I am working on that. In addition to what JanSz suggested would a 10,000 lux LED sun lamp help or would that just be adding more nnEMF? (I only have read .0000001 of your posts yet, but I will get there!).

re: JanSz, Thank you for the suggestion on the lamps. I was surprised that I could check off most of the things you suggested. So I will reply to the things I have questions about that I am not doing.

In addition to the vit D I have been adding K2

The iodine I haven't been as high as you suggested, been supplementing with kelp tablets 2-4x 225mcg plus the increase of seafood. Didn't kelp flakes

As far as coconut oil, I tolerate it well. I take 2 tbsp of MCT oil at night before bed, and as with the butter, I hardly add any extra fats now because of the calories. I have opened myself to many things, but upping the calories is hard. I was stalled for a while and when I looked at my diary, I was eating low 2500 to 3k each day. Was feeling good, just stuck. The last 2 weeks down around 2k, and started to lose again. Just remember reading a post of Jack's about where he ate a paleo diet per Wolf and Jamet, but messed up the environment and gained weight...I have a lot to absorb before I understand it.

When I wake up, I currently eat about 35% total calories and 50% protein at 5 am. It seems that at 10:30 am my stomach is growling, been having 2 hard boiled eggs and that ties me over till 12:30 pm when I eat lunch, then I get home around 3 and I try to have dinner eaten by 4:30 pm. I do have 100 grams of mixed frozen berries, heavy creme and 2 tbsp of MCT oil about an hour before bed. (only recently been doing that to see how I sleep, I am doing better, but got a ways to go).

Working on getting more tests, have an appointment with a new provider that I heard good things about, so will see how supportive she is. Otherwise will get them myself.

Post my mid day temp lol. I am friggen cold all the time. I wake up and my moring temp is lucky to be in the high 96 degree range, mostly the high 95's. I just did my tem at 5 PM PST and it was 96.5 I will be interested how that is going forward...so stay tuned!

Think I am getting used to the Darkroom mode in flux...

I hadn't thought about taking off my glasses. There are times that I see better with out them. Though reading a computer screen is tough with out them. One thing I will say, now that I have been eating this way, my eyes don't hurt so much night driving. I would imagine much of that is with the wualityof foods I have been eating...

Thank you again!
