### JanSz — 2015-10-20T17:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-179965>

Please use restraint when using word calories,
specially when suggesting that eating more calories causes weight gain.
That is opinion from other places.
Dr Kruse works hard to change that.
1000calories eaten as sugar are much different than 1000 calories eaten as coconut oil, butter or lard.

Note that there is fasting. People have used that for millenias.
It is ok to skip meals completely two or three times a week.
Drink plenty of spring water.

\================================================
96.5F=35.83C
96F=35.55C
Good underarm midday temperature is 36.6C=97.88F
that is your goal
\================================================
Body temperature is highly correlated to good working thyroid.
Main thyroid hormone is FreeT3.
Low body temperature is main reason for weight gain.
On blood test optimal level of FreeT3 is
FreeT3= (3.5-4.2) pg/mL
Aim at about 4, (obey other conditions first).
CT even as cold shower wakes up thyroid.
\================================================
So,
you want to have optimal amount of thyroid hormones
and
put them to work.
You may have lots of thyroid hormones, but if they do not work, no joy.
\================================================
Doing this test is a good start:
Untill ballanced do it every 6 months.
Hard to get using insurance, cash works better.

[http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com](http://spectracell.mybigcommerce.com)

**Micronutrient Test**

Test report comes with implementation suggestions.
It is better to just comply with them, without much or any corrections.

Post test report here when you get them, the we will continue.
**Find A Draw Site**
[http://www.spectracell.com/patients/find-a-drawsite/](http://www.spectracell.com/patients/find-a-drawsite/)
...............
This is as good place as any other.
It is good idea to keep all in one place.
.

Welcome, Narrew. Warren spelled backwards? Sorta?
I also had sometime similar in my lower legs, back in my SAD days. Blood seeping from a large area of the skin when I was outside working in the garden. Really scary. No skin ruptures, though. I started wearing support hose. Bad as far as getting sunlight to my legs, but helpful for preventing the symptom. Then I also, like you, went on a LCHF paleoish diet, and the problem went away. After finding Jack, I got my 23andMe results, and they said I had a genetic tendency to deep vein thrombosis.

### Narrew — 2015-10-25T03:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-180385>

Water Question

In the Biohacking 101 e-mail course, Lesson 12 talks about "Your well hydrated if your Bun/Creatien Ratio is =/< 10:1"

My recent reading was 22 and have wondered if I was drinking enough water. I have switched totally over to spring water. I had thought I was drinking close to a gallon a day until I ran the numbers. It's more like 64 oz or so (2 Liters). Have had headaches recently and when I check my blood pressure it is normal (my normal cause). Guess lack of enough water could be it?

Jack Wrote in another post: "will see if you think calories matter..or do electrons and protons"

I am starting to understand the idea behind open system and closed system. Long before I heard about low carb, I never belived the saying "a calorie is a calorie". I couldn't articulate it, but based on my experience of reducing my calories very low and still not losing weight, I knew something was off.

Now I find I have a ways to go, haha. Don't get me wrong, I am open to changing my beliefs. Just got a bunch of baggage to discard on the path to understanding. And to be honest, the last 6 months have made me think that I don't have to live this way any more.

Have just spent the last 2-3 hours reading posts and going back to the Epi-Paleo RX book to refresh my memory on some things. I am doing many things right and am adding in other things (and subtracting others). Increasing water even more is a must do. I have been eating seafood at least 4 days a week, but that could be increased as well.

I have added the Exoterra Solar Glo 160 watt lights, today was the first day that I sat under them most of the day (with lots of skin exposure). They have been off for 2 hours and the side of my body that got more direct light still feels warm (looked in the mirror, thought I had a sun burn), and the side that didnt get much direct light is colder. During the week I get about 45 minutes of exposure while I eat my breakfast.

Mid day under arm temp was a constant 98.1 with a low reading of 97.9...now if I could just stick my feet in my armpits, they would be warm and toasty...lol My feet are always cold, even with socks on...

Though over all, I feel like I am moving in a positive direction. Will write more when I get some test results.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-25T07:49:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-180398>

Can't worry about anyone else. Worry about you. Embrace enigma and ambiguity.

A story of another is akin to photograph; It is a secret about a secret. The more it tells you, the less you know about you.

Paint your masterpiece. This sites gives you the paint, canvas, and the brushes. You have to supply the creative effort

Where has your sense of adventure gone? Are we doing this flick or are we wasting what time we have left? It seems to have died under mysterious circumstances. My sense of self-preservation found my body and soul, but my mind assures me it has an airtight alibi. It is time for this ship to sail gentlemen. Salvage is a dying man’s game. We have time yet to burn from my calculations as a metronome. We are students of light and time, givers and takers, performers and dreamers. Our clocks are what our life is made from. In youth, their are tides we swim against, with time we surf those waves to new shores to make the life we get. Ticking clocks never stand still so why do you think you can now in the twilight of life? Or we part of the cure or are we the fabric of the disease?

Start with one tab. If one tab does not make difference keep increasing (up to 10 tabs/meal) with each meal.

We are not what we eat, we are what we assimilate.
Many people are low in gastric acid.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-180937>

A couple of things to update.

I got the glo lamps, over did it last Saturday and cooked my right side. It felt so warm and good, and I didn't think anything of it, but the next day I was very pink. The distance was 3', my upper arm was really sore. Yesterday I woke up with a lot of peeling skin. So will take it easier. LOL

I am not understanding CT yet, but have implemented what I can. I notice if I take a shower before I go to bed and finish up with as cold as I can stand it, then even colder on my legs/feet, when I go to sleep, my feet are not cold. And if I skip, then the feet get cold.

I also got some large gel packs and have kept 1 on my belly for most of the afternoon. No discomfort, skin is icy cold to the touch...would have figured that I would be cold, but haven't been.

Have also added more butter to each meal. Love that Kerrygold was on sale this week!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-02T02:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-180942>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=180503)
>
> Thank you @[Brother John](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5776/)
> \-----------------

> One case:
> Starting with low pregnenolone, progesterone and cortisol
> supplementing with pregnenolone until cortisol was satisfactory, resulted with
> still lowish pregnenolone but possibly ok
> but progesterone became very high, 3-4x over optimal level which resulted in suppression of DHT (and affected my erections)
>
> Help please.
>
> \-----------------------

JanSz you were given the answers long ago. You have to begin to put together what you've learned. This is how you create your masterpiece on this site using the paint, canvas, and brushes. All hormones link to enzymes. How do enzymes work? Proton tunneling. Read Life at the Edge and my blogs in the OSF and Tensegrity series. What about COLD? COLD improves proton tunneling by bring protons closer together lowering the energy barrier to tunnel. Many still think cold is just hormetic because of Robb Wolf's uninformed opinion on biophysics? That is a paleo precept he set forth for his followers. That is not what I told you EVER. Reject nonsense and seek natural truths. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/15276821/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/m/pubmed/15276821/)
Enzymes are proteins that use proton tunneling to serve as biological catalysts, speeding up the rate of metabolic reactions in cells. Most biochemistry books do not even talk about this very fundamental quantum mechanism in all cells. Conventional medicine and cell physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence in cells despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Each cell orchestrates 100,000 biochemical reactions per second and the quantum mechanism in proton tunneling is critical in this. Effects of temperature on enzyme function; this is where CT comes into the story--small changes in temperature, especially at surfaces can radically influence enzyme function in deeper tissue levels by altering the geometry of the enzyme. This leads to a size and shape change that we spoke in detail in the OSF series, especially OSF 3-5. Once the geometry of the enzyme changes, the dimensions of the active site in an enzyme changes and no longer matches the dimensions of the substrate chemical. With cooling proteins condense. When the temperature gets warmer at a surface and changes it opens or uncondenses the protein. At very high temperatures, the bonds of the protein are irretreivably broken and completely disrupted, leading to irreversible denaturation of the enzyme.

About cooking your right side, peeling skin.
The good news is that now we know that those Solar Glow 160 W work.
The bad news is that you have to condition your skin first, before you can sit under those lamps indefinitely.
Same as with original sun.

Post 426, bottom video:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-22#post-180840](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-22#post-180840)
somewhere there is described method on how to condition skin to sun,
for skin that have not seen sun for very long time.
They allotted a whole month of daily incremental exposures to get there.
Use those instructions and make some reasonable modifications.

The other piece of information.
Since I started writing on low-cortisol-levels thread, about Exoterra lights, I am buying them and using them.
Most significantly, I have now two Solar Glo 160W and UVB200, in one cluster shining on me from a distance of 36".
I am not using UVB150, from distance of 36" it likely does not do much. I may even dump the UVB200.
I was wondering if they do anything except raising my electric bill, because I do not feel anything.
Now I know that they are working.
I am white Caucasian, originally from Poland. Heavy suntan. Without sun my skin is likely as white as yours.
I am assuming that (after conditioning) you could be be sitting under those lights indefinitely.

It would be a good idea to figure out how to have those lamps on both sides.
We may look funny on end of winter having suntan on right side of face and white skin on left.
The Phantom Of The Opera comes to mind.

IIRC Kerrygold Butter and Dubliner cheese are has been story (per @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) )

### Narrew — 2015-11-22T22:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warren-found-hope.17020/post-182507>

Wanted to do a quick update...

After I cooked, I took it easy on the Solar Glo 160W lights. I limited my time to 30 minutes first thing in the morning for a couple of weeks. This weekend I used them a bit longer. Yesterday I did about 1 hour in the morning, today I did an hour first thing in the morning, and just finishing up an additional half hour session. So, I am increasing my exposure incrementally. Will see how my skin looks tomorrow. The last time I used them longer, it wasn't till the next day I noticed that I had a sun burn...

My sleep has been getting better. While I have just touched the tip of Jack's iceberg, getting better sleep is helping in so many areas!

Solar Glo 160W lights more better, when they are about more than 3' away.

Overall more lamps in the morning hours.

I think it may not be bad idea to sleep under sunlight, another words, do not fight sleep when under sunlight.

Red LED and IR-A lamps good over sore joints or muscles.
Exposure time (to red) should be limited, not sure how much, but excessive is hurtful.

# Thread: Sister Kenney - a remarkable story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-10-19T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179821>

Does anyone know this story of a remarkable woman who was ostracised In Australia - but found huge success in the U.S.?
She was a nurse with a very innovative treatment for Polio that was not accepted by the orthopaedic Docs in Aus. She was shunned and very abused by the medical community.

There is utube stuff that I couldn't paste here for some reason....

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-19T19:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179879>

Hadn't heard of her.

According to Wiki:

> **Elizabeth Kenny** (20 September 1880 – 30 November 1952) was an unaccredited [Australian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australia)[nurse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nurse) who promoted a controversial new approach to the treatment of [poliomyelitis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poliomyelitis) . Her findings ran counter to conventional medical wisdom; they demonstrated the need to exercise muscles affected by polio instead of immobilizing them. Kenny's principles of muscle rehabilitation became the foundation of [physical therapy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physical_therapy), or physiotherapy.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Kenny#cite_note-1)

May have been "unaccredited" but that doesn't sound so far out. I think medics always used to think that bedrest was better than exercise for pretty much any condition … but is it always?

What I can see when searching under "video"

[http://www.wnyc.org/story/interview-with-patients/](http://www.wnyc.org/story/interview-with-patients/)

Will have a listen.

### caroline — 2015-10-20T01:39:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179908>

Thanks Mike.....
A woman told her story Sunday morning on a radio show we listen to .....

Her Dad had lived in the U.S. and then came to New Zealand and met and married her Mother. When she was 11 she got polio and the Doc's wanted to treat her in the conventional
way - complete hospital bed rest and the legs completely immobilised in irons. Her Dad said no way and she was treated by massaging for 5 hours a day \[ 3people\].

She is now 80+ and has never had any signs of polio.

Nurse Kenny was treated terribly here by the medical profession and so went to the U.S. I think maybe she has a connection to Roosevelt?
I am looking for her biography and am having trouble finding it unless I want to pay $165.00

### caroline — 2015-10-20T01:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179909>

Mike - if you have a chance - and are so inclined ....listen to a program here Sunday mornings called Australia all over with Ian McNamara. People call in from all over the world.

Every Sunday morning I get the most incredible geography lessons and a huge lesson in caring, committed, strong people doing the most wonderful things for others and for nature and the environment.

Every Sunday morning I am blown away by the goodness and mental strength and kindness in people and I want to get in the car and see all this for myself!

### Jude — 2015-10-20T02:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179910>

iirc Sr Kenny was active in southern Queensland. Think there is a street dedicated to her name in ? Clifton?. Heard lots of stories from oldies who loved and respected her therapies. Had no idea she could be still living.

### caroline — 2015-10-20T06:29:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179938>

Jude she has passed away. There is a museum etc.

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-20T09:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-179944>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=179909)
>
> Mike - if you have a chance - and are so inclined ....listen to a program here Sunday mornings called Australia all over with Ian McNamara. People call in from all over the world.
>
> Every Sunday morning I get the most incredible geography lessons and a huge lesson in caring, committed, strong people doing the most wonderful things for others and for nature and the environment.
>
> Every Sunday morning I am blown away by the goodness and mental strength and kindness in people and I want to get in the car and see all this for myself!

That's so nice.

I tried googling under "images" and found pictures of her shaking hands with him:

["sister kenny" roosevelt](https://www.google.co.uk/search?q=%22sister+kenny%22+roosevelt&client=safari&rls=en&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0CAkQ_AUoA2oVChMImf_TsrXQyAIVAoMaCh3VrQf1&biw=1270&bih=600)

This also turned up:

A Nurse Gains Fame in the Days of Polio:

(By Barron H. Lerner, a professor of medicine and population health at New York University School of Medicine)

[http://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2013/12/26/a-nurse-gains-fame-in-the-days-of-polio/?\_r=0](http://well.blogs.nytimes.com/2013/12/26/a-nurse-gains-fame-in-the-days-of-polio/?_r=0)

Among other things it mentions her as a pioneer of physical therapy in general:

> Her emphasis on early mobilization has come to be a mainstay of not only polio treatment but of [physical therapy](http://topics.nytimes.com/top/news/health/diseasesconditionsandhealthtopics/physicaltherapy/index.html?inline=nyt-classifier) more broadly.

I was quite interested in this:

> Plus, Ms. Kenny believed that patients needed to play an active role in their recovery, learning the names of affected muscles and how they worked.

I did a lot of Alexander Technique at one time and while a lot of that is hands-on and experiential there's also somewhat of an emphasis of teaching people the mechanics of their own bodies. AT teachers seem to believe that some people carry out certain movements badly, because they have a false notion of how particular joints work and of the mechanics of movements. Maybe there's a kind of connection there. She also seems to be seeing a link between proper _understanding_ and proper functioning. (Not that theoretical knowledge is the point here: it's practical knowledge that counts at the end of the day: like riding a bike.)

Here's the book that Barron H. Lerner was wiring about:

[https://global.oup.com/academic/pro...613C5B1AC504DF0C9115C4A7ED229D?cc=us&lang=en&](https://global.oup.com/academic/product/polio-wars-9780195380590;jsessionid=63613C5B1AC504DF0C9115C4A7ED229D?cc=us&lang=en&)

As cheap as $9 secondhand on Amazon:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0195380592/](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0195380592/)

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-21T09:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180058>

By the way, on polio - notice the academic writing in the Times there says:

> The successful development of a [polio vaccine](http://health.nytimes.com/health/guides/specialtopic/polio-immunization-vaccine/overview.html?inline=nyt-classifier) in the 1950s made these debates much less pressing.

Hmmm … that's the story, but I wonder. Maybe @[patgrif@hotmail.com](mailto:patgrif@hotmail.com) has some comments on that.

There's recent comment on this from Sally Fallon:

> The most persuasive arguments for vaccinating children have to do with polio. Didn’t the polio vaccine wipe out this truly tragic epidemic of viral disease? **Seems like an open-and-shut case for vaccination**. However, the campaign to eradicate polio is littered with accidents, contradiction, confusion and fraud. Health officials introduced the live attenuated Salk vaccine in the U.S. in 1955. The Sabin oral polio vaccine followed in 1961. At that time, the diagnostic criteria for polio were changed. Before introduction of the vaccine, patients diagnosed with paralytic polio had to exhibit symptoms of paralysis for twenty-four hours; after the vaccines they had to exhibit symptoms of paralysis for sixty days. In addition, before the polio vaccine, coxsackie virus and aseptic meningitis were lumped in together with polio with no lab confirmation, but after the introduction of the vaccine, coxsackie virus and aseptic meningitis were separated out and lab confirmation was required. So naturally the rates of polio seemed to come down. But worse ...
>
> \[My emphasis\]

I'll let anyone who wants to see where the "but worse …" leads look up the original article:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/caustic-commentary-summer-2015/](http://www.westonaprice.org/health-topics/caustic-commentary-summer-2015/)

But I shall add that she concludes:

> Polio has disappeared, case closed. Or has it? In 2014, neurologists began reporting cases of paralysis and death in children fully vaccinated against polio. So of course they don’t call it polio. “Acute flaccid paralysis” has occurred in dozens on American children and tens of thousands of children in heavily vaccinated India.

Well, I don't know. I'm not a doctor. But this does look like smoke and mirrors. And I'd certainly not like to say _for sure_ that Sally's chasing a will-'o-the-wisp. Further, I suspect anyone who did say that would have economic - or ideological - motives for their certainty.

If you keep changing your definitions and criteria, you certainly can make things seem however you want them to - at least in the short term.

“Acute flaccid paralysis” … well, well.

### caroline — 2015-10-21T11:28:43+0300

I have spent way to much time looking for this book - will get back to it tomorrow.

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-21T12:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180063>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=180061)
>
> That quote seems quite appropriate to these times we live in Mike.

You know I feel quite angry about these things.

I don't think most of us have begun to realise to what an extent commercial entities have manipulated our view of so many things in their own interests and regardless of the harm they do to people. My uncle was a world-famous New Left academic at one of our two oldest universities over here - I'll say no more or people will be able to work out who he was - anyway, he bought hook, line and sinker into the low-fat garbage. I myself was always cautious of the Left - I think they've done a hell of a lot of damage around the world (The Great Leap Forward in China alone!) - but ain't it morbidly funny that a New Left academic, a kind of quasi-Marxist, should believe what Big Food wanted him to and never have an inkling. It killed him. Early heart attack.

Tim Noakes:

"Ten companies produce 80% of processed foods in the world, and they're creating billions and billions of dollars in profits by poisoning people. I'd tax them out of existence."

It's not just Big Food, though. Big Pharma is up to the same tricks.

### caroline — 2015-10-22T01:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sister-kenney-a-remarkable-story.17021/post-180107>

I always think that big companies influence us in such subtle ways that we are so very unaware.

I was shocked to learn that department stores turn up the heat so that you get too warm when shopping and you just buy random things as well as what you are looking for quickly so you can get out!

I don't know how we can ever be smart enuf and on top of it enuf to figure out all the subtleties of what all these high powered companies and brain power do to twist us into submission.

I understand we must be a sceptic - to the max I guess ........but it seems to me that some people who are sceptics - it overflows into every area of their life and is kind of a lousy way to live .... in my opinion anyway.

That is part of the reason I love Macca's radio program on Sunday mornings. Macca regularly does his program remote from flooded and ravaged areas and people drive for hundreds of miles to show up at 5:00am to help and donate in any way they can. There is a huge heartbeat out there in the heartland - people that don't have much and helping others.
Macca lives and loves and promotes wildlife and nature and always at least trying to do the right thing. He also loves and promotes local musicians/talent. He has a small band and he sings and they do benefit concerts and often sing about the outback and all the old timers and their songs.....

We saw him here in town a few months ago and Tony bought one of his albums ....in vinyl of course!

# Thread: At the Gulf and need help please!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/at-the-gulf-and-need-help-please.17028/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lookingforanswers — 2015-10-21T00:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/at-the-gulf-and-need-help-please.17028/post-180017>

I've been at the Gulf for the last several weeks. This is my last week here and I just had to move houses due to availability issues. My sleep took a major crap the first night in this house and I just discovered ithas a smart meter. I"m assuming that's the problem! I have my trifield and gauss meter with me and put foil over the tiled shower wall inside the house opposing the smart meter. It did lower the reading after doing that but I'm still getting headaches, nausea and histamine like reactions where my body is burning up so much I feel like it's 100 degrees in here!

I don't have a body voltage meter to check that way. Any other suggestions on what I can do? Thanks for any help.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T03:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/at-the-gulf-and-need-help-please.17028/post-180038>

paint can over the meter.....or aluminum foil. You dont live there you just need protection for a short time

### lookingforanswers — 2015-10-21T15:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/at-the-gulf-and-need-help-please.17028/post-180068>

Thank you Dr. Kruse. We did put several layers of foil on the inside wall adjacent to the meter, but I am just getting worse and worse. My sleep is awful. I'm getting heart palpitations and headaches. We're staying out of the house and on the beach in the ocean or in the forest as much as possible, but I am in bad shape and scared. I have a very long travel day home on Saturday - 4 hour drive to the airport 3 hour flight and then 3 hour drive home. I'm trying to build up so I can sustain the trip.

We even tried sleeping outside, but my body burned up even worse then. Every night I feel like a furnace that I can't turn down!

### lookingforanswers — 2015-10-21T15:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/at-the-gulf-and-need-help-please.17028/post-180069>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - I just want to clarify - you said paint can or foil over the meter - we put it on the inside wall in the house. Are you saying we should put it on the outside of the meter itself? Thank you!

The house has a metal roof (it seems like all houses down here do!). I can't imagine that can be a good thing.

# Thread: Do you need AM UVA sunlight in winter if you are coupled to cold and not light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 70
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curves — 2015-10-21T01:24:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180018>

I get that in winter, when you become cold adapted, your SCN becomes entrained to temperature and not light but I'm wondering about our need for am UVA.

I'm thinking that it's a YES -- you need AM sunlight no matter what time of year for water chemistry to charge your battery etc but just confirming.

Am I right?

**An excerpt from CT6: The Ancient Pathway
**
**Step 3.** The suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) is the circadian pacemaker that monitors this dance between darkness and light and the seasonal cold and hot temperatures in our environment. Cold temperatures reverses all the normal biology that is used when the SCN is entrained to light. This metabolic trap door is huge for mammalian biochemistry**. This is the only way to naturally way to enter this brain pathway now that we know of.** When temperature becomes the dominant environmental trigger and not light cycles, the leptin receptor induces endothelial nitric oxide synthetase (eNOS) formation.

Light penetration in the skin
(attenuation down to 1% occurs for light wavelengths of 250-280 nm at around 40 μm depth; for 300 nm at 100 μm; for 360 nm at 190 μm; for 400 nm at 250 μm; for 700 nm at 400 μm; for 1.2 μm at 800 μm; for 2 μm at 400 μm; for 2.5 μm at 1μ; and for 400 μm at 30 μm)

When you see how UVC only penetrates 55 microns, and UVC is universally toxic to all life it should make you realize how destruction of the surfaces of things trumps the biochemistry of things way below it. This picture says a million words no paleo biochemist could understand with their conventional wisdom ideas. I told you cancer is an epi-oncogenic phenomena in the Ubiquitination series. This also explains why 90% of cancers originate in the epithelial layer of our body and organs. It also shows you why most breast and prostate cancers are associated with low vitamin D levels. The problem is not at the nucleus in the genome. It is at the surface where the environment meets the cell. Light controls carbon flows.

Also note how UVC and IRC both only affect the top layers and yet both are deadly...........anyone connecting dots yet?

UVC is germicidal, no? Also kills skin cells ridiculously quick right? Yep...........but here is the thing.........paleo biochemists tell people it is cellular biochemistry that destroys you........yet UVC light only bothers the first 55 microns.........that means they are missing something quite big.................and they are. What is it?

It is called light effect at surfaces: 1921 Noble Prize : the photoelectric effect by Einstein. This means light alone can change the atomic lattice of anything it hits. And if what it hits has a ton of fluorophores the effect is greater. Think the RPE, skin, and NAD+ as classic examples. The effect is always mediated when light hits electrons and not the nucleus.

So do we glow when light hits us causing us to lose higher energy electrons? We glow and release nitric oxide from our surface cells to change the surface of the skin hit by light. Nitric oxide also happens to have an free electrons with its own spin because it is a free radical. When light hits it, the electron is liberated by scatter to form a triplet state.
Full spectrum sunlight is fully capable of alters charge of NO by making that electron a higher energy electron filled with UV light to change other proteins in the skin that like UV light excited electrons. What might those proteins be?

The thing is there is no way to satisfy the octet rule for both atoms in NO. The structure is a double bond with three non-bonding electrons on N and four on O but really one of the electrons on N is not localized to N but the entire molecule.
Formal charge = electrons in ground state - (non-bonding electrons + 0.5 \* bonding electrons)
Formal charge for N = 5 - ( 3 + 4 \* 0.5) = 0 (there are two bonds so four electrons are involved in bonding)
Formal charge for O = 6 - (4+ 4 \* 0.5) = 0
Sunlight alters that balance of charge.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T02:53:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180030>

Exposing skin to sunlight may help to reduce blood pressure and thus cut the risk of heart attack and stroke, a study published in the Journal of Investigative Dermatology suggests

Research carried out at the Universities of Southampton and Edinburgh shows that sunlight alters levels of the small quantum messenger molecule, nitric oxide (NO) in the skin and blood, reducing blood pressure.

Martin Feelisch, Professor of Experimental Medicine and Integrative Biology at the University of Southampton, comments: "NO along with its breakdown products, known to be abundant in skin, is involved in the regulation of blood pressure. When exposed to sunlight, small amounts of NO are transferred from the skin to the circulation, lowering blood vessel tone; as blood pressure drops, so does the risk of heart attack and stroke."

Dr Richard Weller of the University of Edinburgh, suggest that minimizing exposure may be disadvantageous by increasing the risk of prevalent conditions related to cardiovascular disease and likely will extend to the CNS because it gets 20% of the hearts output.

Cardiovascular disease, often associated with high blood pressure, accounts for 30 per cent of deaths globally each year. Blood pressure and cardiovascular disease are known to vary according to season and latitude, with higher levels observed in winter and in countries further from the equator, where ultraviolet radiation from the sun is lower.

During the study, the skin of 24 healthy individuals was exposed to ultraviolet (UVA alone was able to show this effect) and light from tanning lamps for two sessions of 20 minutes each. In one session, the volunteers were exposed to both the UVA rays and the heat of the lamps. In another, the UV rays were blocked so that only the heat of the lamps affected the skin.

The results suggest that UVA exposure specifically dilates blood vessels, significantly lowers blood pressure, and alters NO metabolite levels in the circulation, without changing vitamin D levels. RNS triplet state free radical metabolites have powerful far reaching effects in humans beyond their skin surfaces. Further experiments indicate that pre-formed stores of NO in the upper skin layers are involved in mediating these effects. The data are consistent with the seasonal variation of blood pressure and cardiovascular risk at temperate latitudes.

Professor Feelisch adds: "These results are significant to the ongoing debate about potential health benefits of sunlight and the role of Vitamin D in this process. It may be an opportune time to reassess the risks and benefits of sunlight for human health and to take a fresh look at current public health advice. Avoiding excess sunlight exposure is critical to prevent skin cancer, but not being exposed to it at all, out of fear or as a result of a certain lifestyle, could increase the risk of cardiovascular disease. Perhaps with the exception of bone health, the effects of oral vitamin D supplementation have been disappointing.

"We believe that NO from the skin is an important, so far overlooked contributor to cardiovascular health. In future studies we intend to test whether the effects hold true in a more chronic setting and identify new nutritional strategies targeted at maximizing the skin's ability to store NO and deliver it to the circulation more efficiently."

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T02:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180033>

Nitric oxide (nitrogen oxide,nitrogen monoxide) is a molecular, chemical compound with chemical formula of NO that is a colorless gas under standard conditions. Nitric oxide is a free radical—i.e., its bonding structure includes an unpaired electron with a specific electronic spin state. It is a practically important intermediate in the chemical industry. In addition, some is unavoidably produced during combustion of fossil fuels in power plants and automobile engines, with excess being created when there is present more air, or higher temperatures, than needed for efficient and complete combustion of the fuel. In skin thermal temps are raised by UV/IR light and oxygen is increased by UV light striking the skin alone. Not only does skin make it with powerful sun but so does the atmosphere in quantum fashion. It is also produced naturally by the extremely high air temperatures produced along the path of lightning in thunderstorms.

It was found that NO acts through the stimulation of the soluble guanylate cyclase, which is a heterodimeric enzyme with subsequent formation of cyclic-GMP. Cyclic-GMP activates protein kinase G, which causes reuptake of Ca2+ and lowers the intra cellular calcium by the opening of calcium-activated potassium channels. This is protective in high nnEMF environments. The fall in concentration of Ca2+ ensures that the myosin light-chain kinase (MLCK) can no longer phosphorylate the myosin molecule, thereby stopping the crossbridge cycle and leading to relaxation of the smooth muscle cell allowing more RBC s loaded with porphyrin to continue absorbing UV light.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T03:00:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180035>

Nitric oxide, is also known as the '[endothelium-derived relaxing factor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endothelium-derived_relaxing_factor)', or 'EDRF', is biosynthesized endogenously from [L-arginine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L-arginine), [oxygen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxygen), and [NADPH](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NADPH) by various [nitric oxide synthase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitric_oxide_synthase) (NOS) [enzymes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enzyme). Reduction of inorganic nitrate may also serve to make nitric oxide. The [endothelium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endothelium) of [blood vessels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood_vessel) uses nitric oxide to signal the surrounding [smooth muscle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smooth_muscle) to relax, thus resulting in [vasodilation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vasodilation) and increasing blood flow so we can collect a lot of UV light. How does Hemoglobin fit in to this blood UV story?

Two important biological reaction mechanisms of nitric oxide are S-[nitrosation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrosation) of thiols, and nitrosylation of transition metal ions. S-nitrosation involves the (reversible) conversion of [thiol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thiol) groups, including [cysteine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cysteine) residues in proteins, to form S-nitrosothiols (RSNOs). S-[Nitrosation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrosation) is a mechanism for dynamic, post-translational regulation of most or all major classes of protein in all living things. This is why the ubiquitination series has so many blogs. Light controls protein turnover by this mechanism. The second mechanism, nitrosylation, involves the binding of NO to a transition metal ion like iron or copper. These are found in the mouth of every cytochrome protein. Did you also know that cytochrome C is also a heme protein? In this nitroslyation function, NO is referred to as a nitrosyl ligand. Typical cases involve the nitrosylation of heme proteins like cytochromes, thereby disabling the normal enzymatic activity of the enzyme when it becomes necessary in cold environments. Nitrosylated ferrous iron is particularly stable, as the binding of the nitrosyl ligand to ferrous iron (Fe(II)) is very strong. **Hemoglobin** is a prominent example of a heme protein that may be modified by NO by both pathways: NO may attach directly to the heme in the nitrosylation reaction, and independently form S-nitrosothiols by S-nitrosation of the thiol moieties. Hemoglobin absorbs light at 280 nm and 3 other IR frequencies. Maybe now you can see why they link to NO.

I contemplated this yesterday - and posted this on the FB Quantum Group.... it fits here....

Thoughts on light as I drove to the office today and inspired by trees. As UV fluctuates as the N.Hemisphere transitions from fall to winter, the decrease in overall UV exposure, as well as exposure of all light diminishes, directly effects the surface chemistry of leaves of deciduous trees resulting in the brilliant explosion of color. Trees then begins their migration toward dormancy until the UV reaches the minimum to facilitate growth of new leaves. So In Animals drop of UV levels signal the last dash before hibernation or migration. So if you're a human you have two choices. Embrace the cold as your signaling for surface chemistry or follow the sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T15:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180139>

Do you understand that all our surfaces are not what they seem in a histology book? They are made to be adaptable. They are the very definition a topologic insulators. What is a TI? My members got a webinar on them over a year ago. Most materials are either insulators or conductors, and semiconductors straddle the divide by becoming conducting or insulating as their charge-carrier density is tuned. Recently, a new class of materials was discovered that combines these properties in a new way: called topological insulators, they have bulks that are insulating and surfaces that are conducting. Moreover, the spin of their surface electrons is locked in a direction that is perpendicular to the direction of motion (a property known as helicity): this reduces the probability of the electrons being backscattered by surface defects and phonons, thereby lowering the surface resistivity. What sets in the direction of motion for human skin? The energy and momentum of the UV/IR sunlight. This guarantees triplet state spins........and not singlet state spins.

Blue light however, prescribes the opposite effect from our skin. It makes huge amounts of ROS and singlet state superoxide that is not buffered by UV and IR light.

Exposure of biological chromophores (skin) to ultraviolet radiation can lead to photochemical damage. However, the role of full spectrum visible light, particularly in the blue region of the spectrum, has been largely ignored. To test the hypothesis that blue light is toxic to non-pigmented epithelial cells, confluent cultures of human primary retinal epithelial cells were exposed to visible light (390–550 nm at 2.8 milliwatts/cm2) for up to 6 h. This light is firmly all in the blue range. What did the experiment show?

A small loss of mitochondrial respiratory activity was observed at 6 h compared with dark-maintained cells, and this loss became greater with increasing time. To investigate the mechanism of cell loss, the damage to mitochondrial and nuclear genes was assessed using the quantitative PCR. Light exposure significantly damaged mitochondrial DNA at 3 hours (0.7 lesion/10 kb DNA) compared with dark-maintained controls. However, by 6 h of light exposure, the number of lesions was decreased in the surviving cells, indicating DNA repair. Isolated mitochondria exposed to light generated singlet oxygen, superoxide anion, and the hydroxyl radical. Antioxidants confirmed the superoxide anion to be the primary species responsible for the mitochondrial DNA lesions. The effect of lipofuscin, a photoinducible intracellular generator of reactive oxygen intermediates, was investigated for comparison. Exposure of lipofuscin-containing cells to visible light caused an increase in both mitochondrial and nuclear DNA lesions compared with non-pigmented cells. The researchers concluded that blue visible light can cause cell dysfunction through the action of reactive oxygen species on DNA and that this may contribute to cellular aging, age-related pathologies, and tumorigenesis.

Moral of the story: UV and IR buffer the singlet state of oxygen made by blue light in the visible spectrum.

It begs the question: What happens to humans who live within blue light chronically?

Google Kevin Ogar.

Your answer is there.

I don't think the cloud cover here is as dense as yours in Seattle but I did have a noticeable shift in my awareness on the days that we had sunshine versus the days it rained last week. Made me a little nervous quite honestly going into winter.

I made a huge boo boo this summer. We bought a new home with some land, but the beautiful trees were all over run with blackberries and the tress were dying.

I know living in Seattle you know the type of blackberries I mean. I'm not talking the thin vine variety; I'm talking thick, thorny Himalayan blackberries.. It took me over 200 hours to cut them out of the trees. Because I was getting cut up, slashed in the face, and getting thorns stuck in my hands, I ended up deciding to wear protective clothing -- long sleeves, long pants, high work boots, two pairs of work gloves and sometimes protective eye wear.

Although we saved the trees, my Vitamin D tanked. So, now, going into the fall, I don't feel as prepared as I have in the last few winters.

Maybe a few trips to LA to visit family might help

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-23T14:39:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180248>

Topological insulators have turned out to be more common than expected in nature, and include well known thermoelectric materials like Bi2Se3 and Bi2Te3. Interesting in their own right, the surface states of topological insulators are also good platforms for studying fundamental physical phenomena such as the quantum anomalous Hall effect and Majorana fermions. Both of these things are huge for surface chemistry.

The **Hall effect** is the production of a [voltage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voltage) difference (the **Hall voltage**) across an [electrical conductor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electrical_conductor), transverse to an [electric current](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electric_current) in the conductor and a [magnetic field](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magnetic_field) perpendicular to the current. It was discovered by [Edwin Hall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edwin_Hall) in 1879.

A **Majorana fermion**, also referred to as a **Majorana particle**, is a [fermion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fermion) that is its own [antiparticle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antiparticle). They were hypothesized by [Ettore Majorana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ettore_Majorana) in 1937. The term is sometimes used in opposition to a [Dirac fermion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dirac_fermion), which describes fermions that are not their own antiparticles.

All of the [Standard Model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_Model) fermions except the [neutrino](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neutrino) behave as Dirac fermions at low energy (after [electroweak symmetry breaking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electroweak_symmetry_breaking)), but the (massive therefore they draw UV light by gravitational lensing) nature of the neutrino is not settled and it may be either Dirac or Majorana. In [condensed matter physics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Condensed_matter_physics), Majorana fermions exist as [quasiparticle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quasiparticle) [excitations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Excited_state) in [superconductors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superconductors) and can be used to form Majorana [bound states](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bound_state) governed by [non-abelian statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anyon#Non-abelian_anyons). Many of you on the cruise heard me and Yew talk about monopoles and neutrinos. This Majorana particle might be the key to TI's and life's creation at surfaces.

In [condensed matter physics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Condensed_matter_physics), Majorana fermions exist as [quasiparticle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quasiparticle) [excitations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Excited_state) in [superconductors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superconductors) and can be used to form Majorana [bound states](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bound_state) governed by [non-abelian statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anyon#Non-abelian_anyons). ### Scompy — 2015-10-23T21:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180299>

Reminds me to brush on my quantum physics for a better understanding of quantum biology.

"Neutrinos have [mass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mass), but tiny even compared to other subatomic particles. Neutrinos can be created in several ways, including in certain types of [radioactive decay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radioactive_decay), in [nuclear reactions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_reaction) such as those that take place in the [Sun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun), in [nuclear reactors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_reactor), when [cosmic rays](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cosmic_ray) hit atoms and in [supernovae](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supernova). The majority of neutrinos in the vicinity of the Earth are from nuclear reactions in the Sun. In fact, about 65 billion (6.5×1010) [solar neutrinos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_neutrino) per second pass through every square centimeter perpendicular to the direction of the Sun in the region of the Earth.[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neutrino#cite_note-J._Bahcall_et_al._2005_L85.E2.80.93L88-6) "

### dantothep — 2015-10-24T18:57:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180340>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=180077)
>
> I contemplated this yesterday - and posted this on the FB Quantum Group.... it fits here....
>
> Thoughts on light as I drove to the office today and inspired by trees. As UV fluctuates as the N.Hemisphere transitions from fall to winter, the decrease in overall UV exposure, as well as exposure of all light diminishes, directly effects the surface chemistry of leaves of deciduous trees resulting in the brilliant explosion of color. Trees then begins their migration toward dormancy until the UV reaches the minimum to facilitate growth of new leaves. So In Animals drop of UV levels signal the last dash before hibernation or migration. So if you're a human you have two choices. Embrace the cold as your signaling for surface chemistry or follow the sun.

I too had The same thought about two weeks ago, when only the leaves on the tree exposed to the sun turned in colour.

It could explain why humanity may go a little haywire and werewolfish because influence upon the circadian signaling and melatonin. ### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-24T20:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-need-am-uva-sunlight-in-winter-if-you-are-coupled-to-cold-and-not-light.17029/post-180358>

_"The results suggest that UVA exposure specifically dilates blood vessels, significantly lowers blood pressure, and alters NO metabolite levels in the circulation, without changing vitamin D levels. RNS triplet state free radical metabolites have powerful far reaching effects in humans beyond their skin surfaces. Further experiments indicate that pre-formed stores of NO in the upper skin layers are involved in mediating these effects. The data are consistent with the seasonal variation of blood pressure and cardiovascular risk at temperate latitudes."_

I may have missed something along the way but reading the above paragraph made me think that if my reptile bulb is only UVB with very little if any UVA the dilation of the blood vessels isn't taking place thus not lowering the BP. My BP is high and I'm hoping this light will help.

I just made this video what I'm doing with cloud cover as a semi-helpful biohack. Assuming I'm getting UVA, but no UVB, with added IR.

Even if it snows, I'm looking forward to it because of back-reflection of light with CT at the same time. UV light on the skin absorbs better in the cold, so doing a weak-ass indoor UV with heating the skin could be a hack-fail. I have some indoor full-spectrum bulbs and an IR machine, but now I rarely use them 'cause I get outside so much now. Maybe if I get sick, it might be a hack. I planned many vacations to Arizona in the coming months. It's really about location. I'm in a crappy location in Seattle. When I moved here in 2010, it was basically 7-straight months of thick cloud cover. I'm curious to find data on sickness rates or the like. I'm thinking it could be interesting to see if someone can conduct a test of # of sunny days vs. sickness-related hospitalizations over a several-year span to see what the numbers reveal.

when you are tightly coupled, with a high membrane potential, you need very little food to generate a lot of energy, but you gain weight easily on high carb western diet; and when you are loosely coupled, you are less efficient in pumping protons out to convert into ATP. You need more calories to generate more heat. These are people who can eat all they want and never gain any weight.

I always associated tightly coupled mitochondrial haplotypes as having the ability to eat a lot without gaining weight (since the focus is the need to escape from predators like lions), and the reverse is true for the loosely coupled haplotypes, as they evolved the ability to store fat for heat generation in colder climates.

I must be missing some critical information somewhere.

# Thread: Sean's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-journal.17030/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean Shick — 2015-10-21T03:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-journal.17030/post-180040>

Howdy!

I found Dr. Kruse via a Youtube video about two years ago; was obviously sucked in right away. The guy is magnetic. My wife an I did not immediately begin any of the protocols since we were already in the middle of another "diet/lifestyle" experiment. I did however begin the long process of trying to digest more of the blog, more of the Doc's concepts, and plan for the inevitable change. When first exposed to Dr. Jack, my wife and I had been living completely Vegan for about 8 months. I had ballooned up to 275 lbs. from about 180 over a several year period(met wife at 180, its her fault); something drastic had to be done. We came across the McDougall plan/philosophy and after much debate decided to give it a try. To be honest, we felt pretty good, I dropped about 45 lbs fairly quickly and then sorta plateaued. Our first child was probably about 3-4 months old when we began the Vegan thing. We cheated, rarely, as you probably could have guessed it usually involved something fatty of some kind. After learning more from Dr. Kruse, I quickly became worried about getting enough fat in to our baby. So, a 180 degree turn was made and a more "paleo" diet was followed. Fast forward to about a month ago. Mom is healthy, Baby is amazing(2.5 now and has never been sick one day of her life), Dad is back to about 255 give or take, and super unhappy about it.

Decided to get serious about a month ago. Im a baby step kinda guy sometimes, so we aren't where I wanna be yet, but here's a summary of what we have started so far.

\- began substituting seafood for every protein possible, best we can source salmon, tuna, shrimp, lobster, sardines, fish sticks(don't laugh!!!)....no oysters yet

\- I had been IFing for ever, usually skipping breakfast, so I have began trying to get a chunk of salmon or something down early early when I can, when I don't I just fast.

\- Water

\- Blue blocking glasses in the evening

\- Im a farmer, so lucky for me, Im up with the Sun all year long, so I get my morning sun on my eyeball.

\- No CT yet

\- Minimal excersise outside of normal daily activities

\- Try to get to bed by 10ish or better

\- Still eat some quality cheese, still eat some yams/sweet potatoes, still eat some rice, working on it

\- Coconut oil and kerry gold butter are only added fats

\- No wifi in the house after 10 at night, we do not cut the breaker in our bedroom, phones are on other side of bedroom, maybe 20 ft. away.

Thats about it.

\*What I have noticed\*

\- Intense sleep and dreaming. Amazing thing about this observation is that this kicked in like night one, after eating a salmon fish salad sandwitch kinda thing for lunch and a fat salmon steak for dinner. This has continued and gotten even better

\- Down to about 245, give or take, without any calorie counting of any kind at all. I sorta feel like this would drop fast at this point with a little portion curbing but Im holding off for now.

\- This is a weird one, Just as Dr. Kruse said, cravings for food have practically vanished. I feel like Im eating out of habit and because its like time to eat or something, rather then actually "needing" to eat. This is a strange one.

\- Feel hot/warmer, all the time. A "change in sweating pattern" is mentioned in Leptin Rx I believe. Im assuming this means sweating my balls off doing light work in 80 degree weather? Seriously, are there any other males here that can chime in on this one. I have always been a sweater I suppose, but feel like I've gotta drink a half a gallon of water after working in the Sun a little. Curious to see what other men have experienced in this regard.

\- An increase in flexibility and range of motion, joint comfort/endurance also.

\- During the first week, I felt like I had more zip in my step, more energy, I don't know if this was placebo or something actually happening, but I swear something was majorly different.

Bam.

Thats basically me so far, probably really boring for most to read, but I wanted to get this written down. I actually liked reading everyone else's stories so I just went for it.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-21T03:50:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-journal.17030/post-180041>

welcome.............and nice to hear you did well by the kid.

### caroline — 2015-10-21T06:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-journal.17030/post-180053>

Welcome Sean! This is not boring in the least - how could someone \[YOU\] getting your life back on track and your family's possibly be boring. It excites the crap outa me!

That is what we are all here for....

JK needs us all to pay this forward......

### fitness@home — 2015-10-21T12:47:10+0300

### Joe Gavin — 2015-10-21T23:25:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-journal.17030/post-180098>

Great work Sean! You're on track. Totally kicking ass!
Your sweat is cooling your skin to increase UV absorbtion--this is a good thing. Expose as much of it to the sun as possible.
Be sure to be adding salt to your food (NOT the water!). Ketogenic diets are very diuretic and want to watch your electrolyte balance.
I've found as I've progressed on this way of life that water consumption and sweating varied. After a period of adjustment, I found I felt I was sweating appropriately.
Don't worry about drinking water, just keep drinking it. Hydration helps with electrical charge being created by the sun and the ketotic electrons.
Very inspiring to read. Keep up the momentum. Plateaus will happen, just review what's happening in your environment (mental, food, light) and go back to basics.
It's an addictive way to live-- the mental benefits are outstanding!
Joe

# Thread: Reference to birds' use of quantum entanglement in mainstream interview
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reference-to-birds-use-of-quantum-entanglement-in-mainstream-interview.17032/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Psyche — 2015-10-21T23:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reference-to-birds-use-of-quantum-entanglement-in-mainstream-interview.17032/post-180100>

I listened to this NPR interview this morning with Gerrit Vyn from Cornell Lab of Ornithology. He says the data supports quantum entanglement, not magnetic crystals, in explaining navigation.

[http://www.npr.org/2015/10/20/45003...ing-the-songs-and-pathways-of-the-living-bird](http://www.npr.org/2015/10/20/450038490/flights-of-fancy-exploring-the-songs-and-pathways-of-the-living-bird)

My favorite part:

"...You know, in terms of orientation, we've known for the better part of a century that birds have a magnetic sense. And when I was in college in the 1970s and took ornithology, I was taught that there's little magnetic crystals in their brain that acted like a little compass and kind of pulled their nose north. And it turns out that's - there's magnetic crystals there, but they don't seem to have much to do with their magnetic sense. It now appears that birds may be using a form of quantum entanglement.

GROSS: I don't even know what that is.

\----------

GROSS: This is FRESH AIR. I'm Terry Gross, back with more of our conversation about the remarkable abilities of birds. I have two guests who have contributed to "The Living Bird," a new book from the Cornell Lab of Ornithology. Gerrit Vyn goes to extremes to photograph birds and record their songs. Scott Weidensaul contributed a couple of essays to the book. He's written extensively about raptors and bird migration and is co-director Project Owlnet, which bans baby owls in order to track their migration. He wrote the new "Peterson Reference Guide To Owls Of North America And The Caribbean."

Birds seem like such remarkable animals. I mean, being able to fly is almost the least of it. You know, they fly. They sing. They travel thousands of miles and know where they're going, and they get there. They have some kind of internal GPS that I can't pretend to comprehend. But tell us some of the reasons - each of you - why you are so preoccupied with birds and have devoted, like, your professional lives to birds.

WEIDENSAUL: Because birds are really cool. I mean, it's - that's a really banal answer to it. But I've been interested in birds since I was a little kid. So, I mean, I can't tease out the moment when I became entranced by birds. But the more I learn about birds, the more astonishing they become. And you mention - you mentioned that they - they know where they're going. Actually, when they start on their migrations, they don't know where they're going. They've never gone before. Most birds, like these bar-tailed godwits making the flight across the Pacific, they're not flying with mom and dad. They're driven in that direction by an instinct that tells them to fly in a certain direction at a certain time of the year for a certain length of time. They have no idea where they're going. It's not a conscious decision. You know, in terms of orientation, we've known for the better part of a century that birds have a magnetic sense. And when I was in college in the 1970s and took ornithology, I was taught that there's little magnetic crystals in their brain that acted like a little compass and kind of pulled their nose north. And it turns out that's - there's magnetic crystals there, but they don't seem to have much to do with their magnetic sense. **It now appears that birds may be using a form of quantum entanglement.**

GROSS: I don't even know what that is.

WEIDENSAUL: Well, it's weird enough that Einstein described it as spooky action at a distance, where if two particles are created at the same moment, they - they're entangled and function as one thing across potentially millions of light-years of space. And this is happening in the eye of a bird in a way that allows them to visualize the earth's magnetic field. It's this bizarre hypothesis, but there's actually an increasing amount of experimental evidence to back it up. So, you know, the house sparrow that's hopping around your feet at an outdoor cafe picking up crumbs can see and hear and experience aspects of the natural world that we're blind, deaf and dumb to. They can see bands of polarized light moving across the sky in lockstep with the sun. They can hear extremely low-frequency sound waves that carry halfway around the world. There are some species of seabirds that will smell their way back to their nest burrows among millions of other nest burrows on - in the blackest of nights just by how that burrow smells.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T04:13:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reference-to-birds-use-of-quantum-entanglement-in-mainstream-interview.17032/post-180125>

Life at the Edge by Jim Al Khalili lays it out in 3D.

# Thread: The Lawlessness of America
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lawlessness-of-america.17035/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-10-22T19:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lawlessness-of-america.17035/post-180158>

I was fascinated to find out that the U.S. actually has some of the most stringent food safety laws in the world.

This is what becomes clear from listening to Steven Druker. Interview here:

[http://foodintegritynow.org/2015/08...d-truth-the-fdas-illegal-release-of-ge-foods/](http://foodintegritynow.org/2015/08/25/altered-genes-twisted-truth-the-fdas-illegal-release-of-ge-foods/)

He says that people run away with the false notion that places that have better food safety, like the EU, must have more stringent laws. It turns out that's just not true. The U.S, actually has very good law on paper; the trouble is it's a dead letter, because _government agencies tasked with enforcing that law, like the FDA, simply ignore it and even work against it_.

The U.S. is a _legalistic_ society - there are a large number of lawyers _per capita_, and it's a good career. But it turns out that law is more an instrument than an end.

There's a larger problem here. Not everything can be solved "on paper". There are economic and societal and, in the final analysis, cultural forces at work here.

See also:

[http://www.amazon.com/Altered-Genes-Twisted-Truth-Systematically/dp/0985616903](http://www.amazon.com/Altered-Genes-Twisted-Truth-Systematically/dp/0985616903)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T20:56:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lawlessness-of-america.17035/post-180168>

The U.S. is a _legalistic_ society = we got that from our mother country England.

### Scompy — 2015-10-22T21:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lawlessness-of-america.17035/post-180179>

The medical establishment promotes H=fw (Health=food+workouts). True Health means true Energy. Energy= one's Mass combined with Light. E=mc^2. I don't think Einstein would approve of E=mfw (mass x food x workouts) because food's benefit is a result of the light it was in under conditional hardships of the food-source. This is why food substance is a bandaid outside of deficiencies in an element or compound our bodies that require like iron, zinc, DHA and harder to consume amino acids (cysteine).

My personal take on the 'medical establishment' is that under this umbrella, surgery specifically has been the greatest benefit. Even though a surgery carries greater risk, it usually provides a more permanent solution and actual cure to a mechanical problem. Most people are wise enough to know that taking pharmicuticals are on par with eating a food source that provides faster, temporary fixes... they are just bandaids and usually do not result in long-term cures.

Also, most people understand that doctors are caught between a rock and a hard-place when it comes to advocating what the medical-establishment promotes versus what actually WORKS. Often, this is the core fight: the promotion of a perception of beliefs or what works in practice. It's why the lawyers win more than the doctors that take an oath to help the rest of us. Sad, sad times for society. One-hundred years from now, people will look back on this period of time and shake their heads in knowing only a few knew the answers, while the masses and even those inside of the medical establishment continued walking around in zombietopia.

### Gagnrad — 2015-10-23T19:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-lawlessness-of-america.17035/post-180283>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=180168)
>
> The U.S. is a _legalistic_ society = we got that from our mother country England.

Yes, a direct line there, isn't there? The Anglo-American approach is usually seen as relatively effective and benign, too:

[http://www.alanmacfarlane.com/TEXTS/law\_effective.pdf](http://www.alanmacfarlane.com/TEXTS/law_effective.pdf)

Or in more knockabout and ideological form, say here:

[http://www.steynonline.com](http://www.steynonline.com)

AFAICT, these analyses tend to revolve around the Common Law and how, it's alleged, it can be more flexible and superior to, say, the Roman Law tradition. Here, I suppose, we're concerned with something a little different. But I'd guess I'd have to ask a lawyer about that.

It seems to me there are big problems here … I think it's a serious matter when law is there on paper and governmental bodies charged with enforcing it _won't_. It's not just the FDA either. The EPA seems _never_ to have carried out the function it was set up to carry out:

[http://www.amazon.com/Poison-Spring-Secret-History-Pollution/dp/1608199142/](http://www.amazon.com/Poison-Spring-Secret-History-Pollution/dp/1608199142/)

When the general public wakes up to this, it _will_ not like it. And that's potentially politically destabilising. People don't always think about where their dirty games may end.

I think in all modern societies _money_ talks. (In some ways it perhaps replaces the influence that entrenched power and ability to deploy deadly force had in earlier societies.) But it seems to me that this is even more true of the U.S. than it is of other modern societies. (And, I suspect, that's been true for quite a long time.)

Coca-Cola shelling out millions to dietitians and "scientists" is only the thin edge of the wedge. Big Pharma, from what I can see, is up to its eyes in much nastier stuff:

[http://davidhealy.org/pharmageddon-is-the-story-of-a-tragedy/](http://davidhealy.org/pharmageddon-is-the-story-of-a-tragedy/)

[https://www.rxisk.org](https://www.rxisk.org)

And the Biotech/Pesticide Industry - can one really separate the two? - is probably doing more damage than these. Even if those guys were stopped tomorrow, how long would it take to clean it all up, to re-establish healthy soil-biomes and so on? Who could even hazard a guess at that?

Ido Portal:
"I am not impressed with Wim Hof's method. Wim Hof as a practitioner - very impressive.
Wim Hof's method on the other hand = devoid of detail, progression, logic, coherent process, quality. It is confused, contradictory and scattered.
His book is especially terrible.
His idea is an old one and one that mankind has been aware of since the age of time, but putting it into practice takes not only practice as is often presented but specific talents and predisposition for that direction.

As for the cold resistance specifically - I've been aware of Tummo and the Tibetan methods for over a decade. It is nothing new. It is energy consuming but doable and definitely trainable. I started to play with the cold over 10 years ago. Dousing, Polar Bear swims, Russian cold methods, did the newer technologies as well via cryochambers, cryotherapy, etc."

### DavidL — 2015-10-25T19:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcript-of-blog-talk-lecture.17041/post-180432>

**Kryon** Channeled through Lee Carroll 18 July 2015, Salt Lake City, Utah; Sedona Journal

**Medicine: Where It’s Going**

"I want to show you some other areas to consider. There are several categories that we’d like to talk about, but they’re not in a specific order except for the last one. Let’s talk about medicine and what it is going to look like in the future. The most elegant predictions of medicine fall short of what’s really coming. What you have today is a high-tech approach to designer chemistry. Now, since the body is made up of chemicals, it makes sense to look at this chemistry and work with it. It makes total sense to discover how it reacts to disease and then design cures with more chemistry. What you have done is absolutely normal, but it’s going to reach an end very soon because the future of medicine is physics, dear ones, not chemistry."
...
"You’re going to start understanding and developing new medical physics. You will discover that mediphysics will speak to cellular structure and give it instructions without involving a single chemical. There’s always a reaction to chemistry, isn’t there? There’s always a side effect. When you push one thing, something else reacts, doesn’t it? How do you like it so far? Your most elegant chemical designs, the ones that help with the worst diseases on the planet, all have side effects — and sometimes death is one of them! I say to you, how do you like it so far?"
...
"Alzheimer’s is heartbreaking. There are millions on this planet who develop this condition, more all the time. You’re living longer, and a plaque-like material that obfuscates memory within the brain is becoming more common. It clings to certain parts of the brain, encrusting and restricting it and imprisoning the Human ability to remember and eventually to process information at all. The result is slow death. It’s caused by your environment, a fact that will be discovered eventually. Your long-lived ancients didn’t have it.

Alzheimer’s is going to be cured with physics. Your science is starting to reveal that the plaque-like substance has a resonance that allows it to be weakened with sound. High-frequency sound tuned to a certain frequency and amplitude will cause the sheaths to weaken and dissolve. There’s a lot of research to do yet, but when you realize that physics alone, without chemistry, can change the structure of biology, it will be a “eureka!” moment. There will be no side effects whatsoever. It’s coming."
...
"Dear ones, the future of health and healing is not through better chemistry, yet medicine is still waiting for better pills! Did you connect the dots and realize that all biology is physics based? There are magnetics, leverage, the energy of consciousness, electricity, and much more within the cellular structure. With physics alone, you can tune in to disease and destroy it! You can fine-tune your system with cooperative resonances and extend life with benevolent physical assistance. Sixteen years ago, I told you this when I spoke of the Temple of Rejuvenation. I described the super-cooling needed to do it and the temperatures needed to work with it (–55°C). This is physics!"

### Martin — 2015-10-25T21:52:30+0200

Doc, with each live event you do, you get sharper. The emphasis you put on certain words and phrases really makes it come alive. The last half of this one where you did a "lightning round" where you riffed on certain conditions and broke them down to a string of events had me spellbound. Marry that style of overview with the deep blogs and you come away with the Keys to the Kingdom.

### Scompy — 2015-10-26T21:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcript-of-blog-talk-lecture.17041/post-180510>

Physics drives the biology. Translation: most modern medical treatments are bandaids rather than cures. When you live in an environment with optimal physics, most ailments can be cured. When you live in an altered physics environment, which is now embedded within 95%+ of modernized communities, the medical community profits the most by treating one disease until the next disease manifests.

My niece is just finishing up nursing school and I've already had many interesting conversations with her about her knowledge of quantum biology and the physics that is present in her profession. What do you think she has been taught? How to treat and bandaid, not how to cure and find the root-cause reasons behind why a person suffers. I'm going to make it a point to show her the side we here on the forums we understand. It takes one person at a time to understand all sciences and see the interactions among them, but it can be done when people don't close off themselves under their current belief structures.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-27T04:35:05+0200
