# Thread: My personal biophysics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### oliverg — 2015-10-23T17:36:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-180264>

So, it's been a few months since I posted my first hello here on the optimal health forum, and I've been regretfully un-involved since then, but over the last two months, I have been growing in obsession over the material. Just finished the Epi-Paleo Rx, have listened to all the podcasts featuring Jack at least 5 times, definitely more in some cases, and skulking around the forums, prying at the more basic ideas for a few weeks. I would spend even more time on it all if I didn't have such a crowded schedule.
I'm going to get seriously in depth here, ask a lot of questions (not all of which I expect to be answered immediately or directly). I mostly want to get my fingers in deep, because I love where this is all going, down to the roots of the issue, in terms of health, but also our understandings of our place in the universe.

I feel the need to make these topics more and more a part of my life, if not my life as a whole, and I do not yet know how to do so, I think starting with a journal here, and continuing to study the blog is a good place to start.

First question > after having read the Epi-Paleo Rx, what would be a good blog entry to start at. In some podcasts, Jack mentions that the book goes over the first 2 years of the blog, but if there's a more specific place to continue my reading from, it would be helpful.

Personal history:
Loads of health issues from birth. Born jaundiced, chronic eczema and psoriasis, intolerances to a whole lot of foods. Decent is German/Russian/Syberian (European) and French/Native American, though I look straight caucasian. Alcoholism runs down both sides of the family, though my parents don't drink a drop anymore, it affected my grandparents pretty thoroughly. Doctors only made things worse with cortisone, which would burn and bleach my skin. Having an awesome mother with an understanding of more holistic health (yoga teacher and ancient Indian philosophy teacher), she got me off all of that. I live in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, where it's frigid in winter, and hot and muggy in summer.
I spent probably the first 12 years of my life not getting much sleep, having serious allergies and itching away at my skin all night, every night. On top of this, I had constant fatigue, which kept me from enjoying sports, and migraines. After that, my mother found the Eat Right 4 Your Type book, and we figured that wheat, dairy and nightshades weren't right for me. After a hard time quitting those, I started getting used to it, started overexercising to get rid of the the excess weight I seemed to have had forever. A lot of symptoms cleared up, but I was still far from a healthy specimen.
I became more socially functional (not much) after being moved around the age of 16 to an "alternative" highschool of 75 students, mostly all stoners and delinquents. I drank, smoked, partied a lot, and brought back a lot of health issues. Quit everything at 18 1/2, but had done plenty of damage.
Went into a welding and fitting program for 7 months, hated it, breathing all the metal fumes all the time, quit. I don't have a good relationship with established education, or authority figures, which makes me quite sad, because I love nothing more than learning, reading constantly.
I was desperate for something, so I jumped on fruitarianism for a year and a half, which felt great at first, during the summer. I loved being able to eat as much as I liked without gaining weight - I do so love eating, and eating some more.
After hitchhiking through Canada and California with my girlfriend on 0$$ for a year, I returned home, to be greeted by a terrible winter of some of the worst eczema of my life. Somehow, some of the intuition I got from my mother, or that I developed through all the meditation in my life, or I don't know, I was immediately attracted to raw meat - particularly salmon, at first. That helped a lot, and I discovered paleo, raw paleo, Owsley the Bear. Mark Sisson, etc. Spent a year and a half gorging myself on raw meats and fat, which, again, was great at first, but I started seeing some of my symptoms (though 1000x better than they were as a kid) return. I became obsessed with reading about health - more than I already was. I found Dave Asprey, Wim Hof, Jack Kruse, Primal Edge Health, Stephanie Person, Nora Gedgaudas, etc etc.
That's been in the last year or so. In terms of personal life, I went in and out of a prestigious Illustration and Design program - which was incredibly difficult to get into (for most, not I, being that I've drawn my whole life), but once again, my difficulty with the near-sightedness and rigidity of modern education took over, and regretfully had to leave. I'm a musician, I'm interested in just about everything - from the esoteric to the practical, I love meeting people, talking, exercising, being out in nature (when I can), and above all, learning about health, especially the deeper stuff, which is why I'm posting here. I also love to write, so if this seems like a lot of text, it's nothing for me, it goes by in a flash.
So, now, I live on the 3rd floor in a city (aka an EMF hellhole), working from home, online, for Bing, the search engine, as a web search analyst, wearing my yellow-tinted glasses, with an 250W infrared lamp on, drinking some ice-cold Berkey-filtered water. I am in debt of 10 000$ for my education, which I am paying off little by little, getting involved in a small search engine optimization startup, and constantly fighting with my desire to just play music, go meditate on a freezing mountaintop in the sun, go hunting and fishing, and maybe taking the long haul and getting a full education to become a biophysicist, or at least a naturopath, or something of the sort - though I know how that will end up.
I struggle to just stay in a healthy state, spending all of my time working, learning about my health, doing everything I can to improve it, and maybe fit in a little music or some time with my girlfriend. I meditate, take cold showers, try to get as much AM sunlight (I have always sungazed, on and off), exercise, go to the park and walk barefoot if I can. My diet is something like the Leptin Postscript - a medium meal of 20-25g protein from seafood, and a TON of fat at breakfast, something similar for lunch, and a bigger meal at dinner, along with lots of bone broth (now with oyster shells). I see improvements, my energy is more stable, low still a little on the low side, my eczema is wonderfully tame, though I still have red circles from psioriasis around the eyes, my sleep is pretty good. I am quite fit, I'm a pretty stable guy, with lots of upper body muscle and strength, for how much I exercise, but I have constant bloating and even a little cellulite on my butt and thighs, plus a sense of heaviness in them. I work standing up. I will be starting Wim Hof's techniques, with the breathing exercises, soon. I'm saving up over time for a magnetico, and quantlet when it comes out. I just ordered a cool fat burner, so I can stay cold while working.
Ok, I'm just trying to fit everything in here, so just a little more - my carb tolerance is incredibly low, I seem to only tolerate some lettuce, coconut oil, tea and spices unless I'm particularly unstressed. I'm concerned with my protein intake - Stephanie Person, the youtube keto coach, always talks about how her clients' bloog glucose levels get too high with just 1 g of protein over their _personal_ tolerance, and I've definitely noticed more itching, bloating and bad sleep, if I'm not careful there - though lots of seafood, at the right times seems to mitigate this. Fat, I can eat with a spoon all day long (though i don't). Mostly grass-fed lard, raw beef fat, raw marrow, duck fat and coconut oil. Wouldn't surprise me if I consumed over 3000-4000 calories a day, though I don;t strictly count.
In terms of exercise, it varies, but usually some weights of bodyweight exercise in moderate to high intensity for 20-30 seconds, then a 2 minute cooldown in between with maybe a little light stretching, every day for 20 minutes to an hour, plus lots of standing and walking. For cold, its only 1 to 2 minutes of freezing showers a day, though I'll be starting the face-dunking soon and I'm excited for ice baths, and getting my cool-fat burner. I can just walk around shirtless in winter here - I'm used to get weird looks, in fact I enjoy it.
So, for now, that's all. I want to post here at least once a month, but hopefully a lot more, especially if I have more means of testing, if I come upon a little more money, getting some labs, and buying more ketone and blood glucose strips for my glucometer/ketone monitor.
It is my plan to eventually move out of the city, but at the moment, I neither have the money to do so, or the time to find an ideal place to live, or build a new lifestyle - I'm having a difficult enough time deciding what I want to do with myself, other than be optimal - or more, pay off my debt, and figure out what the next step is.
So yea, if anyone has any tips, general thoughts about anything I said, I like the idea that this could be a pretty open thread, in part about my progression in self-education on the topic, specific tweaks that can be made, but, most importantly, where this is all going, what that means for all of us, what we can do about it. Feel free to let any thoughts come up.

Some questions:
1\. (the one above about what specific blog post to go to next)
2\. protein tolerance and overall redox potential seems to be a large variable based on one's environment and optimal habits, but it's hard to know exactly what balances out the rest. Is is possible to be semi-optimal in the environment I currently exist in, meaning a city, 3rd floor, going to bed early and using blueblockers + everything else I've mentioned? I'm guessing the answer is that its N=1 and I have to gauge that out for myself.
3\. sort of a continuation of #2: tons of excessive protein seems to be a problem, but if its coming from decent-to-high quality seafood, is the benefit balanced out? Or lean towards better or worse? Once again, I assume this depends.
4\. In the world of health, what paths are there to become a real authority in deep matters, without going through a corrupt system? Here, in quebec, practicionners are particularly regulated, and I know of naturopaths and homeopaths being severely chased after by the system. Quantum biology and biophysics seem to be at the heart of health (starting the Body Electric, my bible, which, it turns out, I've owned my entire life). Is continuing my self-education the best course?
5\. Do you believe anything and everything is possible?

I know, all my questions are relative and loaded, but I think that just opens up wider topics of discussion, which I enjoy.

Lots of love,
All my best,
Oliver

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-23T18:21:27+0300

**Time is the only wealth we’re given.**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-23T18:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-180270>

Some questions: **SOME ANSWERS**
1\. (the one above about what specific blog post to go to next) done
2\. protein tolerance and overall redox potential seems to be a large variable based on one's environment and optimal habits, but it's hard to know exactly what balances out the rest. Is is possible to be semi-optimal in the environment I currently exist in, meaning a city, 3rd floor, going to bed early and using blueblockers + everything else I've mentioned? I'm guessing the answer is that its N=1 and I have to gauge that out for myself.
**Food is a tertiary story. That is laid out in the Ubiquitination series which you missed. It is all about Purple and Red light. Focus in on DHA from wild sources. That is all the food context you need now until you move to sea level.**
3\. sort of a continuation of #2: tons of excessive protein seems to be a problem, but if its coming from decent-to-high quality seafood, is the benefit balanced out? Or lean towards better or worse? Once again, I assume this depends. **mTOR is an issue when you lack UV light. Get it and you can stop the nonsense with food. You will also see as you gain _purple light_ you won't be wanting much protein or carbs.**
4\. In the world of health, what paths are there to become a real authority in deep matters, without going through a corrupt system? Here, in quebec, practicionners are particularly regulated, and I know of naturopaths and homeopaths being severely chased after by the system. Quantum biology and biophysics seem to be at the heart of health (starting the Body Electric, my bible, which, it turns out, I've owned my entire life). Is continuing my self-education the best course? **Clay Bostock wrote guest blog 1. He maybe in Quebec by now and he might inspire you. Read his blog and look him up. The path thru the valley of life is filled by ambiguity and wisdom. Collect both and embrace them.**

**Life is akin to a valley. We entered this valley from different places. Some purple and some red. Some even in the blue light. Your journey seems to have begun in the blue. No worries.** **From its depths we see many great visions possible in life, from its peak we see only small things limiting our focus. Traveling constantly between floor and peak we see ambiguity on our travels and some of us learn from it and actually embrace it as a teacher. Mediocrity knows little other than itself; but wisdom instantly recognizes genius in this valley.
**
5\. Do you believe anything and everything is possible? **Of course I do.**

**Do you look "at science" or do you look thru science through a lens? I submit that that most of us are prisoners to modern science perspectives because we see it via "their lenses". I look at nature and turn it 90 degrees, 180 degrees, 270 degrees, and degrees in between. Every time I do this, my perspective of a problem changes and I learn a little bit more about what I missed from the basic perspective from which I was taught. I learned to do this from DaVinci and Michelangelo who were masters of altering the ground and foreground in their works. They changed sizes and shapes of proportions on their art to make the visualization of the observer more life like. They were masters at altering the lens that we see the world through to make it more accurate based upon where we were in space and time I view science in the same way. I need to alter the perception of what we currently believe so you can see the parts of science that are unobservable in action. Just because you can't or don't see it does not make it immaterial. On the contrary, it turns our what you can't see and do not know are the most important parts of science. Do you look at that lens and observe how it might bend reality? _You should._**

**When you ask this it tells me your dopamine level sucks in your frontal lobes. Ubi 24 will solve that. That is all you got?

You were easy.**

**Now stop wasting fucking time and get to it. You've been given a gift here. Use it and share it.**

### oliverg — 2016-01-22T23:32:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-187121>

Havn't updated in a long time, been sticking to Dr Kruse's suggestion to avoid wasting any time, so I've made arrangements to be moving to White Horse, Yukon in a few months from now, to live and work closer to nature, outside, doing carpentry, mushroom picking, and play music.
I have a few questions about the environment there, being that it's a little bit of an extreme zone.
Luckily, it has low population, extremely clean air and water, low non-native EMF and wild food, notably fish, but the light cycles are crazy. Half of the year, the sun is up at 4 AM, until midnight (some tell me it basically doesn't set at all), and the other half, it's up at 10 AM and goes down at 4.
Wondering if anyone has any quick ideas of how to work with this, especially when it comes to sleep in summer time. From what I gather, it's best to have very low light for several hours before bed, and we require a certain amount of sleep, though I'm not sure in this case, if I'm getting tons of sunlight, does it matter?

Thanks

### Inger — 2016-01-23T19:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-187187>

I was born and grew up on the 60th latitude.... and there the summers are looong.... about the same latitude you are moving to!

I did not even used curtains at all in front of my window the last years I lived there... I fell soundly to sleep at 10\_11 PM the evening sun shining into my bedroom..lol
Do not worry. The excess light (natural) that you get in summer, you balance out with excess darkness in winter. I actually like it that way... life never gets boring... and in winter you kinda hibernate a bit
it is very cool  as are the endless days in summer

It never gave me sleep issues, after a short period of adaption to Kruse style living

You will adapt, no doubt, the main thing is, avoid fake lights! The natural lights will not hurt you. I do think you will naturally sleep a bit longer in winter and less in summer tho. But I think it is just fine that way
I am starting to get excited about the longs summer days already, now that the days get longer.... feels nice

I think... when we go all in.... life always get super exciting and fun

> It never gave me sleep issues, after a short period of adaption to Kruse style living
>
> You will adapt, no doubt, the main thing is, avoid fake lights! The natural lights will not hurt you. I do think you will naturally sleep a bit longer in winter and less in summer tho. But I think it is just fine that way
> I am starting to get excited about the longs summer days already, now that the days get longer.... feels nice
>
> I think... when we go all in.... life always get super exciting and fun

Thanks, Inger. That's relieving, and yea, this should be quite the adventure. I also feel that the very sense of adventure has something to do with the hormonal, maybe even vibrational, connection to healing and health.

### oliverg — 2016-07-16T21:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-198430>

So I'm in the Yukon. Love it out here, plenty of work out in the bush, been mostly away from computers. But, straight to the point: I will be starting work on a salmon fishing boat on Taku River soon. Being a fisherman, I will be working 4 months of the year on a boat, with clean air, no blue light, low EMF, tons and tons of sun, it being during the part of the year where the sun rises at 4 AM and sets by 12 PM. Two things concern me, though : I will not be grounded, and the food they serve will require me to live partially on rice ( though we will be eating TONS of fresh wild salmon  ), which I have avoided for a while. Is sun exposure without being grounded an issue? and I know food is far from primary, but will rice affect my performance, considering that its tough physical work? I understand if that depends on my own body and environment, but should I make an extra effort to stock the hell up on coconut oil to replace the energy I'd otherwise be trying to get from the rice? \\

Thanks, wish I could contribute to the forums more, but I'm trying to be as hardcore as possible about the low blue light thing.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-07-17T07:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-personal-biophysics.17042/post-198437>

all boats have to be grounded.

I'm curious if anyone else experiences vasodilation (mainly in hands and feet, sometimes ears) when outside on a sunny and warm day. It's to the point where I can't take walks or do regular errands without feeling like I'm overheating and swelling up. I had to quit running years ago because of it (though I'm glad now that I never completed that marathon goal!) I also have these symptoms if my home is over 71 - 72 degrees, or when I'm stressed. Symptoms x 10 years, numbers medical consults. I'm in my early 30's, main dx is hashimotos and pcos. But have seen major improvements in my blood sugar since starting epi paleo rx. I also stopped wearing contacts x 2 months. (only wore one in right eye.)

Hi Dr Kruse,

I just read time #8 regarding psoriasis. I'm thankful you have written that, as I was just dx with it this week! So far just small (but very itchy) patches on my elbows and knees. So in a way a warning sign that brought me back to the forum and blog.

This quote from your blog spoke to me (though there were so many new concepts that my mind is trying to sort it all out still).

"The lack of full spectrum sunlight is the missing piece. It is supposed to shines on the largest organ in the body, which is the skin, and the skin’s thickness is determined by the amount of light that hits it and how effective the underlying circulatory system is in assimilating UV and IR light. These initially photoelectric interactions determines ultimately, how the atomic lattice of the skin interacts or did not interact, with specific frequencies of light we allowed to hit our skin and eye. If you share the same light environment in your family, it begins to makes sense why you might think there is a genetic component. The interesting aspects that links both cancer and psoriasis is how light plays a deep role in both diseases. "

My adoptive mother and my twin are all struggling with similar autoimmune diseases. Sharing same light environment, bingo!

And, my other question is about the underlying circulatory system in assimilating UV and IR light. Would reynauds and my erythromelalgia ( I refer to myself as sensitive to cold and sun) also be an issue in my development of psoriasis? Sadly I really have avoided the sun the last 10 years due to the development of these issues, and working in the medical field inside, all day.

I also wanted to mention about you discussing those that have metabolic syndrome markers at normal wt. That's me! My last huge panel of blood work that came in. It was like the lab results were for an obese person. I'm an active mom, early 30s and hx of low/normal bmi. Go figure!

Also, I just found a local tanning salon that offers red light therapy and of course traditional types of tanning. What would be my best option at this time? My vit d is still 24. Working on consuming high protein breakfast and dha sources daily.

# Thread: Sunscreen Linked To Coral Destruction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2015-10-25T11:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/post-180406>

Think of all the peoples who have been diving for pearls and shellfish over millenia and never a one put any toxic chemicals on their skin to get in the water to poison the coral!

These unnecessary and actually counterproductive products that have been pushed at the public so assiduously - for a parallel, see also statins, see also Gatorade and the sugar-laden sports-drink "rehydration" mania ... and hand-sanitisers, anti-perspirants and a plethora of other products - have now been linked to the destruction of coral reefs:

"Researchers conducted the study in the U.S. Virgin Islands and Hawaii — areas that attract large amounts of tourists each year to swim in reef areas. They found that the chemical oxybenzone affects coral in three different ways: it alters its DNA, makes coral susceptible to potentially fatal bleaching, and acts as an endocrine disruptor, which causes baby coral to encase itself in its own skeleton and leads to its death.

To make things worse, it does not take a large amount of this chemical to upset coral. According to the research, concentrations of oxybenzone as low as 62 parts per trillion — equivalent to a drop of water in six and a half Olympic-sized swimming pools — are deemed harmful.

Between 6,000 and 14,000 tons of sunscreen lotion make their way into coral reef areas each year, and much of that sunscreen contains oxybenzone. ..."​

Article:

Almost Every Chemical-Based Sunscreen In The U.S. Linked To Coral Destruction:

[http://thinkprogress.org/climate/2015/10/21/3714544/sunscreen-coral-enemy/](http://thinkprogress.org/climate/2015/10/21/3714544/sunscreen-coral-enemy/)

### RobH — 2015-10-25T12:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunscreen-linked-to-coral-destruction.17048/post-180408>

somehow this doesn't surprise me.... probably not so good for your skin eeither!

RobH

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raising-progesterone.17049/post-180407>

It is obvious that my adrenal issues are holding back my recovery at the moment and are at the crux of my problems. Jack says that for chronically depressed cortisol one of the most important steps is to re-establish progesterone levels. Can anyone (including Jack) tell me the best way of doing this? Would pregnenelone supplementation be preferred to progesterone?

### JanSz — 2015-10-27T17:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raising-progesterone.17049/post-180561>

> [omegajosh said:](/goto/post?id=180407)
>
> It is obvious that my adrenal issues are holding back my recovery at the moment and are at the crux of my problems. Jack says that for chronically depressed cortisol one of the most important steps is to re-establish progesterone levels. Can anyone (including Jack) tell me the best way of doing this? Would pregnenelone supplementation be preferred to progesterone?

cortisol, pregnenolone, progesterone, DHEAs
are steroid hormones.
There is many other steroid hormones.
But if you would take healthy person,
measure his commonly measured steroid hormones,
took all of them and treated as 100% by weight
you will find big differences in their participation
DHEAs -- 94.5%
cortisol(AM) -- 4.25%
all the rest take what is left
It makes good sense to mind this two hormones with special care.

Cortisol is made in adrenals, so it becomes first suspect.
Most often it is declared fatigued, sick.
That is bad assumption most of the time.

To nourish adrenals you must provide them with:
numerous micronutrients
and
proper light

I you live
above or below subtropics there is no sufficient light there (even if you are naked)
If you wear clothes, glasses, sunscreen is even worst.

I think you live in UK, say London, latitude 51.5072° N
even on cloudless sunny day at noon, there is not enough light there
subtropic ends at 38th parallel, it is around Gibraltar in your area.
36.1430° N

There are discussions on how to go about artificial lighting.
Some here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-18](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-18)

Remember, lighting is about
proper frequencies
proper intensity
proper timing

all that lead to proper Circadian Rhythm

\-----------------------------------
Lighting is just one item.
The other are large variety of micronutrients.
We are not what we eat, we are what we assimilate.
So
you want to check your micronutrients

# Thread: Morning light bedding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-25T22:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180447>

I remember there was a practice 50+ years ago to take bedding out into the morning sunlight to 'shake out' dust and whatnot. At that time, it was thought (like a spawn from Benjamin Franklin's patterns) that it would help curb disease just like opening up the morning windows. Of course viruses can get fried up in sunlight. I got curious about that older practice and sadly, I've found thousands of articles about **blocking out morning light as a modern-day theme,** like this:

[http://www.houzz.com/discussions/1007034/need-ideas-to-block-light-in-early-morning-for-this-window](http://www.houzz.com/discussions/1007034/need-ideas-to-block-light-in-early-morning-for-this-window)
**
Anyone have any good artiles on past-time behaviors of the benefits of putting out bedding into the morning light?
**

### Lahelada — 2015-10-25T23:41:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180451>

Waddaya mean in olden days??
Still done today in Germany Switzerland and parts of Italy. My gran's favourite sport to see what the bedding looks like ,if it was sufficiently clean and neat ...
It probably had to do with duvets having down fillings.
Have a look

[www.groundedtraveler.com/2012/01/10/sleeping-with-germans/](http://www.groundedtraveler.com/2012/01/10/sleeping-with-germans/)
Pics
[https://www.google.com/search?q=bet...ved=0CAYQ\_AUoAWoVChMI1c2riMreyAIV0KKICh247ACk](https://www.google.com/search?q=bettzeug+lüften&client=ms-opera-mobile&biw=1024&bih=600&source=lnms&tbm=isch&sa=X&ved=0CAYQ_AUoAWoVChMI1c2riMreyAIV0KKICh247ACk)

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-26T00:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180453>

My mom always wanted to hang the sheets to dry so the sun would make them cleaner. I'm curious about this too.

### Scompy — 2015-10-26T03:05:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180459>

In 1994, I spent a summer in the northern part of Finland getting 20 hours of light every day. When I arrived in Finland, I was 275 pounds. When I got back to the states, I was 220 pounds and everyone was shocked what happened to me in 3 months. Additionally, I slept on an 'unplugged' water bed and was generally cold all night long (CT!). I also did a lot of jumping into an ice-cold swimming pool and using saunas. The relative I was staying with always dried sheets outside rather than using a conventional dryer. It's also common there to get sheets out into the morning sun. But be warned, the mosquitoes are as large as birds in Finland--the land of 10,000 lakes.

### nicld — 2015-10-26T18:21:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180497>

> My mom always wanted to hang the sheets to dry so the sun would make them cleaner. I'm curious about this too.

I LOVE putting my sheets out on the line.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-29T13:15:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180693>

I looked into this a bit further and am beginning to think that it dates back to the times of the plague, a collective memory if you will. Pest! house inhabitants were given strict instructions to smoke their houses and air all their bedding. Ships entering harbours had to do the same airing their personal gear on deck before being g allowed to dock.

The German rule is that it works only in months without an R that is May to July .

### Scompy — 2015-10-29T18:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180730>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=180724)

>  Lack of UV today...

@[Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/) Light benefits are still there on the other side of the rain and clouds. Like the rest of you, I find great importance in knowing where the sun rises and moves through the sky behind cloudcover. And I just realized that I'm probably 0.274% of the population that actually could look up to a clouded-sky on any given day and point to the exact location of the sun.

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) Most of us take so many things for granted in 2015: our relationships and spouses, our children, our environment, etc. It's a sad thought, but I'm believing more and more that humanity has regressed from what it was 100-200 years ago. Do people wake up and attempt to practice a virtue for the day? Benjamin Franklin comes to mind again because one of his virtues was Frugality. ([http://www.artofmanliness.com/2008/06/01/the-virtuous-life-wrap-up/](http://www.artofmanliness.com/2008/06/01/the-virtuous-life-wrap-up/))

### Penny — 2015-10-29T19:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180732>

So are we supposed to go outside in the morning light even if it's a cloudy day?
The clouds cover the UVA/UVB and leave the UVC which is a fail...

### Scompy — 2015-10-29T19:21:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180733>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=180732)
>
> So are we supposed to go outside in the morning light even if it's a cloudy day?
> The clouds cover the UVA/UVB and leave the UVC which is a fail...

I always get outside in the morning! Rain or shine!

For UVC, I don't believe so... UVC never reaches earth, unless your under an ozone-depleted region perhaps, or maybe at super-high elevation as a possibility too. I don't think we need to worry about UVC unless it's manmade, like from a welding machine that can generate it.

Some of the UVB can be blocked by cloudcover, but it's not always the case due to conditions. UVA still penetrates well, like up to 60-80% on average based on the type of cloud-cover.

(Would love to get a second-pair of eyes on these statements if I'm misinformed).

### Lahelada — 2015-10-29T20:14:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180735>

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) I did go outside and yes to all you said about UV still being around. I just did not get the same boost as with bright sunshine.
I even did another unthinkable just now. Stood at the open window and gazed out. A forgotten pastime.
@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) yes go outside whenever you can. UVA is always present and B depending on season and location. In certain conditions UV is even enhanced.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cloud-enhanced-uv-cloud-scattering.17031/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cloud-enhanced-uv-cloud-scattering.17031/)

([http://www.artofmanliness.com/2008/06/01/the-virtuous-life-wrap-up/](http://www.artofmanliness.com/2008/06/01/the-virtuous-life-wrap-up/))

What I appreciate the most about "getting back to nature" is the sheer, intoxicating enjoyment one derives from the smallest things. The way the sun feels on your face, the intricate beauty of a flower, the smell of the grass as you lie in it soaking up the earth's energy, the warmth of a crackling fire. THESE simple things give us joy, as do our relationships....we no longer look for elusive happiness in "things" that culture tells us we need, or must attain to be considered "successful." I think frugality naturally arises from that disposition, as you know what is truly important, and it's things money just can't buy.

I think of Ben Franklin every day....I think it was he who said, "Early to bed, early to rise, makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise."

### Psyche — 2015-10-29T21:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-light-bedding.17050/post-180743>

@[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) / @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) - Yes, to confirm, every morning has UV, even with cloud cover. I go out no matter what! I can't wait for the time change this year, which is something new for me. I've always dreaded it, but now it will be easier for me to get light in the morning.

@Hansen- what you are talking about is so lacking in society today. More Ben Franklin=less gun violence.

# Thread: Looking for a solid MSDS on DHA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-solid-msds-on-dha.17052/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-26T03:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-a-solid-msds-on-dha.17052/post-180461>

So back in the mid-90s, I was a biologist at Stanford University. I mostly worked in the Bio-sciences department helping in the pre-med labs during the main school year, but also had several summer jobs when the students were off-campus. At the Environmental Health and Safety Dept, I worked a job catalogueing chemicals and materials on campus to help construct a database of info. For this reason, I became extremely familiar with MSDSs (Material Safety Data Sheets) at that time to denote what hazards existed in various locations.

After learning from the wise Dr.K about the extraordinary significance of DHA and the mitochondria for electron tunneling, it was instinctual for me to understand the full details of DHA (Docosahexaenoic acid). Knowing we can curse DHA into a transfat with heat like other fats and oils, I expected to see find better info on DHA on MSDSs. But most data on DHA I've found poking around today looks to be 'unknown' for DHA. I'll have to search for better info.

My main curiosity has to do with how it denatures. Generally, anything over 105F is a standard for denaturing in living tissues. It's why we see survival rates plummet if you get a fever for 105F. That alone is making me believe we want to take in DHA at a very low-temp if possible, like body-temp or lower to be assurred the DHA is holding up as a molecule for use.

### healthy2015 — 2015-10-26T13:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-blubs.17053/post-180471>

Hello,

I am based in the UK and looking to purchase UV black light bulbs

I came across these - can anyone tell me if these would be suitable?

[http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html](http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html)

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/Skytronic-1...1445856434&sr=8-8&keywords=blacklight+uv+bulb](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Skytronic-160-020-15W-Blacklight-Bulb/dp/B000BN6LMM/ref=sr_1_8?ie=UTF8&qid=1445856434&sr=8-8&keywords=blacklight+uv+bulb)

If these are not please can someone point me in the right direction?

Thank you very much

### healthy2015 — 2015-10-26T18:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-blubs.17053/post-180498>

Sorry to be a pain but does anyone know if these are suitable?
Can anyone help please Thank you

### Optimalbound — 2015-10-26T19:10:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-blubs.17053/post-180501>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=180471)
>
> Hello,
>
> I am based in the UK and looking to purchase UV black light bulbs
>
> I came across these - can anyone tell me if these would be suitable?
>
> [http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html](http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html)

That has nearly identical specs to the ones I'm using (365nm, fluorescent) and should be fine.
Keep in mind that fluorescent blacklights also emit a small amount of x-ray radiation, so if you leave in on all the time, you should probably consider having it some distance away and not right on your desk.

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=180471)
>
> [http://www.amazon.co.uk/Skytronic-160-020-15W-Blacklight-Bulb/dp/B000BN6LMM/ref=sr\_1\_8?ie=UTF8&qid=1445856434&sr=8-8&keywords=blacklight uv bulb](http://www.amazon.co.uk/Skytronic-160-020-15W-Blacklight-Bulb/dp/B000BN6LMM/ref=sr_1_8?ie=UTF8&qid=1445856434&sr=8-8&keywords=blacklight+uv+bulb)

No info on wavelength there, but I given the design, I would assume that most of its output is around 365nm, as well.

### healthy2015 — 2015-11-03T12:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-blubs.17053/post-181050>

Hello,

We have received the light bulbs below.

[http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html](http://www.glow.co.uk/uv-black-light-bulb.html)
We started using them the last few days and they made us feel really strange, I put the first time down to getting used to the light but since then it is making my husband really tired and feel really lethargic, I am not feeling too bad with them but it not having a good effect on my husband.

Can anyone else tell me if they have had this experience and if this is normal or are the lights we have are they the wrong ones? Getting a little concerned.

Thanks

### Linz — 2015-11-03T15:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-light-blubs.17053/post-181056>

Healthy,
You need to be well-hydrated - red light starts EZ s round cells then expanded by uv.
Also are you and husband leptin sensitive? Using a seasonally appropriate diet so that the wrong fats/ not enough sulphur etc don't interfere with response to light?
Many things can prevent a health improving response to UV which need to be put right first.

### Daniel — 2015-11-28T14:36:25+0200

They call this front panel... no idea what this is... Do you guys have any idea?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

Humans have a very low,negligible capacity to synthesise DHA.

# Thread: Admitting to Biohack failures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/admitting-to-biohack-failures.17059/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-27T21:42:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/admitting-to-biohack-failures.17059/post-180583>

I tried a biohack this weekend that I'm now going to chalk it up to a **failure** and share the information with the rest of you. The hack attempt is here:

The reasoning is that the benefits of the cold may simply outweigh the heat provided by the IR machine. Additionally, the nm of IR light probably mismatches the sun's IR. Third and most important, the magnetic EMF coming off of the machine is strong enough that the benefit of the infared light requires a person to be closer to the proximity of the EMF. From my measurements with my cornet, I'm suspecting that I still may be close enough within 12 to 18 inches to be getting some influence from the EMF. Most of the EMF is generated out the top and back, but there is still some coming from the front.

I started thinking about the drawbacks of the biohack when thinking about how it differs with a tanning bed. It quickly occurred to me that the EMF and artifical light generation may be sub-par for the skin, but even more important, the proximity to the EMF generated by the tanning bed. My thinking is that it would be more beneficial to get out in the mornings even under cloud cover, especially when it gets colder. I'm well cold adapted in my CT now, so I'll just use that more and up my DHA to boot. Plus, I get less $$ spent on my electric bill that contributes to their Smartmeter programs LOL--had to add that one.

In my findings online, I'm seeing that UVA often penetrates 70-80% effectiveness through cloud-cover, and some can get through windows. I also have fog to deal with at times too. I cannot verify the accuracy of what I see online in these %s, as the many conditions including time of year, latitude and altitude also plays a large role in the intensity and the wavelengths received. Plans already to get to low-EMF remote sunny locations in AZ.

For the biohack, all thoughts and opinions are welcomed.

# Thread: is epi genetics really > genetics?

### Aerose91 — 2015-10-28T06:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-epi-genetics-really-genetics.17061/post-180606>

I ask this based on my own observations. They go as such:

I started getting sick at 25 and got reeeally sick at 26, declining ever since (31 now). I kept late hours but ate paleo, was in great shape and slept 6ish hours/night.

All of my friends pulled the same schedules as me, many much worse. Add to that they would drink/party, take sleeping meds and sleep less cuz they woke up earlier for work. None of them are remotely sick.

I have a few good friends in some special op.s units of the military. I talk to my one friend, a Navy SEAL, and when he tells me about the training schedule they keep its even more awful than you'd think. Completely overworked every day, no rest, next to no sleep and awful nutrition. Just 100% stress all the time. During deployment they sleep a few hours during the day (many times going days on no sleep) and run missions all night. They call it vampire hours. These guys should be dropping like flies but he can't tell me of one guy who has gotten any type of neolithic disease. Physical injuries yes, but no diseases.

Another friend i have has the worst diet ive ever seen. For the 15 years I've known her she lives on sugar- nothing else. Im talking, one meal a day and its either cereal or gummy bears. For 15 years. She never steps foot outside and is up late every night on the computer. No health problems whatsoever.

I'm not saying these people may not have a few things under the surface but most definitely nothing remotely compared to a disabling health condition. There must be something else that makes certain people susceptible to these things, no?

I spent two years building and prepping stuff and the whole plan got shattered when i found out my brain swells at anything over 1600 feet.

I could have tried to make it back home but i only declined there and i have to try and live outside anyway possible. I recently came in contact with mold and it has started a downward spiral of over a month and counting. However, i still do c/t and earthing every day in the ocean as much as possible.

Several papers have shown that women blind to light have a reduced risk of developing breast cancer. People living in low levels of ambient lighting such as the Arctic also have a lower prevalence of breast cancer. Conversely, women exposed to light at night (eg night and shift workers) have a higher incidence of breast cancer. A simultaneous decline in serum melatonin levels with increasing tumour growth has been demonstrated in preoperative breast cancer patients. Decreased melatonin secretion has also been noted in patients with other type of cancers. Both physiological and pharmacological levels of administered melatonin demonstrate oncostatic properties. CITES: 1. Glickman G, Levin R, Brainard GC. Ocular input for human melatonin regulation: relevance to breast cancer. Neuro Endocrinol Lett 2002; 23(Suppl 2): 17–22.
2\. Lissoni P, Viviani S, Bajetta E, Buzzoni R, Barreca A, Mauri R et al. A clinical study of the pineal gland activity in oncologic patients. Cancer 1986; 57: 837–842.

I'm so confused as to why you chose this enviroment

I Just googled your condition, that sounds tough mate. It sounds like a more drastic version of problems that Dave Asprey faced. I would suggest looking into some of his protocols. Conceptually; it would make sense for you to cover your bases with the three pillars that Jack talks about, and then build on that base with subtle biohacks and products that Asprey talks about. Maybe even consider smart drugs as a temporary solution to study your way out.

Once again i'm sure this is stuff you've already considered and i'm a complete layman

### Aerose91 — 2015-11-25T00:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-epi-genetics-really-genetics.17061/post-182611>

> [Flawed said:](/goto/post?id=182012)
>
> **"Currently i have a checklist of over 60 items to make sure are done correctly each day down to minute details, the bulk of which are grounded from this site."** That's is impressive, you should be commended for your effort. Keep on keeping on, you're like Bruce Wayne in the pit, preparing to make the climb unassisted!
>
> I think that definitely epi-genetics have more of an effect on quality of existence than genetics do. Imagine a perfect set of genes, someone derived from a bloodline of folks who obeyed natures laws to a T and lived their entire lives in perfect states of health, this person would be destined for that same quality of life if their environment would allow that, then place that person in a terrible environment e.g. space. Think of there state of health after a year compared to the genetic goldmine they had at their birth, their environment was the only variable altered.
>
> Although, i think that genetic advantages definitely allow u to cope better with shitty environments. Some people simply have harder lives and more obstacles to overcome, like yourself! normally said ppl will be wise and have great character once they go through hell. Jack's the best example of this, we're all on this forum due to his wisdom and knowledge.
>
> If you traveled half way across the country why did you decide on a desert as a place to stay? sure there's no nnemf and light cycles can be easily followed but what about seafood? Also how are you gonna continue to CT in a desert?
> You've probably thought about this already to be fair but it wasnt mentioned.
>
> My heart goes out to you mate. You're clearly in a struggle. I've lived lifestyles similar to those of your friends that you described, i didn't encounter any life altering health implications but my quality of thought was terrible, my day-to-day life was void of substance and direction. Sure your friend might not be as sick as you while she chows down on her bowls of cereal, but then why, with the great hand of cards she has been dealt, is she sitting in her fucking living room eating cereal. Thats wasted potential

Hey bud,

Per your questions;

I had to choose the desert because of the dry, clean air, most notably lack of mold spores. I can't be in a forest for more than 10 minutes or so before i begin to decline. Hell, im so sensitive to airborne pollutants i can't even be on a carpet. The desert has been the best environment accross the board for people with my illness.

I had to wait until winter to be able to stay in the desert full time due to the heat in the summer. The desert in the winter is coooold. Highs in the 60's and lows in the 20's so there's plenty of cold if i want. However, as of now just a slight but too much cold is very damaging. I tried to get a little too cold last week and havent slept one decent night since.

This desert is only an hour away from the coast so there's plenty of seafood if i want it. However, i recently had a lipid panel done under the patricia kane protocol and my DHA was off the charts. I ate a high dha, ketogenic diet for about a decade before falling ill and it blew my omega 6:3 ratio far out of proportion. My doctor has me on a higher omega 6 diet and phosphatidylcholine currently to get me to the optimal 4:1 ratio.

I am not familiar with Dave Asprey, i will look into him. Thank you.

Ultimately the question i was posing was simply- if you have 10 people with the same epigenetic factors and 1 gets sick vs 9 that do not- what is the difference. Obviously those differences need to be rooted out and addressed if the person wants the best chance of recovery.

I appreciate your concern in lending a helping hand. Thanks, man.

### Allin — 2015-11-25T08:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-epi-genetics-really-genetics.17061/post-182634>

Life isn't fair and never will be. Blame it on your mother, grandmother and Edison... Remember you have your special N=1 and I have my own special N=1.

From what I understand,
1\. your DHA is too high
2\. you can not take the AM sun for more than 10 minutes
3\. CT is not an option now but you do get some cold because of the desert temperatures but only can take a little cold at a time.
4\. you need to wear glasses or contacts to function
5\. I am not sure of your hydration/dehydration status
6\. I am not sure if you can get WiFi or if your cell reception is good or bad currently.

Hi doc - I'm leaning toward the former as it seems less adverse events could happen in someone who weighs over 400 lbs... but the quantlet mentioned edema as something it helped with and the quantlet is IR and UV...

If I could just get this person to use ice packs, he probably wouldn't even have edema...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-29T02:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-someone-use-just-ir-or-ir-uv-therapy-for-lymphodema.17064/post-180667>

400 pounds tells me there is a whole lot of inflammation and protons and no electrons. Ubi Rx has what that person needs to hear.

There is always this dissonance between what you see and hear. When you are ill the dissonance is worse.

Some people look like they sound better than they actually sound, because they look confident and have good posture. A musician, who is a veteran of many auditions, will remark "Other people look awful when they play but sound great. Other people have that belabored look when they play, but you can't hear it in the sound.

CT helps you absorb UV light. You cannot help anyone who will not help themselves. And you should not intercede.

The first two biohacks that come to mind would be more higher-quality water and grounding to the earth during and after these events that are often predicted 2-4 days before impact.

[http://www.luzerneoptical.com/top-w...ical-brand/therablue-uv-hev-lenses.html?sl=EN](http://www.luzerneoptical.com/top-wholesale-optical-labs/new-products/exclusive-optical-brand/therablue-uv-hev-lenses.html?sl=EN)

### Lahelada — 2015-10-29T20:47:54+0200

### Eilee — 2015-10-29T21:53:39+0200

Ok, I misunderstand that. Confused about harmful UV's. What is the type that causes cataracts?

### Lahelada — 2015-10-30T02:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-180752>

### Eilee — 2015-10-30T02:48:31+0200

### RobH — 2015-11-09T18:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-181508>

Just got some BluTechs for my every day glasses and like them very much. They do have a slight yellow tint and a few people at work (and mostly my kids who love to tease me) have noted the tint but it isn't an issue. The TheraBlue look like they would be reasonable for indoor use around screens / fluorescent lights, etc, but remember how important it is to get them off and get your eyes out in the sun when you can! Like all lenses they block UV, and of course advertise that. Would be nice if somebody would design a lens that would just block the blues but pass UV, but that seems unlikely given the conventional wisdom...

Interesting how they break down the blue spectrum into "good blue light" and "bad blue light" sort of reminds me of the "good cholesterol / bad cholesterol" meme that is perpetuated in medicine

RobH

### Summer — 2017-06-20T20:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-216650>

Blutech is better protection. ( There are different materials in each brand that differ in filtering) but on average blutech filters 95% 400- 420nm, 73-90% of 400-440nm, 31-46% of 459-484nm. The Therablue filters 57- 99 % at 415nm, 15- 41% at 430nm and 10% at 450nm. I personally wear blutech max but right now does not come in a prescription.

### JoeT — 2017-06-20T22:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/therablue-lenses.17070/post-216655>

> [Summer said:](/goto/post?id=216650)
>
> Blutech is better protection. ( There are different materials in each brand that differ in filtering) but on average blutech filters 95% 400- 420nm, 73-90% of 400-440nm, 31-46% of 459-484nm. The Therablue filters 57- 99 % at 415nm, 15- 41% at 430nm and 10% at 450nm. I personally wear blutech max but right now does not come in a prescription.

Due to what you state above, I went with BPI 500 tints. They block 100% of 350 - 500. I think Jack has the 600s; I just couldn't pull those off in my office though!

# Thread: Sleeping Masks.. are they bad ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vangirl — 2015-10-30T01:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180750>

This may sound like a bizarre question but in the past 3 weeks I started using a sleeping mask to try to make my sleeping environment as dark as possible. W hat I found was that my eyes got scratchy and dry. I started out with a mask that laid right on my eyes and then switched to newer model that was formed so that the material does not touch the eyes, but both do the same thing. I can't help wonder if these are ruining my eyesight (although I have no signs of that.. yet) by cutting off circulation or exposure to air/ oxygen ? Does anyone know or have had the same experience ?

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T03:32:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180754>

I wear a mask most nights to keep the room as black as possible. I even sleep with a fan going to keep the room as cold as possible. I've not had the dry eye issue and do like that my eyes are kept completely in the dark. I know others who use masks and had not heard that issue.

### vangirl — 2015-10-30T03:34:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180755>

Thanks Patty Cakes for sharing! But how odd then .. I wonder what is wrong with me ?

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T03:36:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180756>

Get some more sun and get more DHA through food (fish). Both should help with dry eyes.

### vangirl — 2015-10-30T03:55:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180758>

Thanks Patty Cakes. I am doing both of those things you advise, but the thing is that my eyes were feeling fine before I started with the sleeping mask. Sometimes I wake up in the middle of the night now and my eyes are burning and scratchy. But once I take it off in the morning they get better. Im starting wonder if I am allergic to the materials...!

### Patty Cakes — 2015-10-30T04:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180761>

I use the Bucky lite or something like that for travel. It's a slightly different material than the regular Bucky and it's thinner. Maybe try a different brand and see what happens.

### Penny — 2015-10-30T18:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180793>

Yes, maybe the material - I wear some neoprene ones and they soothe my eyes actually - maybe try reading with a red incandescent lamp before bed? And more water/seafood/grounding - you also might be glycating - eye drops with l-carnosine might be helpful for that - if your blood sugar is high - or your ubiquitination rate is high, you are glycating...

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19487926](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19487926)

### vangirl — 2015-10-31T22:16:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-masks-are-they-bad.17072/post-180851>

Patty Cakes, thanks, I looked at the Bucky brand on Amazon. It says it is latex free. I then looked at mine (also bought at Amazon) and it doesn't say! I then checked the mask myself and was shocked there was no label that lists the materials used. I think I will have to chuck it.. : (

Thanks Penny! I will look into some made from neoprene, as well! Hopefully I will make a better buy this time!

Thanks for both of your input! Really appreciate it ! : )

p.s. Penny , I dont think I have high blood sugar as I eat only a small amount of carbs, but I do check it and it's most of the time in range. But I will keep this in mind ! thank you !

# Thread: Scompy's Successes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-successes.17073/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scompy — 2015-10-30T07:33:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-successes.17073/post-180766>

Thought I'd take some time to write about the successes and changes that have contributed to my successes of the past month. Most of this can be found on my Journal here.

Biohacks and Experiences:
\--Ditched my mini-EMFer for a truck that emits low EMF and doesn't have a microwave pulsing system
\--Loads of DHA-loaded sources weekly, including oysters; more raw with more spice works great
\--Starting day with 30 hour of morning light,70 grams of protein breakfast, eating ketogenic
\--Cold shower or ice bath at some point each day; cold water face dunks in the morning
\--Hella-lot more water in the day and spending the $$ to get high quality RO or spring water
\--Earthing frequently; even using bathtub to sit on at times at home due to pipe-grounding
\--Taking a morning break at work to get back out into the light for another 20-30min walk
\--Bought/tested many Blue-Light blocker amber glasses; my 6 yo son wears them too and loves them
\--Biohacked my Smart Meter with a Faraday cage
\--Biohacked my office space against some EMF, minimized some EMF, found root sources for EMF
\--Biohacked my 6 yo's room against all EMF for a very secure sleeping space
\--Had meetings with my 6-yo's Principal, provided her literature about their WiFi and EMF
\--NOTHING has been done to hardwire their school; they are dismissive; looking for new school now
\--Ran natural light + IR experiments, which I chalked up to failures... just better to get natural sun
\--Ditched all caffeine; I use a half-cup decaf coffee for its flavor poured over ice and grassfed butter
\--Sewed RF-back-reflecting caps for me and my son if we get into higher RF environments
\--Stetzerized about 14 plug outlets in my home for mitigating electrical EMF
\--Behavioral changes with turning off power at night for better sleep, less electrical EMF overall
\--Switched out bad light sources in-home with various combinations--mostly real amber bulbs
\--Even nightlights and refrigerator lights are amber; never use any monitor without my amber glasses
\--Removed some stupid older RF sources in the home, including the Xbox 360 that pulses when OFF!
\--Bought an old-school Skeleton watch without a battery....fully wind-up and people have noticed
\--Reading as much Dr.K blogs as I have time for in my downtime and the geeking out
\--Have taken 100s of measurements with all of my EMF devices and helped friends in their env's too
\--Planned many winter vacations to Arizona and Cali for sunlight
\--Biohacked video game consoles by hard-wiring controllers and turning off controller-rumble
\--More frequent cysteine sources in my foods: asparagus, garlic, bell pepper, chicken and egg
\--Many more, but are minor; many are in my journal

Specific Successes:
1\. For myself, I've never had dreams like I do now every night. Sleep has been EXTREMELY sound for the past 3 months now, where in the past it was flat-out pathetic. I should be dead or have cancer by now looking at my past behaviors, calcium effluxing as a norm and having been 300 pounds in my 20s. Now every morning, I naturally wake up 1-2 minutes before my alarm does and get outside immediately. Looking forward to daylight savings time-- I'll get able to get even more morning sun. I typically get my entire RPE exposed by looking up, down and all around the sun for a good 5min, go back in and make a high protein breakfast, then go back out as long as I can until I have to take my son to school and get to work. Lots more sun on the weekends. A huge behavioral change with me has been my indoor soccer playing; will be doing as much lunch-time outdoor as possible but others play indoors under fluorescent lights and have league games under fluorescent in the evening (typically 6-8pm range). This has been a no-go for me now and I've seen massive health benefits because I've ditched those night games and the indoor fluorescent lights for just getting out more into the sun. I've compensated by doing more at-home anaerobic/weightlifting type exercises and more yardwork on the weekends outdoors with my garden. Although I lost the majority of my bodyfat before arriving on the scene here, it's all come together for me. I had been hyper-focused on the ketogenic diet for the past 4 years as well as intermittent fasting, but at the same time, developed many gut-issues, periodic heart arrythmia and just didn't feel strong at all. Adding in my biohacks, it's fixed the problems while not having to focus on diet and exercise anymore. Using CT and light has healed my gut, I feel like I have more energy daily, more strength, heart arrythmia gone, and my mood is stable and even 24/7. The biggest benefit has been my cravings are just about gone... i used to get ravenously hungry in the evening or just after any meal. With the Leptin RX and the huge amounts of protein I eat in the morning, my ravenous feelings are under control and at times, gone totally. My body needed the extra protein and I had been starving after waking. Also, I'm 165 pounds still even though the weight is not that relevant, since I'm muscular at a 45 year old, my bodyfat is right where I want it around 10%. Now I'll push keto seasonally for the winter.

Another success has been my joints/bones. I had put my body through a lot of stress over the years, including having run a marathon. By my 40s, I got this really weird 'dry' feeling throughout my body and my joints would pop loud in my legs, arms, hands, etc. I used to take a hyaluronic acid supplement that helped little at times. But worse, it was an internal wornout feeling that no longer exists. I'm dead serious that these changes/biohacks have fixed this issue for me as a huge boon. Suspecting it's the DHA and water along with the EMF mitigations.

2\. After telling a bunch of people on Facebook about getting into morning sunlight rain or shine, and blue-blocking in the evening, a few friends have done this and reported back major improvements in their mood, with stress, depression and most important, sleep quality. One friend in particular that has Lyme's disease and disc problems and lives with a lot of pain says that the morning light alone has helped him out bigtime.

3\. My son is/was electrosensitive. I've gotten him to drink RO water, spring water and just more water in general in the day; this alone has created marked improvements in his overall heatlh and wellness. After I biohacked his room environment and play-areas, I've noticed his energy is much better over the past few months. He was easily catching colds all summer; he's been a home-body in his behaviors and very stubborn, but after working with him and explaining the benefits of sunlight, he's 'warmed' up to it more. At home, I've mitigated and reduced his video game playing, especially in the evening, and won't let him play anymore on one console that is pure RF at stupid-high levels. Also, he grounds with me on the bathtub knowing the benefits. Getting seafood into children at this age is also a challenge; i've also been able to increase his DHA load from more grassfed sources, but will be continuing to work on his taste-palette. With all of these combinational changes, I still need him to get into morning sunlight, so will be attacking too. Although I've made little progress at his school environment, they do get him outside 3x for recess during the day and I told his teacher that he hates fluorescent lights, so they have half of them turned off above him in the classroom. The WiFi RF-EMF is a major problem still at the school and it pisses me off to no end that they'd prefer Wifi over hardwiring.

I'll end it here. Dr. Kruse has changed my life in outstanding ways and many other lives in my circle as well. We all need to be extremely grateful for his insights and the expanding knowledge base that he provides to the public on a continual basis!

### Scompy — 2018-09-28T21:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scompys-successes.17073/post-251906>

Luckily, if they are using an ERT or similar thing on the gas-lines or water lines, they claim to only activate them once per month, rather than leaving them on 24/7. However, I think it's a longer term scam because even if they are doing it 1/month at the moment, they may just leave them on in the future and just change their policies without anyone knowing. I'm guessing (and hoping) at some point in the future that all of the people that opted out and had to pay for meters will have to be repaid to the public at some point in the future when the legal-folks get involved in the issue several years from now once the health-concerns reach a public majority. I look at paying to opt-out as a policy that I'm getting money back for in the future, not to mention this life we get.

The square-root falloff from nearby homes are a lower concern for me if they are far enough away and not right up near my home. I had issues with my neighbor's smart-meter in Seattle because they acted as the Smart meter 'hub' for the neighborhood and more RF/Microwave was in their place (and my neighbor was getting all types of illnesses of course). I put higher db attenuated sheets along my wall of my home versus their home to prevent the density of microwave coming into our spaces, which helped. But this was a rare case because their meters were within 35 feet of my wall to my bedroom. If it would had been 60+ feet away, I still may have mitigated, but it would have been a much better situation due to the microwave falloff. Or if my home was made of materials that could absorb or reflect easier (like concrete), it would have been fine.

### vangirl — 2015-10-31T22:27:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ideal-basal-temperature-but-lowish-blood-pressure.17076/post-180852>

I have had this all year long now and think it's really strange. I have not come across anybody who has this. .

I sleep on an earthing sheet all night and when I wake up my basal temperature is always ideal between 97.8-98.2 F. I'm not sure if this is the earthing sheet's healing effect , but my temperature stays ideal all day rising to 98.6F . But the problem is that I have lowish blood pressure that is below ideal. It runs usually in the low 100's.

When I check my blood pressure sitting versus standing , my blood pressure is always higher than sitting, so I don't think I can have adrenal fatigue. I feel like I have a lot of energy to do what I need to , but yet underneath I am very tired. I also don't sleep too well these days, which doesn't help the matter.

Has anyone had or have this ? Any suggestions on how I can raise my bp ?

As of 2 weeks ago, I am doing the light therapy in the morning, putting on blue blocker's at night, staving off my computer after 7pm. I am eating fish 3x a week, taking DHA supplements, eating coconut oil and ghee .It's cold out here now, so can't go out unclothed.

Thanks : )

> full time and sunglasses all the time. But all of that is changed since 2 weeks ago when I discovered you . I don't know how I'm going to get that strong sun now that it's Fall and shortly Winter, but I'll do what I can. Thanks! : )
>
> What about Calamari supplements? Would their purity be better than fish oil ?

Grounding sheets are great if they are used properly- they need to be grounded straight into a stake in the yard or something else. Sometimes they can be a 'collector' of EMF as well. You really need to test to be sure. If you just plug them into the wall you run the risk of receiving 'dirty electricity', even if the voltmeter reads 'zero'
.
That being said, my wife plugs her sheet straight into the wall socket ground and it gets rid of the burning she gets in her hands from MS.

It's all about your N=1

### vangirl — 2015-11-12T22:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ideal-basal-temperature-but-lowish-blood-pressure.17076/post-181822>

[http://www.earthinginstitute.net/?page\_id=2486](http://www.earthinginstitute.net/?page_id=2486)

My mom has the earthing blanket that is plugged into the wall socket and she tells me something similar. As soon as she lays her hands on the blanket, the pain from her arthritis goes away.

hey,

Dopamine not a hormone but thought this was the best place.

I re-read Jacks dopamine blog about a week back and feel like i need to do something with regards to my own dopamine levels. I definitely have a problem with procrastination. It's a great read, probably my favourite so far.

I think that managing dopamine is a big issue with my generation, we grow up with porn, video games and the internet.

Anyway i want to kill procrastination and start executing. I don't play video games or watch porn atm but still i think i carry the burden of past usage (there was one stage in my life where every night was video games and porn till i got shitty sleep at 4am, not to mention the late blue light damage!). I was without question dopamine resistant at one stage, might still be...

Jack wrote about retreating to a cold dark hole. That sounds gangster as shit and i think i'm gonna do something similar next summer, a big reset button that will be my first major biohack. But can't do that b4 next summer.

Anyway, my questions:
1) CT's effect on dopamine?
2) chasteberry extract vs procrastination?
3) Anyway to test Dopamine functioning?
4) what was it that Jacks biohack in the ground actually did? i know there has been other experiments done that do the same. is it just that they're completely void of stimuli?
5) Any advice you lot have for me to achieve optimal dopamine?

Sorry for the long post and all the questions
Thanks for reading

The cooler your surfaces are the more the biochemistry changes at deeper tissues levels because when any human surface is cooled it allows us to absorb and assimilate UV light to add more electrons to our body.

The paleo solution is fitness solution not a Rx for a medical problem. The Epi-paleo Rx is a biophysical plan to innovate optimal wellness.
Don't place a "fitness solution" on a medical problem. That would be what unwise. But that is what a gym owner would want you to believe.

UV and IR light and cold are a medical solution to a medical problem.
Cold water immersion at 14°C increased metabolic rate by 350%, norepinephrine by 530% & dopamine by 250%. Wow. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/)

Full spectrum sunlight = whole food. Blue light (iPads, phones and computers) = Funyuns. What are you feeding your body today? Get some sun!

So instead of "You are what you eat", it's more like, "You are what you absorb, repel and emit.

The best way to avoid procrastinating is to begin. If you're stuck in your past, you go forward in reverse and nothing changes. We may delay, but time won't. The most pernicious aspect of procrastination is that it can become a habit. A habit institutionalizes us to mediocrity. Action allows us to step out of the crowd of average people. # Thread: Please advise on my emf shielding efforts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liam Taylor — 2015-11-02T12:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-180969>

I live in a house where I do not have the option to turn the wifi router off, it currently sits in the room below where I sleep and I am conscious of this.

Two efforts I am making to negate against the nnemf are to put my phone and ipad in a aluminum lined shoebox with airplane mode on, and to wrap my pillow in a foil space blanket underneath the pillowcase. Do these sound like sensible and useful moves?

### Lahelada — 2015-11-02T13:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-180971>

Not sure. There is an I phone app you can download to measure. I am dubious about the actual values it gives butg it may help you to ascertain where exactly the router is and move your bed away . I would be more interested in getting a barrier under my bed.

### Scompy — 2015-11-02T22:03:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-181017>

> [Liam Taylor said:](/goto/post?id=180969)
>
> I live in a house where I do not have the option to turn the wifi router off, it currently sits in the room below where I sleep and I am conscious of this.
>
> Two efforts I am making to negate against the nnemf are to put my phone and ipad in a aluminum lined shoebox with airplane mode on, and to wrap my pillow in a foil space blanket underneath the pillowcase. Do these sound like sensible and useful moves?

Do you have an RF meter and what #s are you getting? It sounds like you don't have control over the WiFi router, else you'd just unplug it for the night. If I were in your place, I'd get a silver netting/cloth to put under the bed. But you'll see that with RF, it's insideous. Even if you block off just one wall or floor, RF can still be getting in because it doesn't behave like a square-root falloff from the source exactly. For this reason, you won't know unless you have a meter. Magnetico makes a lot of sense too.

### SpiritGuide — 2015-11-04T20:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-on-my-emf-shielding-efforts.17081/post-181158>

Jack will probably tell you to move.

Why can't you turn off the wifi router out of curiosity?
